stamina.example: He has a lot of stamina. I suppose that's why he can run for a long time.
stamina.exampleZh: 他有很強的耐力。我想這就是他能跑很久的原因。

ploy.example: The free T-shirt is really a ploy to get you inside to see their sales pitch.
ploy.exampleZh: 免費T卹其實是一種讓你進去看看他們的銷售宣傳的策略。

overalls.definition: A garment worn over other clothing to protect it; a coverall or boiler suit. A garment, for manual labor or for casual wear, often made of a single piece of fabric, with long legs and a bib upper, supported from the shoulders with straps, and having several large pockets and loops for carrying tools.

unison.definition: (acoustics) Identical pitch between two notes or sounds; the simultaneous playing of notes of identical pitch (or separated by one or more octaves).
unison.example: The unison has a pitch ratio of 1:1.

dither.definition: To tremble, shake, or shiver with cold.
dither.example: She tends to dither when faced with choices.
dither.exampleZh: 面對選擇時，她往往會猶豫不決。

inexhaustible.example: Books are inexhaustible sources of knowledge.
inexhaustible.exampleZh: 書籍是取之不盡、用之不竭的知識來源。

heifer.definition: A young female cow, (particularly) one over one year old but which has not calved.
heifer.example: Once a heifer has a calf, it is no longer a heifer; it is a cow.
heifer.exampleZh: 一旦小母牛生下了小母牛，它就不再是小母牛了；它是一頭牛。

ewe.definition: A female sheep, as opposed to a ram.

adversary.example: He prepared to fight his adversary.
adversary.exampleZh: 他準備與他的對手戰鬥。

profound.definition: Descending far below the surface; opening or reaching to great depth; deep.

victorious.definition: Being the winner in a contest, struggle, war, etc.
victorious.example: The team were met with a victorious cheer.
victorious.exampleZh: 球隊迎來了勝利的歡呼聲。

baldly.example: He stated the bad news baldly, without trying to soften it.
baldly.exampleZh: 他直率地陳述了這個壞消息，沒有試圖軟化它。

bald.example: The bald cypress is a tree that loses its leaves in winter.
bald.exampleZh: 禿柏是一種在冬天會落葉的樹。

profoundly.exampleZh: 他的思考和寫作都很深刻。

artistically.example: The cakes were artistically decorated with delicate sugar flowers.
artistically.exampleZh: 蛋糕上裝飾著精緻的糖花。

pendulum.definition: A body suspended from a fixed support so that it swings freely back and forth under the influence of gravity, commonly used to regulate various devices such as clocks.
pendulum.example: Its motion resembles the oscillation of a pendulum.
pendulum.exampleZh: 它的運動類似鐘擺的擺動。

boast.definition: A brag; ostentatious positive appraisal of oneself.
boast.example: It was his regular boast that he could eat two full English breakfasts in one sitting.
boast.exampleZh: 他常吹噓自己可以一口氣吃兩頓全套英式早餐。

predecessor.definition: One who precedes; one who has preceded another in any state, position, office, etc.; one whom another follows or comes after, in any office or position.
predecessor.example: This candidate is significantly better compared to his predecessor.
predecessor.exampleZh: 這位候選人比他的前任好得多。

lapse.example: It was a serious lapse in judgment.
lapse.exampleZh: 這是一個嚴重的判斷失誤。

ceaseless.example: Her ceaseless caterwauling alarmed every dog within a 500-foot radius.
ceaseless.exampleZh: 她不斷的叫聲驚動了方圓 500 英尺內的每隻狗。

ceaselessly.example: He worked ceaselessly to achieve his goals.
ceaselessly.exampleZh: 他為實現自己的目標而不懈努力。

climax.definition: (originally rhetorical) A rhetorical device in which a series is arranged in ascending order.
climax.example: Tom doesn't know the difference between climax and climacteric.
climax.exampleZh: 湯姆不知道高潮和更年期的差別。

climactic.definition: Of, pertaining to, or constituting a climax; reaching a decisive moment or point of greatest tension.
climactic.example: The race ended in a climactic, neck-and-neck scramble for the finish line.
climactic.exampleZh: 比賽在激烈的終點線爭奪中結束。

scramble.definition: A rush or hurry, especially making use of the limbs against a surface.
scramble.example: a last-minute scramble to the finish line

anticlimactic.definition: Lacking climax, disappointing or ironically insignificant following of impressive foreshadowing.
anticlimactic.example: The first half of the movie was captivating, but the ending was anticlimactic.
anticlimactic.exampleZh: 電影的前半段很扣人心弦，但結局卻有些虎頭蛇尾。

intellect.definition: The faculty of thinking, judging, abstract reasoning, and conceptual understanding; the cognitive faculty (uncountable)
intellect.example: Intellect is one of man's greatest powers.
intellect.exampleZh: 智力是人類最偉大的力量之一。

descent.definition: An instance of descending; act of coming down.
descent.example: We climbed the mountain with difficulty, but the descent was easier.
descent.exampleZh: 我們爬山很困難，但下山就輕鬆多了。

exodus.definition: A sudden departure of a large number of people.
exodus.example: There was an exodus when the show ended.

shed.example: A metal comb shed her golden hair.
shed.exampleZh: 一把金屬梳子梳理著她金色的頭髮。

genome.definition: The complete genetic information (either DNA or, in some viruses, RNA) of an organism.
genome.example: The double helix that encodes our genome is an elegant structure.
genome.exampleZh: 編碼我們基因組的雙螺旋是一種優雅的結構。

fraction.definition: A part of a whole, especially a comparatively small part.
fraction.example: Find the reciprocal of this fraction.

faction.definition: A group of people, especially within a political organization, which expresses a shared belief or opinion different from people who are not part of the group.
faction.example: The chief said that this faction cannot be considered dismantled.
faction.exampleZh: 首領說這個派係不能算解散。

physique.definition: The natural constitution, or physical structure, of a person.
physique.example: Yanni is known for his imposing physique.
physique.exampleZh: 雅尼以其雄偉的體格而聞名。

clammy.definition: Cold and damp, usually referring to hands or palms.
clammy.example: His hands were clammy from fright.

otter.definition: An aquatic or marine carnivorous mammal in the subfamily Lutrinae of the family Mustelidae, which also includes weasels, polecats, badgers, and others.
otter.example: The otter features in mythology and folklore across the world.
otter.exampleZh: 水獺出現在世界各地的神話和民間傳說中。

dispense.definition: To issue, distribute, or give out.

dispensary.definition: A place or room where something is dispensed.
dispensary.example: I thought it was a tea shop, but it was a marijuana dispensary.
dispensary.exampleZh: 我以為這是一家茶館，但它是一家大麻藥局。

diverge.definition: (of lines or paths) To run apart; to separate; to tend into different directions.
diverge.example: Both stories start out the same way, but they diverge halfway through.
diverge.exampleZh: 兩個故事的開頭相同，但中途卻出現了分歧。

indispensable.definition: Not admitting ecclesiastical dispensation; not subject to release or exemption; that cannot be allowed by bending the canonical rules.
indispensable.example: The law was moral and indispensable. -Bp. Burnet
indispensable.exampleZh: 法律是道德的且不可或缺的。 -Bp。伯內特

divergence.definition: The degree to which two or more things diverge.
divergence.example: An angle is made by the divergence of straight lines.
divergence.exampleZh: 角度是由直線的發散形成的。

aesthetic.definition: Concerned with beauty, artistic impact, or appearance.
aesthetic.example: It works well enough, but the shabby exterior offends his aesthetic sensibilities.
aesthetic.exampleZh: 它運作得很好，但破舊的外觀冒犯了他的美學情感。

aesthetically.definition: In an aesthetic manner; with a pleasing sensory effect.
aesthetically.example: These diacritics are unnecessary and aesthetically displeasing.
aesthetically.exampleZh: 這些變音符號是不必要的並且不美觀。

scrutiny.definition: Intense study of someone or something.
scrutiny.example: Corporate political donations are coming under heavy scrutiny.
scrutiny.exampleZh: 企業政治捐款正受到嚴格審查。

quip.definition: A smart, sarcastic turn or jest; a taunt; a severe retort or comeback; a gibe.
quip.example: That quip was just not funny enough.
quip.exampleZh: 那句俏皮話還不夠好笑。

woo.definition: To endeavor to gain someone's support.
woo.example: Algeria needs to woo Western tourists.
woo.exampleZh: 阿爾及利亞需要吸引西方遊客。

plausible.definition: Seemingly or apparently valid, likely, or acceptable; conceivably true or likely

plausibility.definition: The quality of deserving applause, praiseworthiness; something worthy of praise.
plausibility.example: The detective questioned the plausibility of the suspect's alibi.
plausibility.exampleZh: 偵探質疑嫌疑人不在場的合理性。

plausibly.example: She lied plausibly, but the police suspected her anyway.
plausibly.exampleZh: 她謊話連篇，但警方還是懷疑她。

instinctively.definition: Innately; by instinct; without being taught.
instinctively.example: Fish instinctively know how to swim; most humans don't.
instinctively.exampleZh: 魚本能地知道如何游泳；大多數人沒有。

stylistic.definition: Of or pertaining to style, especially to linguistic or literary style.

stylistically.example: Stylistically, her later novels are very different from her early work.
stylistically.exampleZh: 從風格上來說，她後期的小說與她早期的作品有很大不同。

inheritance.definition: The passing of title to an estate upon death.
inheritance.example: The Indo-European languages share various similarities as a result of their inheritance from a common ancestor.
inheritance.exampleZh: 印歐語係由於繼承自共同的祖先而具有許多相似之處。

tune.definition: To adjust (a musical instrument) so that it produces the correct pitches.
tune.example: to tune a piano or a violin

inherently.definition: In an inherent way; naturally, innately.
inherently.exampleZh: 民族國家本質上是壓迫性的。

spontaneous.definition: Self-generated; happening without any apparent external cause.
spontaneous.example: He made a spontaneous offer of help.
spontaneous.exampleZh: 他自發性地提出提供協助。

spontaneously.definition: In a spontaneous manner; naturally; voluntarily.

honk.example: They stood and observed how long it took for the other cars to honk.
honk.exampleZh: 他們站起來，觀察其他車輛鳴喇叭需要多長時間。

innovate.definition: To alter, to change into something new; to revolutionize.
innovate.example: to innovate a word or an act
innovate.exampleZh: 創新一個字或一個行為

innovative.definition: Characterized by the creation of new ideas or inventions.

innovation.definition: The act of innovating; the introduction of something new, in customs, rites, etc.
innovation.example: The others, whose time had been more actively employed, began to shew symptoms of innovation,—"the good wine did its good office." The frost of etiquette, and pride of birth, began to give way before the genial blessings of this benign constellation, and the formal appellatives with which the three dignitaries had hitherto addressed each other, were now familiarly abbreviated into Tully, Bally, and Killie.
innovation.exampleZh: 其他人的時間得到了更積極的利用，開始表現出創新的跡象——「好酒發揮了它的作用。」在這個仁慈的星座的親切祝福面前，禮儀的嚴酷和出身的驕傲開始消失，三位貴賓迄今為止互相稱呼的正式稱呼現在被人們熟悉地縮寫為塔利、巴利和基利。

abstraction.definition: The act of abstracting, separating, withdrawing, or taking away; withdrawal; the state of being taken away.
abstraction.example: Abstraction is necessary for the classification of things into genera and species.
abstraction.exampleZh: 抽象化對於將事物分類為屬和種是必要的。

connoisseur.definition: A specialist in a given field whose opinion is highly valued, especially in one of the fine arts or in matters of taste
connoisseur.example: Gustavo is a connoisseur of classic movies.
connoisseur.exampleZh: 古斯塔沃是一位經典電影鑑賞家。

tantrum.definition: An often childish display or fit of bad temper.
tantrum.example: Baby Shawn threw a tantrum when he was told the bicycle was not his.
tantrum.exampleZh: 當小肖恩被告知自行車不是他的時，他大發脾氣。

institute.definition: An organization founded to promote a cause
institute.example: I work in a medical research institute.
institute.exampleZh: 我在一家醫學研究所工作。

stiffen.example: Dad used spray starch to stiffen his dress shirts.
stiffen.exampleZh: 爸爸用噴霧澱粉讓他的正裝襯衫變硬。

wooded.example: They built a small cabin on a quiet, wooded hillside.
wooded.exampleZh: 他們在一個安靜、樹木繁茂的山坡上建造了一間小木屋。

flattery.definition: Excessive praise or approval, which is often insincere and sometimes contrived to win favour.
flattery.example: She is easily taken in by flattery.
flattery.exampleZh: 她很容易被阿諛奉承所蒙蔽。

aimlessly.definition: Without an aim, purpose or direction; in an aimless manner.
aimlessly.example: He wandered aimlessly through the crowd.
aimlessly.exampleZh: 他漫無目的地在人群中徘徊。

meaningless.example: The word "gugugu" is meaningless in English.
meaningless.exampleZh: 「gugugu」這個字在英文中是沒有意義的。

provoke.definition: To cause someone to become annoyed or angry.
provoke.example: Don't provoke the dog; it may try to bite you.
provoke.exampleZh: 不要激怒狗；它可能會試圖咬你。

vitality.definition: The capacity to live and develop.

provocative.definition: Serving or tending to elicit a strong, often negative sentiment in another person; exasperating.
provocative.example: Provocative clothing is banned at school.
provocative.exampleZh: 學校禁止穿著挑釁性服裝。

scornful.exampleZh: 湯姆做出一臉輕蔑的表情。

obscure.definition: To render obscure; to darken; to make dim; to keep in the dark; to hide; to make less visible, intelligible, legible, glorious, beautiful, or illustrious.
obscure.example: an obscure passage or inscription;    The speaker made obscure references to little-known literary works.
obscure.exampleZh: 晦澀的段落或銘文；    演講者含糊地提到了鮮為人知的文學作品。

elevation.definition: The act of raising from a lower place, condition, or quality to a higher; said of material things, persons, the mind, the voice, etc.
elevation.example: the elevation of grain; elevation to a throne; elevation to sainthood; elevation of mind, thoughts, or character
elevation.exampleZh: 穀物的海拔；登上王位；晉升為聖人；心靈、思想或品格的提升

pose.exampleZh: 我可以再問一個問題嗎？

jog.definition: To push slightly; to move or shake with a push or jerk, as to gain the attention of; to jolt.

quest.definition: A journey or effort in pursuit of a goal (often lengthy, ambitious, or fervent); a mission.
quest.example: to rove in quest of game, of a lost child, of property, etc.
quest.exampleZh: 四處遊蕩尋找遊戲、迷失的孩子、財產等。

transatlantic.definition: On, spanning or crossing, or from the other side of the Atlantic Ocean.
transatlantic.example: Lindbergh's solo nonstop transatlantic flight was a remarkable accomplishment.
transatlantic.exampleZh: 林德伯格的單人不間斷跨大西洋飛行是一項了不起的成就。

rudiments.definition: (often in the plural) A fundamental principle or skill, especially in a field of learning.
rudiments.example: We'll be learning the rudiments of thermodynamics next week.
rudiments.exampleZh: 下週我們將學習熱力學的基礎知識。

rudimentary.definition: Of or relating to one or more rudiments.
rudimentary.example: I have only a rudimentary grasp of chemistry.
rudimentary.exampleZh: 我對化學只有初步的了解。

paddle.definition: To propel something through water with a paddle, oar, hands, etc.
paddle.example: We had a nice paddle this morning.
paddle.exampleZh: 今天早上我們劃了一個很好的槳。

immensely.definition: Greatly; hugely; extremely; vastly; to a great extent.

advisory.example: The advisory committee could only offer advice, but since that was almost always accepted they had real power.
advisory.exampleZh: 諮詢委員會只能提供建議，但由於建議幾乎總是被接受，因此他們擁有實權。

adrenaline.exampleZh: 我的腎上腺素真的在飆升。

tinker.definition: To fiddle with something in an attempt to fix, mend or improve it, especially in an experimental or unskilled manner.
tinker.example: Tom likes to tinker with things because he believes there's always a better way.
tinker.exampleZh: 湯姆喜歡修補東西，因為他相信總有更好的方法。

lavatory.definition: A vessel or fixture for washing, particularly:
lavatory.example: Tom got caught smoking in the airplane lavatory.
lavatory.exampleZh: 湯姆在飛機廁所吸煙被抓。

insecure.example: He's a nice guy and all, but seems to be rather insecure around other people.
insecure.exampleZh: 他是個好人，但在其他人面前似乎相當缺乏安全感。

pinnacle.example: Is man really the pinnacle of Creation, or just a freak of nature?
pinnacle.exampleZh: 人類真的是受造物的巔峰，還是只是大自然的怪物？

reassurance.definition: The feeling of being reassured, of having confidence restored, of having apprehensions dispelled.
reassurance.example: The nurse offered a few words of reassurance before the operation.
reassurance.exampleZh: 手術前，護士說了幾句安慰的話。

drawback.definition: A disadvantage; something that detracts or takes away.
drawback.example: Poor fuel economy is a common drawback among larger vehicles.
drawback.exampleZh: 燃油經濟性差是大型車輛的常見缺點。

oversee.definition: To survey, look at something in a wide angle.
oversee.example: It is congress's duty to oversee the spending of federal funds.
oversee.exampleZh: 監督聯邦資金的支出是國會的職責。

resort.definition: To have recourse (to), now especially from necessity or frustration.

liaison.definition: Communication between two parties or groups.
liaison.example: Administrators need to establish a close liaison with employees.
liaison.exampleZh: 管理者需要與員工建立密切的聯繫。

notwithstanding.example: He, notwithstanding, persisted in his inquiries.
notwithstanding.exampleZh: 儘管如此，他還是堅持詢問。

loom.definition: A utensil; tool; a weapon; (usually in compound) an article in general.
loom.example: The Kabyle loom is very old.

structurally.example: After the hurricane, the building was structurally sound but aesthetically displeasing.
structurally.exampleZh: 颶風過後，這棟建築結構完好，但美觀程度令人不快。

structural.definition: Of, relating to, or having structure.
structural.exampleZh: 阿爾及利亞需要結構性改革。

rapport.definition: A relationship of mutual trust and respect. A close and harmonious relationship in which the people or groups concerned understand each other's feelings or ideas and communicate well.
rapport.example: He always tried to maintain a rapport with his customers.
rapport.exampleZh: 他總是盡力與顧客保持融洽的關係。

sedentary.definition: Not moving; relatively still; staying in the vicinity.
sedentary.example: The oyster is a sedentary mollusk; the barnacles are sedentary crustaceans.
sedentary.exampleZh: 牡蠣是一種久坐的軟體動物。藤壺是定居的甲殼類動物。

consultation.example: After a long consultation with her doctor, she decided to have the surgery.
consultation.exampleZh: 經過與醫生的長時間協商後，她決定進行手術。

landscape.definition: To create or maintain a landscape.
landscape.example: The software patent landscape has changed considerably in the last years
landscape.exampleZh: 軟體專利格局在過去幾年中發生了很大變化

bloom.definition: A blossom; the flower of a plant; an expanded bud.
bloom.example: The cherry trees are in bloom.

consultancy.example: For a PR campaign like never before, one needs a consultancy of unprecedented scope and power.
consultancy.exampleZh: 對於一場前所未有的公關活動，需要一家規模和實力前所未見的顧問公司。

quarry.definition: A site for mining stone, limestone, or slate.
quarry.example: Michelangelo personally quarried marble from the world-famous quarry at Carrara.
quarry.exampleZh: 米開朗基羅親自從世界著名的卡拉拉採石場開採大理石。

retention.definition: The act of retaining or something retained
retention.example: Language learning stimulates brain activity and improves memory retention.
retention.exampleZh: 語言學習可以刺激大腦活動並提高記憶力。

specification.definition: An explicit set of requirements to be satisfied by a material, product, or service.
specification.example: The parts were manufactured exactly to the engineer's specification.
specification.exampleZh: 這些零件完全按照工程師的規格製造。

query.example: The teacher answered the student’s query concerning biosynthesis.
query.exampleZh: 老師解答了學生關於生物合成的疑問。

contractor.definition: A person or company that builds or improves buildings.
contractor.example: Tom hired a local contractor to remodel his kitchen.
contractor.exampleZh: 湯姆聘請了當地的承包商來改造他的廚房。

chill.definition: To lower the temperature of something; to cool

possessor.definition: Agent noun of possess; one who possesses
possessor.example: Knowledge is a treasure that goes everywhere with its possessor.
possessor.exampleZh: 知識是一種寶藏，擁有者就可以隨身攜帶。

chilly.example: I’m getting rather chilly over here – could you shut the window please?
chilly.exampleZh: 我這裡有點冷——你能把窗戶關上嗎？

unmanned.definition: Not operated by a person or a crew
unmanned.example: an automated e-mail message sent from an unmanned mailbox
unmanned.exampleZh: 從無人值守的郵箱發送的自動電子郵件

impersonal.definition: Not personal; not representing a person; not having personality.
impersonal.example: She sounded impersonal as she gave her report of the Nazi death camps.
impersonal.exampleZh: 當她講述納粹死亡集中營的情況時，她的語氣聽起來毫無人情味。

impersonate.definition: To pretend to be (a different person); to assume the identity of.
impersonate.example: The conman managed to impersonate several executives.
impersonate.exampleZh: 該騙子設法冒充幾名高階主管。

buzzer.definition: One who, or that which, buzzes; an insect that buzzes.
buzzer.example: If you think you know the answer to the question, hit the buzzer as fast as you can.
buzzer.exampleZh: 如果您認為您知道問題的答案，請盡快按下蜂鳴器。

impersonation.example: Tom is a master of impersonation.

desert.definition: (usually in the plural) That which is deserved or merited; a just punishment or reward

stuffy.example: I can’t smell very well today – I have a stuffy nose.
stuffy.exampleZh: 我今天聞起來不太好——我鼻子不通氣。

unoccupied.definition: (of a house etc) Not inhabited, especially by a tenant
unoccupied.example: Paul checked the stall to see that it was unoccupied.
unoccupied.exampleZh: 保羅檢查了攤位，發現沒有人。

gape.definition: To open the mouth wide, especially involuntarily, as in a yawn, anger, or surprise.
gape.example: I can only gape at such perfection.
gape.exampleZh: 我只能目瞪口呆地看著這樣的完美。

toughness.example: Years of training had given the climbers both physical and mental toughness.
toughness.exampleZh: 多年的訓練，讓登山者的身體和心理都變得堅韌。

recollection.definition: The act of recollecting, or recalling to the memory; the act of recalling to memory
recollection.example: Alas that distant event isn't within my recollection anymore.
recollection.exampleZh: 唉，那遙遠的事已經不在我的記憶中了。

plumbing.definition: The pipes, together with the joints, tanks, stopcocks, taps and other fixtures of a water, gas or sewage system in a house or other building.
plumbing.example: My plumbing was playing up, so I had to see the doctor.
plumbing.exampleZh: 我的水管出了問題，所以我必須去看醫生。

usher.definition: A person, in a church, cinema etc., who escorts people to their seats.
usher.example: The usher showed us to our seats.
usher.exampleZh: 引座員帶我們到座位上。

glum.example: Robert looked glum for a long time.
glum.exampleZh: 羅伯特鬱悶了很長一段時間。

gamely.definition: In a game or plucky manner; in a willing and spirited fashion.
gamely.example: Though clearly exhausted, she gamely finished the last mile of the race.
gamely.exampleZh: 儘管她顯然已經筋疲力盡，但她還是勇敢地完成了比賽的最後一英里。

dissect.definition: To study an animal's anatomy by cutting it apart; to perform a necropsy or an autopsy.

commendation.definition: The act of commending; praise; favorable representation in words; recommendation.
commendation.example: The firefighter received a commendation for his bravery.
commendation.exampleZh: 這名消防員因其勇敢而受到表彰。

flick.definition: To move or hit (something) with a short, quick motion.

flicker.example: the flicker of a poorly tuned television set
flicker.exampleZh: 調諧不良的電視機的閃爍

thereby.definition: By it; by that; by that means, or as a consequence of that.
thereby.example: The failure was detected in time, thereby preventing a disaster.
thereby.exampleZh: 及時發現故障，避免了災難的發生。

voice.definition: Sound uttered by the mouth, especially by human beings in speech or song; sound thus uttered considered as possessing some special quality or character
voice.example: His low voice allowed him to become a bass in the choir.
voice.exampleZh: 他低沉的聲音使他成為合唱團的貝斯手。

tread.definition: To step or walk (on or over something); to trample.

unenviable.definition: Difficult, undesirable, or unpleasant; not to be envied.
unenviable.example: Valjean was in the unenviable position of either living with the knowledge an innocent would suffer for his crime or revealing his true identity and going back to prison.
unenviable.exampleZh: 冉阿讓處於令人羨慕的境地，要麼生活在無辜者因犯罪而遭受痛苦的知識中，要麼揭露自己的真實身份並回到監獄。

bribe.definition: To give a bribe to; specifically, to ask a person to do something, usually against his/her will, in exchange for some type of reward or relief from potential trouble.
bribe.example: She was accused of trying to bribe the jury into making false statements.
bribe.exampleZh: 她被指控試圖賄賂陪審團做出虛假陳述。

inclusion.definition: An addition or annex to a group, set, or total.
inclusion.example: The poem was a new inclusion in the textbook.
inclusion.exampleZh: 這首詩被新列入教科書。

bribery.definition: The making of illegal payment, or bribes, to persons in official positions as a means of influencing their decisions
bribery.example: This is what is called a 'present' in some countries and 'bribery' in others.
bribery.exampleZh: 這在一些國家被稱為“禮物”，在其他國家被稱為“賄賂”。

inclusive.example: an inclusive list of data formats

unconvincing.example: He gave me an unconvincing explanation of the fault, but I have my doubts.
unconvincing.exampleZh: 他給了我一個令人信服的錯誤解釋，但我有疑問。

conditional.example: I made my son a conditional promise: I would buy him a bike if he kept his room tidy.
conditional.exampleZh: 我向兒子提出了一個有條件的承諾：如果他保持房間整潔，我就買一輛腳踏車給他。

tone.definition: To give a particular tone to
tone.example: Her rousing speech gave an upbeat tone to the rest of the evening.
tone.exampleZh: 她激動人心的演講為當晚剩下的時間帶來了樂觀的基調。

conditionally.example: The loan was approved conditionally, provided she found a guarantor.
conditionally.exampleZh: 只要她找到擔保人，貸款就會有條件地獲得批准。

manicure.definition: A cosmetic treatment for the fingernails.
manicure.example: Sami liked to manicure his lawn.
manicure.exampleZh: 薩米喜歡修剪他的草坪。

facial.example: The facial constitutionality of the law is in question.
facial.exampleZh: 該法表面上的合憲性受到質疑。

pedicure.definition: Superficial cosmetic treatment of the feet and toenails.
pedicure.example: When was the last time you got a pedicure?
pedicure.exampleZh: 您最後一次修腳是什麼時候？

refurbish.definition: To rebuild or replenish with all new material; to restore to original (or better) working order and appearance.
refurbish.example: David took a loan to refurbish his home.
refurbish.exampleZh: 大衛借了一筆貸款來翻新他的房子。

refurbishment.example: That ship was towed into the shipyard for refurbishment.
refurbishment.exampleZh: 那艘船被拖到造船廠進行整修。

lethargy.definition: A condition characterized by extreme fatigue or drowsiness, or prolonged sleep patterns.
lethargy.example: Lethargy is a common symptom of that disease.
lethargy.exampleZh: 嗜睡是該疾病的常見症狀。

lethargic.example: The forum looks lethargic; maybe it's your diet.
lethargic.exampleZh: 論壇看起來毫無生氣；也許是你的飲食習慣。

conserve.definition: To save for later use, sometimes by the use of a preservative.

conservatory.example: Tchaikovsky enrolled at the Saint Petersburg Conservatory.
conservatory.exampleZh: 柴可夫斯基就讀於聖彼得堡音樂學院。

fiercely.example: The wind blew fiercely and the rain fell heavily.
fiercely.exampleZh: 風吹得很猛，雨下得很大。

grapefruit.definition: The tree of the species Citrus paradisi, a hybrid of pomelo (Citrus maxima) and sweet orange.
grapefruit.exampleZh: 葡萄柚會影響我的藥物嗎？

spacious.example: The apartment has a spacious bedroom.

flask.definition: A narrow-necked vessel of metal or glass, used for various purposes; as of sheet metal, to carry gunpowder in; or of wrought iron, to contain quicksilver; or of glass, to heat water in, etc.

gourmet.definition: (of food and drink) Fine; of superior quality.
gourmet.example: The restaurant offered gourmet coffee and cigars after the meal.
gourmet.exampleZh: 餐後餐廳提供美味的咖啡和雪茄。

rich.definition: Wealthy: having a lot of money and possessions.
rich.example: a rich dish; rich cream or soup; rich pastry
rich.exampleZh: 豐盛的菜餚；濃鬱的奶油或湯；豐富的糕點

sauna.definition: A room or a house designed for heat sessions.
sauna.example: The hotel has a sauna in the basement.
sauna.exampleZh: 飯店的地下室設有桑拿浴室。

clutch.definition: To seize, as though with claws.

stagger.example: the stagger of a drunken man

staggering.example: The army suffered a staggering defeat.

dutiful.definition: Accepting of one's legal or moral obligations and willing to do them well, and without complaint.
dutiful.example: Ralph was a dutiful child, and took the trash out without being told.
dutiful.exampleZh: 拉爾夫是個孝順的孩子，他不經吩咐就把垃圾倒了出去。

calorie.definition: (nutrition) Kilogram calorie or large calorie. A unit of energy 1000 times larger than the gram calorie. It is equivalent to the gram kilocalorie, approximately 4.2 kilojoules.
calorie.example: You should check your calorie intake.
calorie.exampleZh: 您應該檢查您的卡路里攝取量。

firm.definition: A business partnership; the name under which it trades.

eclectic.definition: Selecting a mixture of what appears to be best of various doctrines, methods or styles.
eclectic.example: Her eclectic taste was evident from the unusual decor of the room.
eclectic.exampleZh: 從房間不尋常的裝飾中可以看出她不拘一格的品味。

premises.definition: Land, and all the built structures on it, especially when considered as a single place.

revere.definition: To regard someone or something with great awe or devotion.

battery.definition: A device used to power electric devices, consisting of a set of electrically connected electrochemical or, archaically, electrostatic cells. A single such cell when used by itself.
battery.example: Schoolchildren take a battery of standard tests to measure their progress.
battery.exampleZh: 小學生要參加一系列標準測驗來衡量他們的進步。

waterfront.definition: The land alongside a body of water.
waterfront.example: We enjoy a jaunt to the wonderful, waterfront city of Portsmouth.
waterfront.exampleZh: 我們享受前往美麗的海濱城市朴茨茅斯的短途旅行。

amplification.definition: The act, or result of amplifying, enlarging, extending or adding.
amplification.example: The amplification of DNA via PCR is a necessary step for many kinds of tests.
amplification.exampleZh: 以 PCR 擴增 DNA 是多種測試的必要步驟。

battered.definition: Beaten up through a lot of use; in rough condition; weathered.
battered.example: The firemen battered down the door.

amply.example: As George Bush has amply demonstrated, being president of the U.S. requires only a modicum of intelligence.
amply.exampleZh: 正如喬治布希充分證明的那樣，成為美國總統只需要一點智力。

comply.definition: To yield assent; to accord; to acquiesce, agree, consent; to adapt oneself, to conform.

compliant.definition: Willing to comply; submissive; willing to do what someone wants.
compliant.example: Tendon is compliant compared to bone.
compliant.exampleZh: 與骨頭相比，肌腱具有順應性。

compliance.example: Compliance with a robber's demands does not guarantee your safety.
compliance.exampleZh: 遵守強盜的要求並不能保證您的安全。

exuberant.definition: (of people) Very high-spirited; extremely energetic and enthusiastic.

exuberance.definition: The quality of being exuberant; cheerful or vigorous enthusiasm; liveliness.
exuberance.example: What exuberance in gesture and tone!
exuberance.exampleZh: 手勢和語氣多麼豐富啊！

linear.definition: Having the form of a line; straight or roughly straight; following a direct course.

linearly.example: The effect related linearly to the amount of causative agent added.
linearly.exampleZh: 此效應與添加的致病劑的量呈線性相關。

anchored.definition: Having the extremities turned back, like the flukes of an anchor.

dominant.example: The dominant party controlled the government.

interject.definition: To insert something between other things.

gush.definition: To flow forth suddenly, in great volume.
gush.example: Water began to gush from the broken pipe.
gush.exampleZh: 水開始從破裂的管道中湧出。

voracious.definition: Wanting or devouring great quantities of food.
voracious.example: I know that many Japanese are voracious readers.
voracious.exampleZh: 我知道許多日本人都是如飢似渴的讀者。

sketch.definition: A rapidly executed freehand drawing that is not intended as a finished work, often consisting of a multitude of overlapping lines.

prop.definition: (sometimes figurative) To support or shore up something.
prop.example: Try using a phone book to prop up the table where the foot is missing.
prop.exampleZh: 試著用電話簿支撐桌子缺腳的地方。

sketchbook.definition: A book or pad with blank pages for sketching; a sketch pad.
sketchbook.example: This sketchbook is not good for alcohol markers.
sketchbook.exampleZh: 這本素描本不適合酒精標記。

imp.definition: A small, mischievous sprite, or a malevolent supernatural creature, somewhat comparable to a demon but smaller and less powerful.
imp.example: That pesky imp must be punished.
imp.exampleZh: 那個討厭的小鬼必須受到懲罰。

impish.definition: Mischievous; of or befitting an imp.
impish.example: That impish man over there is my ex-husband.
impish.exampleZh: 那邊那個頑皮的男人是我的前夫。

wicked.example: That was a wicked guitar solo, bro!
wicked.exampleZh: 那是一首邪惡的吉他獨奏，兄弟！

gusto.example: He sang with more gusto than talent.
gusto.exampleZh: 他唱歌的熱情多於才華。

tantamount.definition: Equivalent in meaning or effect; amounting to the same thing in practical terms, even if being technically distinct.
tantamount.example: In this view, disagreement and treason are tantamount.
tantamount.exampleZh: 在這個觀點中，分歧和叛國就等於叛國。

macabre.example: How can you watch such macabre movies without having nightmares?
macabre.exampleZh: 看這麼恐怖的電影怎麼能不做惡夢呢？

antics.definition: A grotesque representation of a figure; a gargoyle.
antics.example: He turned us into a laughing stock with his antics.
antics.exampleZh: 他的滑稽動作使我們成為笑柄。

impede.definition: To get in the way of; to hinder.
impede.example: A lack of motivation can impede your language learning progress.
impede.exampleZh: 缺乏動力會阻礙你的語言學習進度。

impediment.definition: A hindrance; that which impedes or obstructs progress.
impediment.example: Working in a noisy factory left me with a slight hearing impediment.
impediment.exampleZh: 在吵雜的工廠工作使我有輕微的聽力障礙。

taut.definition: Under tension, like a stretched bowstring, rope, or sail; tight.
taut.example: The walls of the tent must be taut when you set it up.
taut.exampleZh: 搭建帳篷時，帳篷的牆壁必須拉緊。

jersey.definition: A garment knitted from wool, worn over the upper body.
jersey.example: Which Houston Rockets jersey looks the nicest?
jersey.exampleZh: 休士頓火箭隊哪一款球衣看起來最好看？

slack.definition: The part of anything that hangs loose, having no strain upon it.

unwavering.definition: Never doubted; always steady and on course
unwavering.example: I'm inspired by their unwavering patience.
unwavering.exampleZh: 他們堅定不移的耐心讓我深受鼓舞。

removed.definition: Separated in time, space, or degree.
removed.example: Now that we are here one week removed...
removed.exampleZh: 現在我們已經離開這裡一週了...

startlingly.definition: In a startling manner; surprisingly; shockingly.
startlingly.example: The results were startlingly different from what we had expected.
startlingly.exampleZh: 結果與我們的預期大不相同。

buffoon.definition: One who acts in a silly or ridiculous fashion; a clown or fool.
buffoon.example: Zelensky may well be a buffoon, but he is hardly fascist.
buffoon.exampleZh: 澤倫斯基很可能是個小丑，但他絕對不是法西斯主義者。

stray.definition: Any domestic animal that has no enclosure, or its proper place and company, and wanders at large, or is lost; an estray.
stray.exampleZh: 埃利亞斯收養了這隻流浪貓。

blithely.example: As the bombs fell on the city, the woman blithely continued with her chores.
blithely.exampleZh: 當炸彈落在這座城市時，這位婦女愉快地繼續做家事。

mythological.definition: Of, or relating to myths or mythology.
mythological.example: Yanni wrote a book about mythological creatures.
mythological.exampleZh: 雅尼寫了一本關於神話生物的書。

mythology.definition: The collection of myths of a people, concerning the origin of the people, history, deities, ancestors and heroes.
mythology.example: Avunculicide is more common in mythology than nepoticide.
mythology.exampleZh: 在神話中，殺父殺比殺姦夫更常見。

venerate.definition: To treat with great respect and deference.
venerate.example: We dishonor the living and venerate the dead.
venerate.exampleZh: 我們侮辱生者，尊敬死者。

ethical.definition: Of or relating to the study of ethics.
ethical.example: The philosopher Kant is particularly known for his ethical writings.
ethical.exampleZh: 哲學家康德以其倫理著作而聞名。

veneration.definition: The act of venerating or the state of being venerated.
veneration.example: His ideas were more fierce and free; and he contemned many of the opinions which had been inculcated upon her as chiefly demanding her veneration.
veneration.exampleZh: 他的思想更加激烈、自由；他蔑視許多灌輸給她的觀點，認為這些觀點主要是要求對她的尊敬。

devotee.example: He was a devotee of Arnold Schwarzenegger.
devotee.exampleZh: 他是阿諾史瓦辛格的忠實粉絲。

zany.definition: Unusual and awkward in a funny, comical manner; outlandish; clownish.
zany.example: The otherwise dark and serious comic is punctuated by pages of zany side stories.
zany.exampleZh: 這部原本黑暗而嚴肅的漫畫不時穿插著幾頁滑稽的旁白故事。

salvation.definition: The process of being saved, the state of having been saved (from hell).
salvation.example: Collective salvation is not possible without personal salvation, but the latter is achievable.
salvation.exampleZh: 沒有個人救贖就不可能實現集體救贖，但後者是可以實現的。

salve.definition: An ointment, cream, or balm with soothing, healing, or calming effects.
salve.example: A lovely bit of cake like this is the best salve for a wounded heart.
salve.exampleZh: 像這樣可愛的蛋糕是對受傷的心最好的安慰。

salvage.definition: (of property, people or situations at risk) to rescue.

staccato.definition: Describing a passage having this mark.
staccato.example: Now, play the same passage very staccato.
staccato.exampleZh: 現在，斷奏地彈奏同一樂段。

verve.definition: Excitement of imagination such as that which animates a poet, artist, or musician, in composing or performing
verve.example: Tom's got a lot of verve, it's going to carry him to success.
verve.exampleZh: 湯姆充滿活力，這將帶領他走向成功。

motley.definition: Comprising greatly varied elements, to the point of incongruity; heterogeneous.
motley.example: A motley crew of educators and students gathered at the seminar.
motley.exampleZh: 研討會上聚集了一群形形色色的教育工作者和學生。

preach.example: A learned local Muslim used to preach in the small mosque every Friday.
preach.exampleZh: 當地一位博學的穆斯林每週五都會在小清真寺傳教。

behind.definition: At the back of; positioned with something else in front of.
behind.example: The car is behind the wall.

preacher.definition: Someone who preaches a worldview, philosophy or religion, especially someone who preaches the gospel; a clergyman.
preacher.exampleZh: 他成為伊斯蘭教傳教士。

justly.definition: In a just or fair manner; rightfully.
justly.example: He was justly proud of everything his children had achieved.
justly.exampleZh: 他為他的孩子們所取得的一切成就感到自豪。

wry.definition: Turned away, contorted (of the face or body).
wry.example: His interjection prompts wry laughs and nervousness.
wry.exampleZh: 他的感嘆引起了苦笑和緊張。

wryly.example: 1966: The sins of the father, she thought wryly. Well, they had visited Tony, all right — only he didn't know it. — Jacqueline Susann, Valley of the Dolls (Grove Press 1997, p. 206)
wryly.exampleZh: 1966年：父親的罪過，她苦澀地想。好吧，他們拜訪過托尼，好吧——只是他不知道。 — Jacqueline Susann，《玩偶谷》（Grove Press 1997，第 206 頁）

confide.example: I confide this mission to you alone.
confide.exampleZh: 我將這個使命託付給你一個人。

poetess.example: Do you know Umm Qirfa, the 80-year-old Arab poetess who was murdered by Muhammad?
poetess.exampleZh: 你知道被穆罕默德謀殺的80歲阿拉伯女詩人烏姆吉爾法嗎？

sentiment.definition: A general thought, feeling, or sense.
sentiment.example: The sentiment emerged that we were acting too soon.
sentiment.exampleZh: 人們開始認為我們行動得太早了。

sentient.example: He thinks that they are not sentient beings.

sentimental.definition: Characterized by sentiment, sentimentality or excess emotion.

tangled.definition: To become mixed together or intertwined
tangled.example: Her hair was tangled from a day in the wind.
tangled.exampleZh: 她的頭髮因一天的風吹而打結。

untangle.definition: To remove tangles or knots from.
untangle.example: With gentle combing, untangle your hair.
untangle.exampleZh: 輕輕梳理，解開打結的頭髮。

outset.definition: The beginning or initial stage of something.
outset.example: He agreed and understood from the outset, so don't bother explaining again.
outset.exampleZh: 他一開始就答應了，也明白了，所以不用再解釋了。

perceptive.definition: Having or showing keenness of perception, insight, understanding, or intuition.
perceptive.example: He is so perceptive when it comes to other people's feelings.
perceptive.exampleZh: 當談到別人的感受時，他非常敏銳。

tycoon.definition: A wealthy and powerful business person.
tycoon.example: Chairman Yu is a tycoon who owns multiple hotel chains.
tycoon.exampleZh: 於董事長是一位擁有多家連鎖飯店的大亨。

launch.definition: The movement of a vessel from land into the water; especially, the sliding on ways from the stocks on which it is built. (Compare: to splash a ship.)

engrossing.definition: Utterly consuming one's time and attention.
engrossing.exampleZh: 一部引人入勝的紀錄片

detritus.definition: (chiefly geological) Pieces of rock broken off by ice, glacier, or erosion.
detritus.example: The garnets in the detritus are accompanied by zircon, spinel, corundum, cyanite, tourmaline, olivine, etc.
detritus.exampleZh: 碎屑中的石榴石也伴隨鋯石、尖晶石、剛玉、藍晶石、電氣石、橄欖石等。

detrimental.example: Smoking tobacco can be detrimental to your health.
detrimental.exampleZh: 吸煙可能有害您的健康。

detriment.example: How quickly to my detriment cruel Luck has changed!
detriment.exampleZh: 殘酷的運氣變化得多快，對我不利啊！

domestically.exampleZh: 阿爾及利亞在國內製造飛彈嗎？

tailor.definition: A person who makes, repairs, or alters clothes professionally, especially suits and men's clothing.
tailor.example: He works as a tailor on Swanston Street.
tailor.exampleZh: 他在斯旺斯頓街做裁縫。

pathos.definition: The quality or property of anything which touches the feelings or excites emotions and passions, especially that which awakens tender emotions, such as pity, sorrow, and the like; contagious warmth of feeling, action, or expression; pathetic quality.
pathos.example: Not everyone like the pathos of Bollywood films, but I do.
pathos.exampleZh: 不是每個人都喜歡寶萊塢電影的悲情，但我喜歡。

personification.definition: A person, thing or name typifying a certain quality or idea; an embodiment or exemplification.
personification.example: Adolf Hitler was the personification of anti-Semitism.
personification.exampleZh: 阿道夫·希特勒是反猶太主義的化身。

limitless.definition: Without limits in extent, size, or quantity; boundless.

precision.definition: The state of being precise or exact; exactness.

perfect.definition: (grammar) The perfect tense, or a form in that tense.

apprenticeship.definition: The condition of, or the time served by, an apprentice.
apprenticeship.example: Tom has completed his apprenticeship as an electrician.
apprenticeship.exampleZh: 湯姆已完成電工學徒期。

silhouette.definition: An illustrated outline filled in with a solid color(s), usually only black, and intended to represent the shape of an object without revealing any other visual details; a similar appearance produced when the object being viewed is situated in relative darkness with brighter lighting behind it; a profile portrait in black, such as a shadow appears to be.
silhouette.example: I could see a silhouette of a figure looking out from the window, but I couldn't tell if it was a man or a woman.
silhouette.exampleZh: 我看到窗外有一個人影，但我分不清楚是男是女。

nondescript.example: He drove a nondescript silver sedan.
nondescript.exampleZh: 他開著一輛不起眼的銀色轎車。

negligible.definition: Able to be neglected, ignored or excluded from consideration; too small or unimportant to be of concern.
negligible.example: We found errors, but their effects were negligible.
negligible.exampleZh: 我們發現了錯誤，但其影響可以忽略不計。

negligent.definition: Careless, without appropriate or sufficient attention.

altruistic.example: His helping the old woman with her shopping was deemed highly altruistic by everyone, especially since her home was a mile away.
altruistic.exampleZh: 每個人都認為他幫助老婦人購物是非常無私的行為，尤其是因為她的家距離一英里遠。

altruism.definition: Regard for others, both natural and moral without regard for oneself; devotion to the interests of others; brotherly kindness.
altruism.example: Some have characterized altruism as selfishness on behalf of others.
altruism.exampleZh: 有些人將利他主義描述為代表他人的自私。

scenario.definition: An outline of the plot of a dramatic or literary work.
scenario.exampleZh: 米羅斯拉夫提出了一個雙贏的方案。

voluntary.definition: Done, given, or acting of one's own free will.
voluntary.example: If a man accidentally kills another by lopping a tree, it is not voluntary manslaughter.
voluntary.exampleZh: 如果一個人砍伐一棵樹時意外殺死了另一個人，則不屬於故意殺人。

paternal.definition: Of or pertaining to one's father, his genes, his relatives, or his side of a family

reap.definition: To cut (for example a grain) with a sickle, scythe, or reaping machine
reap.example: to reap a benefit from exertions

maternal.definition: Of or pertaining to a mother; having the characteristics of a mother; motherly.
maternal.example: His weakness seemed to bring out her maternal instincts.
maternal.exampleZh: 他的軟弱似乎激發了她的母性本能。

injustice.example: Silence in the face of gross injustice, or support for it, or even active involvement therein, comes at a price.
injustice.exampleZh: 面對嚴重的不公現象，保持沉默，或支持它，甚至積極參與其中，都是要付出代價的。

paternalistic.definition: The quality of being paternal, i.e. like a father, e.g. characterized by behaving in benevolent and yet intrusive manner towards underlings
paternalistic.example: As far as I'm concerned, laws that require drivers to wear seatbelts are paternalistic.
paternalistic.exampleZh: 就我而言，要求司機繫安全帶的法律是家長式的。

impoverished.example: English has an impoverished inflectional system.
impoverished.exampleZh: 英語的屈折變化系統很貧乏。

unsustainable.example: In Algeria, state-controlled economic growth is unsustainable.
unsustainable.exampleZh: 在阿爾及利亞，國家控制的經濟成長是不可持續的。

sustainable.exampleZh: 阿爾及利亞需要永續工業。

imperative.example: It is imperative that you come here right now.

bypass.definition: To avoid an obstacle etc, by constructing or using a bypass
bypass.example: Fish ladders help salmon bypass man-made obstacles, such as dams.
bypass.exampleZh: 魚梯幫助鮭魚繞過水壩等人為障礙。

cynic.definition: A person who believes that all people are motivated by selfishness.
cynic.example: the cynic, or Sothic, year; cynic cycle
cynic.exampleZh: 憤世嫉俗的或索思式的年份；憤世嫉俗的循環

cynical.definition: Of or relating to the belief that human actions are motivated only or primarily by base desires or selfishness.

sarcasm.definition: Use of acerbic language to mock or convey contempt, often using irony and (in speech) often marked by overemphasis and a sneering tone of voice.
sarcasm.example: Sarcasm is the lowest form of wit.
sarcasm.exampleZh: 諷刺是機智的最低形式。

sarcastic.example: a sarcastic quip; the teacher's sarcastic tone
sarcastic.exampleZh: 諷刺的俏皮話；老師諷刺的語氣

sarcastically.exampleZh: 德米特里諷刺地向裡瑪表示感謝。

symptomatic.example: The city's problems are symptomatic of the crisis that is spreading throughout the country.
symptomatic.exampleZh: 這座城市的問題是正在全國蔓延的危機的症狀。

degrading.example: Retrieving my dropped possessions from the mud was a degrading experience.
degrading.exampleZh: 從泥巴里找回掉在地上的東西是一次有辱人格的經驗。

degradation.definition: The act of reducing in rank, character, or reputation, or of abasing; a lowering from one's standing or rank in office or society
degradation.example: Algeria needs to combat land degradation.
degradation.exampleZh: 阿爾及利亞需要應對土地退化問題。

discourse.example: The preacher gave us a long discourse on duty.
discourse.exampleZh: 傳道人在值班時給我們做了長篇演講。

amid.definition: In the middle of; in the center of; surrounded by.
amid.example: Lucy felt a secret awe, amid all her affection for Ravenswood.
amid.exampleZh: 露西在對雷文斯伍德的深情中暗暗感到敬畏。

hedgehog.definition: A small mammal, of the family Erinaceidae or subfamily Erinaceinae (spiny hedgehog, the latter characterized by their spiny back and often by the habit of rolling up into a ball when attacked.)

nocturnal.definition: (of a person, creature, group, or species) Primarily active during the night.

hibernate.definition: To spend winter time in hibernation.

latent.definition: Existing or present but concealed or inactive.
latent.example: You have a latent problem here that will cause trouble in the future.
latent.exampleZh: 你在這裡有一個潛在的問題，將來會帶來麻煩。

classy.exampleZh: 這是一套非常優雅的服裝。

wrench.definition: A movement that twists or pulls violently; a tug.
wrench.example: Is that the right Allen wrench?
wrench.exampleZh: 這是正確的內六角扳手嗎？

reminisce.definition: To recall the past in a private moment, often fondly or nostalgically.

intuitive.example: The intuitive response turned out to be correct.
intuitive.exampleZh: 事實證明，直覺的反應是正確的。

intuition.definition: Immediate cognition without the use of conscious rational processes.

intuitively.definition: By intuition; with skill or accuracy, but without special training or planning; instinctively.
intuitively.example: Though he had never been to art school, he intuitively painted vivid landscapes.
intuitively.exampleZh: 儘管他從未上過藝術學校，但他憑直覺畫出了生動的風景。

marginally.definition: In a marginal manner, or to a marginal extent; barely sufficiently; slightly.

marginal.definition: Of, relating to, or located at or near a margin or edge; also figurative usages of location and margin (edge).
marginal.example: In recent years there has been an increase in violence against marginal groups.
marginal.exampleZh: 近年來，針對邊緣群體的暴力行為增加。

strap.definition: A long, narrow, pliable strip of leather, cloth, or the like.

scoff.definition: To jeer; to laugh with contempt and derision.
scoff.example: I'm not going to change my opinion no matter how much you scoff.
scoff.exampleZh: 無論你如何嘲笑，我都不會改變我的觀點。

fiddly.example: The buttons on the tiny mobile phone were too fiddly.
fiddly.exampleZh: 小小的手機上的按鈕太繁瑣了。

fiddler.example: The fiddler added a gentle trill to spice up the melody.
fiddler.exampleZh: 小提琴手加入了輕柔的顫音，為旋律增添了趣味。

unmusical.definition: Not musical: lacking in musical ability.
unmusical.example: Despite years of lessons, he remained hopelessly unmusical.
unmusical.exampleZh: 儘管接受了多年的訓練，他仍然毫無音樂天賦。

repertory.example: But his command of the concert repertory and the required technique is astonishing.
repertory.exampleZh: 但他對音樂會曲目的掌握和所需的技巧是驚人的。

concerto.definition: A piece of music for one or more solo instruments and orchestra.
concerto.example: I didn't know Mozart wrote a bassoon concerto.
concerto.exampleZh: 我不知道莫札特寫了一首巴鬆管協奏曲。

fanatic.definition: A person who is zealously enthusiastic for some cause, especially in religion.

acoustic.definition: Pertaining to the sense of hearing, the organs of hearing, or the science of sounds; auditory.

acoustics.definition: The physical quality of a space for conveying sound.

daunt.example: The prospect was almost enough to daunt her.
daunt.exampleZh: 這個前景幾乎足以讓她感到畏懼。

daunting.example: It was a daunting task, but it was accomplished with some forward planning.
daunting.exampleZh: 這是一項艱鉅的任務，但它是通過一些前瞻性計劃完成的。

recess.example: Spring recess offers a good chance to travel.
recess.exampleZh: 春假期間，正是出遊的好時機。

realism.definition: A concern for fact or reality and rejection of the impractical and visionary.
realism.example: What terrible tragedies realism inflicts on people.
realism.exampleZh: 現實主義給人們造成了多麼可怕的悲劇。

prolific.definition: Fertile; producing offspring or fruit in abundance — applied to plants producing fruit, animals producing young, etc.
prolific.example: Thomas Edison was a prolific inventor.
prolific.exampleZh: 湯瑪斯·愛迪生是一位多產的發明家。

fragmentation.definition: The act of fragmenting or something fragmented; disintegration.
fragmentation.example: The fragmentation of the market into tiny niches made it hard to compete.
fragmentation.exampleZh: 市場被分割成很小的利基市場，導致競爭變得困難。

duration.definition: An amount of time or a particular time interval.
duration.example: Rationing will last at least for the duration.
duration.exampleZh: 配給至少會持續一段時間。

astray.definition: In a wrong or unknown and wrongly-motivated direction.
astray.example: Pornography leads individuals astray from God.
astray.exampleZh: 色情作品使人偏離上帝。

likelihood.definition: The probability of a specified outcome; the chance of something happening; probability; the state or degree of being probable.
likelihood.example: In all likelihood the meeting will be cancelled.

durable.definition: Able to resist wear, decay; lasting; enduring.
durable.example: Cardboard is more durable than paper.

naive.definition: Lacking worldly experience, wisdom, or judgement; unsophisticated.

impervious.definition: Unaffected or unable to be affected by something.
impervious.example: The man was completely impervious to the deception we were trying.
impervious.exampleZh: 這個人對我們試圖欺騙的行為完全無動於衷。

ludicrous.definition: Idiotic or unthinkable, often to the point of being funny.
ludicrous.example: He made a ludicrous attempt to run for office.
ludicrous.exampleZh: 他做出了一次可笑的競選公職嘗試。

naivety.definition: Lack of sophistication, experience, judgement or worldliness; artlessness; gullibility; credulity.
naivety.example: I'm getting tired of your ignorant naivety.
naivety.exampleZh: 我已經厭倦了你的無知天真。

cost-effective.definition: Returning a benefit that justifies the initial investment; economical.
cost-effective.example: Algeria needs to solve its water problem with cost-effective ways.
cost-effective.exampleZh: 阿爾及利亞需要以具成本效益的方式解決其水問題。

gruesome.definition: Repellently frightful and shocking; horrific or ghastly.
gruesome.example: I saved him from a gruesome and dishonorable death.
gruesome.exampleZh: 我把他從可怕且不光彩的死亡中救了出來。

screenwriter.definition: One who writes for the screen, who writes drama for film or television; especially a professional who knows the conventions appropriate to such work.
screenwriter.example: He works in the film industry as a screenwriter.

mingle.definition: To intermix; to combine or join, as an individual or part, with other parts, but commonly so as to be distinguishable in the product
mingle.example: Ziri learnt to mingle with people around him.
mingle.exampleZh: 齊裡學會了與周遭的人打交道。

irate.example: How would you deal with an angry or irate customer?
irate.exampleZh: 您將如何應對生氣或憤怒的顧客？

fiend.definition: A devil or demon; a malignant or diabolical being; an evil spirit.
fiend.example: Religion teaches us to love everybody, be one fiend or friend.
fiend.exampleZh: 宗教教導我們去愛每一個人，成為一個惡魔或朋友。

docile.definition: Ready to accept instruction or direction; obedient; subservient.
docile.example: Pet owners sometimes castrate male animals to make them more docile.
docile.exampleZh: 寵物主人有時會閹割雄性動物以使它們更加溫順。

fiendish.example: I have come to warn you of a fiendish plot.
fiendish.exampleZh: 我是來警告你注意一個邪惡的陰謀的。

temperament.definition: A moderate and proportionable mixture of elements or ingredients in a compound; the condition in which elements are mixed in their proper proportions.
temperament.example: Sami discovered the truth about Layla's temperament too late.
temperament.exampleZh: 薩米發現蕾拉性格的真相時為時已晚。

temperamental.definition: (notcomp) Of, related to, or caused by temperament.

screenplay.definition: (authorship) A script for a movie or a television show.
screenplay.example: It was originally written as a screenplay.
screenplay.exampleZh: 它最初是作為劇本編寫的。

demise.definition: The conveyance or transfer of an estate, either in fee for life or for years, most commonly the latter.
demise.example: The lack of funding ultimately led to the project's demise.
demise.exampleZh: 缺乏資金最終導致該專案的失敗。

disillusionment.definition: A feeling of disappointment, akin to depression, arising from the realization that something is not what it was expected or believed to be, possibly accompanied by philosophical angst from having one's beliefs challenged.
disillusionment.example: There are two things in this war that are causing our disillusionment.
disillusionment.exampleZh: 這場戰爭中有兩件事讓我們感到幻滅。

overdraft.definition: To extract groundwater from (an aquifer) beyond the safe yield or equilibrium yield.

synopsis.definition: (authorship) A brief summary of the major points of a written work, either as prose or as a table; an abridgment or condensation of a work.
synopsis.example: Why didn't you provide a synopsis?

contest.example: I will contest for the open seat on the board.
contest.exampleZh: 我將爭奪董事會的空缺席位。

verandah.definition: A gallery, platform, or balcony, usually roofed and often partly enclosed, extending along the outside of a building.
verandah.example: I usually read on the verandah.

occupational.definition: Of, belonging or relating to an occupation (in any sense).
occupational.example: Boredom is an occupational hazard if you are a checkout girl.
occupational.exampleZh: 如果你是收銀員，無聊就是一種職業危害。

verdict.definition: A decision on an issue of fact in a civil or criminal case or an inquest.
verdict.example: The jury returned a “not guilty” verdict.

occupant.definition: A person who occupies an office or a position.
occupant.example: I cannot say the same of the current occupant of the position
occupant.exampleZh: 我不能對這個職位的現任佔有者說同樣的話

ventilation.definition: The replacement of stale or noxious air with fresh.

homely.definition: Lacking in beauty or elegance, plain in appearance, physically unattractive.
homely.example: I am seeking a beautiful homely girl for marriage.
homely.exampleZh: 我正在尋找一位美麗、樸素的女孩結婚。

radiator.definition: Anything which radiates or emits rays.

bureaucratic.definition: Of or pertaining to bureaucracy or the actions of bureaucrats.
bureaucratic.example: the European Union is legalistic, bureaucratic and runs by precedent.
bureaucratic.exampleZh: 歐盟是法律主義的、官僚主義的，並且遵循先例。

adjustable.example: The chair has an adjustable backrest to suit people of different heights.
adjustable.exampleZh: 椅子有可調式靠背，適合不同身高的人。

versatile.definition: Capable of doing many things competently.
versatile.example: 2004, "queenchaser1158", Horny Versatile TV/TS Wanted in Phoenix (on newsgroup alt.personals.gay)
versatile.exampleZh: 2004年，“queenchaser1158”，鳳凰城通緝性慾旺盛的多功能電視/TS（在新聞群組alt.personals.gay上）

fatigue.definition: A weariness caused by exertion; exhaustion.
fatigue.exampleZh: 食物不耐受會導致疲勞。

uninspiring.exampleZh: 正常的生活可能會讓人感到平淡無奇。

mutually.definition: In the same way, each to the other; reciprocally
mutually.exampleZh: 該合約具有相互約束力。

clutter.definition: A confused disordered jumble of things.

carpenter.definition: A person skilled at carpentry, the trade of cutting and joining timber in order to construct buildings or other structures.

carpentry.definition: The trade of cutting and joining timber in order to construct buildings or other structures; woodworking.

transit.definition: The act of passing over, across, or through something.
transit.example: the transit of goods through a country
transit.exampleZh: 貨物經過一個國家過境

circulate.definition: To move in circles or through a circuit

demography.definition: The study of human populations and how they change.
demography.example: Tom just finished writing a 300-page treatise on demography.
demography.exampleZh: 湯姆剛寫完一篇 300 頁的人口學論文。

circulation.definition: The act of moving in a circle, or in a course which brings the moving body to the place where its motion began.
circulation.example: The new bills will come into circulation next Friday.
circulation.exampleZh: 新鈔票將於下週五開始流通。

demographically.example: Demographically, the town is much younger than the national average.
demographically.exampleZh: 從人口結構來看，該鎮比全國平均年齡年輕得多。

phenomenally.definition: In a manner that is extraordinary or amazing.
phenomenally.example: The tennis player's serve was phenomenally fast.
phenomenally.exampleZh: 這位網球運動員的發球速度快得驚人。

demographic.example: Which demographic groups are under-represented in parliament?
demographic.exampleZh: 哪些人口群體在議會中代表性不足？

prophet.definition: Someone who speaks by divine inspiration.
prophet.example: Muslims believe that Muhammad was the final prophet sent to mankind.
prophet.exampleZh: 穆斯林相信穆罕默德是派遣給人類的最後一位先知。

prohibitive.definition: Tending to prohibit, preclude, or disallow.
prohibitive.example: Some countries are more prohibitive than others when it comes to hot topics like euthanasia and cloning.
prohibitive.exampleZh: 當談到安樂死和克隆等熱門話題時，有些國家比其他國家更加禁止。

acutely.example: I minored in physics at university, so am acutely aware of atomic structure.
acutely.exampleZh: 我在大學輔修物理，所以對原子結構有敏銳的認知。

prohibitively.exampleZh: 昂貴得令人望而卻步的禮物

pendulous.definition: Hanging as if from a support
pendulous.example: The willow's pendulous branches trailed gently in the river.
pendulous.exampleZh: 柳樹下垂的枝條在河水中輕輕地拖著。

fissure.definition: A crack or opening, as in a rock.
fissure.example: A cinder-and-spatter cone is building at fissure 8 as the cone continues to feed a lava flow that reaches the ocean at Kapoho during ongoing eruptions of the Kilauea Volcano in Hawaii.
fissure.exampleZh: 在夏威夷基拉韋厄火山持續噴發期間，火山灰和飛濺錐體正在裂縫 8 處形成，該錐體繼續注入熔岩流，最終到達卡波霍的海洋。

aboard.definition: On board of; onto or into a ship, boat, train, plane.
aboard.example: We all went aboard the ship.

drill.definition: To create (a hole) by removing material with a drill (tool).
drill.example: Drill a small hole to start the screw in the right direction.
drill.exampleZh: 鑽一個小孔，以正確的方向旋入螺絲。

suspension.definition: The act of suspending, or the state of being suspended.

anthropology.definition: The holistic scientific and social study of humanity, mainly using ethnography as its method.
anthropology.example: According to anthropology, there are six basic patterns of kinship terminology (i.e., "kin naming systems"): Sudanese, Hawaiian, Eskimo, Crow, Omaha, and Iroquois.
anthropology.exampleZh: 根據人類學，親屬稱謂（即「親屬命名系統」）有六種基本模式：蘇丹語、夏威夷語、愛斯基摩語、克羅語、奧馬哈語和易洛魁語。

anthropologist.definition: One who is versed in anthropology.
anthropologist.example: Tassadit Yacine is an Algerian anthropologist.
anthropologist.exampleZh: 塔薩迪特·亞辛 (Tassadit Yacine) 是一位阿爾及利亞人類學家。

adjoining.definition: Being in contact at some point or line; joining to

adjoin.definition: To be in contact or connection with.
adjoin.example: The living room and dining room adjoin each other.

graft.definition: To insert (a graft) in a branch or stem of another tree; to propagate by insertion in another stock; also, to insert a graft upon.
graft.example: The graft started expanding in size.
graft.exampleZh: 移植物的尺寸開始擴大。

wizened.definition: Withered; lean and wrinkled by shrinkage as from age or illness.
wizened.example: The wizened, old sorcerer took me under his wing and taught me everything he knew about magic.
wizened.exampleZh: 那個乾癟的老巫師把我保護在他的羽翼之下，並教我他所知道的關於魔法的一切。

orchard.definition: A garden or an area of land for the cultivation of fruit or nut trees.
orchard.exampleZh: 彼得羅的果園看起來幾乎被遺棄了。

folktale.definition: A tale or story that is part of the oral tradition of a people or a place.
folktale.example: A folktale wasn't created by a single known author. It arose among the people.
folktale.exampleZh: 民間故事並不是某個知名作家創作的。它在民間興起。

hypocrisy.definition: The contrivance of a false appearance of virtue or goodness, while concealing real character or inclinations, especially with respect to religious and moral beliefs; hence in general sense, dissimulation, pretence, sham.
hypocrisy.exampleZh: 虛偽真的讓我心煩意亂。

hypocrite.definition: Someone who practices hypocrisy, who pretends to hold beliefs, or whose actions are not consistent with their claimed beliefs.

hypocritical.definition: Characterized by hypocrisy or being a hypocrite.

midwife.definition: A person, usually a woman, who is trained to assist women in childbirth, but who is not a physician.
midwife.example: A hundred years ago, a midwife would bring the baby into the world - going to a hospital to deliver a baby was either impossible or unheard of.
midwife.exampleZh: 一百年前，助產士將嬰兒帶到這個世界上——去醫院接生要么是不可能的，要么是聞所未聞的。

uniformity.definition: The state of being uniform, alike and lacking variety.

tastebud.definition: Any of the small organs on the tongue used for tasting.
tastebud.example: The chilli was so hot it seemed to set every tastebud on fire.
tastebud.exampleZh: 辣椒太辣了，似乎讓每個味蕾都著火了。

numb.definition: Physically unable to feel, not having the power of sensation.

chart.definition: To draw a chart or map of.
chart.example: Let's chart how we're going to get from here to there.
chart.exampleZh: 讓我們繪製一下我們將如何從這裡到達那裡。

insubordinate.example: Why are you so insubordinate to your boss?
insubordinate.exampleZh: 為什麼這麼不服從你的老闆？

insubordination.definition: The quality or state of being insubordinate; disobedience to lawful authority; specifically, an employee's failure or refusal to comply with a request or an assignment given by his/her supervisor.

subordinate.example: In the sentence “The barbecue finished before John arrived”, the subordinate clause “before John arrived” specifies the time of the main clause, “The barbecue finished”.
subordinate.exampleZh: 在句子“燒烤在約翰到達之前完成”中，從句“在約翰到達之前”指定了主句“燒烤完成”的時間。

embark.definition: To get on a boat or ship or (outside the USA) an aeroplane.

contextual.definition: Of, pertaining to, or depending on the context of information; relating to the situation or location in which the information was found.

whilst.definition: (rare or literary in North America) While, at the same time.
whilst.example: Drivers must switch off engines whilst on stand.
whilst.exampleZh: 駕駛員必須在停車時關閉引擎。

plunge.definition: To thrust into liquid, or into any penetrable substance; to immerse.
plunge.example: to plunge the body into water

debut.definition: A performer's first performance to the public, in sport, the arts or some other area.
debut.example: Since making its debut two years ago, the program has gained cult status.
debut.exampleZh: 自兩年前首次亮相以來，該節目已獲得了狂熱的關注。

turmoil.definition: A state of great disorder or uncertainty.
turmoil.example: Morocco is plunged into domestic turmoil.

convoluted.definition: Having numerous overlapping coils or folds; convolute.
convoluted.example: He gave a convoluted explanation that amounted to little more than a weak excuse for his absence.
convoluted.exampleZh: 他給了一個令人費解的解釋，只不過是他缺席的一個軟弱的藉口。

scathing.example: Her scathing putdown made it clear what she thought of his comments.
scathing.exampleZh: 她嚴厲的貶低清楚地表明了她對他的評論的看法。

undaunted.example: They move forward, undaunted by the challenges they face.
undaunted.exampleZh: 他們勇往直前，毫不畏懼所面臨的挑戰。

deprivation.definition: The act of depriving, dispossessing, or bereaving; the act of deposing or divesting of some dignity.
deprivation.example: He was suffering from deprivation of sleep.

farce.definition: A style of humor marked by broad improbabilities with little regard to regularity or method.
farce.example: The farce that we saw last night had us laughing and shaking our heads at the same time.
farce.exampleZh: 昨晚看到的鬧劇讓我們一邊笑一邊搖頭。

portray.definition: To paint or draw the likeness of.
portray.example: I will portray a king on horseback.
portray.exampleZh: 我將描繪一位騎在馬背上的國王。

gutsy.definition: Marked by courage and determination in the face of difficulties or danger; having guts
gutsy.example: People are cool when they're gutsy, doesn't matter if they're men or women.
gutsy.exampleZh: 人只要有勇氣就很酷，不管是男人還是女人。

elude.definition: To evade, or escape from someone or something, especially by using cunning or skill
elude.example: Emotions often elude scientific explanation, revealing their complexity.
elude.exampleZh: 情緒常常無法用科學解釋，揭示其複雜性。

incisor.definition: One of the front teeth of mammals, between the canines.

chiselled.example: She chiselled a sculpture out of the block of wood.
chiselled.exampleZh: 她用木塊鑿出了一座雕塑。

incision.definition: A cut, especially one made by a scalpel or similar medical tool in the context of surgical operation; the scar resulting from such a cut.
incision.example: The surgeon made a small incision just below the umbilicus.
incision.exampleZh: 外科醫生在肚臍下方做了一個小切口。

elongate.definition: To make long or longer by pulling and stretching; to make elongated.
elongate.example: Painted turtles lay oval, elongate eggs.
elongate.exampleZh: 錦龜產下橢圓形、細長的蛋。

telltale.definition: Revealing something, especially something not intended to be known.
telltale.example: He blushed when he approached, a telltale sign that he was happy to see him.
telltale.exampleZh: 當他走近時，他臉紅了，這表明他很高興見到他。

glean.definition: To collect (grain, grapes, etc.) left behind after the main harvest or gathering.

lust.definition: A feeling of strong desire, especially such a feeling driven by sexual arousal.
lust.example: Seeing Kim fills me with a passionate lust.
lust.exampleZh: 看到金讓我充滿了強烈的慾望。

unsettle.example: Don't unsettle the horses or they'll bolt.
unsettle.exampleZh: 不要驚動馬，否則它們會逃跑。

suburbia.definition: The suburbs and all that pertains to or characterizes them; the suburbs as represented or encapsulated by their typical qualities or characteristics.
suburbia.example: Nicolette loved living in suburbia: not too far from the city, but not too close, either.
suburbia.exampleZh: 尼可萊特喜歡住在郊區：離城市不太遠，但也不太近。

suburban.definition: Relating to or characteristic of or situated on the outskirts of a city.
suburban.example: Karl was uncomfortable with the suburban lifestyle.
suburban.exampleZh: 卡爾對郊區的生活方式感到不舒服。

quirky.definition: Given to quirks or idiosyncrasies; strange in a somewhat silly, awkward manner, potentially cute.

dramatist.definition: A writer and creator of theatrical plays.
dramatist.example: Shakespeare is recognized by all as the greatest dramatist.
dramatist.exampleZh: 莎士比亞被公認為最偉大的劇作家。

ingenuity.definition: The ability to solve difficult problems, often in original, clever, and inventive ways.
ingenuity.example: Ingenuity is one of the characteristics of a beaver.
ingenuity.exampleZh: 聰明才智是海狸的特徵之一。

sprawl.exampleZh: 城市擴張對環境造成破壞。

panorama.definition: An unbroken view of an entire surrounding area.
panorama.example: A picturesque panorama opened before us.
panorama.exampleZh: 一幅如畫的全景展現在我們面前。

panoramic.example: A restaurant with a panoramic view is located inside the tower.

legacy.definition: Money or property bequeathed to someone in a will.
legacy.example: John Muir left as his legacy an enduring spirit of respect for the environment.
legacy.exampleZh: 約翰·繆爾留下了一種持久的尊重環境的精神作為他的遺產。

elastic.definition: An elastic material used in clothing, particularly in waistbands and cuffs.
elastic.example: running shorts use elastic to eliminate the need for a belt
elastic.exampleZh: 跑步短褲採用鬆緊帶，無需繫腰帶

synthetic.example: This synthetic voice sounds very robotic.
synthetic.exampleZh: 這種合成聲音聽起來非常機械化。

appalling.example: That was an appalling waste of money.
appalling.exampleZh: 這是一種令人震驚的金錢浪費。

pounce.definition: To sprinkle or rub with pounce powder.
pounce.example: to pounce paper, or a pattern

abound.example: Wild animals abound wherever man does not stake his claim.
abound.exampleZh: 只要人類不提出主張，野生動物就會大量存在。

sap.definition: The juice of plants of any kind, especially the ascending and descending juices or circulating fluid essential to nutrition.
sap.example: In spring, sap is extracted from the birch trees.
sap.exampleZh: 春天，從白樺樹中提取汁液。

monotonous.definition: Having an unvarying tone or pitch.
monotonous.example: Reality without humor would be monotonous.
monotonous.exampleZh: 沒有幽默的現實將會是單調的。

ingenious.definition: Displaying genius or brilliance; tending to invent.
ingenious.example: This fellow is ingenious; he fixed a problem I didn't even know I had.
ingenious.exampleZh: 這傢伙很聰明；他解決了一個我甚至不知道的問題。

monotony.definition: Tedium as a result of repetition or a lack of variety.
monotony.example: Tom looks forward to his lunchtime run, to break up the monotony of his working day.
monotony.exampleZh: 湯姆期待著午餐時間跑步，以打破工作日的單調。

cessation.definition: A ceasing or discontinuance, for example of an action, whether temporary or final.
cessation.example: Whatever is subject to origination is all subject to cessation.
cessation.exampleZh: 凡有生起的事物，皆有滅盡。

remorse.definition: A feeling of regret or sadness for doing wrong or sinning.
remorse.exampleZh: 安東尼奧表現出悔恨之情。

remorseless.example: Bruno reacted in a very remorseless way.
remorseless.exampleZh: 布魯諾的反應非常無情。

inexcusable.exampleZh: 這種混亂是不可原諒的。

heighten.definition: To make high; to raise higher; to elevate.
heighten.example: to heighten a flavor or a tint

unassisted.example: After months of therapy, he could finally walk unassisted.
unassisted.exampleZh: 經過幾個月的治療，他終於可以獨立行走了。

bulky.definition: Being large in size, mass, or volume.
bulky.example: Don't throw large or bulky items down the garbage chute.
bulky.exampleZh: 不要將大件或笨重的物品丟進垃圾槽。

bulk.example: I understood the bulk of what you were saying, just one of two points I need to hear again.
bulk.exampleZh: 我明白你所說的大部分內容，只是我需要再次聽到的兩點之一。

complementary.definition: Acting as a complement; making up a whole with something else.
complementary.example: Complementary colors are opposite one another on a color wheel.
complementary.exampleZh: 互補色在色輪上彼此相對。

complimentary.definition: In the nature of a compliment.

staple.definition: A town containing merchants who have exclusive right, under royal authority, to purchase or produce certain goods for export; also, the body of such merchants seen as a group.
staple.example: Rice is a staple in the diet of many cultures.
staple.exampleZh: 米是許多文化中的主食。

testament.definition: A solemn, authentic instrument in writing, by which a person declares his or her will as to disposal of his or her inheritance (estate and effects) after his or her death, benefiting specified heir(s).
testament.example: The ancient aqueducts are a testament to the great engineering skill of the Roman Empire.
testament.exampleZh: 古老的渡槽證明了羅馬帝國偉大的工程技術。

succulent.example: The pork tenderloin at Benedito's restaurant is succulent and juicy.
succulent.exampleZh: Benedito's 餐廳的豬裡肌肉肉質多汁。

tranquil.definition: Free from emotional or mental disturbance.
tranquil.exampleZh: 野花點綴著寧靜的草地。

spartan.example: I went on the retreat to the monastery, thinking I would be sleeping in a spartan cell, only to discover a simple but comfortable bedroom.
spartan.exampleZh: 我到修道院靜修，以為自己會睡在一間簡陋的牢房裡，卻發現一間簡單但舒適的臥室。

peninsula.definition: A piece of land projecting into water from a larger land mass.
peninsula.example: The peninsula has been inhabited since prehistoric times.
peninsula.exampleZh: 該半島自史前時代起就有人居住。

billiards.definition: (games) A two-player cue sport played with two cue balls and one red ball, on a snooker sized table.

mickey.definition: A small bottle of liquor, holding 375 ml or 13 oz., typically shaped to fit in one's pocket.
mickey.example: My favourite Disney character is not Mickey Mouse.
mickey.exampleZh: 我最喜歡的迪士尼角色不是米老鼠。

farewell.definition: A wish of happiness or safety at parting, especially a permanent departure
farewell.example: a farewell discourse;  the band's farewell tour
farewell.exampleZh: 告別演說；  樂團的告別巡演

citizen.definition: A resident of a city or town, especially one with legally-recognized rights or duties.
citizen.example: Diogenes reckoned himself a citizen of the world.
citizen.exampleZh: 狄奧根尼認為自己是世界公民。

clown.definition: A slapstick performance artist often associated with a circus and usually characterized by bright, oversized clothing, a red nose, face paint, and a brightly colored wig.

cheated.definition: To violate rules in order to gain advantage from a situation.
cheated.example: My brother flunked biology because he cheated on his mid-term.
cheated.exampleZh: 我哥哥生物沒及格，因為他期中考作弊。

humanity.definition: Mankind; human beings as a group.
humanity.example: Philosophy is a humanity while psychology is a science.
humanity.exampleZh: 哲學是一門人文學科，而心理學是一門科學。

technically.example: Technically he was Canadian, but everyone assumed he was American.
technically.exampleZh: 從技術上講，他是加拿大人，但每個人都認為他是美國人。

oxygen.definition: The chemical element (symbol O) with an atomic number of 8 and relative atomic mass of 15.9994. It is a colorless and odorless gas.

profile.definition: The outermost shape, view, or edge of an object.
profile.example: His fingers traced the profile of the handle.
profile.exampleZh: 他的手指描繪著手柄的輪廓。

scientific.definition: Of, or having to do with science.

chaos.definition: The unordered state of matter in classical accounts of cosmogony.
chaos.example: After the earthquake, the local hospital was in chaos
chaos.exampleZh: 地震發生後，當地醫院一片混亂

stubborn.example: Blood can make a very stubborn stain on fabrics if not washed properly.
stubborn.exampleZh: 如果清洗不當，血跡會在織物上留下非常頑固的污漬。

rocket.exampleZh: 齊裡又發射了一枚火箭。

august.example: an august patron of the arts
august.exampleZh: 一位威嚴的藝術贊助人

intense.exampleZh: 緊張的學習；  強烈的思考

invitation.definition: The act of inviting; solicitation; the requesting of a person's company.
invitation.example: an invitation to a party, to a dinner, or to visit a friend
invitation.exampleZh: 參加聚會、晚宴或拜訪朋友的邀請

terrorist.definition: A person, group, or organization that uses violent action, or the threat of violent action, to further political goals.

respond.example: to respond to a question or an argument

procedure.definition: A particular method for performing a task.
procedure.example: Ensure that you follow procedure when accessing customers' personal information.
procedure.exampleZh: 確保您在存取客戶的個人資訊時遵循程序。

investigate.definition: To inquire into or study in order to ascertain facts or information.
investigate.example: to investigate the causes of natural phenomena

tragedy.definition: A drama or similar work, in which the main character is brought to ruin or otherwise suffers the extreme consequences of some tragic flaw or weakness of character.
tragedy.exampleZh: 這場悲劇是可以避免的嗎？

session.definition: An informal gathering of musicians to play music, especially improvised jazz or a similar genre.
session.example: "Are we having a recording session?" / "Yes. We've even got some session musicians to provide some brass."
session.exampleZh: “我們有錄音嗎？” /“是的。我們甚至還有一些會議音樂家來提供一些銅管樂。”

capture.definition: An act of capturing; a seizing by force or stratagem.
capture.example: the capture of a lover's heart

stretch.example: I was right in the middle of a stretch when the phone rang.
stretch.exampleZh: 正當我正在伸懶腰的時候，電話響了。

unable.definition: Not able; lacking a certain ability.
unable.example: Are you unable to mind your own business or something?

dates.definition: The fruit of the date palm, Phoenix dactylifera, somewhat in the shape of an olive, containing a soft, sweet pulp and enclosing a hard kernel.
dates.example: We made a nice cake from dates.
dates.exampleZh: 我們用棗做了一個漂亮的蛋糕。

bottles.definition: A container, typically made of glass or plastic and having a tapered neck, used primarily for holding liquids.
bottles.example: Beer is often sold in bottles.
bottles.exampleZh: 啤酒通常以瓶裝形式出售。

whirring.definition: To move or vibrate (something) with a buzzing sound.
whirring.example: The fan is making a whirring sound.

grabbed.definition: To grip suddenly; to seize; to clutch.
grabbed.example: I grabbed her hand to pull her back from the cliff edge.
grabbed.exampleZh: 我抓住她的手，將她從懸崖邊拉了回來。

shocked.definition: To cause to be emotionally shocked, to cause (someone) to feel surprised and upset.
shocked.exampleZh: 這場災難震驚了全世界。

meanwhile.example: Meanwhile, the circumstances have changed drastically.
meanwhile.exampleZh: 同時，情況發生了巨大變化。

reverend.definition: A member of the Christian clergy; a minister.
reverend.example: By the chalice of Jesus, Reverend hermit, what is this infernal liquor?
reverend.exampleZh: 以耶穌的聖杯起誓，隱士牧師，這地獄之酒是什麼？

scale.definition: A ladder; a series of steps; a means of ascending.
scale.example: Please rate your experience on a scale from 1 to 10.
scale.exampleZh: 請按照 1 到 10 的等級對您的體驗進行評分。

academy.definition: (usually capitalized) The garden where Plato taught.
academy.example: the military academy at West Point; a riding academy; the Academy of Music.; a music academy; a language academy
academy.exampleZh: 西點軍校；馬術學院；音樂學院。音樂學院；語言學院

exists.definition: (stative) to be; have existence; have being or reality

judges.definition: A public official whose duty it is to administer the law, especially by presiding over trials and rendering judgments; a justice.
judges.example: At a boxing match, the decision of the judges is final.
judges.exampleZh: 在拳擊比賽中，裁判的決定是最終決定。

furniture.definition: Large movable item(s), usually in a room, which enhance(s) the room's characteristics, functionally or decoratively.
furniture.example: A chair is furniture. Sofas are also furniture.
furniture.exampleZh: 椅子是家具。沙發也是家具。

ralph.example: As Ralph Waldo Emerson puts it, "language is fossil poetry".
ralph.exampleZh: 正如拉爾夫·沃爾多·愛默生所說，「語言是詩歌化石」。

consequences.definition: That which follows something on which it depends; that which is produced by a cause.
consequences.example: I'm warning you. If you don't get me the report on time, there will be consequences.
consequences.exampleZh: 我警告你。如果你沒有準時給我報告，就會產生後果。

shark.definition: A scaleless, predatory fish of the superorder Selachimorpha, with a cartilaginous skeleton and 5 to 7 gill slits on each side of its head.

transport.example: The transport of goods is not included in the price given on the website.
transport.exampleZh: 網站上給出的價格不包含貨物運輸。

caroline.definition: An old silver coin of Italy.
caroline.example: Caroline Herschel was a singer, mathematician and astronomer.
caroline.exampleZh: 卡洛琳·赫歇爾是一位歌手、數學家和天文學家。

population.definition: The people living within a political or geographical boundary.
