population.example: The population of New Jersey will not stand for this!
population.exampleZh: 新澤西州的人民不會容忍這一點！

sneak.definition: One who sneaks; one who moves stealthily to acquire an item or information.
sneak.example: My little brother is such a sneak; yesterday I caught him trying to look through my diary.
sneak.exampleZh: 我的弟弟真是個鬼鬼祟祟的人；昨天我發現他試圖翻看我的日記。

destruction.example: The destruction of the condemned building will take place at noon.
destruction.exampleZh: 這座被譴責的建築將於中午時分被摧毀。

lightning.definition: A flash of light produced by short-duration, high-voltage discharge of electricity within a cloud, between clouds, or between a cloud and the earth.
lightning.example: Although we did not see the lightning, we did hear the thunder.
lightning.exampleZh: 雖然我們沒有看到閃電，但我們確實聽到了雷聲。

punished.definition: To cause to suffer for crime or misconduct, to administer disciplinary action.
punished.example: If a prince violates the law, then he must be punished like an ordinary person.
punished.exampleZh: 如果王子觸犯了法律，那麼他就必須像普通人一樣受到懲罰。

chamber.definition: A room or set of rooms, particularly:
chamber.example: The resolution, which speedily passed the Senate, was unable to gain a majority in the lower chamber.
chamber.exampleZh: 該決議很快在參議院獲得通過，但未能在眾議院獲得多數票。

instance.definition: Urgency of manner or words; an urgent request; insistence.
instance.example: It sends some precious instance of itself/ After the thing it loves. Hamlet IV. v. ca. 1602
instance.exampleZh: 它在它所愛的事物之後發送一些珍貴的自身實例。哈姆雷特四世。 v. ca. 1602

romance.definition: A story relating to chivalry; a story involving knights, heroes, adventures, quests, etc.

exhausted.definition: To draw or let out wholly; to drain off completely
exhausted.example: Moisture of the earth is exhausted by evaporation.
exhausted.exampleZh: 地球上的水分透過蒸發而耗盡。

testify.definition: To make a declaration, or give evidence, under oath.

studied.exampleZh: 她經過深思熟慮地回答。

walker.definition: The agent noun of to walk: a person who walks or a thing which walks, especially a pedestrian or a participant in a walking race.

unravel.example: Mother couldn't unravel the ball of wool after the cat had played with it.
unravel.exampleZh: 貓玩過毛線球後，媽媽就無法將它解開。

unpromising.example: From unpromising beginnings, he went on to become a respected leader.
unpromising.exampleZh: 他從一開始不起眼，後來成為一位受人尊敬的領導人。

outpatient.definition: A patient who receives treatment at a hospital or clinic but is not admitted overnight; a receiver of ambulatory care.
outpatient.example: Larry is participating in an outpatient treatment program.
outpatient.exampleZh: 拉里正在參加門診治療計畫。

constrained.example: We are constrained to and restrained from an action.
constrained.exampleZh: 我們受到某項行動的約束和限制。

indignant.definition: Showing anger or indignation, especially at something unjust or wrong.

eccentricity.definition: The quality of being eccentric or odd; any eccentric behaviour.
eccentricity.example: For an ellipse, the eccentricity is the ratio of the distance from the center to a focus divided by the length of the semi-major axis.
eccentricity.exampleZh: 對於橢圓，偏心率是從中心到焦點的距離除以半長軸的長度的比值。

locomotive.definition: The power unit of a train that pulls the coaches or wagons.
locomotive.example: The locomotive was decoupled from the railway carriages.

locomotion.definition: The ability to move from place to place, or the act of doing so.
locomotion.example: A car is a means of locomotion.

recluse.definition: A person who lives in self-imposed isolation or seclusion from the world, especially for religious purposes; a hermit

stoicism.definition: A school of philosophy popularized during the Roman Empire that emphasized reason as a means of understanding the natural state of things, or logos, and as a means of freeing oneself from emotional distress.
stoicism.example: There's more to Stoicism than Marcus Aurelius and Epictetus.
stoicism.exampleZh: 斯多葛主義比馬可·奧勒留和愛比克泰德更重要。

reclusive.definition: Of, characterized by, or preferring privacy and isolation; secluded.
reclusive.example: North Korea is a reclusive country.
reclusive.exampleZh: 北韓是一個與世隔絕的國家。

facsimile.example: The facsimile proved that the inscription was genuine.

whir.definition: To move or vibrate (something) with a buzzing sound.
whir.example: The scientist noted the microphone picked up a gust of wind, along with a gentle “whir” sound from the rover itself.
whir.exampleZh: 這位科學家指出，麥克風捕捉到了一陣風，以及火星車本身發出的輕微的「呼呼」聲。

puritanical.definition: Of or pertaining to the Puritans, or to their doctrines and practice.
puritanical.example: My parents had a very puritanical view towards religion.
puritanical.exampleZh: 我的父母對宗教有非常清教徒的看法。

disdainful.definition: Showing contempt or scorn; having a pronounced lack of concern for others viewed as unworthy.
disdainful.example: He was disdainful of those he thought of as the little people. He openly sneered at them. They mocked him behind his back.
disdainful.exampleZh: 他鄙視那些他認為是小人物的人。他公開地嘲笑他們。他們在背後嘲笑他。

zoology.definition: The part of biology relating to the animal kingdom, including the structure, embryology, evolution, classification, habits, and distribution of all animals, both living and extinct.
zoology.example: Tom is planning on majoring in zoology.

brood.definition: To keep an egg warm to make it hatch.
brood.example: In some species of birds, both the mother and father brood the eggs.
brood.exampleZh: 在某些鳥類中，母親和父親都會孵蛋。

encumber.definition: To load down something with a burden
encumber.example: In the mean time Larry, now as happy as an emperor, was tripping round the room without any shoes to encumber him as he withdrew the plates from the table.
encumber.exampleZh: 與此同時，拉里現在像皇帝一樣高興，他在房間裡走來走去，從桌子上拿走盤子，沒有鞋子阻礙他。

hypothesize.definition: To believe or assert on uncertain grounds.
hypothesize.example: Please read the manual and hypothesize 3 possible outcomes.
hypothesize.exampleZh: 請閱讀手冊並假設3種可能的結果。

parasitic.definition: Of or pertaining to a biological or symbolic parasite.

plumage.definition: Layer or collection of feathers covering a bird’s body; feathers used ornamentally.
plumage.example: Tawny owls are well camouflaged by the colour of their plumage.
plumage.exampleZh: 黃褐色的貓頭鷹被羽毛的顏色偽裝得很好。

hypothesis.definition: Used loosely, a tentative conjecture explaining an observation, phenomenon or scientific problem that can be tested by further observation, investigation and/or experimentation. As a scientific term of art, see the attached quotation. Compare to theory, and quotation given there.

utilitarian.example: His callously utilitarian outlook doesn't earn him many friends.
utilitarian.exampleZh: 他冷酷無情的功利主義觀點並沒有為他贏得很多朋友。

utility.definition: The state or condition of being useful; usefulness.
utility.example: I've bought a new disk utility that can recover deleted files.
utility.exampleZh: 我購買了一個新的磁碟實用程序，可以恢復已刪除的檔案。

predilection.definition: Condition of favoring or liking; tendency towards; proclivity; predisposition.
predilection.example: She always had a predilection for round numbers.

utilize.definition: To make use of; to use.
utilize.example: Pietro decided to utilize the opportunity.
utilize.exampleZh: 彼得羅決定利用這個機會。

vindicate.definition: To clear of an accusation, suspicion or criticism.

commensurate.definition: Of a proportionate or similar measurable standard.
commensurate.example: The benefits will be commensurate with the size of the project.
commensurate.exampleZh: 收益將與項目的規模相稱。

minster.example: Tourists flock to the ancient minster to admire its stained-glass windows.
minster.exampleZh: 遊客紛紛湧向這座古老的大教堂，欣賞它的彩色玻璃窗。

ironic.definition: Characterized by or constituting (any kind of) irony.
ironic.example: It's ironic to have a blizzard in the middle of the summer.
ironic.exampleZh: 仲夏時節竟然下起了暴風雪，真是諷刺。

callous.definition: Emotionally hardened; unfeeling and indifferent to the suffering/feelings of others.
callous.example: She was so callous that she could criticise a cancer patient for wearing a wig.
callous.exampleZh: 她冷酷無情到可以批評一個戴假髮的癌症病人。

derelict.definition: Abandoned, forsaken; given up by the natural owner or guardian; (of a ship) abandoned at sea, dilapidated, neglected; (of a spacecraft) abandoned in outer space.
derelict.example: There was a derelict ship on the island.

callously.definition: In a callous manner; done without regard to others' sensitivities.
callously.example: Was Dan callously murdered like Linda?
callously.exampleZh: 丹是否像琳達一樣被無情地謀殺了？

materialism.definition: Constant concern over material possessions and wealth; a great or excessive regard for worldly concerns.

materialistic.definition: Being overly concerned with material possessions and wealth.

ferocity.example: His expression is full of ferocity and astonishment at the same time.
ferocity.exampleZh: 他的表情充滿了兇猛，同時也充滿了驚愕。

recitation.definition: The act of publicly reciting something previously memorized.
recitation.example: The class listened to her flawless recitation of the poem.
recitation.exampleZh: 全班同學都在聽她完美地背誦這首詩。

equable.definition: Unvarying, calm and steady; constant and uniform.
equable.example: It's lucky that his parents are so equable.
equable.exampleZh: 慶幸的是，他的父母如此平和。

denounce.definition: To make known in a formal manner; to proclaim; to announce; to declare.
denounce.example: to denounce someone as a swindler, or as a coward
denounce.exampleZh: 譴責某人是騙子或膽小鬼

extant.example: But there is no publication extant authenticated with his name.
extant.exampleZh: 但目前還沒有任何出版物證實他的名字。

denunciation.example: Colonization's injustices demand global denunciation and sanctions.
denunciation.exampleZh: 殖民化的不公正行為需要全球的譴責和製裁。

requisition.example: a requisition for a public meeting

substantiate.definition: To verify something by supplying evidence; to authenticate or corroborate

inscrutable.definition: Difficult or impossible to comprehend, fathom or interpret.
inscrutable.example: His inscrutable theories would years later become the foundation of a whole new science.
inscrutable.exampleZh: 多年後，他的深奧理論將成為一門全新科學的基礎。

stratosphere.definition: Collectively, those layers of the Earth’s crust which primarily comprise stratified deposits.
stratosphere.example: The ozone in the stratosphere absorbs ultraviolet radiation.
stratosphere.exampleZh: 平流層中的臭氧吸收紫外線輻射。

indulge.definition: (often followed by "in"): To yield to a temptation or desire.
indulge.example: He looked at the chocolate but didn't indulge.
indulge.exampleZh: 他看著巧克力，但沒有沉迷。

laudatory.definition: Of or pertaining to praise, or the expression of praise.

laud.example: Most will laud the crackdown as steps toward transparency, fairness in business and better-run local partner companies, economists predict.
laud.exampleZh: 經濟學家預測，大多數人都會稱讚這次打擊行動是邁向透明度、商業公平和改善當地合作夥伴公司營運的一步。

glitz.example: Being a movie star, she was used to a life of glitz and glamour.
glitz.exampleZh: 作為一名電影明星，她已經習慣了浮華和魅力的生活。

glitzy.example: Aren't they a little too glitzy for you?
glitzy.exampleZh: 它們對你來說是不是有點太耀眼了？

automate.definition: To replace or enhance human labor with machines.
automate.example: Many offices still need to automate.
automate.exampleZh: 許多辦公室仍然需要自動化。

athleticism.definition: The state of being an athlete, or of taking part in athletic events.
athleticism.example: The gymnast's athleticism left the entire audience in awe.
athleticism.exampleZh: 這位體操運動員的運動天賦讓所有觀眾驚嘆不已。

acreage.example: It's a property with a lot of acreage.
acreage.exampleZh: 這是一處面積很大的房產。

affectation.definition: An attempt to assume or exhibit what is not natural or real; false display; artificial show.
affectation.example: His posh accent was pure affectation; he had grown up two streets away from me.
affectation.exampleZh: 他時髦的口音純粹是做作。他在離我兩條街的地方長大。

imposition.definition: The act of imposing, laying on, affixing, enjoining, inflicting, obtruding, and the like.
imposition.example: Is to reason and to be mistaken, an imposition?
imposition.exampleZh: 推理和犯錯是一種強加嗎？

stipulation.definition: The act of stipulating; a contracting or bargaining; an agreement.
stipulation.example: If I lend you my car, my only stipulation is that you fill up the gas tank before returning it.
stipulation.exampleZh: 如果我把車借給你，我唯一的規定就是你在還車之前把油箱加滿。

stipulate.definition: To require (something) as a condition of a contract or agreement.
stipulate.example: Did you stipulate that it was to be delivered by August first?
stipulate.exampleZh: 你們有規定八月一號之前交付嗎？

allotment.example: Membership of an allotment association is not grounds for dismissal.
allotment.exampleZh: 分配協會的會員資格不能作為解僱的理由。

allot.definition: To distribute or apportion by (or as if by) lot.
allot.example: Let's see how much time they allot you.
allot.exampleZh: 讓我們看看他們分配了多少時間給你。

forfeiture.definition: A legal action whereby a person loses all interest in the forfeit property.
forfeiture.example: Forfeiture of your assets is the last thing you should be worried about.
forfeiture.exampleZh: 您的資產被沒收是您最不應該擔心的事情。

primeval.example: You went back to the camp leaving me alone in the primeval forest.
primeval.exampleZh: 你回到了營地，留下我一個人在原始森林裡。

tenacity.definition: The quality or state of being tenacious, or persistence of purpose; tenaciousness.
tenacity.example: It will take a lot of patience and tenacity to complete this project.
tenacity.exampleZh: 完成這個專案需要很大的耐心和毅力。

tenacious.definition: Clinging to an object or surface; adhesive.
tenacious.example: The tenacious depths of the quicksand, as is usual in such cases, retained its prey.
tenacious.exampleZh: 流沙的深處頑強地保留著獵物，在這種情況下是常見的。

exact.definition: To demand and enforce the payment or performance of, sometimes in a forcible or imperious way.
exact.example: to exact tribute, fees, or obedience from someone
exact.exampleZh: 向某人索取貢品、費用或服從

unequivocal.definition: Unambiguous; without equivocation or ambiguity; singularly clear, unmistakable, or unquestionable
unequivocal.example: I want you to give me an unequivocal guarantee on that.
unequivocal.exampleZh: 我希望你對此給予我明確的保證。

capacious.definition: Having a lot of space inside; roomy.
capacious.example: I found the suites capacious, the sofas commodious, the sandwiches copious.
capacious.exampleZh: 我發現套房寬敞，沙發寬敞，三明治豐富。

unequivocally.definition: In a way that leaves no doubt; in an unequivocal or unambiguous manner, unquestionably.
unequivocally.example: They strove to make their product unequivocally the best in the industry.
unequivocally.exampleZh: 他們努力使自己的產品成為業內最好的產品。

provocation.definition: The act of provoking, inciting or annoying someone into doing something
provocation.example: When it's time to check for provocation ask the patient about what makes their chief complaint better or worse.
provocation.exampleZh: 當需要檢查是否有刺激時，詢問患者什麼會使他們的主訴好轉或惡化。

portraiture.definition: A portrait; a likeness; a painted resemblance; hence, that which is copied from some example or model.
portraiture.example: She is best known for her portraiture, especially her studies of elderly faces.
portraiture.exampleZh: 她以肖像畫而聞名，尤其是對老人面孔的研究。

liaise.example: I'll liaise with the marketing team to coordinate the launch.
liaise.exampleZh: 我將與行銷團隊聯絡以協調發布。

contractual.definition: Of, relating to, or enforced by a contract.

circumnavigation.definition: The act of circumnavigating, or sailing round.
circumnavigation.example: He began his circumnavigation of the world in a sailing boat.
circumnavigation.exampleZh: 他開始了他乘坐帆船的環球航行。

abject.definition: Sunk to or existing in a low condition, state, or position.
abject.example: Tom, a self-made millionaire, had lived in abject poverty as a child.
abject.exampleZh: 湯姆是一位白手起家的百萬富翁，小時候住在赤貧中。

sheaf.definition: A quantity of the stalks and ears of wheat, rye, or other grain, bound together; a bundle of grain or straw.

articulation.definition: A joint or the collection of joints at which something is articulated, or hinged, for bending.
articulation.example: The articulation allowed the robot to move around corners.
articulation.exampleZh: 這種關節允許機器人在角落處移動。

spurious.example: His argument was spurious and had no validity.
spurious.exampleZh: 他的論點是虛假的，沒有任何依據。

calorific.example: I love cured cheeses even though I know they're very calorific.
calorific.exampleZh: 我喜歡醃製乳酪，儘管我知道它們的熱量很高。

octogenarian.definition: One who is between the age of eighty and eighty-nine, inclusive.
octogenarian.example: Apparently once you become an octogenarian you can say what you like and get away with it. I can't wait to turn eighty.
octogenarian.exampleZh: 顯然，一旦你成為八十多歲的老人，你就可以隨心所欲地暢所欲言。我等不及要八十歲了。

unencumbered.definition: Not burdened with worries, cares or responsibilities.
unencumbered.example: Life is best lived unencumbered by materialism.
unencumbered.exampleZh: 最好的生活是不受物質主義的束縛。

buffoonery.definition: The behaviour expected of a buffoon; foolishness, silliness.
buffoonery.exampleZh: 默劇的滑稽表演為路人帶來了歡樂。

gauche.definition: Awkward or lacking in social graces; bumbling.
gauche.example: Tom is so naive that his gauche manners can hardly offend anyone.
gauche.exampleZh: 湯姆太天真了，他粗魯的舉止很難得罪任何人。

proliferation.definition: The process by which an organism produces others of its kind; breeding, propagation, procreation, reproduction.
proliferation.example: The crisis led to the 1968 Nuclear Non-Proliferation Treaty.
proliferation.exampleZh: 這場危機導致了 1968 年《核不擴散條約》的簽訂。

pluck.example: Those tiny birds are hardly worth the tedious pluck.
pluck.exampleZh: 那些小鳥根本不值得費力去採摘。

blandishment.definition: Flattering speech or actions designed to persuade or influence.
blandishment.example: Never again will I hear from her a teasing whisper or soft blandishment, never again feel around me her tight embrace.
blandishment.exampleZh: 我再也不會聽到她戲弄的低語或溫柔的甜言蜜語，再也不會感受到她緊緊的擁抱在我身邊。

monastic.definition: Of or relating to monasteries or monks.
monastic.example: Some monastic orders engage in repetitive labor as a form of meditation.
monastic.exampleZh: 有些僧團將重複勞動作為冥想的一種形式。

adornment.example: The draperies did little to keep out the light; rather, they were mainly there as adornment for the windows.
adornment.exampleZh: 窗簾幾乎沒有遮擋光線。相反，它們主要是作為窗戶的裝飾。

midwifery.definition: The practice and science of being a midwife.
midwifery.example: She trained in midwifery before opening her own birthing centre.
midwifery.exampleZh: 在開設自己的分娩中心之前，她接受了助產士訓練。

idle.definition: To spend in idleness; to waste; to consume.
idle.example: My computer hibernates after it has been idle for 30 minutes.
idle.exampleZh: 我的電腦閒置 30 分鐘後就會進入休眠狀態。

malleable.definition: Able to be hammered into thin sheets; capable of being extended or shaped by beating with a hammer, or by the pressure of rollers.
malleable.example: My opinion on the subject is malleable.
malleable.exampleZh: 我對這個問題的看法是可塑的。

ore.definition: Rock or other material that contains valuable or utilitarian materials; primarily a rock containing metals or gems for which it is typically mined and processed.
ore.example: Its principal exports are iron ore, rubber, diamonds, and gold.
ore.exampleZh: 其主要出口產品為鐵礦石、橡膠、鑽石和黃金。

gale.example: My dream, made entirely of breeze, is now ending in a gale.
gale.exampleZh: 我的夢想，完全由微風組成，現在卻在大風中結束。

promenade.example: The market on the promenade sells second-hand clothes and bric-a-brac.
promenade.exampleZh: 海濱長廊上的市場有販售二手衣服和小玩意。

testimonial.definition: A statement, especially one given under oath; testimony

distillation.definition: The act of falling in drops, or the act of pouring out in drops.
distillation.example: The distillation of the mixture separates the alcohol from the water.
distillation.exampleZh: 混合物的蒸餾將醇與水分離。

distill.definition: To subject a substance to distillation.

nostalgia.definition: A longing for home or familiar surroundings; homesickness.
nostalgia.example: Flipping through the pages, nostalgia washed over me.
nostalgia.exampleZh: 翻閱書頁，懷舊之情湧上心頭。

overlay.definition: To lay, spread, or apply something over or across; cover.
overlay.example: Tor is an example of an overlay network.
overlay.exampleZh: Tor 是覆蓋網路的一個範例。

grievance.example: If you want the problem fixed, you'll have to file a grievance with the city.
grievance.exampleZh: 如果您想解決問題，則必須向市政府提出申訴。

nostalgic.definition: Of, having, or relating to nostalgia.
nostalgic.exampleZh: 那天晚上，弗拉基米爾感到懷念。

residue.definition: Whatever remains after something else has been removed.
residue.example: Maintain good ventilation while cooking to minimize greasy residue.
residue.exampleZh: 烹飪時保持良好的通風，以盡量減少油膩殘留。

residual.definition: Of, relating to, or remaining as a residue; left over.
residual.example: Now clean up any residual dust.
residual.exampleZh: 現在清理所有殘留的灰塵。

physiological.example: My attitude to the comma is physiological; the more breath I have, the fewer commas I use.
physiological.exampleZh: 我對逗號的態度是生理性的；我的呼吸越多，我使用的逗號就越少。

methodology.definition: (originally science) The study of methods used in a field.

physiologically.definition: In accordance with the science of physiology.
physiologically.example: To stay physiologically, emotionally and psychologically fit in isolation, astronauts exercise, eat healthy, follow a sleep and work schedule, and make time for leisure activities.
physiologically.exampleZh: 為了在隔離期間保持生理、情緒和心理健康，太空人鍛鍊身體、健康飲食、遵循睡眠和工作時間表，並騰出時間進行休閒活動。

proportionally.definition: In proportion; in due degree; adapted relatively.
proportionally.example: Taxes should rise proportionally with income.
proportionally.exampleZh: 稅收應與收入成比例增加。

habitation.definition: The act of inhabiting; state of inhabiting or dwelling, or of being inhabited; occupancy.
habitation.example: The remote island showed no signs of human habitation.
habitation.exampleZh: 這座偏遠的島嶼沒有任何人類居住的跡象。

ornate.example: The wardrobe is made from dark wood and has ornate gold handles.
ornate.exampleZh: 衣櫃由深色木材製成，配有華麗的金色把手。

inartistic.example: The plain, inartistic packaging did little to attract buyers.
inartistic.exampleZh: 樸素、缺乏藝術感的包裝對吸引買家毫無幫助。

presuppose.definition: To assume some truth without proof, usually for the purpose of reaching a conclusion based on that truth.
presuppose.example: We must presuppose that the wage paid is economically just.
presuppose.exampleZh: 我們必須預設支付的工資在經濟上是公平的。

transmitter.definition: One who or that which transmits something (in all senses).
transmitter.example: The transmitter on Voyager was not reachable due to solar winds.
transmitter.exampleZh: 由於太陽風的影響，航行者號上的發射器無法到達。

presupposition.definition: An assumption made beforehand; a preliminary conjecture or speculation.
presupposition.example: His argument rests on the presupposition that everyone acts rationally.
presupposition.exampleZh: 他的論點建立在每個人都理性行事的前提上。

archival.definition: Of or pertaining to an archive or archiving.
archival.example: Our nightly backups include the archival of old e-mail messages.
archival.exampleZh: 我們的夜間備份包括舊電子郵件的存檔。

regulatory.example: The committee recommends devolution of regulatory power to the provinces.
regulatory.exampleZh: 委員會建議將監管權下放給各省。

egalitarian.definition: Characterized by social equality and equal rights for all people.
egalitarian.example: The lack of gender in English is not due to the English having a more egalitarian culture.
egalitarian.exampleZh: 英語中缺乏性別並不是因為英國文化比較平等。

hindsight.definition: Realisation or understanding of the significance and nature of events after they have occurred
hindsight.example: In hindsight, I should have quit that job sooner.
hindsight.exampleZh: 事後看來，我應該早點辭掉那份工作。

pervasive.definition: Manifested throughout; pervading, permeating, penetrating or affecting everything.
pervasive.example: The medication had a pervasive effect on the patient's health.
pervasive.exampleZh: 該藥物對患者的健康產生了普遍影響。

pervasiveness.definition: The state or quality of being present in all parts of a particular thing or place.
pervasiveness.example: The pervasiveness of smartphones has changed how children play.
pervasiveness.exampleZh: 智慧型手機的普及改變了孩子們的玩耍方式。

secure.definition: To make safe; to relieve from apprehensions of, or exposure to, danger; to guard; to protect.
secure.example: to secure a creditor against loss; to secure a debt by a mortgage
secure.exampleZh: 確保債權人免受損失；透過抵押貸款擔保債務

autonomous.definition: Self-governing. Intelligent, sentient, self-aware, thinking, feeling, governing independently.
autonomous.exampleZh: 米洛舍維奇推翻了科索沃的自治地位。

outmoded.example: His pipe was as outmoded as his ideas.
outmoded.exampleZh: 他的煙鬥和他的想法一樣過時。

plum.example: She landed a plum position as an executive for the firm.
plum.exampleZh: 她在公司獲得了一個不錯的高階主管職位。

notorious.definition: Widely known, especially for something negative; infamous.
notorious.example: This junction is notorious for accidents.
notorious.exampleZh: 這個路口因事故而臭名昭著。

notoriously.definition: In a notorious or notable manner; as is commonly known.
notoriously.exampleZh: 雅尼的攻擊性是出了名的。

menial.definition: Of or relating to work normally performed by a servant.

notoriety.definition: The condition of being infamous or notorious.
notoriety.example: Layla wasn't receiving the attention and notoriety that she wanted.
notoriety.exampleZh: 萊拉沒有得到她想要的關注和惡名。

antidote.definition: A remedy to counteract the effects of poison (often followed by "against," "for," or "to").
antidote.example: She reached the hospital in time to receive the antidote for the snake venom.
antidote.exampleZh: 她及時趕到醫院，領取了蛇毒的解藥。

insular.definition: Of, pertaining to, being, or resembling an island or islands.
insular.example: The fact that no one ever moves in makes the community more and more insular.
insular.exampleZh: 沒人搬進來這一事實使得這個社區變得越來越孤立。

posturing.definition: The assumption of an exaggerated pose or attitude.
posturing.example: If you're finished posturing in front of the mirror, can I use the bathroom now?
posturing.exampleZh: 如果你在鏡子前擺好了姿勢，現在可以去趟浴室嗎？

revelatory.definition: Of, pertaining to, or in the nature of a revelation
revelatory.exampleZh: 他一直在做一些具有啟發意義的夢。

prostrate.example: He was prostrate from the extreme heat.

haphazardly.definition: In a haphazard manner; in a random, chaotic, and incomplete manner.

haphazard.definition: Random; chaotic; incomplete; not thorough, constant, or consistent.
haphazard.example: Do not make such haphazard changes to the settings; instead, adjust the knobs carefully, a bit at a time.
haphazard.exampleZh: 請勿隨意更改設定；相反，請小心調整旋鈕，一次調整一點。

deficient.definition: Lacking something essential; often construed with in.
deficient.example: The structure is deficient in harmony.

delicatessen.example: A delicatessen caught fire and burned down, leaving a heap of ashes.
delicatessen.exampleZh: 一家熟食店著火了，燒毀了，留下一堆灰燼。

muscle-bound.definition: Having pronounced muscle development through weightlifting, bodybuilding, or other physical exercise.
muscle-bound.example: It was obvious that the muscle-bound bodybuilder had been taking steroids.
muscle-bound.exampleZh: 很明顯，這位肌肉發達的健美運動員一直在服用類固醇。

dive.definition: A jump or plunge into water.
dive.example: the dive of a hawk after prey

cocoon.definition: To envelop in a protective case
cocoon.example: Caterpillars transform into butterflies while they're in a cocoon.
cocoon.exampleZh: 毛毛蟲在繭裡會變成蝴蝶。

chrysalis.definition: The pupa of a butterfly or moth, enclosed inside a cocoon, in which metamorphosis takes place.
chrysalis.example: What's the difference between a cocoon and a chrysalis?

euphoria.definition: An excited state of joy; a feeling of intense happiness.
euphoria.example: The runner was in absolute euphoria after winning his first marathon.
euphoria.exampleZh: 這位跑者在贏得他的第一場馬拉鬆比賽後感到非常高興。

ignominious.example: Dr. Jackson suffered an ignominious downfall after a celebrated career.
ignominious.exampleZh: 傑克遜博士在輝煌的職業生涯之後遭遇了可恥的垮台。

euphoric.definition: Feeling great well-being or elation or intense happiness; characterized by euphoria
euphoric.example: In this moment, I am euphoric.

treachery.definition: Deliberate, often calculated, disregard for trust or faith.
treachery.example: The translator is a traitor and the translation is a treachery.
treachery.exampleZh: 翻譯者是叛徒，翻譯者是背叛者。

maggot.definition: A soft, legless larva of a fly or other dipterous insect, that often eats decomposing organic matter.
maggot.example: Drop and give me fifty, maggot.

palatable.example: For some instant noodles make a palatable, if not especially nutritious, meal.
palatable.exampleZh: 對於某些泡麵來說，即使不是特別有營養，也是美味的一餐。

palate.definition: The roof of the mouth; the uraniscus.
palate.example: Salmon has been delighting the human palate since prehistoric times.
palate.exampleZh: 自史前時代起，鮭魚就一直是人類味蕾的享受。

hermit.definition: A religious recluse; someone who lives alone for religious reasons; an eremite.
hermit.example: A hermit is a person whose vices and follies are not sociable.
hermit.exampleZh: 隱士是指那些惡習和愚行不與人交往的人。

hermetic.definition: (chiefly with capital initial) Pertaining to Hermes Trismegistus or the writings attributed to him.
hermetic.example: With a hermetic seal there is no need to worry about the ingress of moisture.
hermetic.exampleZh: 採用氣密密封，無需擔心濕氣進入。

rift.example: My marriage is in trouble: the fight created a rift between us and we can't reconnect.
rift.exampleZh: 我的婚姻遇到了麻煩：爭吵在我們之間造成了裂痕，我們無法重新建立聯繫。

mere.definition: A body of standing water, such as a lake or a pond. More specifically, it can refer to a lake that is broad in relation to its depth. Also included in place names such as Windermere.

helm.definition: The steering apparatus of a ship, especially the tiller or wheel.

autopilot.definition: A mechanical, electrical, or hydraulic system used to guide a vehicle without assistance from a human being.
autopilot.example: I set the autopilot to due south, so I could get some rest.
autopilot.exampleZh: 我將自動駕駛儀設定為正南，這樣我就可以休息一下。

eminence.definition: Someone of high rank, reputation or social status.
eminence.example: She figured as a gray eminence.
eminence.exampleZh: 她被認為是一位灰色的顯赫人物。

eminent.example: His eminent good sense has been a godsend to this project.
eminent.exampleZh: 他卓越的判斷力是這個計畫的天賜之物。

preeminent.definition: Exceeding others in quality or rank; of outstanding excellence, extremely notable or important.
preeminent.example: Monge stood preeminent above his contemporaries.
preeminent.exampleZh: 蒙日在他的同時代人中脫穎而出。

unsuspected.definition: Not suspected; not having raised suspicion.
unsuspected.example: The police uncovered his unsuspected drug dealing whilst investigating another matter.
unsuspected.exampleZh: 警方在調查另一起案件時發現了他毫無嫌疑的毒品交易。

paralysis.definition: The complete loss of voluntary control of part of a person's body, such as one or more limbs.
paralysis.example: The government has been in a paralysis since it lost its majority in the parliament.
paralysis.exampleZh: 自從失去議會多數席次以來，政府一直處於癱瘓狀態。

preoccupation.definition: The state of being preoccupied or an idea that preoccupies the mind; enthrallment.
preoccupation.example: The question of Palestine remained a central preoccupation for Algeria.
preoccupation.exampleZh: 巴勒斯坦問題仍然是阿爾及利亞關注的焦點。

preoccupy.definition: To distract; to occupy or draw attention elsewhere.
preoccupy.example: The father tried to preoccupy the child with his keys.
preoccupy.exampleZh: 父親試圖讓孩子專注於他的鑰匙。

prolong.definition: To extend in space or length.
prolong.example: The government shouldn't prolong deciding on this issue any further.
prolong.exampleZh: 政府不應再拖延對此問題的決定。

drudgery.example: With all its sham, drudgery and broken dreams; it is still a beautiful world.
drudgery.exampleZh: 帶著所有的虛假、苦差事和破碎的夢想；這仍然是一個美麗的世界。

intermediary.definition: An agent acting as a mediator between sides that may disagree.
intermediary.example: The intermediary between the manufacturer and retailer is the wholesaler
intermediary.exampleZh: 製造商和零售商之間的中間人是批發商

denote.example: The yellow blazes denote the trail.

profitability.definition: The quality or state of being profitable; capacity to make a profit.
profitability.example: Cutting costs sharply improved the factory's profitability.
profitability.exampleZh: 削減成本大幅提高了工廠的獲利能力。

declination.definition: At a given point, the angle between magnetic north and true north.

jargon.definition: A technical terminology unique to a particular subject.
jargon.example: Chinook Jargon was once widespread in the Pacific Northwest.
jargon.exampleZh: 奇努克行話曾經在太平洋西北地區廣泛傳播。

vocational.definition: Of or pertaining to a vocation.
vocational.exampleZh: 印度需要更好的職業項目。

spatial.example: Vertigo is a feeling of spatial disorientation.
spatial.exampleZh: 眩暈是一種空間迷失方向的感覺。

extracurricular.definition: Outside of the normal curriculum of an educational establishment
extracurricular.example: The students enjoy a number of extracurricular activities at weekends.
extracurricular.exampleZh: 學生們在周末享受許多課外活動。

curriculum.definition: The set of courses, coursework, and their content, offered at a school or university.
curriculum.example: What curriculum does Sami use to teach Arabic?
curriculum.exampleZh: 薩米使用什麼課程來教阿拉伯語？

all-encompassing.example: The night was silent, and the darkness was all-encompassing.
all-encompassing.exampleZh: 夜色靜悄悄的，黑暗籠罩著一切。

accountability.definition: The state of being accountable; liability to be called on to render an account; accountableness; responsible for; answerable for.
accountability.example: Voters are demanding greater accountability from their elected officials.
accountability.exampleZh: 選民要求他們選出的官員承擔更大的責任。

abundant.definition: Fully sufficient; found in copious supply; in great quantity; overflowing.
abundant.example: [W]ith their magical words they [poets] bring forth to our eyesight the abundant images and beauties of creation. — Leigh Hunt, On the Realities of Imagination
abundant.exampleZh: 他們[詩人]用他們神奇的文字為我們帶來了豐富的意象和創造之美。 ——利‧亨特，《論想像力的現實》

minefield.definition: An area in which land mines have been laid.
minefield.example: Holding a conversation with her feels like walking across a minefield.
minefield.exampleZh: 與她交談感覺就像走過雷區。

abundantly.definition: In an abundant manner; in a sufficient degree; in large measure.

anarchic.definition: Relating to, supporting, or likely to cause anarchy.
anarchic.example: Tom is too invested in the system to support anarchic revolution.
anarchic.exampleZh: 湯姆對這個體系投入太多，無法支持無政府主義革命。

slab.definition: A large, flat piece of solid material; a solid object that is large and flat.
slab.example: A slab of concrete underlies nearly every permanent structure on Earth.
slab.exampleZh: 地球上幾乎所有永久性建築的下方都有一塊混凝土板。

retrospect.example: 1800: Alexander Hamilton, Letter from Alexander Hamilton, Concerning the Public Conduct and Character of John Adams, Esq. President of the United States - To give a correct idea of the circumstances.., it may be useful to retrospect to an early period.
retrospect.exampleZh: 1800 年：亞歷山大·漢密爾頓，亞歷山大·漢密爾頓的信，關於約翰·亞當斯先生的公共行為和性格。美國總統 - 為了正確了解當時的情況......，回顧早期可能會有所幫助。

retrospective.definition: Of, relating to, or contemplating the past.
retrospective.example: Kirsty went to the art gallery to see the Matisse retrospective.
retrospective.exampleZh: 柯斯蒂去美術館看馬蒂斯回顧展。

uncanny.definition: Strange, and mysteriously unsettling (as if supernatural); weird.
uncanny.example: He bore an uncanny resemblance to the dead sailor.
uncanny.exampleZh: 他與死去的水手有著驚人的相似之處。

brevity.definition: The quality of being brief in duration.
brevity.example: Brevity will help you to be understood by others.
brevity.exampleZh: 簡潔將有助於別人理解您。

tactic.definition: A maneuver, or action calculated to achieve some end.

tactically.definition: In a tactical manner; in a manner calculated to achieve some end.
tactically.example: The team played tactically, defending their narrow lead until the final whistle.
tactically.exampleZh: 球隊打出了戰術，一直保持著微弱的領先優勢，直到終場哨聲響起。

gambit.definition: An opening in chess, in which a minor piece or a pawn is sacrificed to gain an advantage.
gambit.example: Her clever gambit gave her an advantage.
gambit.exampleZh: 她的巧妙策略為她帶來了優勢。

tactile.definition: Tangible; perceptible to the sense of touch.
tactile.example: After three days, Tom began to experience tactile hallucinations.
tactile.exampleZh: 三天后，湯姆開始出現觸覺幻覺。

correlation.definition: A reciprocal, parallel or complementary relationship between two or more comparable objects.

combustion.definition: The act or process of burning.
combustion.example: Is spontaneous human combustion scientifically proven?
combustion.exampleZh: 人體自燃有科學根據嗎？

painstaking.definition: Carefully attentive to details; diligent in performing a process or procedure.
painstaking.example: Vaccines typically take years of painstaking research to develop.
painstaking.exampleZh: 疫苗通常需要多年的艱苦研究才能開發出來。

painstakingly.definition: In a painstaking manner; very slowly and carefully.
painstakingly.example: I lost everything I had so painstakingly collected over the years.
painstakingly.exampleZh: 我失去了多年來辛苦收集的一切。

contraption.definition: A machine that is complicated and precarious.
contraption.example: Why, I wouldn't know what this contraption is supposed to do.
contraption.exampleZh: 為什麼，我不知道這個裝置該做什麼。

lyricist.definition: A person who writes the lyrics of a song.
lyricist.example: The lyricist changed his mind over the years.
lyricist.exampleZh: 多年來，作詞家改變了主意。

lyrically.definition: In a manner that is pleasing to hear, as singing or speaking; in a lyrical manner.
lyrically.exampleZh: 一首歌詞缺乏想像力的歌曲

moribund.definition: Approaching death; about to die; dying; expiring.
moribund.example: The organization was once quite active, but now it's effectively moribund.
moribund.exampleZh: 該組織曾經非常活躍，但現在實際上已處於停滯狀態。

solicit.definition: To persistently endeavor to obtain an object, or bring about an event.
solicit.example: to solicit alms, or a favour

perch.definition: Any of the three species of spiny-finned freshwater fish in the genus Perca.
perch.example: The owl hooted softly from its perch.
perch.exampleZh: 貓頭鷹從棲木上輕輕地叫著。

philanthropy.definition: Benevolent altruism with the intention of increasing the well-being of humankind.
philanthropy.example: As public funding is reduced, we depend increasingly on private philanthropy.
philanthropy.exampleZh: 隨著公共資金的減少，我們越來越依賴私人慈善事業。

vocalist.definition: A singer; a person who likes to sing.
vocalist.example: Tom isn't the lead vocalist. He's one of the backup singers.
vocalist.exampleZh: 湯姆不是主唱。他是和聲歌手之一。

philanthropic.definition: Of or pertaining to philanthropy; characterized by philanthropy; loving or helping mankind

philanthropist.definition: A person who loves humankind in general.
philanthropist.example: The philanthropist tried to use her wealth to help people in need.
philanthropist.exampleZh: 這位慈善家試圖用她的財富來幫助有需要的人。

wordsmith.example: She has always been known as a capable wordsmith, but in this latest novel she also shows surprising emotional depth.
wordsmith.exampleZh: 她一直被認為是一位能幹的文字大師，但在這部最新的小說中，她也展現了令人驚訝的情感深度。

precocious.definition: Characterized by exceptionally early development or maturity.
precocious.example: The precocious plant was already blooming flowers by day 4.
precocious.exampleZh: 這種早熟的植物在第四天就已經開花了。

herald.definition: To proclaim or announce an event.
herald.exampleZh: 水仙花預示著春天的到來。

philistine.definition: A person who is ignorant or uneducated; specifically, a person who lacks appreciation of or is antagonistic towards art or culture, and who has pedestrian tastes.
philistine.example: He hit the Philistine in the forehead.
philistine.exampleZh: 他擊中了非利士人的額頭。

innuendo.definition: A derogatory hint or reference to a person or thing. An implication, intimation or insinuation.
innuendo.example: She made a devious innuendo about her husband, who was embarrassed.
innuendo.exampleZh: 她對她的丈夫做了一些狡猾的暗示，她的丈夫感到很尷尬。

inducement.definition: An incentive that helps bring about a desired state. In some contexts, this can imply bribery.
inducement.example: A free holiday was offered as an inducement to sign the contract.
inducement.exampleZh: 提供免費假期作為簽署合約的誘因。

anthology.definition: A collection of literary works, such as poems or short stories, especially a collection from various authors.
anthology.example: He compiled a Japanese folklore anthology for use in schools.
anthology.exampleZh: 他編撰了一本日本民俗選集供學校使用。

lamentation.example: The village was filled with lamentation after the tragic accident.
lamentation.exampleZh: 悲慘事故發生後，村裡一片哀悼。

conduit.definition: A pipe or channel for conveying water etc.
conduit.example: The screams are harmonic tremors, and researchers at the University of Washington believe they are caused when magma is forced through a narrow conduit at greater and greater pressure into the heart of the volcano.
conduit.exampleZh: 這些尖叫聲是諧波震動，華盛頓大學的研究人員認為，它們是當岩漿以越來越大的壓力通過狹窄的管道進入火山中心時引起的。

chronological.definition: Relating to time, or units of time.
chronological.example: He is 67 in chronological age, but has the mind and body of someone 55.
chronological.exampleZh: 以實際年齡計算，他已經 67 歲了，但他的思想和身體卻像 55 歲的人一樣。

chronology.definition: The science of determining the order in which events occurred.
chronology.example: I here aver that his chronology is correct.
chronology.exampleZh: 我在此斷言他的年表是正確的。

chronologically.definition: (manner) In a chronological manner; with reference to time.
chronologically.example: He had aged but a year chronologically, but in appearance a decade.
chronologically.exampleZh: 按時間順序，他隻老了一歲，但看起來卻老了十歲。

equilibrium.definition: The condition of a system in which competing influences are balanced, resulting in no net change.
equilibrium.example: We could apply the theory of punctuated equilibrium to languages.
equilibrium.exampleZh: 我們可以將間斷均衡理論應用於語言。

rebound.definition: To bound or spring back from a force.

proposition.definition: The act of offering (an idea) for consideration.
proposition.example: “'Wiktionary is a good dictionary' is a proposition” is a proposition.
proposition.exampleZh: 「『維基字典是好字典』是一個命題」是一個命題。

electrode.definition: The terminal through which electric current passes between metallic and nonmetallic parts of an electric circuit
electrode.example: The negative electrode is usually made of carbon.

cosmic.definition: Of or from or pertaining to the cosmos or universe.

interlude.example: Democracy will only have been a short interlude in the history of humanity.
interlude.exampleZh: 民主在人類歷史上只是一個短暫的插曲。

beneficiary.definition: One who benefits or receives an advantage.
beneficiary.example: You are the lucky beneficiary of this special offer.
beneficiary.exampleZh: 您是此特別優惠的幸運受益者。

haven.definition: A harbour or anchorage protected from the sea.

recuperate.definition: To recover, especially from an illness; to get better from an illness.
recuperate.example: Santiago took a seat to recuperate.

recuperative.example: Obtaining recuperative rest is aided by quiet, not by having ambulance sirens go by every five minutes.
recuperative.exampleZh: 安靜有助於獲得恢復性休息，而不是每五分鐘就響起救護車警報聲。

seismic.definition: Related to, or caused by an earthquake or other vibration of the Earth.
seismic.example: The host country's triumph has a seismic effect.
seismic.exampleZh: 東道國的勝利產生了巨大的影響。

irrevocable.definition: Unable to be retracted or reversed; final.
irrevocable.example: Yanni made an irrevocable decision to end his affair with Rima.
irrevocable.exampleZh: 雅尼做出了結束與裡瑪戀情的不可撤銷的決定。

irrevocably.definition: In an irrevocable manner; beyond recall; in a manner precluding repeal.
irrevocably.example: Are we harming the planet irrevocably?
irrevocably.exampleZh: 我們是否正在對地球造成不可挽回的傷害？

unnerve.definition: To deprive of nerve, force, or strength; to weaken; to enfeeble.

slither.definition: To move about smoothly and from side to side.
slither.example: Ziri felt something slither under his left leg.
slither.exampleZh: 齊裡感覺到有東西在他的左腿下滑行。

protrude.definition: To extend from, above or beyond a surface or boundary; to bulge outward; to stick out.
protrude.example: In this type of break, the bone will actually protrude from the skin.
protrude.exampleZh: 在這種類型的骨折中，骨頭實際上會從皮膚中突出。

protrusion.example: What is that protrusion? Is something hidden behind the curtain?
protrusion.exampleZh: 那個突出物是什麼？難道窗簾後面藏著什麼東西？

procedural.example: The judge dismissed the case on procedural grounds: it wasn't the facts or the law, but just that they hadn't filed the correct forms.
procedural.exampleZh: 法官以程序為由駁回了此案：這不是事實或法律，而是他們沒有提交正確的表格。

qualitative.definition: Of descriptions or distinctions based on some quality rather than on some quantity.

demystify.definition: To remove the mystery from something; to explain or clarify.
demystify.example: The article was written to demystify the mechanics of the internal combustion engine.
demystify.exampleZh: 寫這篇文章是為了揭開內燃機機械原理的神秘面紗。

proprietary.definition: Of or relating to property or ownership.

proprietorial.example: He cast a proprietorial glance around the shop he had just bought.
proprietorial.exampleZh: 他環視了一下他剛買下的店家。

floodgates.definition: An adjustable gate or valve used to control the flow of water through a sluice.
floodgates.example: The fountains also of the deep, and the floodgates of heaven, were shut up, and the rain from heaven was restrained.
floodgates.exampleZh: 深淵的泉源和天上的水閘也都關閉了，天上的雨也被抑制了。

imperceptible.definition: Not perceptible, not detectable, too small in magnitude to be observed
imperceptible.example: We all missed the imperceptible shake of his head as he tried to warn us without being seen.
imperceptible.exampleZh: 當他試圖在不被發現的情況下警告我們時，我們都錯過了他難以察覺的搖頭。

imperceptibly.definition: Not noticeably; too small to be detected; too little to be perceived.
imperceptibly.example: No one noticed that he had moved imperceptibly backwards.
imperceptibly.exampleZh: 沒有人注意到他已經不知不覺地向後退了一步。

hapless.example: Many children are hapless victims of this conflict.
hapless.exampleZh: 許多兒童是這場衝突的不幸受害者。

wayward.definition: Given to wilful, perverse deviation from the expected norm; tending to stray
wayward.example: The father had trouble communicating with his wayward son.
wayward.exampleZh: 這位父親很難與任性的兒子溝通。

catastrophically.definition: In a catastrophic manner, or to a catastrophic extent; disastrously.
catastrophically.example: The plan failed catastrophically, costing the company millions.
catastrophically.exampleZh: 該計劃災難性地失敗了，給公司造成了數百萬美元的損失。

blurb.definition: A short description of a book, film, or other work, written and used for promotional purposes.
blurb.example: He wrote a short blurb about the movie he had just watched.
blurb.exampleZh: 他寫了一篇關於他剛剛看過的電影的簡短簡介。

provincial.definition: Of or pertaining to a province.

indolent.definition: Habitually lazy, procrastinating, or resistant to physical labor
indolent.example: The indolent girl resisted doing her homework.
indolent.exampleZh: 這個懶惰的女孩拒絕做作業。

indolence.example: I like the word "indolence". It makes my laziness seem classy.
indolence.exampleZh: 我喜歡「懶惰」這個詞。這讓我的懶惰顯得很優雅。

deviant.definition: A person who deviates, especially from norms of social behavior.
deviant.example: He was branded as a deviant and ostracized.
deviant.exampleZh: 他被貼上離經叛道的標籤並受到排斥。

munificence.definition: The quality of being munificent; generosity.
munificence.example: Unfortunately, munificence is not one of the salient features of my husband.
munificence.exampleZh: 不幸的是，慷慨並不是我丈夫的顯著特徵之一。

emolument.definition: Payment for an office or employment; compensation for a job, which is usually monetary.
emolument.example: Those who have been once intoxicated with power, and have derived any kind of emolument from it, even though but for one year, never can willingly abandon it.
emolument.exampleZh: 那些曾經沉醉於權力，並從中獲得任何報酬的人，即使只有一年，也永遠不會心甘情願地放棄它。

posterity.definition: All the future generations, especially the descendants of a specific person.

venomous.example: The villain tricked him into drinking the venomous concoction.
venomous.exampleZh: 惡棍欺騙他喝下了有毒的混合物。

archetype.definition: An original model of which all other similar concepts, objects, or persons are merely copied, derivative, emulated, or patterned; a prototype.
archetype.example: Mr. Jackson fits the archetype of a wizard.
archetype.exampleZh: 傑克森先生符合巫師的原型。

choreography.definition: The art of creating, arranging and recording the dance movements of a work, such as a ballet.
choreography.example: She has staged many successful ballets, so her choreography skills must be excellent.
choreography.exampleZh: 她上演過很多成功的芭蕾舞劇，所以她的編舞技巧一定很出色。

archetypal.definition: Of or pertaining to an archetype.

dissension.definition: An act of expressing dissent, especially spoken.
dissension.example: They had never been very seriously cliqued up, but what there was of dissension was forgotten, and they were now one solid crowd.
dissension.exampleZh: 他們從來沒有嚴重地拉幫結派，但那些不和的事都被忘記了，他們現在成了一群堅定的人。

inert.definition: Unable to move or act; inanimate.
inert.example: It doesn't make any difference, guys. The balls are inert.
inert.exampleZh: 這沒有什麼區別，夥計們。球是惰性的。

hitherto.definition: Up to this or that time.
hitherto.example: The history of all hitherto existing societies is the history of class struggles.
hitherto.exampleZh: 迄今為止一切社會的歷史都是階級鬥爭的歷史。

angst.example: Yanni was fourteen and was in the throes of teenage angst.
angst.exampleZh: 雅尼十四歲，正處於青少年焦慮的痛苦之中。

melodious.definition: Having a pleasant melody or sound; tuneful.
melodious.exampleZh: 嗚嗚嗚祖拉發出非常悅耳的聲音。

magpie.definition: One of several kinds of bird in the family Corvidae
magpie.exampleZh: 喜鵲收集一切閃亮的東西。

melancholy.definition: Black bile, formerly thought to be one of the four "cardinal humours" of animal bodies.

meteorological.example: The trip was postponed because the meteorological conditions were so bad.
meteorological.exampleZh: 由於氣象條件太差，行程延後。

meteorology.definition: The science that deals with the study of the atmosphere and its phenomena, especially with weather and weather forecasting.

brink.definition: The edge, margin, or border of a steep place, as of a precipice; a bank or edge.

unsurpassed.definition: Surpassing all others in some way
unsurpassed.example: For sheer beauty, the view from the summit is unsurpassed.
unsurpassed.exampleZh: 就純粹的美麗而言，山頂的景色是無與倫比的。

intrinsic.definition: Innate, inherent, inseparable from the thing itself, essential.
intrinsic.example: the intrinsic merit of an action

intrinsically.definition: In an intrinsic manner; internally; essentially.
intrinsically.example: At the time, astronomy was still intrinsically linked to astrology.
intrinsically.exampleZh: 當時，天文學仍與占星學有著內在的關聯。

anonymity.definition: The quality or state of being anonymous; anonymousness.
anonymity.exampleZh: 法迪爾犧牲了自己的匿名性。

splinter.definition: A long, sharp fragment of material, often wood.

dissemble.example: Dissemble all your griefs and discontents.
dissemble.exampleZh: 掩飾你所有的悲傷和不滿。

dissemination.definition: The act of disseminating, or the state of being disseminated; diffusion for propagation and permanence; a scattering or spreading abroad, as of ideas, beliefs, etc.
dissemination.example: Without dissemination of the facts, conspiracy theories will abound.
dissemination.exampleZh: 如果不傳播事實，陰謀論就會氾濫成災。

mangle.definition: To change, mutilate or disfigure by cutting, tearing, rearranging etc.
mangle.example: Christianity is not the answer, and it has even managed to mangle the question.
mangle.exampleZh: 基督教不是答案，它甚至成功地解決了這個問題。

soliloquy.definition: The act of a character speaking to themselves so as to reveal their thoughts to the audience.
soliloquy.example: At the end of the second act the main villain gave a soliloquy detailing his plans to attack the protagonist.
soliloquy.exampleZh: 在第二幕結束時，主要反派發表了一段獨白，詳細說明了他攻擊主角的計劃。

solipsism.definition: The theory that the self is all that exists or that can be proven to exist.
solipsism.example: I taught a class on solipsism, but there was nobody there but me.
solipsism.exampleZh: 我教了一堂關於唯我論的課，但除了我之外沒有人。

soliloquize.definition: To perform a soliloquy; (of a character) to talk to oneself.
soliloquize.example: Alone on stage, the actor began to soliloquize about his doubts.
soliloquize.exampleZh: 演員獨自一人在舞台上，開始自言自語地表達他的疑慮。

reptilian.definition: Of or relating to the members of the class Reptilia.
reptilian.example: A reptilian creature jumped out of the lake and attacked Martino.
reptilian.exampleZh: 一隻爬行動物從湖裡跳出來襲擊了馬蒂諾。

contagion.example: The contagion is damaging the Italian economy and its important tourism industry.
contagion.exampleZh: 這種蔓延正在損害義大利經濟及其重要的旅遊業。

homestead.definition: A house together with surrounding land and buildings, especially on a farm; the property comprising these.
homestead.example: Tom bought a remote homestead in the Scottish Highlands.
homestead.exampleZh: 湯姆在蘇格蘭高地買了一個偏遠的宅基地。

incumbent.definition: The current holder of an office, such as ecclesiastical benefice or an elected office.
incumbent.example: Proper behavior is incumbent on all holders of positions of trust.
incumbent.exampleZh: 所有受信任職位的持有者都有責任採取正確的行為。

nonchalant.example: We handled the whole frenetic situation with a nonchalant attitude.
nonchalant.exampleZh: 我們以一種漫不經心的態度處理了整個瘋狂的局面。

nonchalantly.example: Fyodor answered all the questions nonchalantly.
nonchalantly.exampleZh: 費奧多爾漫不經心地回答了所有問題。

wallow.definition: To roll oneself about in something dirty, for example in mud.

wax.example: What role does the wax in your earhole fulfill?
wax.exampleZh: 耳孔中的耳垢有什麼作用？

lassitude.definition: Lethargy or lack of energy; fatigue.
lassitude.example: Counterintuitively, the cure for my lassitude was exercise.
lassitude.exampleZh: 與直覺相反，運動是治療我疲倦的方法。

wane.definition: To progressively lose its splendor, value, ardor, power, intensity etc.; to decline.
wane.example: As the evening wore on, my enthusiasm for the party began to wane.
wane.exampleZh: 隨著夜幕降臨，我對聚會的熱情開始減弱。

ostensibly.example: He invited her up to his room, ostensibly to look at his stamp collection.
ostensibly.exampleZh: 他邀請她到他的房間，表面上是為了看看他的集郵。

ostensible.definition: Apparent, evident; meant for open display.
ostensible.example: The ostensible reason for his visit to New York was to see his mother, but the real reason was to get to the Yankees game the next day.
ostensible.exampleZh: 他來紐約表面上是為了探望母親，但真正的原因是為了去看第二天洋基隊的比賽。

undemonstrative.definition: Not given to showing emotion or feelings; reserved or distant.
undemonstrative.example: Though undemonstrative, he clearly cared deeply about his family.
undemonstrative.exampleZh: 雖然不愛表達，但他顯然非常關心他的家人。

interlocking.definition: To fit or clasp together securely.
interlocking.example: Railway interlocking plants are controlled by balises.
interlocking.exampleZh: 鐵路聯鎖工廠由平衡器控制。

menacingly.exampleZh: 伊戈爾威脅地接近安娜。

opacity.definition: The state or quality of being opaque, not allowing light to pass through
opacity.example: We could not see the sandbar due to the opacity of the muddy water.
opacity.exampleZh: 由於渾水不透明，我們看不到沙洲。

opaque.example: Opaque? Not at all. Intuitively obvious to the most casual observer.
opaque.exampleZh: 不透明？一點也不。對於最不經意的觀察者來說，直觀上是顯而易見的。

remit.definition: To transmit or send (e.g. money in payment); to supply.
remit.example: to remit the performance of an obligation

remittance.definition: The act of transmitting money, bills, etc. to a distant place, in return or payment for goods purchased.
remittance.example: I'm sorry, without an I.D. you cannot receive a remittance.
remittance.exampleZh: 抱歉，沒有身分證您無法收到匯款。

pedestal.definition: The base or foot of a column, statue, vase, lamp.
pedestal.example: He has put his mother on a pedestal. You can't say a word against her.
pedestal.exampleZh: 他把他的母親捧為神壇。你不能對她說半句話。

pedestrian.definition: Of or intended for those who are walking.

recourse.definition: The act of seeking assistance or advice.
recourse.example: If the law is not repealed, in toto, what recourse do citizens have?
recourse.exampleZh: 如果該法律沒有全部廢除，公民有什麼追索權？

lurid.example: The accident was described with lurid detail.
lurid.exampleZh: 對這起事故的描述非常詳細。

fallacy.definition: Deceptive or false appearance; that which misleads the eye or the mind.
fallacy.example: Affirming the consequent is an example of a formal logical fallacy.
fallacy.exampleZh: 肯定結果是形式邏輯謬誤的例子。

rife.definition: Widespread, common, prevalent, current (mainly of unpleasant or harmful things).
rife.example: Smallpox was rife after the siege had been lifted.

polysyllabic.definition: (of a word) Having more than one syllable; having multiple or many syllables.
polysyllabic.example: "Antidisestablishmentarianism" definitely qualifies as a polysyllabic word.
polysyllabic.exampleZh: 「反建制主義」絕對是個多音節字。

mercenary.definition: One motivated by gain, especially monetary.

superfluous.definition: In excess of what is required or sufficient.
superfluous.example: With a full rain suit, carrying an umbrella may be superfluous.
superfluous.exampleZh: 穿著全套雨衣，攜帶雨傘可能是多餘的。

syllabic.definition: Of, relating to, or consisting of a syllable or syllables.
syllabic.example: It's a kinda ridicule unnecessary fibonacci-syllabic-heap.
syllabic.exampleZh: 這有點嘲笑不必要的斐波那契音節堆。

snippet.definition: A small part of something, such as a song or fabric; sample.
snippet.example: From the snippet I heard of their rehearsal, they sound pretty good.
snippet.exampleZh: 從我聽到他們排練的片段來看，他們聽起來相當不錯。

practitioner.definition: A person who practices a profession or art, especially law or medicine.

ephemera.definition: Objects that are designed to be short-lived.

ephemeral.definition: Lasting for a short period of time.
ephemeral.example: What is eternal in nature when everything is so ephemeral?
ephemeral.exampleZh: 當一切都如此短暫，自然界中什麼是永恆的？

summarily.example: They were fired summarily at a single plant-wide meeting.
summarily.exampleZh: 他們在一次全廠會議上被立即解僱。

infallibility.definition: The property of being infallible; the ability to never make a mistake.
infallibility.example: No scientist should claim infallibility; every theory can be revised.
infallibility.exampleZh: 任何科學家都不應聲稱自己絕對正確；每個理論都可以被修改。

infallible.definition: Without fault or weakness; incapable of error or fallacy.
infallible.example: He knows about many things, but even he is not infallible.
infallible.exampleZh: 他知道很多事情，但即使他也不是絕對正確的。

depraved.definition: Perverted or extremely wrong in a moral sense.
depraved.exampleZh: 他們墮落的生活方式讓我感到厭惡。

revile.example: Love me or revile me, but do not ignore me.
revile.exampleZh: 愛我或辱罵我，但不要忽視我。

revulsion.definition: Abhorrence, a sense of loathing, intense aversion, repugnance, repulsion, horror.
revulsion.example: Tom has a natural revulsion towards weirdos with bad hobbies.
revulsion.exampleZh: 湯姆對有壞嗜好的怪人有一種天生的厭惡。

divination.definition: The act of divining; a foreseeing or foretelling of future events.
divination.example: In ancient times, priests used the flight of birds for divination.
divination.exampleZh: 在古代，祭司利用鳥類的飛行來占卜。

scavenger.definition: Someone who scavenges, especially one who searches through rubbish for food or useful things.
scavenger.example: Tom and Mary organized a scavenger hunt.
scavenger.exampleZh: 湯姆和瑪麗組織了一次尋寶遊戲。

vermin.definition: Any one of various common types of small insects or animals which cause harm and annoyance.
vermin.example: The area was plagued by all sorts of vermin: fleas, lice, mice, and rats to name a few.
vermin.exampleZh: 該地區受到各種害蟲的困擾：跳蚤、蝨子、老鼠等等。

emulate.definition: To attempt to equal or be the same as.
emulate.example: The West is standing as a shining moral light to emulate.
emulate.exampleZh: 西方是值得效法的道德之光。

totemic.definition: Serving as, or relating to, a totem.
totemic.example: Many Brexit supporters saw regaining control of the country’s sovereign waters as totemic.
totemic.exampleZh: 許多英國脫歐支持者將重新控制該國主權水域視為圖騰。

emulation.definition: The endeavor or desire to equal or excel someone else in qualities or actions.
emulation.example: a great figure who is worthy of respect and emulation
emulation.exampleZh: 值得尊敬與效法的偉大人物

avian.definition: Characteristic of or pertaining to birds, or to bird-like or flying creatures.
avian.example: Today he works at the Lab of Ornithology, unraveling avian mysteries.
avian.exampleZh: 如今，他在鳥類學實驗室工作，解開鳥類之謎。

brouhaha.definition: A stir; a fuss or uproar.
brouhaha.example: It caused quite a brouhaha when the school suspended one of its top students for refusing to adhere to the dress code.
brouhaha.exampleZh: 當一名優秀學生因拒絕遵守服裝要求而被學校停職時，引起了軒然大波。

vestigial.definition: Of or pertaining to a vestige or remnant; like a trace from the past.
vestigial.example: All non-winged insects have at least vestigial wings.
vestigial.exampleZh: 所有無翅昆蟲至少有退化的翅膀。

magnate.example: I have decided to become an oil magnate, after spending quite some time reading the dictionary definition of the word magnate.
magnate.exampleZh: 在花了相當長的時間閱讀字典中“巨頭”一詞的定義後，我決定成為石油巨頭。

magnanimous.example: He thinks himself ardent, impulsive, passionate, magnanimous — capable of boundless enthusiasm for an idea or a sentiment.
magnanimous.exampleZh: 他認為自己熱情、衝動、熱情、寬宏──能夠對一個想法或一種情感懷有無限的熱情。

whirlwind.definition: A violent windstorm of limited extent, as the tornado, characterized by an inward spiral motion of the air with an upward current in the center; a vortex of air. It usually has a rapid progressive motion.
whirlwind.example: Once he got that new scooter he turned into a whirlwind and damaged all the flowers.
whirlwind.exampleZh: 一旦他得到了那輛新摩托車，他就變成了旋風，損壞了所有的花朵。

magnanimity.definition: The quality of being magnanimous; greatness of mind; elevation or dignity of soul.
magnanimity.example: Magnanimity is a great treasure; harmonious affinity with others is wonderful, Detachment from name and gain averts trouble; happiness makes older people feel young.
magnanimity.exampleZh: 寬宏大量，是一筆巨大的財富；和睦相處為妙，超脫名利，避免煩惱。幸福使老年人感覺年輕。

secular.definition: Not specifically religious; lay or civil, as opposed to clerical.
secular.exampleZh: 天主教的世俗神職人員

strait.definition: A narrow channel of water connecting two larger bodies of water.

euphemism.definition: The use of a word or phrase to replace another with one that is considered less offensive, blunt or vulgar than the word or phrase which it replaces.
euphemism.example: The term sanitation worker is a euphemism for a garbage man.
euphemism.exampleZh: 環衛工人這個詞是垃圾工人的委婉說法。

euphemistic.example: Most languages have euphemistic expressions to describe death and dying.
euphemistic.exampleZh: 大多數語言都有委婉的表達方式來描述死亡和垂死。

euphemistically.example: Prisons are euphemistically called rehabilitation centers.
euphemistically.exampleZh: 監獄被委婉地稱為康復中心。

condensation.definition: The act or process of condensing or of being condensed
condensation.example: Rivulets of condensation ran down the draughty windows.
condensation.exampleZh: 凝結的水流順著通風良好的窗戶流下來。

colloquial.definition: Denoting a manner of speaking or writing that is characteristic of familiar conversation, of common parlance; informal.
colloquial.example: The colloquial and at times sarcastic tone of her books make her popular with teenagers.
colloquial.exampleZh: 她的書的口語和有時諷刺的語氣使她深受青少年的歡迎。

colloquially.example: Cattle, colloquially referred to as cows, are domesticated ungulates.
colloquially.exampleZh: 牛，俗稱乳牛，是馴化的有蹄類動物。

colloquium.definition: A colloquy; a meeting for discussion.
colloquium.example: Graduate students presented their research at the weekly colloquium.
colloquium.exampleZh: 研究生在每週的研討會上展示了他們的研究成果。

allusion.definition: An indirect reference; a hint; a reference to something supposed to be known, but not explicitly mentioned
allusion.example: Fortunately the guest of honor did not pick up on the allusion.
allusion.exampleZh: 幸運的是，主賓並沒有註意到這個暗示。

disparage.definition: To match unequally; to degrade or dishonor.
disparage.example: We didn't mean to disparage our contenders.
disparage.exampleZh: 我們並不是有意貶低我們的競爭者。

disparaging.example: The candidate made disparaging remarks about his opponent, but they only made him seem small for insulting a worthy adversary.
disparaging.exampleZh: 這位候選人對他的對手發表了貶低性的言論，但這只會讓他因侮辱一位有價值的對手而顯得渺小。

topography.definition: A precise description of a place.
topography.example: It's easy to see the area's majestic topography from the cockpit of a helicopter.
topography.exampleZh: 從直升機的駕駛艙裡可以輕鬆看到該地區雄偉的地形。

topographical.example: It was impossible to build the city according to a grid since the topographical features of the land were so irregular.
topographical.exampleZh: 由於土地的地形特徵非常不規則，不可能按照網格來建造城市。

confabulate.definition: To speak casually with; to chat.
confabulate.example: The two old friends would confabulate for hours over coffee.
confabulate.exampleZh: 這兩個老朋友會一邊喝咖啡一邊聊上好幾個小時。

surrealist.example: Dalí is the surrealist for people who don't like surrealism.
surrealist.exampleZh: 對於不喜歡超現實主義的人來說，達利是超現實主義者。

surrealism.definition: An artistic movement and an aesthetic philosophy that aims for the liberation of the mind by emphasizing the critical and imaginative powers of the subconscious.
surrealism.exampleZh: 超現實主義是純粹的心理自動主義。

contrive.definition: To invent by an exercise of ingenuity; to devise
contrive.example: He doesn’t know what causes it, but just wait. He’ll contrive an explanation.
contrive.exampleZh: 他不知道是什麼原因造成的，但只是等待。他會想出一個解釋。

malicious.definition: Of, pertaining to, or as a result of malice or spite
malicious.example: He was sent off for a malicious tackle on Jones.
malicious.exampleZh: 他因惡意鏟斷瓊斯而被罰下場。

malice.definition: Intention to harm or deprive in an illegal or immoral way. Desire to take pleasure in another's misfortune.
malice.example: Love brings beauty; malice brings ugliness.
malice.exampleZh: 愛帶來美麗；惡意帶來醜陋。

maliciously.definition: In a malicious manner, or for malicious reasons
maliciously.example: Miroslav was deliberately and maliciously killed.
maliciously.exampleZh: 米羅斯拉夫是被蓄意惡意殺害的。

cinematography.definition: The art, process, or job of filming movies.
cinematography.example: Although the cinematography was well-executed, the sound effects were amateurish.
cinematography.exampleZh: 儘管電影攝影做得很好，但音效卻很業餘。

cinematographer.definition: A photographer who operates a movie camera, or who oversees the operations of the cameras and lighting when making a film.
cinematographer.example: The film's haunting beauty owed much to its talented cinematographer.
cinematographer.exampleZh: 這部電影令人難忘的美麗很大程度上歸功於其才華橫溢的攝影師。

maverick.example: He made a maverick decision.  She is such a maverick person.
maverick.exampleZh: 他做出了一個特立獨行的決定。  她就是這樣一個特立獨行的人。

diligence.definition: Steady application; industry; careful work involving long-term effort.

diligent.definition: Performing with industrious concentration; hard-working and focused.
diligent.example: Scientists are very diligent in their work.
diligent.exampleZh: 科學家們非常勤奮地工作。

diligently.definition: In a diligent manner, with appropriate effort, carefully.

turret.definition: A little tower, frequently a merely ornamental structure at one of the corners of a building or castle.
turret.example: He pointed in the direction of the turret.

parochial.example: Some people in the United States have been accused of taking a parochial view, of not being interested in international matters.
parochial.exampleZh: 美國一些人被指責觀點狹隘，對國際事務不感興趣。

snobbery.definition: The property or trait of being a snob.
snobbery.example: The English class system is known for its snobbery.
snobbery.exampleZh: 英國的階級制度以其勢利而聞名。

gravitate.definition: To move under the force of gravity.
gravitate.example: Children naturally gravitate to such a big, friendly man.
gravitate.exampleZh: 孩子自然會被這樣一個大而友善的男人所吸引。

gravitational.definition: Pertaining to, or caused by, gravity or gravitation.
gravitational.exampleZh: 科學家已經探測到重力波。

consternation.definition: Amazement or horror that confounds the faculties, and incapacitates for reflection; terror, combined with amazement; dismay.
consternation.example: I learned with consternation that my fears had come to pass.
consternation.exampleZh: 我驚愕地發現我的恐懼成真了。

haughty.definition: Conveying in demeanour the assumption of superiority; disdainful, supercilious.
haughty.example: That haughty girl thinks she's better than everyone.
haughty.exampleZh: 那個傲慢的女孩認為她比所有人都優秀。

axiomatic.example: You think it's axiomatic, but I think we need to prove it.
axiomatic.exampleZh: 你認為這是不言而喻的，但我認為我們需要證明它。

sulphur.definition: A chemical element (symbol S) with an atomic number of 16.
sulphur.example: Iron has an affinity for sulphur, and so does hydrogen for oxygen.
sulphur.exampleZh: 鐵對硫有親和力，氫對氧也有親和力。

sulphuric.definition: Of, or relating to sulfur, especially in its higher oxidation state
sulphuric.example: Sulphuric acid was formally referred to as Melanterite oil.
sulphuric.exampleZh: 硫酸的正式名稱為Melanerite 油。

moor.definition: An extensive waste covered with patches of heath, and having a poor, light soil, but sometimes marshy, and abounding in peat; a heath
moor.example: A cold, biting wind blew across the moor, and the travellers hastened their step.
moor.exampleZh: 一陣寒冷刺骨的風吹過荒原，旅行者加快了腳步。

solitary.definition: Living or being by oneself; alone; having no companion present

solitude.definition: Aloneness; state of being alone or solitary, by oneself.
solitude.exampleZh: 孤獨使人充滿活力；隔離會殺人。

adhesion.definition: The ability of a substance to stick to an unlike substance.
adhesion.example: They theorized that the easily reversible adhesion of the gecko's footpads is not dependent solely on those microscopic hairs, but also on the unique internal anatomy of the lizard's foot.
adhesion.exampleZh: 他們的理論是，壁虎足墊的易逆黏附不僅取決於那些微小的毛髮，還取決於蜥蜴腳獨特的內部解剖結構。

dribble.definition: (basketball, soccer) In various ball games, to move (with) the ball, controlling its path by kicking or bouncing it repeatedly
dribble.example: He managed to dribble past all the defenders.
dribble.exampleZh: 他成功地運球過了所有的防守球員。

ingrate.example: 1843, But Mr Pecksniff, dismissing all ephemeral considerations of social pleasure and enjoyment, concentrated his meditations on the one great virtuous purpose before him, of casting out that ingrate and deceiver, whose presence yet troubled his domestic hearth, and was a sacrilege upon the altars of his household gods. — Charles Dickens, Martin Chuzzlewit
ingrate.exampleZh: 1843年，佩克斯尼夫先生拋開了所有短暫的社交快樂和享受的考慮，集中精力思考擺在他面前的一個偉大的道德目標，即驅逐那個忘恩負義和騙子，這個人的出現仍然困擾著他的家庭，並且是對他家庭神的祭壇的褻瀆。 ——查爾斯狄更斯、馬丁查茲勒維特

rickety.definition: Of an object: not strong or sturdy, as because of poor construction or upkeep; not safe or secure.
rickety.example: He hesitated about climbing such a small, rickety ladder.
rickety.exampleZh: 他猶豫著要不要爬這麼小的、搖搖晃晃的梯子。

consign.definition: To transfer to the custody of, usually for sale, transport, or safekeeping.
consign.example: to consign the body to the grave

consignment.definition: A collection of goods to be sent, in transit or having been sent
consignment.example: In this store, we take items on consignment. We only pay you after someone buys them.
consignment.exampleZh: 本店接受寄售商品。我們只會在有人購買後才向您付款。

fete.definition: A festival open to the public, the proceeds from which are often given to charity.
fete.example: Every village, however small, has its annual fete.
fete.exampleZh: 每個村莊，無論多麼小，都會舉辦一年一度的節慶。

opulent.example: Sami lived in an opulent mansion in Cairo.
opulent.exampleZh: 薩米住在開羅的一棟豪華宅邸裡。

plutocrat.definition: Someone who rules by virtue of his or her wealth.
plutocrat.example: The plutocrat is a person whose power comes from their wealth.
plutocrat.exampleZh: 富豪是個權力來自於財富的人。

averse.definition: Having a repugnance or opposition of mind.
averse.example: This investment is not for the risk-averse.
averse.exampleZh: 這項投資不適合規避風險的人。

edify.example: Good books can edify the mind as well as entertain it.
edify.exampleZh: 好書既可以陶冶心靈，又可以娛樂心靈。

edification.definition: The act of edifying, or the state of being edified or improved; a building process, especially morally, emotionally, or spiritually
edification.example: The geology course isn't required; I'm taking it for my own edification.
edification.exampleZh: 地質課程不是必需的；我把它當作我自己的教誨。

rhetoric.definition: The art of using language, especially public speaking, as a means to persuade.

rhetorical.definition: Part of or similar to rhetoric, the use of language as a means to persuade.
rhetorical.example: A rhetorical question is one used merely to make a point, with no response expected.
rhetorical.exampleZh: 反問句僅用於表達觀點，不期望得到答覆。

flighty.definition: Given to unplanned and silly ideas or actions.
flighty.example: My boss accused me of being flighty, so I immediately quit.
flighty.exampleZh: 老闆指責我輕浮，所以我立刻辭職了。

brimstone.definition: The sulfur of Hell; Hell, damnation.
brimstone.example: Medieval alchemists believed brimstone held purifying powers.
brimstone.exampleZh: 中世紀的煉金術士相信硫磺具有淨化的能力。

impeccable.definition: Perfect, without faults, flaws or errors
impeccable.example: He grew up in Norway, but he writes impeccable English.
impeccable.exampleZh: 他在挪威長大，但他的英文寫得無可挑剔。

romp.definition: To play about roughly, energetically or boisterously.
romp.example: When the kids're allowed to romp in the bedroom, they break something.
romp.exampleZh: 當孩子們被允許在臥室裡玩耍時，他們會打破一些東西。

impeccably.definition: In a perfect or flawless manner.

meticulous.definition: Characterized by very precise, conscientious attention to details.

romper.example: The lively toddler was a restless little romper who never sat still.
romper.exampleZh: 這個活潑的小孩是個不安分的小連身褲，永遠不會安靜地坐著。

meticulously.exampleZh: 費奧多爾小心翼翼地收拾好他的裝備。

ingratiating.definition: Which ingratiates; which attempts to bring oneself into the favour of another, often with flattery or insincerity.

ingratiate.definition: To bring oneself into favour with someone by flattering or trying to please him or her.
ingratiate.example: Go over there, meet my grandparents, and ingratiate yourself.
ingratiate.exampleZh: 去那裡見見我的祖父母，然後討好自己。

shimmer.definition: To shine with a veiled, tremulous, or intermittent light; to gleam faintly.
shimmer.example: The shimmer of the city lights was mesmerizing.
shimmer.exampleZh: 城市燈光的閃爍令人著迷。

swivel.definition: To swing or turn, as on a pin or pivot.

cull.definition: To pick or take someone or something (from a larger group).
cull.example: I breed betta fish and cull my unwanted stock by feeding them to my oscar.
cull.exampleZh: 我飼養鬥魚，然後剔除不需要的魚群，將它們餵給我的奧斯卡。

quiver.definition: A container for arrows, crossbow bolts or darts, such as those fired from a bow, crossbow or blowgun.
quiver.example: He's got lots of sales pitches in his quiver.
quiver.exampleZh: 他的箭袋裡有很多推銷詞。

mottled.example: The mottled skin of the snake was a camouflage that helped it blend in with the shadows.
mottled.exampleZh: 蛇斑駁的皮膚是一種偽裝，有助於它融入陰影。

angular.definition: Relating or pertaining to an angle or angles.

epoxy.definition: A thermosetting polyepoxide resin used chiefly in strong adhesives, coatings and laminates; epoxy resin.
epoxy.example: After bonding, more ductile epoxy is used to waterproof the bond.
epoxy.exampleZh: 黏合後，使用更具延展性的環氧樹脂來防水黏合。

dampen.definition: To make damp or moist; to make slightly wet.
dampen.example: Benedito used water to dampen the cloth for cleaning.
dampen.exampleZh: 貝內迪托用水沾濕布清潔。

buccaneer.definition: To engage in piracy against any but one's own nation's ships.
buccaneer.example: Life as a buccaneer wasn't as fascinating as he thought. It was too bloody, so Mario decided to drop it and go back home.
buccaneer.exampleZh: 海盜的生活並不像他想像的那麼令人著迷。這太血腥了，所以馬裡奧決定放棄它並回家。

cay.definition: A small, low island largely made of sand or coral.
cay.example: We spent the afternoon snorkeling around the tiny cay, surrounded by crystal-clear waters and colorful fish.
cay.exampleZh: 我們整個下午都在這個小珊瑚礁周圍浮潛，周圍環繞著清澈的海水和色彩繽紛的魚。

singe.example: Be careful not to singe your hair when you light the candles.
singe.exampleZh: 點燃蠟燭時要小心，不要燒焦頭髮。

cordon.definition: A ribbon normally worn diagonally across the chest as a decoration or insignia of rank etc.
cordon.example: Several people tried to break through the cordon.
cordon.exampleZh: 有幾個人試圖衝破警戒線。

infernal.definition: Of or relating to hell, or the world of the dead; hellish.
infernal.example: No one has crossed that infernal desert in hundreds of years.
infernal.exampleZh: 數百年來沒有人穿越過那片地獄般的沙漠。

presentational.example: The team focused on the presentational side, polishing every slide.
presentational.exampleZh: 團隊專注於簡報方面，完善每張投影片。

embed.definition: To lay as in a bed; to lay in surrounding matter; to bed.
embed.example: to embed something in clay, mortar, or sand
embed.exampleZh: 將某物嵌入黏土、砂漿或沙中

happenstance.definition: The chance or random quality of an event or circumstance.

plume.definition: A feather of a bird, especially a large or showy one used as a decoration.
plume.example: There was a plume of smoke coming from the shack.

reconcile.definition: To restore a friendly relationship; to bring back to harmony.
reconcile.example: to reconcile people who have quarrelled

reconciliation.definition: The reestablishment of friendly relations; conciliation or rapprochement.
reconciliation.example: He longed for reconciliation with his estranged father, but too many painful memories kept him from making contact again.
reconciliation.exampleZh: 他渴望與疏遠的父親和解，但太多痛苦的回憶讓他無法再次聯繫。

conciliatory.definition: Willing to conciliate, or to make concessions.
conciliatory.example: He was still mad about the accident despite his wife's conciliatory words.
conciliatory.exampleZh: 儘管妻子說了安撫的話，他仍然對這次事故感到憤怒。

conciliation.definition: The process of bringing peace and harmony; the ending of strife.
conciliation.example: The conciliation effort resulted in a mutually acceptable solution.
conciliation.exampleZh: 調解努力達成了雙方都能接受的解決方案。

preconception.definition: An opinion formed before obtaining adequate evidence, especially as the result of bias or prejudice.
preconception.example: This will challenge any preconception you may have of modern art.
preconception.exampleZh: 這會挑戰你對現代藝術可能抱有的任何先入之見。

baroque.definition: Ornate, intricate, decorated, laden with detail.
baroque.example: She prefers quiet music - the baroque, for example.
baroque.exampleZh: 她更喜歡安靜的音樂——例如巴洛克音樂。

unobtrusive.example: On her head, she wore an unobtrusive microphone that matched her skin color.
unobtrusive.exampleZh: 她頭上戴著一個與她膚色相配的不顯眼的麥克風。

hauntingly.example: The violin played a hauntingly beautiful melody.
hauntingly.exampleZh: 小提琴奏出了優美動聽的旋律。

hazard.definition: To expose to chance; to take a risk.

brim.example: She filled the glass right up to the brim.

authenticity.definition: The quality of being genuine or not corrupted from the original.
authenticity.example: I hereby certify the authenticity of this copy.
authenticity.exampleZh: 本人特此證明本副本的真實性。

artifact.definition: An object made or shaped by human hand or labor.
artifact.example: The spot on his lung turned out to be an artifact of the X-ray process.
artifact.exampleZh: 他肺上的斑點原來是X光過程的偽像。

cognitive.definition: Relating to the part of mental functions that deals with logic, as opposed to affective which deals with emotions.
cognitive.example: Introverted thinking is my dominant cognitive function.
cognitive.exampleZh: 內向思維是我的主要認知功能。

annex.example: Netanyahu wants to annex the West Bank.
annex.exampleZh: 內塔尼亞胡希望吞併約旦河西岸。

cloistered.definition: Dwelling or raised in, or as if in, cloisters; solitary.
cloistered.example: The architect cloistered the college just like the monastery which founded it.
cloistered.exampleZh: 建築師像創建它的修道院一樣將學院隱居。

resuscitate.definition: To restore consciousness, vigor, or life to.
resuscitate.example: to resuscitate a drowned person; to resuscitate withered plants
resuscitate.exampleZh: 使溺者甦醒； to resuscitate withered plants

fabrication.definition: The act of fabricating, framing, or constructing; construction; manufacture
fabrication.example: the fabrication of a bridge, a church, or a government

patchy.definition: Full of, or covered with, patches; abounding in patches.

fabricate.definition: To form into a whole by uniting its parts; to construct; to build.
fabricate.example: to fabricate a bridge or ship

resounding.definition: Having a deep, rich sound; mellow and resonant.
resounding.example: We had a resounding win against the rival team.
resounding.exampleZh: 我們對對手隊取得了一場徹底的勝利。

ordain.example: The bishop's role includes the power to select, ordain, and supervise ministers.
ordain.exampleZh: 主教的職責包括選擇、任命和監督牧師的權力。

elated.definition: Extremely happy and excited; delighted; pleased, euphoric.
elated.example: She was elated with her new car.

inchoate.definition: Recently started but not fully formed yet; just begun; only elementary or immature.
inchoate.example: This definition is inchoate, and thus it must be interpreted.
inchoate.exampleZh: 這個定義還很不成熟，因此必須對其進行解釋。

elation.definition: An exhilarating psychological state of pride and optimism.
elation.exampleZh: 雅尼並不像斯庫拉那樣興高采烈。
