hysteria.definition: A condition where the patient has neurological symptoms such as numbness, blindness, paralysis, or fits, but without any neurological explanation.
hysteria.example: Tom doesn't know the difference between passion and hysteria.
hysteria.exampleZh: 湯姆不知道激情和歇斯底里之間的區別。

pyre.definition: A funeral pile; a combustible heap on which corpses are burned.
pyre.example: The pyre burns the dead, but anxiety burns the living.
pyre.exampleZh: 柴堆焚燒死者，但焦慮焚燒生者。

contemptuous.definition: Showing contempt; expressing disdain; showing a lack of respect.
contemptuous.example: I don't know that guy, but he just gave me a contemptuous look.
contemptuous.exampleZh: 我不認識那個人，但他只是輕蔑地看了我一眼。

contemptuously.definition: In a disrespectful or discourteous manner; condescendingly.
contemptuously.example: He argued with the judge contemptuously, showing no respect or remorse for his actions.
contemptuously.exampleZh: 他輕蔑地與法官爭論，對自己的行為沒有表現出尊重或悔恨。

forlorn.example: She and her uncle were sad and forlorn in their neglect and disgrace.
forlorn.exampleZh: 她和她的叔叔因自己的忽視和恥辱而感到悲傷和孤獨。

rebuke.example: O Lord, do not rebuke me in Your anger or discipline me in Your wrath. (Psalm 6, BSB)
rebuke.exampleZh: 主啊，求祢不要在怒中責備我，也不要在怒中管教我。 （詩篇 6，BSB）

abhor.definition: To regard with horror or detestation; to shrink back with shuddering from; to feel excessive repugnance toward; to detest to extremity; to loathe.
abhor.example: I absolutely abhor being stuck in traffic jams

abhorrent.definition: Inconsistent with, or far removed from, something; strongly opposed

aberration.definition: The act of wandering; deviation from truth, moral rectitude; abnormal; divergence from the straight, correct, proper, normal, or from the natural state.

emulsifier.definition: A substance that helps an emulsion form, or helps keep an emulsion from separating.
emulsifier.example: The lecithin in egg yolks is often used as an emulsifier.
emulsifier.exampleZh: 蛋黃中的卵磷脂常被用作乳化劑。

additive.definition: A substance added to another substance or product to produce specific properties in the combined substance.
additive.example: Oil may be used as an additive in gasoline to improve the lubrication of a small engine.
additive.exampleZh: 油可用作汽油添加劑，以改善小型引擎的潤滑。

paradoxically.definition: In a paradoxical manner; so as to create a paradox.

paradoxical.example: Many paradoxes are paradoxical only because they sound strange.
paradoxical.exampleZh: 許多悖論之所以顯得悖論，只是因為它們聽起來很奇怪。

stringent.definition: Strict; binding strongly; making strict requirements; restrictive; rigid; severe
stringent.example: They have stringent quality requirements outlining what is acceptable.
stringent.exampleZh: 他們有嚴格的品質要求，概述了可接受的範圍。

laryngitis.definition: An inflammation of the larynx, typically resulting in hoarseness.
laryngitis.example: Skillful listening is the best remedy for loneliness, loquaciousness, and laryngitis.
laryngitis.exampleZh: 熟練的傾聽是治療孤獨、多嘴、喉炎的最佳良藥。

standing.definition: (heading) To position or be positioned physically.

astringent.definition: A substance which draws tissue together, thus restricting the flow of blood.
astringent.example: These grapes are fairly astringent, so they make my mouth pucker.
astringent.exampleZh: 這些葡萄相當澀，所以它們讓我的嘴皺起來。

breadwinner.definition: The primary income-earner in a household.
breadwinner.example: Yanni is the essential breadwinner in the family.
breadwinner.exampleZh: 雅尼是家裡重要的經濟支柱。

counsel.definition: The exchange of opinions and advice especially in legal issues; consultation.
counsel.example: Psychiatrists, psychologists, social workers and other mental health professionals counsel clients.
counsel.exampleZh: 精神科醫生、心理學家、社會工作者和其他心理健康專業人員為客戶提供諮詢。

aptitude.definition: Natural ability to acquire knowledge or skill.
aptitude.example: I finally passed the aptitude test for higher management studies.
aptitude.exampleZh: 我終於通過了高階管理研究的能力測試。

banish.definition: (heading) To send someone away and forbid that person from returning.

torrential.definition: Coming or characterized by torrents; flowing heavily or in large quantities.
torrential.example: There was a torrential downpour and we were all soaked.
torrential.exampleZh: 一場傾盆大雨，我們都濕透了。

banishment.example: The judge pronounced banishment upon the war criminal.
banishment.exampleZh: 法官宣布驅逐這名戰犯。

knack.definition: A readiness in performance; aptness at doing something.
knack.example: Their knack for discord is quite impressive.
knack.exampleZh: 他們製造不和的本領令人印象深刻。

maelstrom.example: The maelstrom of passions is not what we need now.
maelstrom.exampleZh: 激情的漩渦不是我們現在所需要的。

fruition.definition: The fulfillment of something worked for.
fruition.example: After six years of hard work, the engineers had brought the project to fruition.
fruition.exampleZh: 經過六年的努力，工程師們使這個計畫取得了成果。

vortex.definition: A whirlwind, whirlpool, or similarly moving matter in the form of a spiral or column.
vortex.example: 2004: the consumer vortex that is East Hampton — The New Yorker, 30 August 2004, p.38
vortex.exampleZh: 2004 年：東漢普頓的消費者漩渦 — 《紐約客》，2004 年 8 月 30 日，第 38 頁

scroll.definition: A roll of paper or parchment; a writing formed into a roll.
scroll.example: I ordered a glass of lemonade and a coffee scroll.
scroll.exampleZh: 我點了一杯檸檬水和一份咖啡捲。

thicket.definition: A dense, but generally small, growth of shrubs, bushes or small trees; a copse.
thicket.example: He had to complete a thicket of paperwork before he was allowed to join the company.
thicket.exampleZh: 在獲準加入公司之前，他必須完成一大堆文書工作。

edifice.definition: A building; a structure; an architectural fabric, especially a large and spectacular one
edifice.example: Marriage is an edifice that has to be rebuilt every day.
edifice.exampleZh: 婚姻是一座大廈，每天都需要重建。

leaven.example: Seven days there shall not be found any leaven in your houses: he that shall eat leavened bread, his soul shall perish out of the assembly of Israel, whether he be a stranger or born in the land.
leaven.exampleZh: 你們家中七日不得有酵；凡吃有酵的餅，無論是外人，是本地生的，他的命必從以色列會中滅亡。

ethos.definition: The character or fundamental values of a person, people, culture, or movement.
ethos.example: Settling down and raising a family was against the ethos of the times.
ethos.exampleZh: 安定下來並建立家庭是違背時代的精神。

interweave.example: Tom's works interweave science fiction and fantasy elements.
interweave.exampleZh: 湯姆的作品交織著科幻和奇幻元素。

indiscrete.example: "That is an indiscrete question." - "There are no indiscrete questions, only indiscrete answers."
indiscrete.exampleZh: “這是一個不明確的問題。” - “沒有不明確的問題，只有不明確的答案。”

telescopic.definition: Pertaining to, or carried out by means of, a telescope.

indiscretion.definition: The quality or state of being indiscreet; lack of discretion
indiscretion.example: Your frivolity and indiscretion in choosing friends, Tom, can greatly hurt you in life.
indiscretion.exampleZh: 湯姆，你在選擇朋友時的輕率和輕率會為你的生活帶來極大的傷害。

amorphous.definition: Lacking a definite form or clear shape.
amorphous.example: The enormous pile of spaghetti landed on the floor in an amorphous heap.
amorphous.exampleZh: 一大堆義大利麵落在地板上，形成了無定形的一堆。

deterrent.example: That would act as a deterrent, don't you think?
deterrent.exampleZh: 這會起到威懾作用，你不覺得嗎？

selectivity.definition: The quality of being selective; usually the extent to which something is selective.
selectivity.example: The university is famous for the selectivity of its admissions process.
selectivity.exampleZh: 該大學以其招生過程的選擇性而聞名。

fractious.example: A fractious child is a troublesome thing to deal with, but a fractious man is far worse.
fractious.exampleZh: 一個脾氣暴躁的孩子是一件很麻煩的事情，但一個脾氣暴躁的男人更糟。

mystique.definition: An aura of heightened interest, meaning or mystery surrounding a person or thing.
mystique.example: Westerners opine that there are magic and mystique in sinograms.
mystique.exampleZh: 西方人認為正弦圖具有魔力和神秘性。

assimilation.definition: The act of assimilating or the state of being assimilated.
assimilation.example: Mountain and desert Berbers resisted assimilation.
assimilation.exampleZh: 山區和沙漠的柏柏爾人抵制同化。

verily.example: I verily believe that your convictions are false.
verily.exampleZh: 我堅信你的信念是錯的。

verbatim.definition: (of a document) Corresponding with the original word for word.
verbatim.example: Date unknown: Joint Committee on Printing Congress of the United States, General Statement of Procedure for Verbatim Reporting of Proceedings in Senate Chamber, page five:
verbatim.exampleZh: 日期未知：美國國會印刷聯合委員會，參議院會議記錄逐字報告程序的一般聲明，第五頁：

veritable.exampleZh: 他是個名副其實的天才。

verisimilitude.definition: The property of seeming true, of resembling reality; resemblance to reality, realism.
verisimilitude.example: It is interesting how this conspiracy theory author abandons all attempts at verisimilitude.
verisimilitude.exampleZh: 有趣的是，這位陰謀論作者如何放棄所有真實性的嘗試。

artifice.example: The language of truth must be, with no doubt, simple and without artifice.
artifice.exampleZh: 毫無疑問，真理的語言必須簡單、不做作。

unfeasible.example: This plan is not just unfeasible, but impossible.
unfeasible.exampleZh: 這個計劃不僅不可行，而且不可能。

behest.definition: A command, bidding; sometimes also, an authoritative request; now usually in the phrase at the behest of.
behest.example: He testified that the wire-tapping was carried out at the behest of his superiors.
behest.exampleZh: 他作證說，竊聽是在他的上級的要求下進行的。

virtuoso.definition: An expert in virtù or art objects and antiquities; a connoisseur.
virtuoso.exampleZh: 瑪麗是一位演奏家長笛演奏家。

virtuosity.definition: The technical skills and fluent style of a virtuoso.
virtuosity.example: This pianist is known for his virtuosity.
virtuosity.exampleZh: 這位鋼琴家以其精湛的技藝而聞名。

nascent.example: The nascent VR industry is still having trouble breaking into the mainstream.
nascent.exampleZh: 新興的 VR 產業在打入主流方面仍然遇到困難。

smattering.definition: A superficial or shallow knowledge of a subject.
smattering.example: She knows a smattering of Greek, but not enough to carry on a conversation.
smattering.exampleZh: 她懂一點希臘語，但還不足以進行對話。

wheeze.definition: To breathe hard, and with an audible piping or whistling sound, as persons affected with asthma.
wheeze.example: The new comedy is a wheeze.

corpus.definition: A collection of writings, often on a specific topic, of a specific genre, from a specific demographic or a particular author, etc.
corpus.example: I regularly contribute to the Berber corpus.
corpus.exampleZh: 我定期為柏柏爾語料庫做出貢獻。

hoist.definition: To raise; to lift; to elevate (especially, to raise or lift to a desired elevation, by means of tackle or pulley, said of a sail, a flag, a heavy package or weight).
hoist.example: Give me a hoist over that wall.
hoist.exampleZh: 給我一台起重機翻過那堵牆。

neophyte.definition: A beginner; a person who is new to a subject, skill, or belief.
neophyte.example: I was still a neophyte in Congress and eagerly followed his leadership in this fight.
neophyte.exampleZh: 我還是國會的新手，熱切地追隨他在這場鬥爭中的領導。

buzzard.definition: Any of several Old World birds of prey of the genus Buteo with broad wings and a broad tail.
buzzard.example: Can you tell the difference between a buzzard and a kite?
buzzard.exampleZh: 你能分辨禿鷹和風箏嗎？

derisive.definition: Expressing or characterized by derision; mocking; ridiculing.
derisive.example: The critic's review of the film was derisive.
derisive.exampleZh: 影評人對這部電影的評論是嘲諷的。

derision.example: There are quite a few ways to express derision in Esperanto.
derision.exampleZh: 世界語中有很多表達嘲笑的方式。

truism.example: To say that technology changes rapidly is to utter a truism.
truism.exampleZh: 說科技日新月異是不言而喻的。

prurient.definition: Uneasy with desire; itching; especially, having a lascivious anxiety or propensity; lustful.
prurient.example: Much to my dismay, Mary came to the party wearing a prurient dress.
prurient.exampleZh: 令我非常沮喪的是，瑪莉穿著一件淫蕩的衣服來參加派對。

wither.definition: To shrivel, droop or dry up, especially from lack of water.

tetchy.definition: Easily annoyed or irritated; peevish, testy or irascible.
tetchy.example: But she soon grew weary of that occupation; she became careless in her reports, impatient and tetchy when reprimanded for her negligence.
tetchy.exampleZh: 但她很快就厭倦了這份職業。她在報告中變得粗心大意，當因疏忽而受到斥責時，她變得不耐煩，脾氣暴躁。

agile.definition: Having the faculty of quick motion in the limbs; apt or ready to move

agility.definition: The quality of being agile; the power of moving the limbs quickly and easily; quickness of motion
agility.example: His superior agility countered his lack of strength.
agility.exampleZh: 他卓越的敏捷彌補了力量的不足。

aghast.definition: Terrified; struck with amazement; showing signs of terror or horror.
aghast.example: I was aghast when the incident unfolded in front of me.
aghast.exampleZh: 當這件事發生在我面前時，我感到震驚。

dwarfism.definition: The condition of being a dwarf.
dwarfism.example: The condition, a form of dwarfism, affects the growth of the long bones.
dwarfism.exampleZh: 這種情況是侏儒症的一種，會影響長骨的生長。

rapture.example: Tom believes that the rapture is going to happen any day now.
rapture.exampleZh: 湯姆相信被提隨時都會發生。

rapturous.example: the rapturous emotions on the pulpit

ad-lib.definition: To improvise all or part of a speech or other performance, especially in comedy.
ad-lib.example: He forgot part of his speech and had to ad-lib for a while.
ad-lib.exampleZh: 他忘了部分演講內容，不得不即興發揮一段時間。

arduous.definition: Needing or using up much energy; testing powers of endurance.
arduous.example: The movement towards a peaceful settlement has been a long and arduous political struggle.
arduous.exampleZh: 爭取和平解決的運動是一場長期而艱苦的政治鬥爭。

counterproductive.definition: More of a hindrance than a help.
counterproductive.example: Punching those who hold beliefs you oppose is counterproductive.
counterproductive.exampleZh: 打擊那些持有你反對的信念的人會適得其反。

proverbial.definition: Of, resembling, or expressed as a proverb, cliché, fable, or fairy tale.

instantaneous.definition: Occurring, arising, or functioning without any delay; happening within an imperceptibly brief period of time.
instantaneous.example: He knew it was so, with an unaccountable but instantaneous certainty.
instantaneous.exampleZh: 他知道事情就是這樣，帶著一種無法解釋但瞬間的確定性。

ruminate.definition: To chew cud. (Said of ruminants.) Involves regurgitating partially digested food from the rumen.
ruminate.example: A camel will ruminate just as a cow will.

instantaneously.definition: Without any delay; in an instantaneous manner.
instantaneously.example: If a thermonuclear bomb hit that base, we'd be vaporized instantaneously.
instantaneously.exampleZh: 如果熱核炸彈擊中那個基地，我們就會立即蒸發。

apostle.definition: A missionary, or leader of a religious mission, especially one in the early Christian Church (but see Apostle).
apostle.example: The apostle Paul was born in Tarsus.

blubber.definition: A fatty layer of adipose tissue found immediately beneath the epidermis.
blubber.example: Atuqtuaq helps his mother take the blubber from the seal carcass.
blubber.exampleZh: 阿圖克圖阿克幫助他的母親從海豹屍體上取出鯨脂。

trifle.definition: An English dessert made from a mixture of thick custard, fruit, sponge cake, jelly and whipped cream.
trifle.example: You must not trifle with her affections.
trifle.exampleZh: 你絕不能玩弄她的感情。

merger.example: The office has been topsy-turvy since the merger upset everything.
merger.exampleZh: 自從合併擾亂一切以來，辦公室一直亂七八糟。

paramount.example: Getting those credit cards paid off is paramount.
paramount.exampleZh: 還清這些信用卡是至關重要的。

squirm.definition: To twist one's body with snakelike motions.
squirm.example: The prisoner managed to squirm out of the straitjacket.
squirm.exampleZh: 囚犯設法從束縛衣中掙脫出來。

embody.definition: To represent in a physical or concrete form; to incarnate or personify.
embody.example: As the car salesman approached, wearing a plaid suit and slicked-back hair, he seemed to embody sleaze.
embody.exampleZh: 當汽車推銷員穿著格子西裝和光滑的頭髮走近時，他似乎體現了卑鄙。

floe.definition: A low, flat mass of floating ice.
floe.example: A polar bear drifted past on a small ice floe.
floe.exampleZh: 一隻北極熊在一塊小浮冰上漂過。

embodiment.example: You are the very embodiment of beauty.

wistful.example: His eyes grew wistful as he recalled his university days.
wistful.exampleZh: 回憶起大學時光，他的眼神變得充滿渴望。

wistfully.example: Tom gazes wistfully over the rooftops to where his lover lives.
wistfully.exampleZh: 湯姆渴望地凝視著屋頂上他愛人居住的地方。

mercantile.definition: Concerned with the exchange of goods for profit.
mercantile.example: To what extent was Igor wrong calling her "mercantile"?
mercantile.exampleZh: 伊戈爾稱她為「商人」在多大程度上是錯的？

subsistence.example: the subsistence of qualities in bodies

subsist.definition: To survive on a minimum of resources.
subsist.example: Giraffes subsist mainly on acacia leaves.
subsist.exampleZh: 長頸鹿主要以金合歡葉為食。

legion.definition: The major unit or division of the Roman army, usually comprising 3000 to 6000 infantry soldiers and 100 to 200 cavalry troops.
legion.example: Russia’s labor and capital resources are woefully inadequate to overcome the state’s needs and vulnerabilities, which are legion.
legion.exampleZh: 俄羅斯的勞動力和資本資源嚴重不足以克服國家的巨大需求和脆弱性。

bout.definition: A period of something, usually painful or unpleasant.

confrontational.definition: Pertaining to a confrontation; characterized by, or given to initiating hostile encounter; in-your-face.

gout.definition: An extremely painful inflammation of joints, especially of the big toe, caused by a metabolic defect resulting in the accumulation of uric acid in the blood and the deposition of urates around the joints.
gout.example: Drink wine and get gout, or drink no wine and get gout anyway.
gout.exampleZh: 喝酒就會得痛風，或是不喝酒也會得痛風。

hindrance.definition: Something which hinders: something that holds back or causes problems with something else.
hindrance.example: High-heeled shoes may be fashionable, but they can also be a hindrance to walking.
hindrance.exampleZh: 高跟鞋可能很時尚，但也可能成為行走的障礙。

myriad.definition: (modifying a singular noun) Multifaceted, having innumerable elements
myriad.exampleZh: 地球上棲息著無數的動物。

marketability.definition: The likelihood that something will sell; market appeal.
marketability.example: This is a beautiful house with high marketability.
marketability.exampleZh: 這是一棟漂亮的房子，具有很高的適銷性。

histrionic.definition: Of or relating to actors or acting.
histrionic.example: No need to get all histrionic. You're not on a soap opera.
histrionic.exampleZh: 沒必要裝腔作勢。你不是在演肥皂劇。

tacit.definition: Expressed in silence; implied, but not made explicit; silent.
tacit.example: tacit consent : consent by silence, or by not raising an objection
tacit.exampleZh: 預設同意：以沉默或不提出反對的方式表示同意

tacitly.definition: In a tacit manner; done in silence or implied.
tacitly.example: Your failure to object to the request resulted in you tacitly approving the change.
tacitly.exampleZh: 您未能反對該請求導致您預設了更改。

taciturn.definition: Silent; temperamentally untalkative; disinclined to speak.
taciturn.example: The two sisters could hardly have been more different, one so boisterous and expressive, the other so taciturn and calm.
taciturn.exampleZh: 兩姊妹截然不同，一個如此喧鬧而富有表現力，另一個則如此沉默寡言和冷靜。

surreptitious.definition: Stealthy, furtive, well hidden, covert (especially movements).
surreptitious.example: The surreptitious gesture went unnoticed by the rest of the room.
surreptitious.exampleZh: 這個偷偷摸摸的舉動沒有被房間裡的其他人注意到。

surreptitiously.definition: In a surreptitious manner; stealthily, furtively, secretly.
surreptitiously.example: He was seen surreptitiously eating a piece of cake.
surreptitiously.exampleZh: 有人看到他偷偷地吃著一塊蛋糕。

allegory.definition: The representation of abstract principles by characters or figures.
allegory.example: As an allegory of war, chess is a game of attack and defense.
allegory.exampleZh: 作為戰爭寓言，國際象棋是一種進攻和防守的遊戲。

seedy.example: Pomegranates are as seedy as any fruit you are likely to see.
seedy.exampleZh: 石榴和您可能看到的任何水果一樣多籽。

allegorical.definition: Of, relating to, or containing allegory
allegorical.example: Fifty years ago when these books were first written that was a much more allegorical message and now it's something that looks really cool.
allegorical.exampleZh: 五十年前，當這些書第一次寫出來時，這是一個更具寓言性的信息，現在看起來真的很酷。

punitive.example: The jury awarded $10,000 in punitive damages.
punitive.exampleZh: 陪審團裁定給予 10,000 美元的懲罰性賠償。

temper.exampleZh: 圍繞孩子調整你的語言。

milieu.example: The quiet suburban neighborhood provides the perfect milieu for raising a family.
milieu.exampleZh: 安靜的郊區為養家糊口提供了完美的環境。

adulterate.example: to adulterate food, drink, drugs, coins, etc.
adulterate.exampleZh: 在食品、飲料、藥品、硬幣等中摻假

unadulterated.definition: Pure; not mixed or adulterated with anything
unadulterated.example: The only thing flowing through Tom's veins was pure, unadulterated rage.
unadulterated.exampleZh: 湯姆的血管裡流淌的只有純粹的、不摻雜任何雜質的憤怒。

cogent.definition: Reasonable and convincing; based on evidence.
cogent.example: The prosecution presented a cogent argument, convincing the jury of the defendant's guilt.
cogent.exampleZh: 控方提出了令人信服的論據，使陪審團相信被告有罪。

incongruous.definition: Not similar or congruent; not matching or fitting in.
incongruous.example: 20 and 25 are incongruous with respect to 4.

deaden.definition: To render less lively; to diminish; to muffle.
deaden.example: to deaden a wall or a floor
deaden.exampleZh: 使牆壁或地板變得不活躍

attributive.definition: (grammar, of a word or phrase) Modifying a noun, while in the same phrase as that noun.
attributive.example: In "this big house", "big" is attributive, whereas in "this house is big", it is predicative.
attributive.exampleZh: 在「this big house」中，「big」是定語，而在「this house is big」中，「big」是表語。

ecotourism.definition: Responsible travel to natural areas supporting the fauna, flora, and local economy
ecotourism.example: Ecotourism serves the environment and improves the well-being of indigenous peoples, too.
ecotourism.exampleZh: 生態旅遊服務於環境，也改善了原住民的福祉。

farcical.example: He made a farcical attempt to save face during the fallout of his sex scandal.
farcical.exampleZh: 在性醜聞爆發期間，他做出了一場滑稽的挽回面子的嘗試。

incipient.definition: In an initial stage; beginning, starting, coming into existence.

echolocation.definition: The use of echoes to detect objects as observed in bats and other natural creatures. Also known as biosonar.
echolocation.example: The bat's echolocation allows it to navigate in the dark.
echolocation.exampleZh: 蝙蝠的迴聲定位能力使其能夠在黑暗中導航。

reverb.definition: (audio effects) An electronic effect which simulates echoes or reverberations in the sound signal being processed.
reverb.example: I'd like a little less reverb on the lead singer's voice.
reverb.exampleZh: 我希望主唱聲音的殘響少一點。

reverberation.example: The discomfort caused by the bat's reverberation surprised Tommy.
reverberation.exampleZh: 蝙蝠的迴響所引起的不適讓湯米感到驚訝。

ingenuous.exampleZh: 瑪莉是一位天真爛漫的學生。

elasticity.definition: The property by virtue of which a material deformed under load can regain its original dimensions when unloaded
elasticity.example: If the sales of an item drop by 5% when the price increases by 10%, its price elasticity is -0.5.
elasticity.exampleZh: 如果一種商品的價格上漲10%，銷量下降5%，則其價格彈性為-0.5。

impresario.definition: A manager or producer in the entertainment industry, especially music or theatre.
impresario.exampleZh: 他是一位著名的經理人。

synthesis.definition: The formation of something complex or coherent by combining simpler things.
synthesis.example: The molecule's complexity makes its synthesis almost impossible.
synthesis.exampleZh: 這種分子的複雜性使它幾乎不可能合成。

salivary.example: The parasympathetic nerves of a developing salivary gland innervate the salivary gland epithelium which is essential for organogenesis.
salivary.exampleZh: 發育中的唾液腺的副交感神經支配唾液腺上皮，這對於器官發生至關重要。

wholly.definition: Completely and entirely; to the fullest extent.
wholly.example: A creature wholly given to brawls and wine.
wholly.exampleZh: 一種完全沉迷於爭吵和酗酒的生物。

hypnotherapy.definition: Treatment of disease by means of hypnotism.
hypnotherapy.example: Tom underwent hypnotherapy to help him give up smoking.
hypnotherapy.exampleZh: 湯姆接受了催眠療法來幫助他戒菸。

remorseful.definition: (of a person) Feeling or filled with remorse.
remorseful.example: He was so remorseful that he voluntarily paid full restitution.
remorseful.exampleZh: 他非常懊悔，自願全額賠償。

remonstrate.definition: To object; to express disapproval (with, against).
remonstrate.example: I remonstrate with my boss about my performance evaluation.
remonstrate.exampleZh: 我就我的績效評估向老闆提出抗議。

unexceptional.example: The meal was pleasant but ultimately unexceptional.
unexceptional.exampleZh: 這頓飯很愉快，但最終也不例外。

inexorably.definition: In an inexorable manner; without the possibility of stopping or prevention.
inexorably.example: We watched as the storm clouds advanced inexorably closer to us.
inexorably.exampleZh: 我們看著暴風雲無情地逼近我們。

inexorable.definition: Impossible to prevent or stop; inevitable.
inexorable.example: Holmes sat in a great, old-fashioned chair, his inexorable eyes gleaming out of his haggard face.
inexorable.exampleZh: 福爾摩斯坐在一張老式大椅子上，憔悴的臉上閃爍著冷酷的目光。

paycheck.definition: Money received on payday as payment for work performed.
paycheck.example: He was delighted after getting his first paycheck, but subsequently blew it all on the horses
paycheck.exampleZh: 拿到第一份薪水後，他很高興，但隨後把一切都毀在了馬身上

panther.definition: Any of various big cats with black fur; most especially, the black-coated leopard of India.
panther.example: The black panther prowled silently through the jungle.
panther.exampleZh: 黑豹在叢林中默默地徘徊。

maids.definition: A girl or an unmarried young woman; maiden.

lambert.definition: A cgs unit of luminance; the brightness of a surface that emits one lumen per square centimetre
lambert.example: Connie Lambert keeps active, travels and defies her age of 76.
lambert.exampleZh: 康妮·蘭伯特 (Connie Lambert) 保持活力、旅行並挑戰自己 76 歲的年齡。

omega.definition: The twenty-fourth letter of the Classical and the Modern Greek alphabet, and the twenty-eighth letter of the Old and the Ancient Greek alphabet, i.e. the last letter of every Greek alphabet. Uppercase version: Ω; lowercase: ω.
omega.example: A fireplace is a family hearth, the alpha and omega of comfort and home.
omega.exampleZh: 壁爐是家庭的壁爐，是舒適和家庭的首要條件。

stationed.definition: (usually passive) To put in place to perform a task.
stationed.example: I was stationed on the pier.

whichever.example: These chocolates all look delicious. Whichever shall I choose?
whichever.exampleZh: 這些巧克力看起來都很好吃。我該選哪一個呢？

liability.definition: An obligation, debt or responsibility owed to someone.
liability.example: These terms cover limits to liability and other terms and conditions.
liability.exampleZh: 這些條款涵蓋責任限制以及其他條款和條件。

trout.definition: Any of several species of fish in Salmonidae, closely related to salmon, and distinguished by spawning more than once.
trout.example: Many anglers consider trout to be the archetypical quarry.
trout.exampleZh: 許多釣魚者認為鱒魚是典型的獵物。

crystals.definition: A solid composed of an array of atoms or molecules possessing long-range order and arranged in a pattern which is periodic in three dimensions.

repent.definition: To feel pain, sorrow, or regret for what one has done or omitted to do; the cause for repenting may be indicated with "of".
repent.example: If you are a true Muslim, you should repent to Allah.
repent.exampleZh: 如果你是真正的穆斯林，你應該向真主懺悔。

prosperity.definition: The condition of being prosperous, of having good fortune
prosperity.exampleZh: 阿爾及利亞人希望普遍繁榮。

northwest.definition: The compass point halfway between north and west, bearing 315°, abbreviated as NW.
northwest.example: Phoenician and Hebrew belong to the Northwest Semitic group.
northwest.exampleZh: 腓尼基語和希伯來文屬於西北閃族語族。

martyr.definition: One who willingly accepts being put to death for adhering openly to one's religious beliefs; notably, saints canonized after martyrdom.
martyr.example: Saint Stephen was the first Christian martyr.
martyr.exampleZh: 聖斯蒂芬是第一位基督教殉道者。

excess.definition: The state of surpassing or going beyond limits; the being of a measure beyond sufficiency, necessity, or duty; that which exceeds what is usual or proper
excess.example: The excess of heavy water was given away to the neighbouring country.
excess.exampleZh: 多餘的重水被送給鄰國。

viking.definition: One of the Scandinavian or other Northern European seafaring warriors that raided (and then settled) the British Isles and other parts of Europe in the 8th to the 11th centuries and, according to many historians, were the first Europeans to reach North America.
viking.example: The lur was a viking instrument.

unemployment.definition: The state of having no job; joblessness.

install.definition: Installer. A software utility that installs an application.
install.example: After inserting the disk, you need to run the install.
install.exampleZh: 插入磁碟後，您需要運行安裝。

jeopardy.definition: Danger of loss, harm, or failure.
jeopardy.example: The poor condition of the vehicle put its occupants in constant jeopardy.
jeopardy.exampleZh: 車輛狀況不佳使車內人員始終處於危險之中。

arrogance.definition: The state of being arrogant; a type of extreme or foolish pride in which someone feels much superior to another
arrogance.exampleZh: 傲慢蒙蔽了人們的雙眼。

hacker.definition: One who is expert at programming and solving problems with a computer.

reset.definition: To set back to the initial state.
reset.example: Please request a new password reset.

goons.definition: A thug; a usually muscular henchman with little intelligence (also known as a 'hired goon').
goons.example: Gustavo was suddenly attacked by four of Rima’s goons.
goons.exampleZh: 古斯塔沃突然遭到裡瑪手下四名暴徒的攻擊。

milady.definition: An English noblewoman or gentlewoman; the form of address to such a person; a lady.
milady.example: 'This way, milady,' said the butler, opening the carriage door.
milady.exampleZh: 「這邊走，夫人，」管家打開馬車門說。

bandage.definition: A strip of gauze or similar material used to protect or support a wound or injury.
bandage.example: this new healthcare proposal merely applies a bandage to the current medical crisis
bandage.exampleZh: 這項新的醫療保健提案只是為當前的醫療危機包紮了繃帶

abnormal.definition: A person or object that is not normal.
abnormal.exampleZh: 你注意到什麼異常了嗎？

bullying.definition: An act of intimidating a person to do something, especially such repeated coercion.
bullying.example: Bullying is a punishable offense in schools.
bullying.exampleZh: 在學校霸凌是一種應受懲罰的罪。

litter.definition: A platform mounted on two shafts, or a more elaborate construction, designed to be carried by two (or more) people to transport one (in luxury models sometimes more) third person(s) or (occasionally in the elaborate version) a cargo, such as a religious idol.

peaches.definition: A tree (Prunus persica), native to China and now widely cultivated throughout temperate regions, having pink flowers and edible fruit.

composed.definition: To make something by merging parts.
composed.example: The editor composed a historical journal from many individual letters.
composed.exampleZh: 編輯根據許多個人信件撰寫了一本歷史日記。

immigrants.definition: A non-native person who comes to a country from another country in order to permanently settle there.

probable.example: It's probable that it will rain tomorrow.

flank.definition: The flesh between the last rib and the hip; the side.

functioning.example: Oh, he looked pretty functioning to me.
functioning.exampleZh: 哦，他對我來說看起來很有效。

clint.definition: The relatively flat part of a limestone pavement between the grikes
clint.example: He was really excited about seeing Clint Eastwood.
clint.exampleZh: 他對見到克林伊斯威特感到非常興奮。

scrambled.definition: To move hurriedly to a location, especially by using all limbs against a surface.
scrambled.example: I scrambled some eggs with spinach and cheese.
scrambled.exampleZh: 我用菠菜和起司炒了一些雞蛋。

predictable.example: a boring film with a predictable ending
predictable.exampleZh: 一部結局可預見的無聊電影

scarlet.definition: A brilliant red colour tinged with orange.

natives.definition: An aboriginal inhabitant of a region colonized by English-speaking people; in particular:

itchy.definition: Having or creating an itch, causing a person or animal to tend to want to scratch.

voters.example: In your opinion, should we allow 16 and 17 year olds to become voters?
voters.exampleZh: 您認為我們應該允許16歲和17歲的人成為選民嗎？

madeleine.definition: A French type of small gateau or sponge cake, often shaped like an elongated scallop shell.
madeleine.example: Grease the madeleine molds and pour in the batter (but not all the way to the top; the madeleines will rise!)
madeleine.exampleZh: 在瑪德琳模具上塗上油脂，倒入麵糊（但不要一直到頂部；瑪德琳會膨脹！）

commotion.exampleZh: 盧卡斯聽到樓下有騷動。

extinct.definition: To make extinct; to extinguish or annihilate.
extinct.example: Poor Edward's cigarillo was already extinct.
extinct.exampleZh: 可憐的愛德華的小雪茄已經絕跡了。

heist.definition: A robbery or burglary, especially from an institution such as a bank or museum.
heist.example: Police revealed that the heist was the work of professionals.
heist.exampleZh: 警方透露，這起搶劫案是專業人士所為。

influenced.definition: To have an effect on by using gentle or subtle action; to exert an influence upon; to modify, bias, or sway; to persuade or induce.
influenced.example: I must admit that this book influenced my outlook on life.
influenced.exampleZh: 我必須承認這本書影響了我的人生觀。

slash.definition: A slashing action or motion, particularly:
slash.example: Slash generated during logging may constitute a fire hazard.
slash.exampleZh: 伐木過程中產生的切痕可能會引發火災。

eagles.definition: Any of several large carnivorous and carrion-eating birds in the family Accipitridae, having a powerful hooked bill and keen vision.

bottoms.example: Footers appear at the bottoms of pages.

pineapple.definition: A tropical plant, Ananas comosus, native to South America, having thirty or more long, spined and pointed leaves surrounding a thick stem.

guided.definition: To serve as a guide for someone or something; to lead or direct in a way; to conduct in a course or path.

spence.example: She kept the cheese and cured meats cool in the stone spence.
spence.exampleZh: 她把起司和醃肉放在石棚裡冷藏。

morals.definition: (of a narrative) The ethical significance or practical lesson.

colleen.example: A bright young colleen from Galway showed us around the village.
colleen.exampleZh: 來自戈爾韋的一位聰明的年輕科琳帶我們參觀了村莊。

fireman.definition: Someone (especially one who is male) who is skilled in the work of fighting fire.

thorn.definition: A sharp protective spine of a plant.

workout.definition: An exercise session; a period of physical exercise.

sleeves.definition: The part of a garment that covers the arm.

infantry.definition: Soldiers who fight on foot (on land), as opposed to cavalry and other mounted units, regardless of external transport (e.g. airborne).
infantry.example: Dan studied psychological warfare techniques at infantry academy.
infantry.exampleZh: 丹在步兵學院學習心理戰技術。

oranges.definition: An evergreen tree of the genus Citrus such as Citrus sinensis.

honourable.example: It was the honourable thing to do, even though it cost him dearly.
honourable.exampleZh: 這是一件光榮的事，儘管他付出了高昂的代價。

militia.definition: An army of trained civilians, which may be an official reserve army, called upon in time of need, the entire able-bodied population of a state which may also be called upon, or a private force not under government control.
militia.example: The government supplied weapons to local militia.
militia.exampleZh: 政府向當地民兵提供武器。

mechanics.definition: The branch of physics that deals with the action of forces on material objects with mass
mechanics.example: the mechanics of a board game

hasty.definition: Acting in haste; being too hurried or quick
hasty.example: Without much thinking about it they made a hasty decision to buy it.
hasty.exampleZh: 他們沒有多想，就匆匆決定買下它。

boulevard.definition: A broad, well-paved and landscaped thoroughfare.
boulevard.example: I was in hot pursuit of the scooter when I cycled down the boulevard.
boulevard.exampleZh: 當我騎車走下林蔭大道時，我正熱衷於追逐滑板車。

necks.definition: The part of the body connecting the head and the trunk found in humans and some animals.

gents.example: On the right as you look at it is the Gents, and on the left is the Ladies.
gents.exampleZh: 你看右邊是男士，左邊是女士。

milord.definition: An English nobleman, especially one traveling Europe in grand style; a wealthy British gentleman.
milord.example: I beg you pardon, milord. It's not Hebrew, but Latin.
milord.exampleZh: 請您原諒，大人。這不是希伯來語，而是拉丁語。

aimed.definition: To point or direct a missile, or a weapon which propels as missile, towards an object or spot with the intent of hitting it
aimed.example: He aimed at the target, but the arrow flew straight over it.
aimed.exampleZh: 他瞄準了目標，但箭卻筆直地飛過目標。

emerged.example: He emerged unscathed from the accident.

clarity.definition: The state or measure of being clear, either in appearance, thought or style; lucidity.
clarity.example: Lack of clarity on the part of the teacher will cause confusion among the students.
clarity.exampleZh: 老師的表達不清晰，會導致學生的困惑。

grumpy.example: The old man was grumpy because he couldn’t find his glasses.
grumpy.exampleZh: 老人因為找不到眼鏡而脾氣暴躁。

pesos.definition: A former unit of currency in Spain and Spain's colonies, worth 8 reales; the Spanish dollar.
pesos.example: The cost amounted to twelve pesos.

infirmary.definition: A place where sick or injured people are cared for, especially a small hospital; sickhouse.
infirmary.example: Damiano was taken to the infirmary.
infirmary.exampleZh: 達米亞諾被送往醫務室。

scratches.definition: A disruption, mark or shallow cut on a surface made by scratching.
scratches.example: Her skin was covered with tiny scratches.
scratches.exampleZh: 她的皮膚上佈滿了細小的抓痕。

sermon.definition: Religious discourse; a written or spoken address on a religious or moral matter.
sermon.example: The preacher gave a beautiful sermon.
sermon.exampleZh: 傳道人做了一次精彩的講道。

greasy.definition: Having a slippery surface; having a surface covered with grease.

anatomy.definition: The art of studying the different parts of any organized body, to discover their situation, structure, and economy.
anatomy.example: Animal anatomy is also called zootomy; vegetable anatomy, phytotomy; and human anatomy, anthropotomy.
anatomy.exampleZh: 動物解剖學又稱為動物解剖學；蔬菜解剖學、植物解剖學；以及人體解剖學、人體解剖學。

straightforward.definition: Proceeding in a straight course or manner; not deviating.

milky.definition: Resembling milk in color, consistency, smell, etc.; consisting of milk.

glamorous.example: Mary leads a glamorous life in Paris.
glamorous.exampleZh: 瑪麗在巴黎過著光鮮亮麗的生活。

referee.definition: An umpire or judge; an official who makes sure the rules are followed during a game.
referee.example: The referee kicked Jim out of the game for fighting.
referee.exampleZh: 裁判因吉姆打架而將其踢出了比賽。

cinnamon.definition: A small evergreen tree native to Sri Lanka and southern India, Cinnamomum verum or Cinnamomum zeylanicum, belonging to the family Lauraceae.
cinnamon.example: Cinnamon is an incredibly powerful spice.
cinnamon.exampleZh: 肉桂是一種非常強大的香料。

threshold.definition: The bottom-most part of a doorway that one crosses to enter; a sill.
threshold.example: The catalyst system efficiency is below the threshold.
threshold.exampleZh: 催化劑系統效率低於閾值。

tends.definition: (Old English law) To make a tender of; to offer or tender.
tends.example: It tends to snow here in winter.

sensor.definition: A device or organ that detects certain external stimuli and responds in a distinctive manner.
sensor.exampleZh: 羅德里戈無法偵測到感測器。

torpedo.definition: An electric ray of the genus Torpedo.
torpedo.example: The aircraft carrier sustained a direct hit from a torpedo.
torpedo.exampleZh: 航空母艦遭到魚雷直接命中。

housewife.definition: (plural "housewives") A woman whose main employment is homemaking, maintaining the upkeep of her home and tending to household affairs; often, such a woman whose sole [unpaid] employment is homemaking.
housewife.exampleZh: 萊拉是一位被忽視的家庭主婦。

abdomen.example: He was all bent over complaining of pains in the abdomen.
abdomen.exampleZh: 他彎著腰抱怨腹部疼痛。

replied.definition: To give a written or spoken response, especially to a question, request, accusation or criticism; to answer.
replied.example: Joanne replied to Pete's insult with a slap to his face.
replied.exampleZh: 喬安妮用一巴掌回應了皮特的侮辱。

educational.definition: A free (or low cost) trip for travel consultants, provided by a travel operator or airline as a means of promoting their service. A fam trip
educational.example: A school is an educational establishment.

latte.definition: A drink of coffee made from espresso and steamed milk, generally topped with foam.
latte.example: It’s hip to sip a latte with friends while dissing Trump.
latte.exampleZh: 和朋友一邊喝拿鐵一邊嘲笑川普是很時髦的。

politically.example: The decision was politically risky, but he believed it was right.
politically.exampleZh: 這個決定有政治風險，但他相信這是正確的。

madly.definition: In a mad manner; without reason or understanding; wildly.
madly.example: I'm madly in love with you.

lighthouse.definition: A tower or other structure exhibiting a light or lights to warn or guide sailors.
lighthouse.example: Dormitory-style accommodation is available in the lighthouse.
lighthouse.exampleZh: 燈塔內提供宿舍式住宿。

fluids.definition: Any substance which can flow with relative ease, tends to assume the shape of its container, and obeys Bernoulli's principle; a liquid, gas or plasma.

chronic.example: chronic unemployment; chronic poverty; chronic anger; chronic life
chronic.exampleZh: 長期失業；長期貧窮；長期憤怒；慢性生活

deleted.definition: To remove, get rid of or erase, especially written or printed material, or data on a computer or other device.

valet.definition: A man's personal male attendant, responsible for his clothes and appearance.
valet.example: They handed their keys to the valet.
valet.exampleZh: 他們把鑰匙交給了服務生。

southeast.definition: The direction of the cardinal compass point halfway between south and east, specifically 135°, abbreviated as SE.
southeast.example: There are twelve counterinsurgents just southeast of the village.
southeast.exampleZh: 村莊東南部有十二名反叛亂分子。

suicidal.definition: Someone suicidal, someone likely to kill themselves
suicidal.example: He is an authority on the precursors of suicidal behavior.
suicidal.exampleZh: 他是研究自殺行為前兆的權威。

resemblance.definition: The quality or state of resembling
resemblance.exampleZh: 他們的相似之處令人驚訝。

wireless.example: Only about a hundred years ago, wireless was a new technology.
wireless.exampleZh: 大約在一百年前，無線還是一項新技術。

avatar.definition: The incarnation of a deity, particularly Vishnu.
avatar.example: I think Lorenzo was watching Avatar.
avatar.exampleZh: 我認為洛倫佐正在看《阿凡達》。

tablets.definition: A slab of clay used for inscription.
tablets.example: Many people take vitamin tablets as a food supplement.
tablets.exampleZh: 許多人服用維生素片作為食物補充劑。

gibberish.definition: Speech or writing that is unintelligible, incoherent or meaningless.

sovereign.definition: A monarch; the ruler of a country.

voicemail.definition: A computerized interactive system for storing, processing and reproducing verbal messages left through a conventional telephone network.
voicemail.example: The call went straight to voicemail.
voicemail.exampleZh: 電話直接轉到語音信箱。

cadet.definition: A student at a military school who is training to be an officer.
cadet.example: a cadet branch of the family

immature.definition: An immature member of a species.
immature.example: The man was immature for throwing a tantrum.
immature.exampleZh: 這個男人發脾氣實在太不成熟了。

marguerite.example: Marguerite was born in Bordeaux in 1908.
marguerite.exampleZh: 瑪格麗特 1908 年出生於波爾多。

chemo.definition: Any chemical treatment intended to be therapeutic with respect to a disease state.
chemo.example: That last round of chemo really sapped his energy.
chemo.exampleZh: 最後一輪化療確實耗盡了他的能量。

dairy.definition: (also dairy products or dairy produce) Products produced from milk.

spectacle.definition: An exciting or extraordinary scene, exhibition, performance etc.
spectacle.example: The horse race was a thrilling spectacle.
spectacle.exampleZh: 賽馬真是一場驚心動魄的盛會。

delusional.definition: A person suffering from a delusion.

slope.definition: An area of ground that tends evenly upward or downward.
slope.example: I had to climb a small slope to get to the site.
slope.exampleZh: 我必須爬一個小斜坡才能到達現場。

allegiance.definition: Loyalty to some cause, nation or ruler.
allegiance.example: The knight swears an oath of allegiance to the King.

awareness.definition: The state or level of consciousness where sense data can be confirmed by an observer.
awareness.example: I gradually passed from sleep to full awareness.
awareness.exampleZh: 我逐漸從睡眠狀態進入完全清醒狀態。

pooja.definition: A form of worship in south Asia; a religious ceremony.
pooja.example: Pooja, the elegant lady with mellifluous voice, is to star in an upcoming Bollywood movie.
pooja.exampleZh: 普賈 (Pooja) 是一位優雅的女士，聲音甜美，將出演一部即將上映的寶萊塢電影。

decker.definition: One who, or that which, decks or adorns; a coverer.

competitors.definition: A person or organization against whom one is competing.
competitors.exampleZh: 尤里有非常不公平的競爭對手。

paste.definition: A soft moist mixture, in particular:
paste.example: Paste these labels on the jars.
paste.exampleZh: 將這些標籤貼到罐子上。

typically.definition: In a typical or common manner.

spire.definition: The stalk or stem of a plant.
spire.example: The spire of the church rose high above the town.
spire.exampleZh: 教堂的尖塔高高聳立在城鎮之上。

elephantine.definition: Of, pertaining to, or characteristic of elephants.
elephantine.example: He crossed the room with slow, elephantine steps.
elephantine.exampleZh: 他以緩慢而巨大的步伐穿過房間。

agency.definition: The capacity, condition, or state of acting or of exerting power.

condone.definition: To forgive, excuse or overlook (something that is considered morally wrong, offensive, or generally disliked).
condone.exampleZh: 門納德不能容忍這樁婚姻。

hemp.definition: A tall annual herb, Cannabis sativa, native to Asia.
hemp.example: During this period, there was also a growing market for flax, hemp and cotton.
hemp.exampleZh: 在此期間，亞麻、大麻和棉花的市場也不斷成長。

helper.example: This is Tom. He's my helper.
helper.exampleZh: 這是湯姆。他是我的幫手。

necklace.definition: An article of jewelry that is worn around the neck, most often made of a string of precious metal, pearls, gems, beads or shells, and sometimes having a pendant attached.

experienced.definition: To observe certain events; undergo a certain feeling or process; or perform certain actions that may alter one or contribute to one's knowledge, opinions, or skills.

loyalty.definition: The state of being loyal; fidelity.
loyalty.exampleZh: 他們的忠誠帶給他安慰。

oysters.definition: Any of certain marine bivalve mollusks, especially those of the family Ostreidae (the true oysters), usually found adhering to rocks or other fixed objects in shallow water along the seacoasts, or in brackish water in the mouth of rivers.

revenue.definition: The income returned by an investment.
revenue.exampleZh: 退休後他的收入翻了一番。

heritage.definition: An inheritance; property that may be inherited.
heritage.example: The university requires heritage Spanish students to enroll in a specially designed Spanish program not available to non-heritage students.
heritage.exampleZh: 該大學要求西班牙裔學生參加專門設計的西班牙語課程，該課程不適用於非西班牙裔學生。

highlight.definition: An area or a spot in a drawing, painting, or photograph that is strongly illuminated.
highlight.example: Meeting my future wife was the highlight of my trip to Spain.
highlight.exampleZh: 遇見我未來的妻子是我西班牙之旅的亮點。

hesitation.example: She carried out the order without hesitation.
hesitation.exampleZh: 她毫不猶豫地執行了命令。

hilarious.definition: Very funny; causing great merriment and laughter.

hijack.definition: An instance of hijacking; the illegal seizure of a vehicle; a hijacking.
hijack.example: Edmundo managed to hijack a spaceship.
hijack.exampleZh: 埃德蒙多成功劫持了一艘太空船。

holder.example: Put your umbrella in the umbrella holder.

hint.example: I needed a hint to complete the crossword.
hint.exampleZh: 我需要提示才能完成填字遊戲。

hollow.example: He built himself a cabin in a hollow high up in the Rockies.
hollow.exampleZh: 他在落基山脈高處的一個窪地裡為自己建造了一間小屋。

hone.definition: A sharpening stone composed of extra-fine grit used for removing the burr or curl from the blade of a razor or some other edge tool.
hone.example: Kittens play-fight to hone their hunting and fighting skills.
hone.exampleZh: 小貓透過打架來磨練狩獵和戰鬥技巧。

hook.definition: A rod bent into a curved shape, typically with one end free and the other end secured to a rope or other attachment.
hook.example: He is not handling this job, so we're giving him the hook.
hook.exampleZh: 他不負責這項工作，所以我們給他一個機會。

holocaust.definition: A sacrifice that is completely burned to ashes.
holocaust.example: 1895 September 10, "ANOTHER ARMENIAN HOLOCAUST; Five Villages Burned, Five Thousand Persons Made Homeless, and Anti-Christians Organized.", in The New York Times
holocaust.exampleZh: 1895 年 9 月 10 日，“另一場亞美尼亞大屠殺；五個村莊被燒毀，五千人無家可歸，反基督徒組織起來。”，《紐約時報》

hood.definition: A covering for the head attached to a larger garment such as a jacket or cloak.

hospitality.definition: The act or service of welcoming, receiving, hosting, or entertaining guests.
hospitality.example: Please thank our hosts for their hospitality during the week that we stayed.
hospitality.exampleZh: 請感謝我們的主人在我們入住的一周內的熱情款待。

hospice.definition: A lodging for pilgrims or the destitute, normally provided by a monastic order.
hospice.example: Cancer is the leading cause for hospice care.
hospice.exampleZh: 癌症是臨終關懷的主要原因。

housekeeper.definition: Someone who owns a house as a place of residence; a householder.
housekeeper.example: She was their third housekeeper, but after a month or so she also gave up.
housekeeper.exampleZh: 她是他們的第三個管家，但大約一個月後她也放棄了。

hourly.exampleZh: 有每小時一班的特快巴士。

humanist.definition: A scholar of one of the subjects in the humanities.
humanist.example: Kabylia is a humanist and democratic project.
humanist.exampleZh: 卡比利亞是一個人文主義和民主的計畫。

humiliating.definition: Liable to humiliate, degrade, shame or embarrass someone.

hunting.definition: The act of finding and killing a wild animal, either for sport or with the intention of using its parts to make food, clothes, etc.

hush.example: A hush came over the crowd.

hygiene.definition: The science of health, its promotion and preservation.
hygiene.example: Hygiene is an important consideration in places where food is prepared.
hygiene.exampleZh: 在準備食物的地方，衛生是一個重要的考慮因素。

hyphen.definition: The symbol "‐", typically used to join two or more words to form a compound term, or to indicate that a word has been split at the end of a line.
hyphen.example: How is it written: as one word, separately or with a hyphen?
hyphen.exampleZh: 它是怎麼寫的：作為一個單詞，單獨還是用連字符？

hyperbole.definition: Deliberate or unintentional overstatement, particularly extreme overstatement.
hyperbole.example: Tom Jackson makes frequent use of hyperbole in his writing.
hyperbole.exampleZh: 湯姆傑克森在他的作品中經常使用誇張的手法。

icon.definition: An image, symbol, picture, or other representation usually as an object of religious devotion.
icon.example: That man is an icon in the business; he personifies loyalty and good business sense.
icon.exampleZh: 這個人是這個行業的偶像；他體現了忠誠和良好的商業頭腦。

ideally.example: Ideally, we would have one textbook per student, but unfortunately some of you will have to share.
ideally.exampleZh: 理想情況下，我們每個學生都會有一本教科書，但不幸的是，你們中的一些人將不得不分享。

iceman.definition: A person who trades in ice.
iceman.example: European scientists say a recently completed DNA map of the Iceman—the well-preserved, 5,300-year-old mummy discovered in a melting Alpine glacier in 1991—is yielding new details about the man's physical look, his ethnic origins and his health—including an apparent predisposition for heart disease.
iceman.exampleZh: 歐洲科學家表示，最近完成的冰人 DNA 圖譜（1991 年在融化的阿爾卑斯冰川中發現的保存完好、有 5300 年歷史的木乃伊）正在提供有關冰人外貌、種族起源和健康狀況的新細節，包括明顯的心臟病傾向。

identification.definition: The act of identifying, or proving to be the same.
identification.example: Much education and experience is required for proper identification of bird species
identification.exampleZh: 正確識別鳥類需要大量的教育和經驗

icy.definition: Pertaining to, resembling, or abounding in ice; cold; frosty.

identify.definition: To establish the identity of someone or something.
identify.example: The Associated Press will not identify the suspect of the crime because he is a juvenile.
identify.exampleZh: 美聯社不會識別犯罪嫌疑人，因為他是未成年人。

idiot.definition: A person of low general intelligence.

identical.definition: Bearing full likeness by having precisely the same set of characteristics; indistinguishable.

ideology.definition: Doctrine, philosophy, body of beliefs or principles belonging to an individual or group.
ideology.example: A dictatorship, in order to secure its reign, bans things that do not conform to its ideology.
ideology.exampleZh: 獨裁政權為了鞏固自己的統治，會禁止一切不符合其意識形態的事物。

ignorant.definition: Unknowledgeable or uneducated; characterized by ignorance.
ignorant.example: His manner was at best off-hand, at worst totally ignorant.
ignorant.exampleZh: 他的態度往好裡說是漫不經心，往壞了說就是完全無知。

illustrate.example: We illustrate our definitions by including quotations or simple examples.
illustrate.exampleZh: 我們透過引用或簡單的例子來說明我們的定義。

imagery.definition: The work of one who makes images or visible representation of objects.
imagery.example: The poem relies heavily on imagery associated with night.
imagery.exampleZh: 這首詩很大程度上依賴與夜晚相關的意象。

immortal.definition: Not susceptible to death; living forever; never dying.

immigrant.definition: A non-native person who comes to a country from another country in order to permanently settle there.

immoral.definition: Not moral; inconsistent with rectitude, purity, or good morals; contrary to conscience or the divine law.

impartial.definition: Treating all parties, rivals, or disputants equally; not partial; not biased

impermanent.example: As you won't live forever, of course, your housing accommodations are impermanent.
impermanent.exampleZh: 當然，由於你不會永遠活著，所以你的住宿條件是無常的。

impolite.definition: Not polite; not of polished manners; wanting in good manners.

import.definition: To bring (something) in from a foreign country, especially for sale or trade.
import.example: How can I import files from older versions of this application?
import.exampleZh: 如何從此應用程式的舊版本匯入檔案？

impossibility.example: Meeting the deadline is an impossibility; there is no way we can be ready in time.
impossibility.exampleZh: 在截止日期前完成是不可能的；我們無法及時做好準備。

impressed.example: You impressed me with your command of Urdu.
impressed.exampleZh: 你對烏爾都語的掌握給我留下了深刻的印象。

impractical.example: We all consider that your idea is impractical.
impractical.exampleZh: 我們都認為你的想法不切實際。

impressionist.definition: One who adheres to the theory or method of impressionism.
impressionist.example: This chord progression is typical of Impressionist music.
impressionist.exampleZh: 這種和弦進行是典型的印象派音樂。

improbable.example: It's highly improbable that aliens abducted you.
improbable.exampleZh: 外星人綁架你的可能性極低。

imprison.definition: To put in or as if in prison; confine.

impulse.definition: A thrust; a push; a sudden force that impels.
impulse.example: The impulse to learn drove me to study night and day.
impulse.exampleZh: 學習的衝動驅使我日以繼夜地學習。

improperly.definition: In an improper manner; not properly
improperly.example: Are you suggesting I behaved improperly?
improperly.exampleZh: 你是在暗示我行為不當嗎？

inadequate.definition: Not adequate; not fit for the purpose

impulsive.definition: Having the power of driving or impelling; giving an impulse; moving; impellent.

inaccurate.example: The measurement is far too inaccurate.

incense.definition: A perfume used in the rites of various religions.
incense.example: I think it would incense him to learn the truth.
incense.exampleZh: 我認為了解真相會激怒他。

inanimate.definition: Lacking the quality or ability of motion; as an inanimate object.
inanimate.example: The spirits can be good or evil and can inhabit inanimate objects.
inanimate.exampleZh: 靈魂可以是善的，也可以是惡的，並且可以居住在無生命的物體上。

incentive.definition: Something that motivates, rouses, or encourages.
incentive.example: I have no incentive to do housework right now.
incentive.exampleZh: 我現在沒有動力做家事。

inborn.definition: Innate, possessed by an organism at birth.
inborn.example: One's success in life has little to do with one's inborn gift.
inborn.exampleZh: 一個人人生的成功與他與生俱來的天賦沒有太大關係。

incur.definition: To bring upon oneself or expose oneself to, especially something inconvenient, harmful, or onerous; to become liable or subject to

incurable.definition: Of an illness, condition, etc, that is unable to be cured; healless.
incurable.exampleZh: 這種疾病被認為是無法治癒的。

indent.definition: To notch; to jag; to cut into points like a row of teeth
indent.example: to indent the edge of paper

indescribable.definition: Impossible, or very difficult to describe.
indescribable.example: He proved it with indescribable mathematics.
indescribable.exampleZh: 他用難以形容的數學證明了這一點。

index.definition: An alphabetical listing of items and their location.
index.example: The index of a book lists words or expressions and the pages of the book upon which they are to be found.
index.exampleZh: 一本書的索引列出了單字或表達方式以及可以在其中找到它們的書頁。

indebted.example: I am forever indebted to them.

indifferent.definition: Not caring or concerned; uninterested, apathetic.
indifferent.example: He was indifferent to the proposal, since it didn’t affect him, either way.
indifferent.exampleZh: 他對這個提議漠不關心，因為無論如何，這對他都沒有影響。

indulgent.definition: Disposed or prone to indulge, humor, gratify, or yield to one's own or another's desires, etc., or to be compliant, lenient, or forbearing;

individual.definition: A person considered alone, rather than as belonging to a group of people.

ineffective.definition: Not having the desired effect; ineffectual
ineffective.example: The government became ineffective at handling security matters.
ineffective.exampleZh: 政府在處理安全事務方面變得無效。

inexperienced.definition: Not experienced; lacking knowledge or experience; green.

infected.example: The infected bodies were hastily piled up and burned.
infected.exampleZh: 被感染的屍體被匆忙堆積起來並焚燒。

infant.definition: A very young human being, from birth to somewhere between six months and two years of age, needing almost constant care and/or attention.
infant.exampleZh: 艾德蒙多聽到嬰兒的哭聲。

infectious.definition: (of an illness) Transmitted from one person to another, usually through the air breathed.

inflammation.definition: The act of inflaming, kindling, or setting on fire.
inflammation.example: an inflammation of the mind, of the body politic, or of parties
inflammation.exampleZh: 思想、政治體或政黨的炎症

infer.definition: To introduce (something) as a reasoned conclusion; to conclude by reasoning or deduction, as from premises or evidence.
infer.example: What do you infer from her strange behavior?
infer.exampleZh: 你從她奇怪的行為推論出什麼？

inflammatory.definition: Tending to inflame or provoke somebody.
inflammatory.example: Sam posted an inflammatory comment to the newsgroup.
inflammatory.exampleZh: 薩姆在新聞組上發表了煽動性評論。

inflation.definition: An act, instance of, or state of expansion or increase in size, especially by injection of a gas.
inflation.example: The inflation of the balloon took five hours.
inflation.exampleZh: 氣球充氣花了五個小時。

inference.definition: The act or process of inferring by deduction or induction.
inference.example: Is this an observation or an inference?

inflate.definition: To enlarge an object by pushing air (or a gas) into it; to raise or expand abnormally
inflate.example: You inflate a balloon by blowing air into it.
inflate.exampleZh: 您可以透過向氣球中吹空氣來為氣球充氣。

influential.example: Jane was very influential in getting the motion passed.
influential.exampleZh: 簡對於這項動議的通過非常有影響力。

influenza.definition: An acute contagious disease of the upper airways and lungs, caused by a virus, which rapidly spreads around the world in seasonal epidemics.
influenza.example: Influenza makes my whole body ache and leaves me exhausted.
influenza.exampleZh: 流感讓我全身酸痛，疲憊不堪。

infringe.definition: Break or violate a treaty, a law, a right etc.
infringe.example: They infringe on our basic freedoms.
infringe.exampleZh: 他們侵犯了我們的基本自由。

inherit.definition: To take possession of as a right (especially in Biblical translations).
inherit.example: Your descendants will inherit the earth.
inherit.exampleZh: 你的子孫將繼承這片土地。

initial.definition: Chronologically first, early; of or pertaining to the beginning, cause or origin.
initial.example: Our initial admiration for their efficiency gave way to disgust about their methods.
initial.exampleZh: 我們最初對他們的效率的欽佩後來變成了對他們方法的厭惡。

injection.definition: The act of injecting, or something that is injected.
injection.example: The troubled business received a much-needed cash injection.
injection.exampleZh: 這家陷入困境的企業獲得了急需的現金注入。

infringement.definition: A violation or breach, as of a law.
infringement.example: Copyright infringement is against the law.

innate.definition: Inborn; existing or having existed since birth.

injured.definition: To wound or cause physical harm to a living creature.

inside.definition: Of or pertaining to the inner surface, limit or boundary.
inside.example: The inside surface of the cup is unpainted.

inspect.definition: To examine critically or carefully; especially, to search out problems or determine condition; to scrutinize.

inseparable.definition: Unable to be separated; bound together permanently.

inspector.definition: A person employed to inspect something.

instantly.example: When the neighbours' dog barked, ours instantly replied with a howl.
instantly.exampleZh: 當鄰居的狗吠叫時，我們的狗立即以嚎叫回應。

instinct.definition: A natural or inherent impulse or behaviour.
instinct.example: Many animals fear fire by instinct.
instinct.exampleZh: 許多動物本能地害怕火。

institution.definition: A custom or practice of a society or community.
institution.example: He's been in an institution since the crash.
institution.exampleZh: 自從車禍發生後，他一直在機構裡。

insult.definition: Action or form of speech deliberately intended to be rude; a particular act or statement having this effect.
insult.example: The way the orchestra performed tonight was an insult to my ears.
insult.exampleZh: 樂團今晚的表演方式對我的耳朵來說是一種侮辱。

insufficient.example: Insufficient time is available to reflect on the problem
insufficient.exampleZh: 沒有足夠的時間反思問題

integrate.definition: To form into one whole; to make entire; to complete; to renew; to restore; to perfect.
integrate.example: They were keen to integrate their new skills into the performance.
integrate.exampleZh: 他們熱衷於將新技能融入表演中。

intent.definition: Firmly fixed or concentrated on something.
intent.exampleZh: 致力於自我提升的思想

interactive.definition: Interacting with or communicating with and reacting to each other; influencing or having an effect on each other; acting or capable of acting on each other or with the other.

intellectual.definition: Belonging to, or performed by, the intellect; mental or cognitive.

interfere.definition: To get involved or involve oneself, causing disturbance.
interfere.example: I always try not to interfere with other people’s personal affairs.
interfere.exampleZh: 我總是盡量不干涉別人的私事。

interior.definition: The inside of a building, container, cavern, or other enclosed structure.
interior.example: Sir Richard Burton explored far into the African interior.
interior.exampleZh: 理查德·伯頓爵士深入非洲內陸進行了探索。

interpersonal.example: Systemic violence kills just as surely as interpersonal violence does.
interpersonal.exampleZh: 系統性暴力和人際暴力一樣肯定會造成死亡。

interpreter.definition: One who conveys what a user of one language is saying or signing, in real time or shortly after that person has finished communicating, to a user of a different language. (Contrasted with a translator.)
interpreter.example: A Japanese man who is tried before a German court is assisted by an interpreter in making oral statements.
interpreter.exampleZh: 一名日本男子在德國法院受審，在翻譯的協助下進行口頭陳述。

interpret.definition: To explain or tell the meaning of; to translate orally into intelligible or familiar language or terms. applied especially to language, but also to dreams, signs, conduct, mysteries, etc.

interpretation.definition: An act of interpreting or explaining what is obscure; a translation; a version; a construction.
interpretation.example: the interpretation of a dream, or of an enigma.

intransitive.definition: (grammar, of a verb) not transitive: not having, or not taking, a direct object
intransitive.example: The word "drink" is a transitive verb in "they drink wine", but an intransitive one in "they drink often."
intransitive.exampleZh: 「drink」一詞在「他們喝葡萄酒」中是及物動詞，但在「他們經常喝酒」中是不及物動詞。

intimate.example: He and his sister deeply valued their intimate relationship as they didn't have much else to live for.
intimate.exampleZh: 他和妹妹非常珍惜他們的親密關係，因為他們沒有太多其他東西可以活下去。

intersection.definition: The junction of two (or more) paths, streets, highways, or other thoroughfares.
intersection.exampleZh: 那個十字路口發生了什麼事？

intravenous.example: Get his morphine intravenous started stat!
intravenous.exampleZh: 開始給他靜脈注射嗎啡！

intrude.definition: To thrust oneself in; to come or enter without invitation, permission, or welcome; to encroach; to trespass.
intrude.example: to intrude on families at unseasonable hours; to intrude on the lands of another
intrude.exampleZh: 在不合時宜的時間打擾家人；侵入他人的土地

intrusion.definition: The forcible inclusion or entry of an external group or individual; the act of intruding.
intrusion.example: He viewed sales calls as an unwelcome intrusion.
intrusion.exampleZh: 他認為銷售電話是一種不受歡迎的侵入。

invariable.definition: Not variable; unalterable; uniform; always having the same value.
invariable.example: The French adjective marron (brown) is invariable: it does not take the usual s in the plural.
invariable.exampleZh: 法文形容詞 marron（棕色）是不變的：它不採用通常的複數 s。

inventor.definition: One who invents, either as a hobby or as an occupation.
inventor.example: Thomas Edison was a prolific inventor.
inventor.exampleZh: 湯瑪斯·愛迪生是一位多產的發明家。

invariably.example: He is invariably late for meetings.

investment.definition: The act of investing, or state of being invested.
investment.example: Giving your children a good education is a wise long-term investment.
investment.exampleZh: 為孩子提供良好的教育是一項明智的長期投資。

invisible.definition: Unable to be seen; out of sight; not visible.
invisible.example: I went invisible so that my ex-girlfriend wouldn't send me instant messages.
invisible.exampleZh: 我隱身了，這樣我的前女友就不會傳即時訊息給我。

investor.definition: A person who invests money in order to make a profit.

ironically.definition: (manner) In an ironic manner; in a way displaying irony.
ironically.example: Ironically, the automated checkout system ended up costing more in repairs than the workers it was meant to replace.
ironically.exampleZh: 諷刺的是，自動結帳系統最終的維修成本比它要取代的工人還要多。

irresistible.exampleZh: 雅尼 (Yanni) 發現斯庫拉 (Skura) 難以抗拒。

irresponsible.definition: Not responsible; exempt from legal responsibility, not to be held accountable.
irresponsible.exampleZh: 反社會者是不負責任的。

irritation.definition: The act of irritating or annoying
irritation.example: What irritation causes you to be so moody?
irritation.exampleZh: 是什麼刺激讓你如此情緒化？

jaw.definition: One of the bones, usually bearing teeth, which form the framework of the mouth.
jaw.exampleZh: 他驚訝得下巴都掉下來了。

joint.definition: The point where two components of a structure join, but are still able to rotate.
joint.example: This rod is free to swing at the joint with the platform.
joint.exampleZh: 該桿在與平台的連接處可以自由擺動。

itch.definition: A sensation felt on an area of the skin that causes a person or animal to want to scratch.

journalism.definition: The activity or profession of being a journalist.

jump.definition: The act of jumping; a leap; a spring; a bound.
jump.example: The boy took a skip and a jump down the lane.
jump.exampleZh: 男孩沿著小巷一蹦一跳。

judo.definition: A Japanese martial art and sport adapted from jujutsu.

justify.definition: To provide an acceptable explanation for.
justify.example: How can you justify spending so much money on clothes?
justify.exampleZh: 你怎麼能證明花這麼多錢買衣服是合理的呢？

jury.definition: A group of individuals chosen from the general population to hear and decide a case in a court of law.
jury.exampleZh: 陪審團遴選將於週一開始。

kangaroo.definition: A member of the Macropodidae family of large marsupials with strong hind legs for hopping, native to Australia.

keen.definition: Often with a prepositional phrase, or with to and an infinitive: showing a quick and ardent responsiveness or willingness; eager, enthusiastic, interested.

karaoke.definition: A form of entertainment popular in clubs, at parties, etc, in which individual members of the public sing along to pre-recorded instrumental versions of popular songs, the lyrics of which are displayed for the singer on a screen in time with the music.
karaoke.exampleZh: 雅尼和里瑪一起唱卡拉OK。

kerosene.definition: A petroleum-based thin and colorless fuel
kerosene.example: The kerosene lasted all winter, so the furnace kept us always warm.
kerosene.exampleZh: 煤油可以持續整個冬天，所以爐子總是讓我們保持溫暖。

knowingly.definition: In the manner of one who knows.
knowingly.example: She smiled knowingly, but kept the secret.
knowingly.exampleZh: 她會意地笑了笑，但還是保守了秘密。

known.definition: Identified as a specific type; famous, renowned.

lame.definition: Unable to walk properly because of a problem with one's feet or legs.
lame.example: a lame leg, arm or muscle

label.definition: A small ticket or sign giving information about something to which it is attached or intended to be attached.
label.example: Although the label priced this poster at three pounds, I got it for two.
label.exampleZh: 雖然這張海報的標籤標價為三英鎊，但我卻花了兩英鎊買了它。

landslide.definition: A natural disaster that involves the breakup and downhill flow of rock, mud, water and anything caught in the path.
landslide.example: The candidate won by a landslide.
landslide.exampleZh: 候選人以壓倒性優勢獲勝。

landlady.exampleZh: 雅尼勒死了他的前房東。

lastly.definition: (sequence) Used to mark the beginning of the last in a list of items or propositions.
lastly.example: Firstly, that's illegal; secondly, it has serious disadvantages; thirdly, it's not necessary; and lastly, did I mention that it's illegal?
lastly.exampleZh: 首先，這是非法的；其次，它有嚴重的缺點；第三，它沒有必要；最後，我是否提到它是非法的？

laptop.exampleZh: 喬納斯合上筆記型電腦。

laser.definition: A device that produces a monochromatic, coherent beam of light.

last-minute.definition: Just before it is too late; in the nick of time.
last-minute.example: He's a last-minute replacement. After today, they'd have had to play with the original roster.
last-minute.exampleZh: 他是最後一刻的替補。今天之後，他們將不得不使用原來的陣容進行比賽。

latter.definition: Relating to or being the second of two items.
latter.example: The latter half of the film was interesting.

laundry.example: You've left your dirty laundry all over the house.
laundry.exampleZh: 你把髒衣服丟得滿屋子都是。

lava.definition: The molten rock ejected by a volcano from its crater or fissured sides.
lava.example: The volcano vomited lava and flames.

lawsuit.definition: In civil law, a case where two or more people disagree and one or more of the parties take the case to a court for resolution.
lawsuit.example: The lawyer advised his client against filing a lawsuit as it would take a lot of time and money to resolve.
lawsuit.exampleZh: 律師建議他的當事人不要提起訴訟，因為這需要花費大量的時間和金錢來解決。

lawmaker.definition: One who makes or enacts laws.
lawmaker.example: Ricks, 54, is now a lawmaker in the Colorado General Assembly, elected in November 2020.
lawmaker.exampleZh: 里克斯現年 54 歲，現任科羅拉多州議員，於 2020 年 11 月當選。

leak.definition: A crack, crevice, fissure, or hole which admits water or other fluid, or lets it escape.

layabout.example: My father was a good-for-nothing layabout who sat about on his posterior all day and ate potato chips.
layabout.exampleZh: 我的父親是個沒出息的遊手好閒者，整天坐著吃薯片。

leap.definition: The act of leaping or jumping.
leap.example: It's quite a leap to claim that those cloud formations are evidence of UFOs.
leap.exampleZh: 聲稱這些雲層是不明飛行物的證據是相當誇張的。

learning.definition: An act in which something is learned.
learning.example: The department head was also a scholar of great learning.
learning.exampleZh: 系主任也是一位博學之士。

least.definition: Something of the smallest possible extent; an indivisible unit.
least.example: It was the least surprising thing.
least.exampleZh: 這是最不令人驚訝的事。

leash.definition: A strap, cord or rope with which to restrain an animal, often a dog.

legend.definition: An unrealistic story depicting past events.
legend.example: According to the legend on the map, that building is a school.
legend.exampleZh: 根據地圖上的傳說，那棟建築是一所學校。

lecturer.definition: A person who gives lectures, especially as a profession.

leek.definition: The vegetable Allium ampeloprasum var. porrum, having edible leaves and an onion-like bulb but with a milder flavour than the onion.
leek.example: The leek is a versatile vegetable that adds flavor to many dishes.
leek.exampleZh: 韭菜是一種用途廣泛的蔬菜，可為許多菜餚增添風味。

legendary.definition: Of or pertaining to a legend or to legends.
legendary.example: The animal kingdom will be legendary!

legislative.definition: Making, or having the power to make, a law or laws; lawmaking
legislative.example: Although enormously influential in shaping the laws of the land, The House of Lords are not actually a legislative body.
legislative.exampleZh: 儘管上議院在製定國家法律方面具有巨大影響力，但實際上並不是立法機構。

legislation.definition: The act of legislating; preparation and enactment of laws; the laws enacted.
legislation.exampleZh: 右派往往不喜歡監管立法。

legitimate.definition: In accordance with the law or established legal forms and requirements.
legitimate.example: legitimate reasoning; a legitimate standard or method
legitimate.exampleZh: 合理的推理；合法的標準或方法

legislature.definition: A governmental body with the power to make, amend and repeal laws.
legislature.example: The bill was eviscerated before being passed by the legislature.
legislature.exampleZh: 該法案在立法機關通過之前就被刪除了。

leisurely.definition: Characterized by leisure; taking plenty of time; unhurried
leisurely.example: As we had a free morning, we went on a leisurely walk around the city.
leisurely.exampleZh: 由於早上空閒，我們就在城市裡悠閒地散步。

lengthy.definition: Having length; long and overextended, especially in time rather than dimension.
lengthy.example: She died last night after a lengthy illness.
lengthy.exampleZh: 她在長期患病後於昨晚去世。

legislator.definition: Someone who creates or enacts laws
legislator.example: The Prime Minister postponed the legislator's punishment.
legislator.exampleZh: 首相推遲了對議員的懲罰。

lest.definition: For fear that; that not; in order to prevent something from happening; in case.
lest.example: He won’t go outside, lest he be eaten by those ravenous eagles.
lest.exampleZh: 他不敢出去，免得被那些貪婪的老鷹吃掉。

level.definition: To adjust so as to make as flat or perpendicular to the ground as possible.
level.example: You can level the table by turning the pads that screw into the feet.
level.exampleZh: 您可以透過轉動旋入腳的墊子來平整桌子。

liberal.definition: (now rare outside set phrases) Pertaining to those arts and sciences the study of which is considered to provide general knowledge, as opposed to vocational/occupational, technical or mechanical training.
liberal.example: 1983, David Leslie Wagner, The Seven liberal arts in the Middle Ages
liberal.exampleZh: 1983，大衛‧萊斯利‧華格納，《中世紀的七門文科》

leopard.definition: Panthera pardus, a large wild cat with a spotted coat native to Africa and Asia, especially the male of the species (in contrast to leopardess).

lifetime.definition: The duration of the life of someone or something.
lifetime.example: I've been waiting a lifetime for a train.

lid.definition: A thin skin membrane that covers and moves over an eye.

lighten.definition: To make brighter or clearer; to illuminate.
lighten.example: to lighten an apartment with lamps or gas; to lighten the streets
lighten.exampleZh: 用燈或瓦斯照亮公寓；照亮街道

likewise.example: Public transportation is virtually inaccessible in this country; likewise, its hospitals are also not very user-friendly.
likewise.exampleZh: 這個國家的公共交通幾乎無法到達；同樣，它的醫院也不太人性化。

lifeless.example: Boris lied lifeless before Melissa's eyes.
lifeless.exampleZh: 鮑里斯在梅莉莎眼前毫無生氣地躺著。

line.definition: To place (objects) into a line (usually used with "up"); to form into a line; to align.

liner.definition: Someone who fits a lining to something.
liner.example: 1973, A good liner has a pretty shrewd idea of the value of the painting he is treating and usually charges accordingly. — Kyril Bonfiglioli, Don't Point That Thing at Me (Penguin 2001, p. 41)
liner.exampleZh: 1973年，一位優秀的畫師對他所處理的畫作的價值有相當精明的了解，並且通常會相應地收取費用。 — Kyril Bonfiglioli，《別用那個東西指著我》（Penguin 2001，第 41 頁）

linen.definition: Thread or cloth made from flax fiber.
linen.example: She put the freshly cleaned linens into the linen closet.
linen.exampleZh: 她把剛洗乾淨的床單放進了衣櫃裡。

linger.definition: To stay or remain in a place or situation, especially as if unwilling to depart or not easily able to do so; to loiter.

list.definition: To create or recite a list.

ling.definition: Any of various marine food fish, of the genus Molva, resembling the cod.

literal.definition: Exactly as stated; read or understood without additional interpretation; according to the letter or verbal expression; real; not figurative or metaphorical.
literal.example: The literal translation is “hands full of bananas” but it means empty-handed.
literal.exampleZh: 直譯是“滿手香蕉”，但它的意思是空手而歸。

literally.definition: Word for word; not figuratively; not as an idiom or metaphor
literally.example: When I saw on the news that there would be no school tomorrow because of the snowstorm, I literally jumped for joy, and hit my head on the ceiling fan.
literally.exampleZh: 當我在新聞上看到因為暴風雪明天不能上學時，我簡直高興得跳了起來，頭撞到了吊扇上。

living.example: Respect for the dead does not preclude respect for the living.
living.exampleZh: 對死者的尊重並不妨礙對生者的尊重。

load.definition: To put a load on or in (a means of conveyance or a place of storage).
load.example: The dock workers refused to load the ship.

loan.definition: An act or instance of lending, an act or instance of granting something for temporary use.
loan.example: Because of the loan that John made to me, I was able to pay my tuition for the upcoming semester.
loan.exampleZh: 由於約翰向我提供的貸款，我能夠支付下學期的學費。

lobby.definition: An entryway or reception area; vestibule; passageway; corridor.
lobby.example: I had to wait in the lobby for hours before seeing the doctor.
lobby.exampleZh: 我不得不在大廳等了幾個小時才去看醫生。

long-distance.definition: Over a great length or distance.

loneliness.definition: A feeling of depression resulting from being alone or from having no companions.

loosely.example: Insert all the bolts loosely, then tighten them.
loosely.exampleZh: 鬆動地插入所有螺栓，然後擰緊。

lousy.definition: Remarkably bad; of poor quality, dirty, or underhanded; mean, contemptible.
lousy.example: No offense, but his cooking is lousy.
lousy.exampleZh: 無意冒犯，但他的廚藝很糟糕。

long-term.definition: Becoming evident after a relatively long time period.
long-term.exampleZh: 身體虐待會產生長期影響。

lower.definition: To let descend by its own weight, as something suspended; to let down
lower.example: lower a bucket into a well

lucrative.example: Ancient Berbers developed a lucrative olive oil industry.
lucrative.exampleZh: 古代柏柏爾人發展了利潤豐厚的橄欖油產業。

lowland.definition: Area which is lower than surrounding areas.
lowland.example: The lowland region was prone to flooding during the rainy season.
lowland.exampleZh: 低地地區在雨季容易發生洪水。

lush.example: That meal was lush! We have to go that restaurant again sometime!
lush.exampleZh: 那頓飯吃得很豐盛！有時間我們一定要再去那家餐廳！

luxurious.definition: Very fine in quality and comfortable.

luxury.exampleZh: 喬納斯過著奢侈的生活。

madam.definition: A polite form of address for a woman or lady.
madam.example: Later, Mrs Grey was sitting in her favourite tea shop. “Would madam like the usual cream cakes and patisserie with her tea?” the waitress asked.
madam.exampleZh: 後來，格雷夫人坐在她最喜歡的茶店。 「女士想要普通的奶油蛋糕和糕點配茶嗎？」女服務生問。

madness.definition: The state of being mad; insanity; mental disease.

maestro.definition: A master in some art, especially a composer or conductor.
maestro.example: David will have the opportunity to learn from a great maestro.
maestro.exampleZh: 大衛將有機會向偉大的大師學習。

major.definition: A military rank between captain and lieutenant colonel.
major.example: He used to be a major in the army.
major.exampleZh: 他曾經是一名陸軍少校。

make.example: What make of car do you drive?

malady.definition: Any ailment or disease of the body; especially, a lingering or deep-seated disorder.
malady.example: The malady of our time is perfectionism.
malady.exampleZh: 我們這個時代的通病是完美主義。

mango.definition: A tropical Asian fruit tree, Mangifera indica.
