chisel.exampleZh: 湯姆拿著一把錘子和一把鑿子。

gnaw.definition: To bite something persistently, especially something tough.
gnaw.example: Concern had started to gnaw at his insides.

niche.definition: A cavity, hollow, or recess, generally within the thickness of a wall, for a statue, bust, or other erect ornament. Hence, any similar position, literal or figurative.
niche.example: There's a niche market for Furbies.
niche.exampleZh: 菲比娃娃有一個利基市場。

somersault.definition: Starting on one's feet, an instance of rotating one's body 360 degree while airborne or on the ground, with one's feet going over one's head.
somersault.example: Tom did a somersault on the trampoline.
somersault.exampleZh: 湯姆在彈翻床上翻了跟頭。

exhilarating.exampleZh: 這是一次令人興奮的經驗。

unsettling.definition: That makes one troubled or uneasy; disquieting or distressing.
unsettling.exampleZh: 有件事讓湯姆感到不安。

plaque.definition: Any flat, thin piece of clay, ivory, metal, etc., used for ornament, or for painting pictures upon, as a dish, plate, slab, etc., hung upon a wall; also, a smaller decoration worn by a person, such as a brooch.
plaque.example: At the inauguration ceremony a plaque was unveiled in honor of the founder.
plaque.exampleZh: 在就職典禮上，為紀念創始人而設立的牌匾揭幕。

salivate.example: The patient began to salivate profusely after taking the medication.
salivate.exampleZh: 患者在服用藥物後開始大量流涎。

fixture.definition: Something that is fixed in place, especially a permanent appliance or other item of personal property that is considered part of a house and is sold with it; compare fitting, furnishing.
fixture.example: What was originally a temporary measure became a permanent fixture.
fixture.exampleZh: 最初的臨時措施變成了永久性措施。

wares.definition: (usually in combination) Goods or a type of goods offered for sale or use.
wares.example: The old man wanders from town to town peddling his wares.
wares.exampleZh: 這位老人從一個城鎮遊蕩到另一個城鎮，兜售他的商品。

region.definition: Any considerable and connected part of a space or surface; specifically, a tract of land or sea of considerable but indefinite extent; a country; a district; in a broad sense, a place without special reference to location or extent but viewed as an entity for geographical, social or cultural reasons.

horror.definition: An intense distressing emotion of fear or repugnance.

wrapped.definition: To enclose (an object) completely in any flexible, thin material such as fabric or paper.

towel.definition: A cloth used for wiping, especially one used for drying anything wet, as a person after a bath.

packing.definition: (physical) To put or bring things together in a limited or confined space, especially for storage or transport.

admitted.definition: To allow to enter; to grant entrance (to), whether into a place, into the mind, or into consideration

sheet.definition: A thin bed cloth used as a covering for a mattress or as a layer over the sleeper.

avenue.definition: A broad street, especially one bordered by trees.
avenue.exampleZh: 他們位於百老匯大道 229 號。

grows.definition: To become larger, to increase in magnitude.

ranch.definition: A large plot of land used for raising cattle, sheep or other livestock.

safer.definition: Not in danger; out of harm's reach.

leather.definition: A tough material produced from the skin of animals, by tanning or similar process, used e.g. for clothing.
leather.exampleZh: 湯姆帶著一個皮革公事包。

ashes.definition: The solid remains of a fire.

boats.definition: A craft used for transportation of goods, fishing, racing, recreational cruising, or military use on or in the water, propelled by oars or outboard motor or inboard motor or by wind.

happier.definition: Having a feeling arising from a consciousness of well-being or of enjoyment; enjoying good of any kind, such as comfort, peace, or tranquillity; blissful, contented, joyous.

larger.definition: Of considerable or relatively great size or extent.
larger.exampleZh: 亞馬遜比哥倫比亞還大。

recognise.definition: To match (something or someone which one currently perceives) to a memory of some previous encounter with the same person or thing.

splendid.definition: Possessing or displaying splendor; shining; very bright.

vulnerable.definition: More or most likely to be exposed to the chance of being attacked or harmed, either physically or emotionally.
vulnerable.example: It's okay to get vulnerable every now and again.
vulnerable.exampleZh: 時不時變得脆弱是沒關係的。

vital.definition: Relating to, or characteristic of life.
vital.example: vital energies; vital functions; vital actions
vital.exampleZh: 生命能量；重要功能；重要行動

franklin.definition: A freeholder, especially as belonging to a class of landowners in the 14th and 15th century ranking below the gentry.
franklin.example: In Paris, Benjamin Franklin was considered a backwoods Voltaire.
franklin.exampleZh: 在巴黎，班傑明·富蘭克林被認為是偏遠地區的伏爾泰。

drawer.definition: An open-topped box that can be slid in and out of the cabinet that contains it, used for storing clothing or other articles.

bureau.definition: An administrative unit of government; office.
bureau.example: a news bureau; a travel bureau; a service bureau; an employment bureau; the Citizens Advice Bureau
bureau.exampleZh: 新聞局；旅遊局；服務局；就業局；公民諮詢局

harper.definition: A harpist, especially one who plays a traditional harp without pedals.
harper.exampleZh: 哈珀在舞台上翩翩起舞。

retreat.definition: The act of pulling back or withdrawing, as from something dangerous, or unpleasant.

benny.example: Benny Hill was a huge Francophile and spoke French fluently.
benny.exampleZh: 班尼希爾是一位狂熱的法國人，法語說得一口流利。

pillow.definition: A soft cushion used to support the head in bed.

parked.definition: To bring (something such as a vehicle) to a halt or store in a specified place.
parked.example: I parked the drive heads of my hard disk before travelling with my laptop.
parked.exampleZh: 在帶著筆記型電腦旅行之前，我把硬盤的磁盤頭停了下來。

announce.definition: To give public notice, especially for the first time; to make known

galaxy.definition: The Milky Way; the apparent band of concentrated stars which appears in the night sky over earth.
galaxy.example: Could life exist in another galaxy?
galaxy.exampleZh: 另一個星系中可能存在生命嗎？

voted.definition: To cast a vote; to assert a formalized choice in an election

muffled.definition: To wrap (a person, face etc.) in fabric or another covering, for warmth or protection; often with up.
muffled.example: Claudio woke to some weird muffled thuds.
muffled.exampleZh: 克勞迪奧被一些奇怪的低沉的撞擊聲吵醒。

jewelry.definition: Collectively, personal ornamentation such as rings, necklaces, brooches and bracelets, made of precious metals and sometimes set with gemstones.

kisses.definition: To touch with the lips or press the lips against, usually to show love or affection or passion, or as part of a greeting.

luggage.definition: The bags and other containers that hold a traveller's belongings.

groom.definition: A man who is about to marry.

denied.example: I wanted to go to the party, but I was denied.
denied.exampleZh: 我想去參加聚會，但被拒絕了。

alike.definition: Having resemblance or similitude; similar; without difference.

lighter.definition: Having light; bright; clear; not dark or obscure.

drown.definition: To die from suffocation while immersed in water or other fluid.

dough.definition: A thick, malleable substance made by mixing flour with other ingredients such as water, eggs, and/or butter, that is made into a particular form and then baked.

relative.definition: Someone in the same family; someone connected by blood, marriage, or adoption.

wreck.definition: Something or someone that has been ruined.
wreck.example: He was an emotional wreck after the death of his wife.
wreck.exampleZh: 妻子過世後，他的情緒崩潰了。

approve.definition: To sanction officially; to ratify; to confirm; to set as satisfactory.
approve.example: Although we may disagree with it, we must nevertheless approve the sentence handed down by the court-martial.
approve.exampleZh: 儘管我們可能不同意，但我們必須批准軍事法庭的判決。

burger.exampleZh: 齊裡狼吞虎嚥地吃掉了漢堡。

spiritual.definition: A Christian religious song, especially one in an African-American style, or a similar non-religious song.

roaring.definition: To make a loud, deep cry, especially from pain, anger, or other strong emotion.

arrival.definition: The act of arriving or something that has arrived.
arrival.example: The early arrival of the bride created a stir.
arrival.exampleZh: 新娘的提前到來引起了轟動。

scandal.definition: An incident or event that disgraces or damages the reputation of the persons or organization involved.
scandal.example: Their affair was reported as a scandal by most tabloids.
scandal.exampleZh: 他們的戀情被大多數小報報道為醜聞。

basket.definition: A lightweight container, generally round, open at the top, and tapering toward the bottom.
basket.example: A basket of fake fruit adorned the table.
basket.exampleZh: 桌上裝飾著一籃假水果。

unexpected.exampleZh: 意想不到的事情發生了。

stink.example: If you don't make a stink about the problem, nothing will be done.
stink.exampleZh: 如果你不去解決這個問題，那什麼都做不了。

homer.definition: Various former units of volume, particularly:
homer.example: According to tradition, Homer was blind.

instant.definition: A very short period of time; a moment.
instant.example: She paused for only an instant, which was just enough time for John to change the subject.
instant.exampleZh: 她只停頓了一會兒，剛好夠約翰轉移話題的時間。

shield.definition: Anything that protects or defends; defense; shelter; protection.

electronic.definition: : Of or pertaining to an electron or electrons.

shooter.definition: Someone who shoots something; a gunner, archer etc.
shooter.exampleZh: 他們確認了槍手的身份。

republic.definition: A state where sovereignty rests with the people or their representatives, rather than with a monarch or emperor; a country with no monarchy.
republic.example: The United States is a republic; the United Kingdom of Great Britain and Northern Ireland is a constitutional monarchy.
republic.exampleZh: 美國是一個共和國；大不列顛及北愛爾蘭聯合王國是君主立憲制國家。

overnight.exampleZh: 一夜之間一切都改變了。

heroin.definition: A powerful and addictive drug derived from opium producing intense euphoria classed as an illegal narcotic in most of the world.

economy.definition: Effective management of a community or system, or especially its resources.

tissue.example: a tissue of forgeries, or of lies

barney.definition: A lark, a romp, some fun.
barney.example: Sami was watching Barney in his spare time.
barney.exampleZh: 薩米在閒暇時看著巴尼。

supreme.exampleZh: 極度厭惡；至高無上的勇氣

impress.exampleZh: 給你的老闆留下深刻印象。

bingo.definition: A game of chance for two or more players, who mark off numbers on a grid as they are announced by the caller; the game is won by the first person to call out "bingo!" or "house!" after crossing off all numbers on the grid or in one line of the grid.
bingo.exampleZh: 齊裡給他的狗取名為「賓果」。

excitement.definition: The state of being excited (emotionally aroused).

broad.definition: A shallow lake, one of a number of bodies of water in eastern Norfolk and Suffolk.

mankind.definition: The human race in its entirety.
mankind.exampleZh: 人類摧毀了它所接觸到的一切。

document.definition: An original or official paper used as the basis, proof, or support of anything else, including any writing, book, or other instrument conveying information pertinent to such proof or support.

vessel.definition: Any craft designed for transportation on water, such as a ship or boat.
vessel.example: Damiano continued exploring the alien vessel.
vessel.exampleZh: 達米亞諾繼續探索外星飛船。

coffin.definition: A rectangular closed box in which the body of a dead person is placed for burial.

cocaine.definition: A stimulant narcotic, derived from cultivated plants of the genus Erythroxylum, in the form of a white powder that users generally self-administer by insufflation through the nose.

announcement.definition: An act of announcing, or giving notice.
announcement.example: He raised his hand to make his announcement and said "Excuse me everyone, I have an announcement to make.
announcement.exampleZh: 他舉手宣布：「各位，對不起，我有件事要宣布。

puppy.definition: A young dog, especially before sexual maturity (12-18 months)
puppy.exampleZh: 博爾迪的小狗一拐一拐的。

cotton.definition: Gossypium, a genus of plant used as a source of cotton fiber.

taxes.definition: Money paid to the government other than for transaction-specific goods and services.

lecture.definition: A spoken lesson or exposition, usually delivered to a group.
lecture.example: During class today the professor delivered an interesting lecture.
lecture.exampleZh: 今天上課時，教授做了一個有趣的講座。

autopsy.definition: A dissection performed on a cadaver to find possible cause(s) of death.
autopsy.example: Have you read the autopsy report?

skinny.definition: The details or facts; especially, those obtained by gossip or rumor.
skinny.example: She called to get the skinny on the latest goings-on in the club.
skinny.exampleZh: 她打電話詢問俱樂部的最新動態。

advise.definition: To give advice to; to offer an opinion to, as worthy or expedient to be followed.

include.definition: A piece of source code or other content that is dynamically retrieved for inclusion in another item.
include.example: I will purchase the vacation package if you will include car rental.
include.exampleZh: 如果您包含汽車租賃，我將購買度假套餐。

posted.definition: To hang (a notice) in a conspicuous manner for general review.

assistance.definition: Aid; help; the act or result of assisting.

practical.definition: A part of an exam or series of exams in which the candidate has to demonstrate their practical ability
practical.example: Jack didn't get an engineering degree, but has practical knowledge of metalworking.
practical.exampleZh: 傑克沒有獲得工程學位，但擁有金屬加工的實用知識。

marked.definition: Having a visible or identifying mark.
marked.example: The eighth century BC saw a marked increase in the general wealth of Cyprus.
marked.exampleZh: 西元前八世紀，塞浦路斯的整體財富顯著增加。

determine.definition: To set the boundaries or limits of.

rubber.definition: Pliable material derived from the sap of the rubber tree; a hydrocarbon polymer of isoprene.
rubber.example: Jones toes the rubber and then fires to the plate.
rubber.exampleZh: 瓊斯用腳尖踩住橡膠，然後朝本壘板射擊。

purple.definition: A colour/color that is a dark blend of red and blue; dark magenta.
purple.example: to put on the imperial purple

adorable.definition: Befitting of being adored; cute or loveable.

suite.definition: A retinue or company of attendants, as of a distinguished personage

arrow.definition: A projectile consisting of a shaft, a point and a tail with stabilizing fins that is shot from a bow.
arrow.example: Arrow left until you reach the start of the text you want to delete.
arrow.exampleZh: 向左箭頭，直到到達要刪除的文字的開頭。

counselor.definition: A professional who counsels people, especially on personal problems.
counselor.exampleZh: 營隊輔導員責罵德米特里。

carpet.definition: A fabric used as a complete floor covering.

spotted.definition: To see, find; to pick out, notice, locate, distinguish or identify.

journalist.definition: The keeper of a personal journal, who writes in it regularly.

eastern.definition: Of, facing, situated in, or related to the east.
eastern.exampleZh: 「東京」翻譯過來就是「東方之都」。

shave.definition: To make bald or shorter by using a tool such as a razor or pair of electric clippers to cut the hair close to the skin.

oldest.definition: Of an object, concept, relationship, etc., having existed for a relatively long period of time.

breast.definition: Either of the two organs on the front of a female human's chest, which contain the mammary glands; also the analogous organs in males.

sixth.definition: (not used in the plural) The person or thing in the sixth position.

error.definition: The state, quality, or condition of being wrong.

roommate.definition: A person with whom one shares a room, as at university etc.

sings.definition: A gathering at which people sing songs.

protest.definition: A formal objection, especially one by a group.
protest.example: They lodged a protest with the authorities.

misery.definition: Great unhappiness; extreme pain of body or mind; wretchedness; distress; woe.
misery.example: Ever since his wife left him you can see the misery on his face.
misery.exampleZh: 自從他的妻子離開他以來，你可以看到他臉上的痛苦。

react.definition: An emoji used to express a reaction to a post on social media.

faithful.definition: (in the plural) The practicing members of a religion or followers of a cause.
faithful.example: The faithful pray five times a day.

hector.definition: Sometimes in the form Hector: a blustering, noisy, turbulent fellow; a blusterer, bully.
hector.example: Hector's uncommonly brash and outspoken today. He's usually deathly quiet.
hector.exampleZh: 赫克托今天異常傲慢和直言不諱。他通常是死一般的安靜。

medal.definition: A stamped metal disc used as a personal ornament, a charm, or a religious object.
medal.exampleZh: 薩米值得獲得一枚獎牌。

banana.definition: An elongated curved tropical fruit that grows in bunches and has a creamy flesh and a smooth skin.

shitty.example: I'm feeling shitty today; I don't want to go out.
shitty.exampleZh: 我今天心情不好；我不想出去。

hopeless.definition: Without hope; despairing; not expecting anything positive.

debate.definition: An argument, or discussion, usually in an ordered or formal setting, often with more than two people, generally ending with a vote or other decision.
debate.example: After a four-hour debate, the committee voted to table the motion.
debate.exampleZh: 經過四個小時的辯論，委員會投票決定擱置該動議。

swell.definition: To become bigger, especially due to being engorged.
swell.example: Rains and dissolving snow swell the rivers in spring.
swell.exampleZh: 春天，雨水和融雪使河水水位上升。

liquid.definition: A substance that is flowing, and keeping no shape, such as water; a substance of which the molecules, while not tending to separate from one another like those of a gas, readily change their relative position, and which therefore retains no definite shape, except that determined by the containing receptacle; an inelastic fluid.
liquid.example: A liquid can freeze to become a solid or evaporate into a gas.
liquid.exampleZh: 液體可以凍結成固體或蒸發成氣體。

luckily.definition: In a lucky manner; by good fortune; fortunately.

feast.definition: A very large meal, often of a ceremonial nature.
feast.example: We had a feast to celebrate the harvest.
feast.exampleZh: 我們舉辦了一場盛宴來慶祝豐收。

application.definition: The act of applying or laying on, in a literal sense
application.example: The application of this cream should reduce the swelling.
application.exampleZh: 使用這種霜可以減輕腫脹。

peggy.definition: Any of several small warblers, the whitethroat, etc.
peggy.example: Peggy did not go to see the drama, and neither did I.
peggy.exampleZh: 佩吉沒有去看這部戲，我也沒去看。

bench.definition: A long seat with or without a back, found for example in parks and schools.
bench.example: They sat on a park bench and tossed bread crumbs to the ducks and pigeons.
bench.exampleZh: 他們坐在公園的長椅上，向鴨子和鴿子丟麵包屑。

murphy.example: Murphy's law states that whatever can go wrong will go wrong.
murphy.exampleZh: 墨菲定律指出，凡是可能出錯的地方就一定會出錯。

profession.definition: A declaration of belief, faith or one's opinion, whether genuine or pretended.

aggressive.definition: Characterized by aggression; unjustly attacking; prone to behave in a way that involves attacking or arguing.
aggressive.example: an aggressive policy, war, person, nation
aggressive.exampleZh: 侵略性政策、戰爭、個人、國家

significant.definition: That which has significance; a sign; a token; a symbol.
significant.example: a significant word or sound; a significant look
significant.exampleZh: 重要的單字或聲音；一個重要的外觀

scout.definition: A person sent out to gain and bring in tidings; especially, one employed in war to gain information about the enemy and ground.
scout.exampleZh: 聖地牙哥是童子軍成員。

defence.definition: The action of defending, of protecting from attack, danger or injury.

display.example: The trapeze artist put on an amazing acrobatic display.
display.exampleZh: 這位空中飛人表演了令人驚嘆的雜技表演。

clubs.definition: An association of members joining together for some common purpose, especially sports or recreation.

filming.definition: To record (activity, or a motion picture) on photographic film.
filming.example: A Hollywood studio was filming on location in NYC.
filming.exampleZh: 好萊塢工作室正在紐約進行拍攝。

visual.definition: Any element of something that depends on sight.

sorrow.exampleZh: 離別是多麼甜蜜的悲傷。

wolves.definition: The gray wolf, specifically all subspecies of the gray wolf (Canis lupus) that are not dingoes or dogs.

vault.definition: An arched masonry structure supporting and forming a ceiling, whether freestanding or forming part of a larger building.
vault.example: Family members had been buried in the vault for centuries.
vault.exampleZh: 幾個世紀以來，家族成員一直被埋在墓穴裡。

pepper.definition: A plant of the family Piperaceae.
pepper.example: Some ballparks have signs saying "No pepper games".
pepper.exampleZh: 有些棒球場有標語「禁止進行胡椒比賽」。

dunno.definition: An utterance of the word dunno.
dunno.example: I dunno if it's a bug or what, but this software doesn't work right.
dunno.exampleZh: 我不知道這是一個錯誤還是什麼，但這個軟體無法正常工作。

compare.example: Compare the tiger's coloration with that of the zebra.
compare.exampleZh: 比較老虎和斑馬的顏色。

quote.definition: A quotation; a statement attributed to a person.

speaker.example: The company hired a motivational speaker to boost morale.
speaker.exampleZh: 該公司聘請了一位勵志演講者來鼓舞士氣。

copies.definition: The result of copying; an identical duplicate of an original.
copies.example: Please bring me the copies of those reports.
copies.exampleZh: 請給我這些報告的副本。

helmet.definition: A protective head covering, usually part of armour.

envelope.definition: A paper or cardboard wrapper used to enclose small, flat items, especially letters, for mailing.

perry.definition: A fermented alcoholic beverage made from pears; somewhat analogous to cider.
perry.example: Perry has acquired the habit of thinking aloud.
perry.exampleZh: 佩里養成了大聲思考的習慣。

sonic.example: Since the 60s, Sonic is the only big chain that still uses carhops.
sonic.exampleZh: 自 60 年代以來，Sonic 是唯一一家仍在使用停車位的大型連鎖店。

belief.definition: Mental acceptance of a claim as true.
belief.example: It's my belief that the thief is somebody known to us.
belief.exampleZh: 我相信小偷是我們認識的人。

storage.definition: The act of storing goods; the state of being stored.
storage.example: There's a lot of storage space in the loft.
storage.exampleZh: 閣樓裡有很多儲存空間。

psycho.definition: A person who is psychotic or otherwise insane.
psycho.example: The psycho killed five people in a day.
psycho.exampleZh: 這個神經病一天內殺了五個人。

alternative.definition: A situation which allows a mutually exclusive choice between two or more possibilities; a choice between two or more possibilities.

fears.definition: A strong, uncontrollable, unpleasant emotion or feeling caused by actual or perceived danger or threat.

bailey.definition: The outer wall of a feudal castle.
bailey.example: the Old Bailey in London; the New Bailey in Manchester
bailey.exampleZh: 倫敦的老貝利；曼徹斯特新貝利酒店

ladder.definition: A frame, usually portable, of wood, metal, or rope, used for ascent and descent, consisting of two side pieces to which are fastened rungs (cross strips or rounds acting as steps).

dining.definition: To eat; to eat dinner or supper.

duties.definition: That which one is morally or legally obligated to do.

heels.definition: The rear part of the foot, where it joins the leg.
heels.exampleZh: 瑪麗亞總是穿著高跟鞋。

rhythm.definition: The variation of strong and weak elements (such as duration, accent) of sounds, notably in speech or music, over time; a beat or meter.
rhythm.example: Dance to the rhythm of the music.

chess.definition: A board game for two players with each beginning with sixteen chess pieces moving according to fixed rules across a chessboard with the objective to checkmate the opposing king.

barrel.definition: A round vessel or cask, of greater length than breadth, and bulging in the middle, made of staves bound with hoops, and having flat ends or heads. Sometimes applied to a similar cylindrical container made of metal, usually called a drum.

primary.definition: A primary election; a preliminary election to select a political candidate of a political party.

motorcycle.definition: An open-seated motor vehicle with handlebars instead of a steering wheel, and having two (or sometimes three) wheels.
motorcycle.exampleZh: 齊裡啟動了他的摩托車。

cargo.definition: Freight carried by a ship, aircraft, or motor vehicle.
cargo.example: Yanni transports heavy cargo to Algiers.
cargo.exampleZh: 雅尼將重型貨物運送到阿爾及爾。

trailer.definition: Someone who or something that trails.

cannon.definition: A complete assembly, consisting of an artillery tube and a breech mechanism, firing mechanism or base cap, which is a component of a gun, howitzer or mortar. It may include muzzle appendages.
cannon.example: In English billiards, a cannon is when one's cue ball strikes the other player's cue ball and the red ball on the same shot; and it is worth two points.
cannon.exampleZh: 在英式撞球中，大砲是指一方的母球同時擊中另一方的母球和紅球；這值得兩點。

tapes.definition: Flexible material in a roll with a sticky surface on one or both sides; adhesive tape.

worthless.definition: Having no worth or use; without value; inconsequential.
worthless.example: Don't be a worthless slouch! Go get yourself a job.
worthless.exampleZh: 別當個毫無價值的懶漢！給自己找份工作吧。

items.example: Tweezers are great for manipulating small items.
items.exampleZh: 鑷子非常適合操作小物品。

rumor.definition: A statement or claim of questionable accuracy, from no known reliable source, usually spread by word of mouth.
rumor.example: There's a rumor going round that he's going to get married.

risks.definition: A possible adverse event or outcome

instrumental.example: He was instrumental in conducting the business.
instrumental.exampleZh: 他在開展業務方面發揮了重要作用。

skirt.definition: An article of clothing, usually worn by women and girls, that hangs from the waist and covers the lower part of the body.

perfume.definition: A pleasant smell; the scent, odor, or odoriferous particles emitted from a sweet-smelling substance; a pleasant odor

loses.definition: To cause (something) to cease to be in one's possession or capability due to unfortunate or unknown circumstances, events or reasons.

gallery.definition: An institution, building, or room for the exhibition and conservation of works of art.
gallery.example: a clip-art gallery in a wordprocessor
gallery.exampleZh: 文字處理器中的剪貼畫畫廊

spike.definition: In a violin-family instrument, the carved wooden plug which sits in the bottom block of the instrument.
spike.exampleZh: 你給我的飲料加酒了嗎？

improve.definition: To make (something) better; to increase the value or productivity (of something).
improve.example: Buying more servers would improve performance.
improve.exampleZh: 購買更多伺服器可以提高效能。

eleven.definition: A cricket team of eleven players. Hence first eleven - the team of best cricket players (at a school), second eleven - the "B" team, etc.
eleven.exampleZh: 該名學生有十一支鉛筆。

ginger.definition: The pungent aromatic rhizome of a tropical Asian herb, Zingiber officinale, used as a spice and as a stimulant and acarminative.
ginger.exampleZh: 薩米喜歡薑汁汽水麵包。

wiped.definition: To move an object over, maintaining contact, with the intention of removing some substance from the surface. (Compare rub.)
wiped.example: I wiped the sweat from my brow with the back of my hand.
wiped.exampleZh: 我用手背擦了擦額頭的汗。

connie.definition: A rabbit, especially the European rabbit, Oryctolagus cuniculus (formerly known as Lepus cuniculus).
connie.example: Connie Lambert keeps active, travels and defies her age of 76.
connie.exampleZh: 康妮·蘭伯特 (Connie Lambert) 保持活力、旅行並挑戰自己 76 歲的年齡。

kindly.definition: Having a kind personality; kind, warmhearted, sympathetic.
kindly.example: A kindly old man sits on the park bench every afternoon feeding pigeons.
kindly.exampleZh: 每天下午，一位慈祥的老人坐在公園的長椅上餵鴿子。

martial.definition: A person under the astrological influence of the planet Mars.
martial.example: martial preparations martial flowers (a reddish crystalline salt of iron)
martial.exampleZh: 武術準備 武術花（微紅色結晶鐵鹽）

tension.definition: The condition of being held in a state between two or more forces, which are acting in opposition to each other.
tension.exampleZh: 緊張氣氛變得難以忍受。

whale.definition: Any of several species of large sea mammals of the infraorder Cetacea.

controlled.definition: To exercise influence over; to suggest or dictate the behavior of.
controlled.exampleZh: 蘇萊曼尼控制了伊拉克。

paranoid.example: Maybe I'm paranoid, but that doesn't mean that they are not out to get me.
paranoid.exampleZh: 也許我有點偏執，但這並不代表他們不會來抓我。

upside.definition: The highest or uppermost side or portion of something

imperial.definition: A bottle of wine (usually Bordeaux) containing 6 liters of fluid, eight times the volume of a standard bottle.
imperial.example: I took him to the Kyoto Imperial Palace.

congress.definition: A coming together of two or more people; a meeting.
congress.example: The National Congress of American Indians
congress.exampleZh: 美洲印第安人全國代表大會

villa.definition: (plural "villas") A house, often larger and more expensive than average, in the countryside or on the coast, often used as a retreat.
villa.example: Aston Villa beat Liverpool seven to two.
villa.exampleZh: 阿斯頓維拉以七比二擊敗利物浦。

volunteer.definition: One who enters into, or offers for, any service of his/her own free will, especially when done without pay.
volunteer.example: to volunteer for doing the dishes

recovery.definition: The act or process of regaining or repossession of something lost.

narrow.definition: (chiefly in the plural) A narrow passage, especially a contracted part of a stream, lake, or sea; a strait connecting two bodies of water.

observe.definition: To notice or view, especially carefully or with attention to detail.
observe.example: From this vantage point we can observe the behavior of the animals in their natural habitat.
observe.exampleZh: 從這個有利位置，我們可以觀察動物在自然棲息地的行為。

cycle.definition: An interval of space or time in which one set of events or phenomena is completed.
cycle.example: the cycle of the seasons, or of the year

psychic.definition: A person who possesses, or appears to possess, extra-sensory abilities such as precognition, clairvoyance and telepathy, or who appears to be susceptible to paranormal or supernatural influence.
psychic.example: She is a psychic person—she hears messages from beyond.
psychic.exampleZh: 她是個通靈者－她能聽到來自彼岸的訊息。

faced.definition: (of a person or animal) To position oneself or itself so as to have one's face closest to (something).

accidentally.definition: In an accidental manner; by chance, unexpectedly.
accidentally.exampleZh: 他發現青黴素很大程度上是偶然的。

conclusion.definition: The end, finish, close or last part of something.
conclusion.example: The board has come to the conclusion that the proposed takeover would not be in the interest of our shareholders.
conclusion.exampleZh: 董事會得出的結論是，擬議的收購不符合我們股東的利益。

spots.definition: A round or irregular patch on the surface of a thing having a different color, texture etc. and generally round in shape.
spots.example: The leopard is noted for the spots of color in its fur.
spots.exampleZh: 豹子以其皮毛上的彩色斑點而聞名。

sweep.example: Give the front steps a quick sweep to get rid of those fallen leaves.
sweep.exampleZh: 快速清掃前面的台階，清除那些落葉。

communist.definition: An advocate of a society based on the common ownership of property; a proponent of communism.
communist.exampleZh: 俄羅斯成為共產主義國家。

risky.example: Investing in this start-up company could be risky.
risky.exampleZh: 投資這家新創公司可能有風險。

glorious.definition: Exhibiting attributes, qualities, or acts that are worthy of or receive glory.

blackmail.definition: The extortion of money by threats of public accusation, exposure, or censure.

filed.definition: To commit (official papers) to some office.
filed.example: She filed for divorce the next day.

pacific.example: Tom hiked the Pacific Northwest trail.
pacific.exampleZh: 湯姆沿著太平洋西北小徑健行。

deals.definition: A division, a portion, a share.
deals.example: We gave three deals of grain in tribute to the king.
deals.exampleZh: 我們向國王進貢了三筆糧食。

scotch.example: a scotch for a wheel or a log on inclined ground
scotch.exampleZh: 傾斜地面上的輪子或圓木的蘇格蘭威士忌

blank.definition: A small French coin, originally of silver, afterwards of copper, worth 5 deniers; also a silver coin of Henry V current in the parts of France then held by the English, worth about 8 pence .

tender.example: tender plants; tender flesh; tender fruit

sucker.definition: A person or animal that sucks, especially a breast or udder; especially a suckling animal, young mammal before it is weaned.
sucker.example: One poor sucker had actually given her his life’s savings.
sucker.exampleZh: 一個可憐的笨蛋竟然把他畢生的積蓄都給了她。

slight.definition: The act of slighting; a deliberate act of neglect or discourtesy.

turner.definition: One who or that which turns.
turner.example: Tina Turner's stage shows were spectacular.
turner.exampleZh: 蒂娜·特納的舞台表演非常精彩。

addition.example: The addition of five more items to the agenda will make the meeting unbearably long.
addition.exampleZh: 議程中再增加五個項目將使會議變得漫長得令人難以忍受。

ritual.definition: Rite; a repeated set of actions
ritual.example: They carried out the ritual carefully.
ritual.exampleZh: 他們小心翼翼地進行儀式。

campus.definition: The grounds or property of a school, college, university, business, church, or hospital, often understood to include buildings and other structures.
campus.example: The campus is sixty hectares in size.

graduate.definition: A person who is recognized by a university as having completed the requirements of a degree studied at the institution.

zombie.definition: A snake god or fetish in religions of West Africa and elsewhere.
zombie.example: After working for 18 hours on the computer, I was a zombie.
zombie.exampleZh: 在電腦前工作了18個小時後，我已經變成殭屍了。

somewhat.definition: More or less; a certain quantity or degree; a part, more or less; something.
somewhat.example: The crowd was somewhat larger than expected, perhaps due to the good weather.
somewhat.exampleZh: 可能是因為天氣好的原因，人潮比想像的還要多。

principle.definition: A fundamental assumption or guiding belief.
principle.exampleZh: 阿爾及利亞尊重這項原則。

therapist.definition: Someone who provides therapy, usually professionally.
therapist.exampleZh: 齊裡拜訪了一位治療師。

compete.definition: To be in battle or in a rivalry with another for the same thing, position, or reward; to contend

worship.definition: The condition of being worthy; honour, distinction.
worship.example: We worship at the church down the road.
worship.exampleZh: 我們在路邊的教會做禮拜。

compliment.definition: An expression of praise, congratulation, or respect.

appetite.definition: Desire to eat food or consume drink.

strictly.example: He adhered strictly to the diet his doctor prescribed.
strictly.exampleZh: 他嚴格遵守醫生規定的飲食。

democracy.definition: Rule by the people, especially as a form of government; either directly or through elected representatives (representative democracy).
democracy.exampleZh: 伊斯蘭主義者批評民主。

formal.example: Jenny took Sam to her Year 12 formal.
formal.exampleZh: 珍妮帶山姆參加她的 12 年級正式舞會。

tanks.definition: A closed container for liquids or gases.
tanks.example: I burned three tanks of gas on the drive to New York.
tanks.exampleZh: 我在去紐約的路上燒了三箱汽油。

technical.definition: A pickup truck with a gun mounted on it.
technical.example: The performance showed technical virtuosity, but lacked inspiration.
technical.exampleZh: 表演展現了精湛的技術，但缺乏靈感。

shepherd.definition: A person who tends sheep, especially a grazing flock.
shepherd.exampleZh: 伽尼米德是一位牧羊人。

brazil.definition: A red-orange dye obtained from brazil wood.

retire.definition: The act of retiring, or the state of being retired.
retire.example: At the retire, the cavalry fell back.

overcome.definition: The burden or recurring theme in a song.

inspiration.definition: The drawing of air into the lungs, accomplished in mammals by elevation of the chest walls and flattening of the diaphragm, as part of the act of respiration.
inspiration.example: She was waiting for inspiration to write a book.
inspiration.exampleZh: 她正在等待寫書的靈感。

dummy.definition: A silent person; a person who does not talk.

brutal.definition: Savagely violent, vicious, ruthless, or cruel

suggestion.definition: Something suggested (with subsequent adposition being for)
suggestion.example: I have a small suggestion for fixing this: try lifting the left side up a bit.
suggestion.exampleZh: 我有一個解決這個問題的小建議：嘗試將左側抬起一點。

sailor.definition: A person in the business of navigating ships or other vessels
sailor.example: He's a talented sailor and has spent many years at sea.
sailor.exampleZh: 他是一位才華橫溢的水手，在海上度過了多年。

destination.definition: The act of destining or appointing.

picnic.definition: An informal social gathering, usually in a natural outdoor setting, to which the participants bring their own food and drink.
picnic.example: We went out for a picnic in the forest.
picnic.exampleZh: 我們出去在森林裡野餐。

formula.example: H2O is the formula for water.

veronica.definition: The image of Jesus's face believed to have been made on the cloth with which St Veronica wiped his face as he went to be crucified; or the cloth used for this.
veronica.example: It has to be said that Veronica has done a great job here.
veronica.exampleZh: 不得不說，維羅妮卡在這裡做得非常出色。

clerk.definition: One who occupationally works with records, accounts, letters, etc.; an office worker.

ministry.definition: Government department, at the administrative level normally headed by a minister (or equivalent rank, e.g. secretary of state), who holds it as portfolio, especially in a constitutional monarchy, but also as a polity
ministry.example: She works for the ministry of finance.

samurai.definition: In feudal Japan, a soldier who served a daimyo.
samurai.example: Sami showed Layla a samurai sword.
samurai.exampleZh: 薩米向萊拉展示了一把武士刀。

brandy.definition: An alcoholic liquor distilled from wine or fermented fruit juice.

locate.definition: To place; to set in a particular spot or position.
locate.example: The council must locate the new hospital
locate.exampleZh: 委員會必須找到新醫院

sweater.definition: A knitted jacket or jersey, usually of thick wool, worn by athletes before or after exercise.

parole.definition: The release of a former prisoner under condition of compliance with specific terms.

apologise.definition: (often followed by “for”) To make an apology or excuse; to acknowledge some fault or offense, with expression of regret for it, by way of amends

candle.definition: A light source consisting of a wick embedded in a solid, flammable substance such as wax, tallow, or paraffin.

prior.definition: A high-ranking member of a monastery, usually lower in rank than an abbot.
prior.example: I had no prior knowledge you were coming.
prior.exampleZh: 我事先並不知道你會來。

stream.definition: A small river; a large creek; a body of moving water confined by banks.
stream.example: He poured the milk in a thin stream from the jug to the glass.
stream.exampleZh: 他把牛奶從罐子裡細細地倒到玻璃杯裡。

examine.definition: To observe or inspect carefully or critically

passage.definition: A paragraph or section of text or music with particular meaning.
passage.example: How would you interpret this passage?

organ.definition: A larger part of an organism, composed of tissues that perform similar functions.
organ.exampleZh: 雅尼遭受了全身器官衰竭。

outer.example: Being in outer space atrophies your muscles.
outer.exampleZh: 在外太空會使你的肌肉萎縮。

sunset.definition: The time of day when the sun disappears below the western horizon.

pistol.definition: A handgun, typically with a chamber integrated in the barrel, a semi-automatic action and a box magazine.
pistol.exampleZh: 卡爾曼給他的手槍上膛。

fortunately.example: Fortunately, Sam's pet hamster was not dead.
fortunately.exampleZh: 幸運的是，山姆的寵物倉鼠沒有死。

funds.definition: A sum or source of money.
funds.exampleZh: 他們浪費了我們的資金。

gotcha.definition: A potential problem or source of trouble.

balloon.definition: An inflatable buoyant object, often (but not necessarily) round and flexible.
balloon.example: the balloon of St. Paul's Cathedral in London
balloon.exampleZh: 倫敦聖保羅大教堂的氣球

spray.definition: A fine, gentle, dispersed mist of liquid.
spray.example: The sailor could feel the spray from the waves.
spray.exampleZh: 水手可以感覺到海浪濺起的水花。

element.definition: One of the simplest or essential parts or principles of which anything consists, or upon which the constitution or fundamental powers of anything are based.
element.exampleZh: 鈉是一種普遍存在的元素。

eternity.example: It's been an eternity since we last saw each other.
eternity.exampleZh: 自從我們上次見面以來已經過了很長時間。

handy.example: Some people regard duct tape as a handy fix-all.
handy.exampleZh: 有些人認為管道膠帶是一種方便的解決辦法。

ninja.example: Michael kicked Walid away like a ninja.
ninja.exampleZh: 麥可像忍者一樣把瓦利德踢開了。

behaviour.definition: The way a living creature behaves or acts.
behaviour.example: She can't stop the strange behaviour because she has OCD (obsessive-compulsive disorder).
behaviour.exampleZh: 她無法阻止這種奇怪的行為，因為她患有OCD（強迫症）。

slice.definition: That which is thin and broad.
slice.example: Jim was munching on a slice of toast.
slice.exampleZh: 吉姆正在嚼著一片吐司。

sausage.definition: A food made of ground meat (or meat substitute) and seasoning, packed in a section of the animal's intestine, or in a similarly cylindrical shaped synthetic casing; a length of this food.

stiff.definition: An average person, usually male, of no particular distinction, skill, or education, often a working stiff or lucky stiff.
stiff.example: A Working Stiff's Manifesto: A Memoir of Thirty Jobs I Quit, Nine That Fired Me, and Three I Can't Remember was published in 2003.
stiff.exampleZh: 《一個工作僵化者的宣言：我辭掉的三十份工作、解僱我的九份工作和我不記得的三份工作的回憶錄》於 2003 年出版。

euros.definition: Person living or originating from Europe

spinning.definition: To rotate, revolve, gyrate (usually quickly); to partially or completely rotate to face another direction.

genetic.example: Chinese has borrowed several words from English, but it does not have a genetic relationship to English.
genetic.exampleZh: 漢語從英語中藉用了幾個單詞，但與英語沒有血緣關係。

constable.definition: One holding the lowest rank in most Commonwealth police forces. (See also chief constable.)
constable.example: The police constable arrested the teenager for shoplifting.
constable.exampleZh: 警方以入店行竊罪逮捕了這名少年。

loudly.definition: In a loud manner; at a high volume.
loudly.example: He spoke loudly so that his brother could hear him from across the street.
loudly.exampleZh: 他大聲說話，這樣他的兄弟就可以在街對面聽到他的聲音。

rolls.definition: The act or result of rolling, or state of being rolled.

marvelous.definition: Exciting wonder or surprise; astonishing; wonderful; delightful.
marvelous.example: I went to a marvelous party last week.
marvelous.exampleZh: 上週我參加了一個很棒的聚會。

subway.definition: An underground railway, especially for mass transit of people in urban areas.

creep.definition: To move slowly with the abdomen close to the ground.

scheme.definition: A systematic plan of future action.
scheme.exampleZh: 加密貨幣是一種金字塔計劃。

invasion.definition: A military action consisting of armed forces of one geopolitical entity entering territory controlled by another such entity, generally with the objective of conquering territory or altering the established government.

affection.definition: The act of affecting or acting upon.
affection.example: I have a lot of affection for my little sister.
affection.exampleZh: 我對我的小妹妹很有感情。

protocol.definition: The minutes, or official record, of a negotiation or transaction; especially a document drawn up officially which forms the legal basis for subsequent agreements based on it.

woody.definition: An early station wagon or estate car in which the rear portion of the car's bodywork is made of wood, often associated with Southern California surfing culture.
woody.example: Subshrubs, shrubs, trees and lianas are all woody plants.
woody.exampleZh: 亞灌木、灌木、喬木和藤本植物都是木本植物。

kidney.definition: An organ in the body that filters the blood, producing urine.

endless.exampleZh: 無盡的時間；無盡的讚美

absence.definition: A state of being away or withdrawn from a place or from companionship; the period of being away.
absence.example: Absence makes the heart grow fonder.

horns.definition: A hard growth of keratin that protrudes from the top of the head of certain animals, usually paired.

dudes.definition: A man, generally a younger man.
dudes.example: So we were at the mall and these two dudes just walk up to us and say "hi".
dudes.exampleZh: 所以我們在購物中心，這兩個傢伙走到我們跟前說「嗨」。

philosophy.example: Philosophy is often divided into five major branches: logic, metaphysics, epistemology, ethics and aesthetics.
philosophy.exampleZh: 哲學通常分為五個主要分支：邏輯學、形上學、知識論、倫理學和美學。

anyhow.definition: (manner) In any way or manner whatever.
anyhow.exampleZh: 無論如何，他現在已經死了。

drake.example: In my opinion, Anna had the best companion quest, followed by Drake.
drake.exampleZh: 在我看來，安娜的同伴任務最好，其次是德雷克。

privilege.definition: (ecclesiastical law) An exemption from certain laws granted by the Pope.
privilege.example: All first-year professors here must teach four courses a term, yet you're only teaching one! What entitled you to such a privilege?
privilege.exampleZh: 這裡所有的一年級教授每學期必須教授四門課程，而你只教一門！是什麼讓您享有這樣的特權？

daylight.definition: The light from the Sun, as opposed to that from any other source.
daylight.example: We should get home while it's still daylight.
daylight.exampleZh: 我們應該趁天還沒亮就回家。

airplane.definition: A powered heavier-than-air aircraft with fixed wings.
airplane.exampleZh: 飛航模式會使設備靜音。

disagree.definition: To fail to agree; to have a different opinion or belief.
disagree.example: Bob says cats are friendlier than dogs, but I disagree.
disagree.exampleZh: 鮑伯說貓比狗更友好，但我不同意。

jolly.example: He was chubby and plump, a right jolly old elf.
jolly.exampleZh: 他又胖又胖，真是個快樂的老精靈。

wizard.definition: Someone, usually male, who uses (or has skill with) magic, mystic items, and magical and mystical practices.
wizard.example: He was a financial wizard, capable of predicting the movements of the stock markets.
wizard.exampleZh: 他是一位金融奇才，能夠預測股市的走勢。

swore.definition: To take an oath, to promise.

breeze.example: The breeze rustled the papers on her desk.
breeze.exampleZh: 微風吹得她桌上的文件沙沙作響。

convenient.definition: Serving to reduce a difficulty, or accessible with minimum difficulty; expedient.
convenient.example: Fast food might be convenient, but it's also very unhealthy.
convenient.exampleZh: 快餐可能很方便，但也很不健康。

entitled.example: Everyone is entitled to their opinion.
entitled.exampleZh: 每個人都有權利發表自己的意見。

porter.definition: A person who carries luggage and related objects.
porter.example: By the time I reached the train station I was exhausted, but fortunately there was a porter waiting.
porter.exampleZh: 到達火車站時我已精疲力竭，幸好有行李員在等我。

bonus.definition: Something extra that is good; an added benefit.
bonus.example: The employee of the week receives a bonus for his excellent work.
bonus.exampleZh: 本週的最佳員工因其出色的工作而獲得獎金。

physics.definition: A medicine or drug, especially a cathartic or purgative.
physics.example: Newtonian physics was extended by Einstein to explain the effects of travelling near the speed of light; quantum physics extends it to account for the behaviour of atoms.
physics.exampleZh: 愛因斯坦對牛頓物理學進行了擴展，以解釋接近光速行進的影響。量子物理學將其擴展以解釋原子的行為。

wrist.definition: The complex joint between forearm bones, carpus, and metacarpals where the hand is attached to the arm; the carpus in a narrow sense.

coins.definition: (money) A piece of currency, usually metallic and in the shape of a disc, but sometimes polygonal, or with a hole in the middle.

phoebe.definition: Any of several birds of the genus Sayornis.
phoebe.example: Phoebe discovered that her sister was a camgirl.
phoebe.exampleZh: 菲比發現她的妹妹是一名攝影女郎。

income.definition: Money one earns by working or by capitalising on the work of others.

accurate.definition: Telling the truth or giving a true result; exact; not defective or faulty

suspicion.definition: The act of suspecting something or someone, especially of something wrong.

boxing.definition: To place inside a box; to pack in one or more boxes.

thumb.definition: The short thick digit of the hand that for humans has the most mobility and can be made to oppose (moved to touch) all of the other fingers.

roots.definition: The part of a plant, generally underground, that anchors and supports the plant body, absorbs and stores water and nutrients, and in some plants is able to perform vegetative reproduction.
roots.example: This tree's roots can go as deep as twenty metres underground.
roots.exampleZh: 這棵樹的根可以深入地下二十公尺。

jessie.definition: (sometimes uncapitalized) A cowardly person, especially a man; an effeminate man.
jessie.example: Though most adults partake, Jessie eschews coffee.
jessie.exampleZh: 儘管大多數成年人都喝咖啡，但傑西卻避開咖啡。

recipe.definition: A formula for preparing or using a medicine; a prescription; also, a medicine prepared from such instructions.
recipe.example: His new approach is definitely a recipe for success.
recipe.exampleZh: 他的新方法絕對是成功的秘訣。

generally.example: It is generally known that the Earth is round.

blues.definition: The colour of the clear sky or the deep sea, between green and violet in the visible spectrum, and one of the primary additive colours for transmitted light; the colour obtained by subtracting red and green from white light using magenta and cyan filters; or any colour resembling this.

seeking.definition: To try to find; to look for; to search for.

ensure.definition: To make a pledge to (someone); to promise, guarantee (someone of something); to assure.
ensure.example: I use an alarm clock to ensure that I get up on time.
ensure.exampleZh: 我使用鬧鐘來確保我準時起床。

tricky.example: They were in a tricky situation.

maximum.example: Use the proper dose for the maximum effect.
maximum.exampleZh: 使用適當的劑量以獲得最大效果。

hockey.definition: Ice hockey, a game on ice in which two teams of six players skate and try to score by shooting a puck into the opposing team's net, using their sticks.

curtain.definition: A piece of cloth covering a window, bed, etc. to offer privacy and keep out light.
curtain.example: He took so long to shave his head that we arrived 45 minutes after curtain and were denied late entry.
curtain.exampleZh: 他花了很長時間才剃光頭，以至於我們在幕後 45 分鐘才到達，但被拒絕遲到。

bubble.definition: A spherically contained volume of air or other gas, especially one made from soapy liquid.

carriage.example: The carriage ride was very romantic.

clues.definition: A strand of yarn etc. as used to guide one through a labyrinth; something which points the way, a guide.

debts.definition: An action, state of mind, or object one has an obligation to perform for another, adopt toward another, or give to another.

rainbow.definition: A multicoloured arch in the sky, produced by prismatic refraction of light within droplets of rain in the air.

honesty.definition: The act, quality, or condition of being honest.
honesty.exampleZh: 學術/藝術/情感/智力誠實

associate.definition: A person united with another or others in an act, enterprise, or business; a partner.
associate.exampleZh: 薩米是我們的銷售助理。

meals.definition: Food that is prepared and eaten, usually at a specific time, and usually in a comparatively large quantity (as opposed to a snack).

platform.definition: A raised stage from which speeches are made and on which musical and other performances are made.
platform.example: This new talk show will give a platform to everyday men and women.
platform.exampleZh: 這個新的脫口秀將為日常生活中的男人和女人提供一個平台。

illusion.definition: Anything that seems to be something that it is not.
illusion.example: Using artificial additives, scientists can create the illusion of fruit flavours in food.
illusion.exampleZh: 使用人工添加劑，科學家可以在食物中製造出水果風味的錯覺。

erica.definition: Any of many heathers, of the genus Erica, used as garden plants
erica.example: Erica got degrees in sociology and psychology, and now works as a market researcher.
erica.exampleZh: 艾麗卡獲得了社會學和心理學學位，現在擔任市場研究員。

dealt.definition: To distribute among a number of recipients, to give out as one’s portion or share.

hears.definition: (stative) To perceive sounds through the ear.

prosecution.definition: The act of prosecuting a scheme or endeavor.
prosecution.example: The prosecution of the war fell to Winston Churchill.
prosecution.exampleZh: 戰爭的起訴權落到了溫斯頓邱吉爾的肩上。

length.definition: The distance measured along the longest dimension of an object.

manhattan.definition: A cocktail made from whiskey, sweet vermouth and bitters.
manhattan.example: Without bedrock, Manhattan's skyscrapers would be unstable.
manhattan.exampleZh: 如果沒有基岩，曼哈頓的摩天大樓就會不穩定。

lesbian.definition: A homosexual woman, one who is mostly or exclusively sexually or romantically attracted to other women.

dentist.definition: A medical doctor who specializes in dentistry.

roast.definition: A cut of meat suited to roasting
roast.example: Dark roast means that the coffee bean has been roasted to a higher temperature and for a longer period of time than in light roast.
roast.exampleZh: 深度烘焙意味著咖啡豆被烘焙到比輕度烘焙更高的溫度和更長的時間。

tournament.definition: During the Middle Ages, a series of battles and other contests designed to prepare knights for war.
tournament.example: He finished eleventh in the tournament.
tournament.exampleZh: 他在錦標賽中獲得第十一名。

harbor.example: The neighborhood is a well-known harbor for petty thieves.
harbor.exampleZh: 該街區是著名的小偷窩點。

flood.definition: A (usually disastrous) overflow of water from a lake or other body of water due to excessive rainfall or other input of water.

website.definition: A collection of interlinked web pages on the World Wide Web that are typically accessible from the same base URL and reside on the same server.

mademoiselle.definition: Courtesy title for an unmarried woman in France or a French-speaking country.
mademoiselle.example: Mademoiselle, I would like to buy a suit, a nice-looking one.
mademoiselle.exampleZh: 小姐，我想買一套西裝，一套好看的。

auction.definition: A public event where goods or property are sold to the highest bidder.
auction.example: Ziri carefully studied the auction catalog.
auction.exampleZh: 紫日仔細研究了拍賣圖錄。

champ.definition: Buddy, sport, mate (as a term of address)

negotiate.definition: To confer with others in order to come to terms or reach an agreement.
negotiate.example: The client and server computers must first negotiate a network protocol to be used.
negotiate.exampleZh: 客戶端和伺服器電腦必須先協商要使用的網路協定。

bachelor.definition: A person, especially a man, who is socially regarded as able to marry, but has not yet.

dizzy.example: I stood up too fast and felt dizzy.
dizzy.exampleZh: 我站起來太快了，感到頭暈。

shotgun.definition: A gun which fires loads typically consisting of small metal balls, called shot, from a cartridge.
shotgun.example: Meat was cooked up within hours after the hunter killed the deer with his shotgun.
shotgun.exampleZh: 獵人用獵槍殺死鹿後幾個小時內肉就煮熟了。

reference.example: You must thoroughly reference your paper before submitting it.
reference.exampleZh: 在提交之前，您必須徹底參考您的論文。

butler.definition: A manservant having charge of wines and liquors.

minimum.example: We need a minimum of three staff members on duty at all time.
minimum.exampleZh: 我們始終需要至少三名工作人員值班。

civilization.definition: An organized culture encompassing many communities, often on the scale of a nation or a people; a stage or system of social, political or technical development.
civilization.example: Modern civilization is a product of industrialization and globalization.
civilization.exampleZh: 現代文明是工業化和全球化的產物。

patty.definition: (US, Australia, New Zealand) A flattened portion of ground meat or a vegetarian equivalent, usually round but sometimes square in shape.
patty.example: He prefers a double patty burger.
patty.exampleZh: 他更喜歡雙層肉餅漢堡。

carmen.definition: A person who transported goods, usually with a horse and cart.
carmen.example: Carmen Sandiego is infamous for her capers.
carmen.exampleZh: 卡門·桑迪戈因其胡鬧而臭名昭著。

depression.definition: In psychotherapy and psychiatry, a state of mind producing serious, long-term lowering of enjoyment of life or inability to visualize a happy future.
depression.example: I used to suffer from depression, but now I'm mostly content with my life.
depression.exampleZh: 我曾經患有憂鬱症，但現在我對自己的生活很滿意。

rented.definition: To occupy premises in exchange for rent.
rented.example: I rented a house from my friend's parents for a year.
rented.exampleZh: 我從朋友的父母那裡租了房子一年。

spill.definition: A mess of something that has been dropped.
spill.example: The bruise is from a bad spill he had last week.
spill.exampleZh: 瘀傷是他上週發生的嚴重洩漏事故造成的。

password.definition: A word used to gain admittance or to gain access to information; watchword.

deceased.example: The deceased was interred in his local churchyard.
deceased.exampleZh: 死者被安葬在當地的墓地。

hardest.definition: (of material or fluid) Having a severe property; presenting difficulty.

sleepy.definition: The gum that builds up in the eye; sleep, gound.

almighty.definition: (sometimes postpositive) Unlimited in might; omnipotent; all-powerful

phrase.definition: A short written or spoken expression.
phrase.example: I wasn't sure how to phrase my condolences without sounding patronising.
phrase.exampleZh: 我不知道如何表達我的哀悼但又不顯得居高臨下。

approval.definition: An expression granting permission; an indication of agreement with a proposal; an acknowledgement that a person, thing or event meets requirements.
approval.example: I need to get an approval on this purchase order.
approval.exampleZh: 我需要獲得此採購訂單的批准。

static.definition: Interference on a broadcast signal caused by atmospheric disturbances; heard as crackles on radio, or seen as random specks on television.
static.example: The static electricity started to make our hair stand on end.
static.exampleZh: 靜電開始讓我們毛骨悚然。

cheek.definition: The soft skin on each side of the face, below the eyes; the outer surface of the sides of the oral cavity.
cheek.example: You’ve got some cheek, asking me for money!

screech.definition: A high-pitched strident or piercing sound, such as that between a moving object and any surface.

legally.definition: As permitted by law; not contrary to law.
legally.example: You can legally park in the lot on weekends without paying the fee, they won't ticket you.
legally.exampleZh: 週末你可以合法地在停車場停車，無需支付費用，他們不會給你開罰單。

ransom.definition: Money paid for the freeing of a hostage.
ransom.example: They were held for two million dollars ransom.
ransom.exampleZh: 他們被扣押了兩百萬美元的贖金

roars.definition: A long, loud, deep shout, as of rage or laughter, made with the mouth wide open.
roars.exampleZh: 獅子發出雷鳴般的吼叫。

spite.definition: Ill will or hatred toward another, accompanied with the desire to irritate, annoy, or thwart; a want to disturb or put out another; mild malice
spite.example: He was so filled with spite for his ex-wife, he could not hold down a job.
spite.exampleZh: 他對前妻充滿怨恨，以至於無法保住工作。

fortunate.example: It is a fortunate sign if the sun shines on a newly wedded couple.
fortunate.exampleZh: 如果陽光照耀在新婚夫婦身上，這是一個幸運的徵兆。

waitress.definition: A female attendant who serves customers in a restaurant, café, or similar.

rebel.definition: A person who resists an established authority, often violently

orbit.definition: A circular or elliptical path of one object around another object, particularly in astronomy and space travel.
orbit.example: The Moon's orbit around the Earth takes nearly one month to complete.
orbit.exampleZh: 月球繞地球運行一周需要近一個月的時間。

reply.definition: A written or spoken response; part of a conversation.

intel.exampleZh: 英特爾在間諜活動中很重要。

growth.definition: An increase in size, number, value, or strength.
growth.example: Growth was dampened by a softening of the global economy in 2001, but picked up in the subsequent years due to strong growth in China.
growth.exampleZh: 2001年，成長因全球經濟疲軟而受到抑制，但由於中國的強勁成長，隨後幾年有所回升。

expose.definition: To reveal, uncover, make visible, bring to light, introduce to.

sympathy.definition: A feeling of pity or sorrow for the suffering or distress of another; compassion.

daphne.definition: Any one of least 50 species of shrub in the genus Daphne of the family Thymelaeaceae, some of which are grown as ornamentals.
daphne.example: Daphne is going to open a school.
daphne.exampleZh: 達芙妮要開辦一所學校。

passenger.definition: One who rides or travels in a vehicle, but who does not operate it and is not a member of the crew.

column.definition: A solid upright structure designed usually to support a larger structure above it, such as a roof or horizontal beam, but sometimes for decoration.
column.exampleZh: 我發現這個專欄很有趣。

tales.definition: Any of several units of measure used in China and elsewhere in eastern Asia, approximately 40 grams.

exclusive.definition: Information (or an artefact) that is granted or obtained exclusively.
exclusive.example: The editor agreed to keep a lid on a potentially disastrous political scoop in exchange for an exclusive of a happier nature.
exclusive.exampleZh: 這位編輯同意對可能帶來災難性的政治獨家新聞進行保密，以換取更快樂的本性的獨家報道。

hallway.definition: A corridor in a building that connects rooms.

bowling.definition: To roll or throw (a ball) in the correct manner in cricket and similar games and sports.

confusion.definition: A lack of clarity or order.

lions.definition: A big cat, Panthera leo, native to Africa, India and formerly much of Europe.
lions.example: Tigers and lions share a common ancestor from a few million years ago.
lions.exampleZh: 老虎和獅子有著數百萬年前的共同祖先。

hooker.definition: A rod bent into a curved shape, typically with one end free and the other end secured to a rope or other attachment.
hooker.example: You're being played by a hooker.

brass.definition: A metallic alloy of copper and zinc used in many industrial and plumbing applications.
brass.example: The brass are not going to like this.
brass.exampleZh: 黃銅們不會喜歡這樣的。

shrink.example: My shrink said that he was an enabler, bad for me.
shrink.exampleZh: 我的心理醫生說他是個推動者，對我不利。

sheila.example: An accidental fire broke loose and everyone believed Sheila perished in the flames.
sheila.exampleZh: 一場意外火災發生，每個人都相信希拉在火災中喪生。

email.definition: A system for transferring messages from one computer to another, usually through a network.
email.example: He sent me his details via email.
email.exampleZh: 他透過電子郵件向我發送了他的詳細資料。

sworn.definition: To take an oath, to promise.
sworn.example: His sworn statement convinced the judge.
sworn.exampleZh: 他的宣誓陳述說服了法官。

tasty.definition: Having a pleasant or satisfying flavor; delicious.
tasty.example: You could make this tasty meal for breakfast.
tasty.exampleZh: 你可以做這頓美味的早餐。

forty.definition: A bottle of beer containing forty fluid ounces.

rarely.definition: Not occurring at a regular interval; seldom; not often.
rarely.example: Rarely do you ever find an eagle this far up the river.
rarely.exampleZh: 你很少能在河上游這麼遠的地方找到一隻鷹。

chemistry.definition: The branch of natural science that deals with the composition and constitution of substances and the changes that they undergo as a consequence of alterations in the constitution of their molecules.

phoenix.definition: A mythological bird, said to be the only one of its kind, which lives for 500 years and then dies by burning to ashes on a pyre of its own making, ignited by the sun. It then arises anew from the ashes.
phoenix.example: Astronomers believe planets might form in this dead star's disk, like the mythical Phoenix rising up out of the ashes.
phoenix.exampleZh: 天文學家相信行星可能會在這顆死亡恆星的圓盤中形成，就像神話中的鳳凰從灰燼中崛起一樣。

companion.definition: A friend, acquaintance, or partner; someone with whom one spends time or keeps company
companion.example: His dog has been his trusted companion for the last five years.
companion.exampleZh: 過去五年來，他的狗一直是他值得信賴的伴侶。

forgiven.definition: To pardon; to waive any negative feeling or desire for punishment, retribution, or compensation.
forgiven.exampleZh: 沒有什麼是可以原諒的。

butterfly.definition: A flying insect of the order Lepidoptera, distinguished from moths by their diurnal activity and generally brighter colouring.

lottery.definition: A scheme for the distribution of prizes by lot or chance, especially a gaming scheme in which one or more tickets bearing particular numbers draw prizes, the other tickets are blanks.

dialogue.definition: A conversation or other form of discourse between two or more individuals.
dialogue.example: Bill and Melinda maintained a dialogue via email over the course of their long-distance relationship.
dialogue.exampleZh: 比爾和梅琳達在遠距離交往過程中透過電子郵件保持對話。

tremendous.example: Van Beethoven's ninth symphony is a tremendous piece of music.
tremendous.exampleZh: 馮貝多芬的第九號交響曲是一部偉大的音樂作品。

pervert.definition: One who has been perverted; one who has turned to error; one who has turned to a twisted sense of values or morals.

establish.definition: To make stable or firm; to confirm.

eager.example: I was eager to show my teacher how much I'd learned over the holidays.
eager.exampleZh: 我渴望向老師展示我在假期裡學到了多少東西。

allergic.definition: A person with a tendency to having allergies; a person with multiple or severe allergies.

dearest.example: Tom is our nearest and dearest friend.
dearest.exampleZh: 湯姆是我們最親密的朋友。

logic.definition: A method of human thought that involves thinking in a linear, step-by-step manner about how a problem can be solved. Logic is the basis of many principles including the scientific method.
logic.example: It's hard to work out his system of logic.
logic.exampleZh: 很難弄清楚他的邏輯系統。

majority.definition: More than half (50%) of some group.
majority.example: The majority agreed that the new proposal was the best.
majority.exampleZh: 大多數人同意新提案是最好的。

gratitude.exampleZh: 雅尼應該表現出更多的感激之情。

essential.example: Don’t mind him being grumpy. That’s the essential Fred.
essential.exampleZh: 別介意他脾氣暴躁。這就是弗雷德的本質。

journal.definition: A diary or daily record of a person, organization, vessel etc.; daybook.

climate.definition: An area of the earth's surface between two parallels of latitude.
climate.example: Industries that require a lot of fossil fuels are unlikely to be popular in the current political climate.
climate.exampleZh: 在當前的政治氣候下，需要大量化石燃料的產業不太可能受到歡迎。

administration.definition: The act of administering; government of public affairs; the service rendered, or duties assumed, in conducting affairs; the conducting of any office or employment; direction.

precise.definition: (NNES or European Union documents) To make or render precise; to specify.

capacity.definition: The ability to hold, receive or absorb
capacity.example: It was hauling a capacity load.

instrument.definition: A device used to produce music.
instrument.example: The violinist was a master of her instrument.
instrument.exampleZh: 這位小提琴家是她的樂器大師。

fries.definition: (usually in the plural, fries) A fried strip of potato.

explore.definition: An exploration; a tour of a place to see what it is like.

survivor.definition: One who survives, especially one who survives a traumatic experience.
survivor.example: From this moment on, I choose to live not as a victim, but as a survivor.
survivor.exampleZh: 從這一刻起，我選擇不再以受害者的身分生活，而是以倖存者的身分生活。

versus.example: It is the Packers versus the Steelers in the Super Bowl.
versus.exampleZh: 這是超級盃比賽中包裝工隊對上鋼人隊的比賽。

ruled.definition: To regulate, be in charge of, make decisions for, reign over.

madison.definition: A two-man track cycling event in which partners take turns to race round the track a number of times, and then must exchange places with a partner by means of a hand sling.
madison.example: Madison Square Garden is the home of the New York Knicks.
madison.exampleZh: 麥迪遜廣場花園是紐約尼克隊的主場。

clarence.definition: A kind of carriage popular in the 19th century; a four-wheeled horse-driven vehicle with a glass front and room for four passengers.
clarence.example: Clarence Thomas and Samuel Alito are compromised and should be removed from office.
clarence.exampleZh: 克拉倫斯·托馬斯和塞繆爾·阿利托受到了損害，應該被解職。

archer.definition: One who shoots an arrow from a bow or a bolt from a crossbow.
archer.example: The archer drew back his bowstring and released the arrow with deadly precision.
archer.exampleZh: 弓箭手拉開弓弦，以致命的精準射出箭。

bicycle.definition: A vehicle that has two wheels, one behind the other, a steering handle, and a saddle seat or seats and is usually propelled by the action of a rider’s feet upon pedals.

wooden.example: On a recent windy day, hundreds of visitors climbed wooden stairs to take pictures in front of the glacier.
wooden.exampleZh: 最近的一個大風天，數百名遊客爬上木樓梯在冰川前拍照。

innocence.definition: Absence of responsibility for a crime, tort, etc.
innocence.example: Her attorney managed to convince the jury of her innocence.
innocence.exampleZh: 她的律師設法讓陪審團相信她是無辜的。

cloth.definition: A woven fabric such as used in dressing, decorating, cleaning or other practical use.
cloth.example: He is a respected man of the cloth.
cloth.exampleZh: 他是一位受人尊敬的布衣人。

drums.definition: A percussive musical instrument spanned with a thin covering on at least one end for striking, forming an acoustic chamber, affecting what materials are used to make it; a membranophone.

assist.definition: A helpful action or an act of giving.
assist.example: The foundation gave a much needed assist to the shelter.
assist.exampleZh: 基金會為避難所提供了急需的幫助。

runway.definition: A defined, narrow section of land or an artificial structure used for access.
runway.example: The aircraft began to descend toward the runway.

facebook.definition: To use the social-networking site Facebook.
facebook.exampleZh: 稍後透過 Facebook 給我詳細資訊。

civilian.definition: A person following the pursuits of civil life, especially one who is not an active member of the armed forces.

shrimp.definition: Any of many swimming, often edible crustaceans, chiefly of the infraorder Caridea or the suborder Dendrobranchiata, with slender legs, long whiskers and a long abdomen.

championship.definition: A competition to determine a champion, especially the final of a series of competitions.
championship.example: His championship of civil rights eventually bore fruit.
championship.exampleZh: 他對民權的追求最終結出碩果。

seventh.definition: The person or thing in the seventh position.
seventh.example: I live on the seventh floor.

household.definition: Collectively, all the persons who live in a given house; a family including attendants, servants etc.; a domestic or family establishment.
household.example: a household word; a household name
household.exampleZh: 家喻戶曉的字眼；家喻戶曉的名字

float.definition: A buoyant device used to support something in water or another liquid.
float.example: Attach the float and the weight to the fishing line, above the hook.
float.exampleZh: 將浮標和重物連接到魚線上的魚鉤上方。

curiosity.definition: (uncountable) Inquisitiveness; the tendency to ask and learn about things by asking questions, investigating, or exploring.
curiosity.example: He put the strangely shaped rock in his curiosity cabinet.
curiosity.exampleZh: 他把這塊形狀奇怪的石頭放進了他的好奇櫃。

manny.example: 2006 [Perry Taylor] has popped up in supermarket tabloids as the male nanny – or "manny" – for Britney Spears, who has a 9-month-old son, Sean Preston and is pregnant. — CBS News, 9 June 2006
manny.exampleZh: 2006 年，[佩里·泰勒] 作為布蘭妮·斯皮爾斯的男保姆（或“曼尼”）出現在超市小報中，布蘭妮·斯皮爾斯有一個 9 個月大的兒子肖恩·普雷斯頓，並且已經懷孕。 — 哥倫比亞廣播公司新聞，2006 年 6 月 9 日

withdraw.definition: To pull (something) back, aside, or away.

begged.definition: To request the help of someone, often in the form of money.
begged.example: He begged on the street corner from passers-by.

forehead.definition: The part of the face above the eyebrows and below the hairline.

reservation.definition: The act of reserving, withholding or keeping back.
reservation.example: The committee authorised the reservation of funds.

greedy.definition: Having greed; consumed by selfish desires.
greedy.example: This regular expression performs a greedy match.
greedy.exampleZh: 此正規表示式執行貪婪匹配。

peanut.definition: A legume resembling a nut, the fruit of the plant Arachis hypogaea.

gamble.example: I had a gamble on the horses last weekend.

stark.example: I picked my way forlornly through the stark, sharp rocks.
stark.exampleZh: 我在荒涼、鋒利的岩石中孤獨地選擇了一條路。

autumn.definition: Traditionally the third of the four seasons, when deciduous trees lose their leaves; typically regarded as being from September 24 to December 22 in parts of the Northern Hemisphere, and the months of March, April and May in the Southern Hemisphere.

contain.example: I'm so excited, I can hardly contain myself!
contain.exampleZh: 我太興奮了，簡直無法自抑！

bracelet.definition: A band or chain worn around the wrist as jewelry/jewellery or an ornament.

gesture.definition: A motion of the limbs or body, especially one made to emphasize speech.
gesture.example: The middle-finger gesture is really a nonverbal swear.
gesture.exampleZh: 中指手勢其實是一種非語言的咒罵。

piper.definition: A musician who plays a pipe.
piper.example: Piper filed a bill to enjoin Brown from infringing two patents.
piper.exampleZh: 派珀提出法案，禁止布朗侵犯兩項專利。

receipt.definition: The act of receiving, or the fact of having been received.
receipt.example: A balance payable on receipt of the goods.
receipt.exampleZh: 收到貨物時應付的餘額。

sperm.definition: The reproductive cell or gamete of the male; a spermatozoon.
sperm.example: Studying sperm helps scientists understand fertility.
sperm.exampleZh: 研究精子有助於科學家了解生育能力。

whatsoever.example: He gave me no answer whatsoever.

savage.definition: An uncivilized or feral human; a barbarian.
savage.example: No matter how anyone might savage me, I should stay strong.
savage.exampleZh: 不管別人如何攻擊我，我都應該堅強。

ticking.definition: A strong cotton or linen fabric used to cover pillows and mattresses.

approximately.definition: Imprecise but close to in quantity or amount.

homeland.definition: The country that one regards as home.

cleaner.definition: A person whose occupation is to clean floors, windows and other things.
cleaner.exampleZh: 他致力於爭取更乾淨的辦公室。

unlikely.example: It's very unlikely that you'll be able to walk perfectly after being in a cast for six months.
unlikely.exampleZh: 打石膏六個月後，您不太可能能夠完美行走。

colony.definition: A governmental unit created on land of another country owned by colonists from a country.
colony.example: Bermuda is a crown colony of Great Britain.
colony.exampleZh: 百慕達是英國的直轄殖民地。

businessman.definition: A man in business, one who works at a commercial institution.

volume.definition: A three-dimensional measure of space that comprises a length, a width and a height. It is measured in units of cubic centimeters in metric, cubic inches or cubic feet in English measurement.
volume.example: The room is 9x12x8, so its volume is 864 cubic feet.
volume.exampleZh: 該房間的尺寸為 9x12x8，因此其體積為 864 立方英尺。

reads.definition: A reading or an act of reading, especially an actor's part of a play.

seeds.definition: A fertilized and ripened ovule, containing an embryonic plant.

beware.definition: (defective) To use caution, pay attention to (used both with and without of).

doomed.definition: To pronounce sentence or judgment on; to condemn.

offensive.example: The Marines today launched a major offensive.
offensive.exampleZh: 海軍陸戰隊今天發動了大規模攻擊。

buffalo.definition: Any of the Old World mammals of the family Bovidae, such as the Cape buffalo, Syncerus caffer, or the water buffalo Bubalus bubalis.

triple.definition: Three times or thrice the number, amount, size, etc
triple.example: I've had a hard day; make that a triple.
triple.exampleZh: 我這一天過得很辛苦；使之成為三倍。

poverty.definition: The quality or state of being poor; lack of money

palmer.definition: A pilgrim who had been to the Holy Land and who brought back a palm branch in signification; a wandering religious votary.
palmer.example: Sally Palmer is not a woman to be trifled with.
palmer.exampleZh: 莎莉·帕爾默不是一個好惹的女人。

ambition.definition: Eager or inordinate desire for some object that confers distinction, as preferment, honor, superiority, political power, or literary fame; desire to distinguish one's self from other people.
ambition.example: My son, John, wants to be a firefighter very much. He has a lot of ambition.
ambition.exampleZh: 我的兒子約翰非常想成為消防員。他有很大的野心。

armor.definition: A protective layer over a body, vehicle, or other object intended to deflect or diffuse damaging forces.
armor.exampleZh: 喬納斯摧毀了莉瑪的盔甲。

leadership.definition: The capacity of someone to lead others.

racist.definition: A person who believes in or supports racism; a person who believes that a particular race is superior to others.
racist.example: They don't allow Muslims and gays to join the club? That's racist!
racist.exampleZh: 他們不允許穆斯林和同性戀者加入俱樂部？那是種族主義！

cakes.definition: A rich, sweet dessert food, typically made of flour, sugar and eggs and baked in an oven, and often covered in icing.

ankle.definition: The skeletal joint which connects the foot with the leg; the uppermost portion of the foot and lowermost portion of the leg, which contain this skeletal joint.

nicer.exampleZh: 列昂尼德變得好一點了。

draft.definition: A current of air, usually coming into a room or vehicle.
draft.example: She took a deep draft from the bottle of water.
draft.exampleZh: 她從瓶子裡深深地喝了一口水。

portrait.definition: A painting or other picture of a person, especially the head and shoulders.
portrait.example: The author painted a good portrait of urban life in New York in his latest book.
portrait.exampleZh: 作者在他的最新著作中很好地描繪了紐約的城市生活。

countess.definition: The wife of a count or earl.
countess.example: Elizabeth Millicent Leveson-Gower is 24th Countess of Sutherland; her son will be the 25th Earl.
countess.exampleZh: 伊莉莎白·米利森特·萊維森-高爾 (Elizabeth Millicent Leveson-Gower) 是薩瑟蘭第 24 代伯爵夫人；她的兒子將成為第25任伯爵。

willy.example: You can't just walk out willy-nilly!

learnt.definition: To acquire, or attempt to acquire knowledge or an ability to do something.
learnt.exampleZh: 她很容易就學會了一切。

reserve.example: We reserve the right to make modifications.
reserve.exampleZh: 我們保留進行修改的權利。

logical.definition: In agreement with the principles of logic.
logical.example: Logical memory appears contiguous to an application program, but may well be stored on several physical devices, including in RAM and on hard-disks, as determined by the operating system.
logical.exampleZh: 邏輯記憶體看起來與應用程式相鄰，但很可能儲存在多個實體設備上，包括 RAM 和硬碟，具體由作業系統決定。

umbrella.definition: Cloth-covered frame used for protection against rain or sun.
umbrella.example: Quick, grab that umbrella before you get rained on!
umbrella.exampleZh: 快點，在被雨淋到之前，帶上雨傘吧！

cancelled.definition: To cross out something with lines etc.
cancelled.example: He cancelled his order on their website.
cancelled.exampleZh: 他在他們的網站上取消了訂單。

eliminate.definition: To completely remove, get rid of, put an end to.

fanny.example: Her dress was so short you could nearly see her fanny.
fanny.exampleZh: 她的裙子太短了，幾乎可以看到她的腰部。

independence.definition: The state or quality of being independent; freedom from dependence; exemption from reliance on, or control by others; self-subsistence or maintenance; direction of one's own affairs without interference.

medium.definition: (plural media or mediums) The chemistry of the surrounding environment, e.g. solid, liquid, gas, vacuum, or a specific substance such as a solvent.

upbeat.definition: An unaccented beat at the start of a musical phrase.
upbeat.example: The conductor said: I give you three beats for nothing and then you come in on the upbeat.
upbeat.exampleZh: 指揮說：我白給你三拍，然後你就以快樂的節奏進來。

messy.definition: (of a place, situation, person, etc) In a disorderly state; chaotic; disorderly.
messy.example: Jim ran his fingers through his messy brown hair.
messy.exampleZh: 吉姆用手指梳理著凌亂的棕色頭髮。

seated.definition: To put an object into a place where it will rest; to fix; to set firm.

fletcher.definition: One who fletches or feathers arrows.
fletcher.example: In the past, Fletcher Fuel was approached by Japanese companies.
fletcher.exampleZh: 過去，日本公司曾與 Fletcher Fuel 接洽。

spoon.definition: An implement for eating or serving; a scooped utensil whose long handle is straight, in contrast to a ladle.

tucker.definition: One who or that which tucks.
tucker.example: Tucker Carlson is a sociopath with an audience of sociopaths.
tucker.exampleZh: 塔克·卡爾森是個反社會者，他的觀眾也是一群反社會者。

involve.definition: To roll or fold up; to wind round; to entwine.
involve.example: to involve in darkness or obscurity

earthquake.definition: A shaking of the ground, caused by volcanic activity or movement around geologic faults.

spectacular.exampleZh: 跳傘運動員的表演非常精彩。
