sailing.definition: To be impelled or driven forward by the action of wind upon sails, as a ship on water; to be impelled on a body of water by steam or other power.

breach.definition: A gap or opening made by breaking or battering, as in a wall, fortification or levee / embankment; the space between the parts of a solid body rent by violence
breach.example: A clear breach is when the waves roll over the vessel without breaking. A clean breach is when everything on deck is swept away.
breach.exampleZh: 明顯的破裂是指波浪翻過船隻而沒有破裂。乾淨的突破是指甲板上的所有東西都被沖走。

indicate.definition: To point out; to discover; to direct to a knowledge of; to show; to make known.
indicate.example: The guard blew his whistle to indicate imminent departure.
indicate.exampleZh: 警衛吹響了哨子，示意即將離開。

reliable.definition: Something or someone reliable or dependable

sophisticated.definition: To make less natural or innocent.

swamp.definition: A piece of wet, spongy land; low ground saturated with water; soft, wet ground which may have a growth of certain kinds of trees, but is unfit for agricultural or pastoral purposes.

compound.definition: An enclosure within which workers, prisoners, or soldiers are confined
compound.example: Prioritize compound exercises for maximum efficiency.
compound.exampleZh: 優先考慮複合練習以獲得最大效率。

revolutionary.definition: A revolutionist; a person who revolts.

pursue.definition: To follow urgently, originally with intent to capture or harm; to chase.

dreamt.definition: To see imaginary events in one's mind while sleeping.

depth.definition: The vertical distance below a surface; the degree to which something is deep
depth.example: Measure the depth of the water in this part of the bay.
depth.exampleZh: 測量海灣這一部分的水深。

tribal.definition: A design or image that has been influenced by indigenous peoples; especially such a tattoo.
tribal.exampleZh: 透過部落法維持社會秩序

occur.example: The liftoff will occur in exactly twelve seconds.
occur.exampleZh: 升空將在十二秒內完成。

camping.definition: To live in a tent or similar temporary accommodation.

assassin.definition: A member of the Nizari Ismaili Muslim community of the Alamut Period

fulfill.definition: To satisfy, carry out, bring to completion (an obligation, a requirement, etc.).
fulfill.example: You made a promise, son, and now you must fulfill it.
fulfill.exampleZh: 兒子，你曾許下諾言，現在你必須兌現。

annual.definition: An annual publication; a book, periodical, journal, report, comic book, yearbook, etc., which is published serially once a year, which may or may not be in addition to regular weekly or monthly publication.
annual.example: Boris went on annual camping trips.
annual.exampleZh: 鮑里斯每年都會去露營。

reunion.definition: The process or act of reuniting.
reunion.example: It was a tearful and heartfelt reunion as the trapped miners finally saw their dear loved ones again.
reunion.exampleZh: 當被困礦工們終於再次見到親人時，這是一次令人熱淚盈眶的團聚。

torch.definition: A stick with a flame on one end, used chiefly as a light source; a similarly shaped implement with a replaceable supply of flammable material.

stall.definition: A compartment for a single animal in a stable or cattle shed.

howling.example: The howling of wolves is haunting at night.

resting.definition: To cease from action, motion, work, or performance of any kind; stop; desist; be without motion.

whereabouts.example: The whereabouts of the escaped snake are unknown.

shops.definition: An establishment that sells goods or services to the public; originally only a physical location, but now a virtual establishment as well.

morgue.definition: A supercilious or haughty attitude; arrogance.
morgue.example: Kwapil, Joseph F. (2 July 1921) "Librarian Talks of Newspaper Morgue", Fourth Estate page 5.
morgue.exampleZh: Kwapil, Joseph F.（1921 年 7 月 2 日）“報紙太平間的圖書館員談話”，第四莊園第 5 頁。

literature.definition: The body of all written works.
literature.example: However, even “literary” science fiction rarely qualifies as literature, because it treats characters as sets of traits rather than as fully realized human beings with unique life stories. —Adam Cadre, 2008
literature.exampleZh: 然而，即使是「文學」科幻小說也很少有資格成為文學，因為它將人物視為一組特徵，而不是作為具有獨特生活故事的完全實現的人類。 ——亞當‧卡德爾，2008 年

sequence.definition: A set of things next to each other in a set order; a series
sequence.example: Complete the listed tasks in sequence.
sequence.exampleZh: 按順序完成列出的任務。

congratulate.definition: To express one’s sympathetic pleasure or joy to the person(s) it is felt for.
congratulate.example: Remind me to congratulate Dave and Lisa on their wedding.
congratulate.exampleZh: 提醒我祝賀戴夫和麗莎的婚禮。

smack.definition: A distinct flavor, especially if slight.
smack.example: rice pudding with a smack of cinnamon

tolerate.example: I can tolerate working on Saturdays, but not on Sundays.
tolerate.exampleZh: 我可以忍受週六工作，但不能忍受週日工作。

transmission.definition: The act of transmitting, e.g. data or electric power.
transmission.example: Do condoms always prevent HIV transmission?
transmission.exampleZh: 保險套真的能預防愛滋病毒傳播嗎？

anxiety.definition: An unpleasant state of mental uneasiness, nervousness, apprehension and obsession or concern about some uncertain event.

welfare.definition: Health, safety, happiness and prosperity; well-being in any respect.

locks.definition: Something used for fastening, which can only be opened with a key or combination.

choir.definition: Singing group; group of people who sing together; company of people who are trained to sing together.
choir.example: The church choir practices Thursday nights.
choir.exampleZh: 教堂唱詩班週四晚上練習。

nickname.definition: A familiar, invented given name for a person or thing used instead of the actual name of the person or thing.
nickname.exampleZh: 唐納德的綽號當之無愧。

equally.definition: (manner) In an equal manner; in equal shares or proportion; with equal and impartial justice; evenly

lipstick.example: Are you wearing any lipstick today?

stunt.definition: A daring or dangerous feat, often involving the display of gymnastic skills.
stunt.exampleZh: 那真是一個驚人的特技。

hesitate.definition: To stop or pause respecting decision or action; to be in suspense or uncertainty as to a determination.
hesitate.example: He hesitated whether to accept the offer or not; men often hesitate in forming a judgment.
hesitate.exampleZh: 他猶豫是否要接受這個提議；人們在做出判斷時常常猶豫不決。

hobby.definition: An activity that one enjoys doing in one's spare time.
hobby.example: I like to collect stamps from different countries as a hobby.
hobby.exampleZh: 我喜歡收集不同國家的郵票作為一種嗜好。

fragile.definition: Easily broken or destroyed, and thus often of subtle or intricate structure.
fragile.example: He is a very fragile person and gets easily depressed.
fragile.exampleZh: 他是一個非常脆弱的人，很容易感到沮喪。

hatch.definition: A horizontal door in a floor or ceiling.
hatch.example: The cook passed the dishes through the serving hatch.
hatch.exampleZh: 廚師將菜餚送入上菜口。

persuade.definition: To successfully convince (someone) to agree to, accept, or do something, usually through reasoning and verbal influence.
persuade.example: That salesman was able to persuade me into buying this bottle of lotion.
persuade.exampleZh: 那個推銷員說服我買了這瓶乳液。

popped.definition: To make a pop, or sharp, quick sound.
popped.example: The muskets popped away on all sides.

fiction.definition: Literary type using invented or imaginative writing, instead of real facts, usually written as prose.
fiction.example: I am a great reader of fiction.

ruins.definition: (sometimes in the plural) The remains of a destroyed or dilapidated construction, such as a house or castle.

sadly.example: “Only when we climb up can we see it,” he said sadly.
sadly.exampleZh: 「只有爬上去才能看到。」他悲傷地說。

industrial.example: I wish they'd play more industrial in this club.
industrial.exampleZh: 我希望他們在這個俱樂部打得更工業化。

dolls.definition: A toy in the form of a human.

labour.definition: Effort expended on a particular task; toil, work.
labour.example: I think we've all got the idea. There's no need to labour the point.
labour.exampleZh: 我想我們都明白了。沒有必要費力說明這一點。

salute.definition: An utterance or gesture expressing greeting or honor towards someone, now especially a formal, non-verbal gesture made with the arms or hands in any of various specific positions.
salute.example: The soldiers greeted the dignitaries with a crisp salute.
salute.exampleZh: 士兵們以清脆的敬禮迎接貴賓。

musician.definition: A composer, conductor, or performer of music; specifically, a person who sings and/or plays a musical instrument as a hobby, an occupation, or a profession.

horny.definition: Hard or bony, like an animal's horn.
horny.example: That girl makes me feel horny.
horny.exampleZh: 那個女孩讓我感到飢渴。

calendar.definition: Any system by which time is divided into days, weeks, months, and years.

regard.example: He is held in great regard in Whitehall.
regard.exampleZh: 他在白廳受到高度尊重。

inaudible.definition: Unable to be heard or not loud enough to be heard.
inaudible.example: Dogs can hear this whistle, but for people it is inaudible.
inaudible.exampleZh: 狗狗可以聽到這種哨聲，但對人來說卻聽不見。

barbecue.definition: A fireplace or pit for grilling food, typically used outdoors and traditionally employing hot charcoal as the heating medium.
barbecue.example: We cooked our food on the barbecue.
barbecue.exampleZh: 我們在燒烤上烹飪食物

suitable.definition: Having sufficient or the required properties for a certain purpose or task; appropriate to a certain occasion.

balcony.definition: An accessible structure extending from a building, especially outside a window.
balcony.exampleZh: 伊戈爾檢查了臥室陽台。

strongly.definition: In a strong or powerful manner.
strongly.example: In the third race, Renowned Blaze finished strongly to win, paying sixteen dollars.
strongly.exampleZh: 在第三場比賽中，Renknown Blaze 強勢獲勝，支付了 16 美元。

corpse.exampleZh: 艾德蒙多發現了一具腐爛的屍體。

belligerent.example: That pot-bellied, belligerent green ogre is back for yet another animated adventure.
belligerent.exampleZh: 那個大腹便便、好戰的綠色食人魔又回來了，開始了另一場動畫冒險。

oblique.definition: Not erect or perpendicular; neither parallel to, nor at right angles from, the base; slanting; inclined.
oblique.example: The internal oblique muscle is located beneath the external oblique muscle in the abdominal region of the body.
oblique.exampleZh: 內斜肌位於身體腹部區域的外斜肌下方。

blight.definition: Any of many plant diseases causing damage to, or the death of, leaves, fruit or other parts.
blight.example: A blight that turned perfectly healthy trees into dead ones.
blight.exampleZh: 一種將原本健康的樹木變成死樹的枯萎病。

encumbrance.definition: Something that encumbers; a burden that must be carried.
encumbrance.example: Since the temperature has warmed, my coat has become an encumbrance.
encumbrance.exampleZh: 氣溫變暖了，我的外套成了累贅。

hypothetical.example: Let's not worry about hypothetical situations.
hypothetical.exampleZh: 我們不必擔心假設的情況。

parable.definition: A short narrative illustrating a lesson (usually religious/moral) by comparison or analogy.
parable.example: The parable of the talents is one of my favorites.
parable.exampleZh: 人才的寓言是我最喜歡的寓言之一。

echo.definition: A reflected sound that is heard again by its initial observer.

blitz.definition: To attack quickly or suddenly, as by an air raid or similar action.
blitz.example: The company is planning an advertising blitz for the new product.
blitz.exampleZh: 該公司正在計劃對新產品進行廣告攻勢。

brandish.definition: To move or swing a weapon back and forth, particularly if demonstrating anger, threat or skill.
brandish.example: You dare brandish your weapons in front of your king?
brandish.exampleZh: 你敢在你的國王面前揮舞你的武器嗎？

repose.definition: To lie at rest; to rest.
repose.example: At times also they would repose side by side under a goatskin.
repose.exampleZh: 有時，他們也會並排躺在山羊皮下。

accrue.example: Interest will begin to accrue after the first year.
accrue.exampleZh: 第一年後將開始累積利息。

conjecture.definition: To guess; to venture an unproven idea.
conjecture.example: Tom wants to win a Nobel Prize for proving Brocard's conjecture.
conjecture.exampleZh: 湯姆希望因證明布羅卡德猜想而獲得諾貝爾獎。

diffuse.definition: To spread over or through as in air, water, or other matter, especially by fluid motion or passive means.
diffuse.example: There were no galaxies or even stars, only diffuse gas.
diffuse.exampleZh: 沒有星系，甚至沒有恆星，只有瀰漫的氣體。

succumb.definition: To yield to an overpowering force or overwhelming desire.

encompass.definition: To form a circle around; to encircle.
encompass.example: Writing cannot express all words, words cannot encompass all ideas.
encompass.exampleZh: 文字無法表達所有的文字，文字也無法包含所有的想法。

frolic.definition: To make merry; to have fun; to romp; to behave playfully and uninhibitedly.
frolic.example: The children frolic in the meadow, chasing butterflies.
frolic.exampleZh: 孩子們在草地上嬉戲，追逐蝴蝶。

mesh.definition: To connect together by interlocking, as gears do.
mesh.example: The demolition site is covered with plastic mesh to keep down the dust.
mesh.exampleZh: 拆除現場用塑膠網覆蓋以防塵。

fluctuate.example: Vegetable prices fluctuate according to the season.

fluctuation.definition: A motion like that of waves; a moving in this and that direction.
fluctuation.example: The fluctuation in prices caused a financial crisis.
fluctuation.exampleZh: 物價波動引發了金融危機。

connotation.definition: A meaning of a word or phrase that is suggested or implied, as opposed to a denotation, or literal meaning. A characteristic of words or phrases, or of the contexts that words and phrases are used in.
connotation.example: Pitbulls often get this connotation of being the dangerous dogs.
connotation.exampleZh: 鬥牛犬通常被認為是危險的狗。

induce.definition: To lead by persuasion or influence; incite or prevail upon.

unnerving.definition: To deprive of nerve, force, or strength; to weaken; to enfeeble.
unnerving.example: Things like that can be unnerving.
unnerving.exampleZh: 類似的事情可能會令人不安。

adept.definition: Well skilled; completely versed; thoroughly proficient
adept.example: Sci-fi readers are adept at hypothetical scenarios.
adept.exampleZh: 科幻讀者擅長假設場景。

loiter.definition: To stand about without any aim or purpose; to stand about idly.
loiter.example: They don't work, they just loiter in the streets.
loiter.exampleZh: 他們不工作，只是在街上閒逛。

topple.definition: To push, throw over, overturn or overthrow something
topple.exampleZh: 我們將推翻這個獨裁政權。

merit.definition: A claim to commendation or a reward.
merit.example: Show gratitude to those who merit it.

clinch.example: Now is the time to clinch the deal.
clinch.exampleZh: 現在是達成協議的時候了。

disseminate.definition: To sow and scatter principles, ideas, opinions, etc, or concrete things, for growth and propagation, like seeds.
disseminate.example: If you're going to disseminate ideas, make sure they're worthwhile.
disseminate.exampleZh: 如果您要傳播想法，請確保它們是有價值的。

bereft.definition: (of a person) Pained by the loss of someone.
bereft.example: Men who were bereft of reason conducted the war.
bereft.exampleZh: 戰爭是由喪失理智的人發動的。

ebb.example: She has been with me through the ebb and flow of my fortunes.
ebb.exampleZh: 她一直陪伴著我，經歷了我命運的起起落落。

idiosyncrasy.definition: A behavior or way of thinking that is characteristic of a person.

interrogate.definition: To question or quiz, especially in a thorough and/or aggressive manner

skim.definition: To pass lightly; to glide along in an even, smooth course; to glide along near the surface.

flit.definition: To move about rapidly and nimbly.
flit.example: The fairy would gracefully flit from flower to flower, gathering nectar and dewdrops for her magic.
flit.exampleZh: 仙女會優雅地從一朵花飛到另一朵花，為她的魔法收集花蜜和露珠。

huddle.example: Penguins often huddle together to keep warm.
huddle.exampleZh: 企鵝經常擠在一起取暖。

axiom.definition: A seemingly self-evident or necessary truth which is based on assumption; a principle or proposition which cannot actually be proved or disproved.
axiom.example: It has long been an axiom of mine that the little things are infinitely the most important.
axiom.exampleZh: 長期以來，我的一條格言是：小事無限重要。

attrition.definition: Wearing or grinding down by friction.
attrition.example: We're fighting a war of attrition with armies of robots.
attrition.exampleZh: 我們正在與機器人大軍進行消耗戰。

reissue.example: Can you reissue them right away?

adhere.definition: To stick fast or cleave, as a glutinous substance does; to become joined or united.
adhere.exampleZh: 阿爾及利亞人信奉遜尼派伊斯蘭教。

proscribe.example: The church does not proscribe monarchy and prescribe democracy, or proscribe democracy and prescribe monarchy.
proscribe.exampleZh: 教會不禁止君主製而規定民主制，或禁止民主製而規定君主制。

prise.definition: To force (open) with a lever; to pry.
prise.example: They were unable to prise open the box because it was too rusty.
prise.exampleZh: 他們無法撬開盒子，因為它太生鏽了。

hull.definition: The outer covering of a fruit or seed.
hull.example: The ship hull is divided into watertight compartments.

glint.example: She was a fine tall woman, black and quick and fierce, with a proud way of carrying her head, and a glint from her eye like a spark from a flint.
glint.exampleZh: 她是個高挑的漂亮女人，皮膚黝黑，敏捷而兇猛，驕傲地昂著頭，眼睛閃爍著火石的火花。

venture.definition: A risky or daring undertaking or journey.

commodity.definition: Anything movable (a good) that is bought and sold.
commodity.example: The commodity exchange attracts foreign investors.
commodity.exampleZh: 商品交易所吸引了外國投資者。

qualm.definition: A feeling of apprehension, doubt, fear etc.
qualm.example: He did not have any qualm of conscience.

cloister.definition: A covered walk with an open colonnade on one side, running along the walls of buildings that face a quadrangle; especially:
cloister.example: What's the difference between a cloister and a church?
cloister.exampleZh: 迴廊和教會有什麼差別？

totter.definition: To walk, move or stand unsteadily or falteringly; threatening to fall.
totter.example: I wish I were a kid again so I could play on a teeter-totter.
totter.exampleZh: 我希望我能回到小時候，這樣我就可以玩蹺蹺板了。

feign.definition: To make a false show or pretence of; to counterfeit or simulate.

shoal.definition: A sandbank or sandbar creating a shallow.
shoal.example: If there is a shoal or a stone under water again, it should indicate them on the map.
shoal.exampleZh: 若水下又出現淺灘或石頭，應在地圖上標示。

collide.definition: To impact directly, especially if violent.

burgeoning.definition: That buds, grows or expands; developing.
burgeoning.example: Back in the 1970s, Matoub was already a burgeoning start.
burgeoning.exampleZh: 早在 1970 年代，Matoub 就已經是一個蓬勃發展的開始。

hamper.example: Large patches of fog continued to hamper visibility for motorists.
hamper.exampleZh: 大片大霧持續阻礙駕駛者的能見度。

assimilate.definition: To incorporate nutrients into the body, especially after digestion.
assimilate.example: The US attempted to assimilate Native Americans.
assimilate.exampleZh: 美國試圖同化美洲原住民。

coax.example: Anna cautiously tried to coax the grasshopper toward the open window.
coax.exampleZh: 安娜小心翼翼地試圖將蚱蜢引向開著的窗戶。

squint.definition: To look with the eyes partly closed, as in bright sunlight, or as a threatening expression.

etching.definition: The art of producing an image from a metal plate into which an image or text has been etched with acid.
etching.example: The Crayon Etching technique is like revealing a rainbow from gloom.
etching.exampleZh: 蠟筆蝕刻技術就像從陰暗中露出彩虹。

skew.definition: To form or shape in an oblique way; to cause to take an oblique position.
skew.example: Despite Republican propaganda to the contrary, people making under $50,000 per year still skew overwhelmingly Democratic.
skew.exampleZh: 儘管共和黨的宣傳與此相反，年收入低於 5 萬美元的人仍然絕大多數傾向民主黨。

subsidy.definition: Financial support or assistance, such as a grant.
subsidy.example: My financial situation is supported by a state subsidy.
subsidy.exampleZh: 我的經濟狀況是靠國家補貼支撐的。

hinder.definition: To make difficult to accomplish; to act as an obstacle; to frustrate.
hinder.example: Patronizing attitudes can hinder productive conversations.
hinder.exampleZh: 居高臨下的態度會阻礙有成效的對話。

attuned.definition: (of a musical instrument) Tuned to the correct pitch.
attuned.example: Ever since the beginning of COVID-19, I am more attuned to my environment.
attuned.exampleZh: 自從 COVID-19 爆發以來，我更加適應我的環境。

surge.definition: To rush, flood, or increase suddenly.
surge.example: We're witnessing a surge in Algerian nationalism.
surge.exampleZh: 我們目睹了阿爾及利亞民族主義的高漲。

reverberate.definition: To ring or sound with many echos.
reverberate.example: The loud sounds of sawing, drilling and scraping reverberate around the room.
reverberate.exampleZh: 鋸切、鑽孔和刮削的響亮聲音在房間裡迴盪。

abdominal.definition: A fish of the order Abdominales.

resource.definition: Something that one uses to achieve an objective, e.g. raw materials or personnel.

scoot.example: Can you scoot over and make room for one more person?
scoot.exampleZh: 你能跑過去騰出地方再容納一個人嗎？

espionage.definition: The act or process of learning secret information through clandestine means.
espionage.exampleZh: 間諜活動揭示了看不見的戰鬥。

wrestler.exampleZh: 摔角手用刀割傷了自己。

loony.definition: An insane or very foolish person.
loony.example: If you carry on like this, they'll send you to the loony bin.
loony.exampleZh: 如果你再這樣下去，他們會把你送進瘋人院。

dialect.definition: A variety of a language that is characteristic of a particular area, community or social group, differing from other varieties of the same language in relatively minor ways as regards grammar, phonology, and lexicon.

tsunami.definition: A very large and destructive wave, generally caused by a tremendous disturbance in the ocean, such as an undersea earthquake or volcanic eruption. Tsunami are usually a series of waves, or wave train.

counsellor.definition: A professional who counsels people, especially on personal problems.
counsellor.example: Tom asked his guidance counsellor how to apply to college.
counsellor.exampleZh: 湯姆問他的輔導員如何申請大學。

incorrect.example: He gave an incorrect answer to a simple question.
incorrect.exampleZh: 他對一個簡單的問題給了錯誤的答案。

locket.definition: A pendant that opens to reveal a space used for storing a photograph or other small item.
locket.example: Mary put the silver locket around her neck.
locket.exampleZh: 瑪麗把銀項鍊戴在脖子上。

sorcerer.definition: A magician or wizard, sometimes specifically male.
sorcerer.example: Donald saved Jessica from an evil sorcerer.
sorcerer.exampleZh: 唐納德從邪惡巫師手中救了潔西卡。

risotto.definition: An Italian savoury dish made with rice and other ingredients.
risotto.exampleZh: 你喜歡義大利調味飯嗎？

agricultural.definition: A product or commodity from agriculture.

marital.exampleZh: 未登記的婚姻關係是非法的。

starvation.definition: A condition of severe suffering due to a lack of nutrition.

wedge.definition: One of the simple machines; a piece of material, such as metal or wood, thick at one edge and tapered to a thin edge at the other for insertion in a narrow crevice, used for splitting, tightening, securing, or levering.
wedge.example: Stick a wedge under the door, will you? It keeps blowing shut.
wedge.exampleZh: 在門下面塞個楔子，可以嗎？它一直被風吹著關閉。

sanity.example: Ziri heard something that questioned his sanity.
sanity.exampleZh: 茲裡聽到了一些質疑他神智的事。

bedside.definition: A position at the side of one's bed.
bedside.example: Mary turned off her bedside lamp.

tribunal.definition: An assembly including one or more judges to conduct judicial business; a court of law.
tribunal.example: The process of creating a “tribunal for Putin” has been launched.
tribunal.exampleZh: 創建「普丁法庭」的進程已經啟動。

automated.definition: To replace or enhance human labor with machines.
automated.example: Companies prefer to use automated systems.
automated.exampleZh: 公司更喜歡使用自動化系統。

kappa.definition: The tenth letter of the Greek alphabet.
kappa.example: At school I had enjoyed reading Japanese literature in English translation, in particular Soseki's I am a Cat and Kokoro, and Akutagawa's The Nose and Kappa.
kappa.exampleZh: 在學校時，我很喜歡閱讀英譯本的日本文學，特別是漱石的《我是貓》和《心》，以及芥川的《鼻子》和《河童》。

goblin.definition: One of various hostile supernatural creatures, now especially (fantasy literature) a malevolent and grotesque diminutive humanoid.
goblin.example: The goblin army will attack at midnight.
goblin.exampleZh: 半夜妖精大軍將發動攻擊。

disclose.example: Lukas refused to disclose that information.
disclose.exampleZh: 盧卡斯拒絕透露該訊息。

politely.example: If pressed about a serious accusation like rape, you can politely reply that you can’t answer questions pertaining to such an allegation without legal representation and wish to remain silent. ― G.S. Luthra, New Jersey Beach Beat Down Shows Why It’s Foolish To Resist Police
politely.exampleZh: 如果被追問強姦等嚴重指控，您可以禮貌地回答說，如果沒有法律代表，您無法回答與此類指控相關的問題，並且希望保持沉默。 ——G.S. Luthra，新澤西州海灘毆打事件顯示抵抗警察是愚蠢的

preoccupied.definition: Concerned with something else; distracted; giving one's attention elsewhere.
preoccupied.example: I was preoccupied with a deadline at work, and I forgot his birthday.
preoccupied.exampleZh: 我全神貫注於工作的最後期限，忘了他的生日。

seaside.definition: The area by and around the sea; including the beach, promenade or cliffs
seaside.exampleZh: 阿爾及利亞擁有令人驚嘆的海濱景色。

graceful.definition: Having or showing grace in movement, shape, or proportion.

lavender.definition: Any of a group of European plants, genus, Lavandula, of the mint family.
lavender.example: Nothing grew there besides wild lavender.
lavender.exampleZh: 那裡除了野生薰衣草外什麼也沒長。

simulation.definition: Something that simulates a system or environment in order to predict actual behaviour.
simulation.example: The most reliable simulation predicts that the hurricane will turn north.
simulation.exampleZh: 最可靠的模擬預測颶風將轉向北方。

stretcher.definition: One who, or that which, stretches.
stretcher.example: The stretcher was shoved into the ambulance.

groovy.definition: Of, pertaining to, or having grooves.
groovy.example: The back of the tile was groovy so that it could hold the adhesive compound.
groovy.exampleZh: 磁磚的背面有凹槽，可以容納黏合劑。

basin.definition: A wide bowl for washing, sometimes affixed to a wall
basin.example: Yanni needs to replace this basin mixer.
basin.exampleZh: 雅尼需要更換這款面盆龍頭。

luncheon.definition: A formal meal served in the middle of the day.
luncheon.example: I'm afraid I won't be able to make it for our luncheon appointment.
luncheon.exampleZh: 恐怕我無法參加午餐約會了。

masculine.exampleZh: 「約翰」、「保羅」和「傑克」是男性名字。

banjo.definition: A stringed musical instrument (chordophone), usually with a round body, a membrane-like soundboard and a fretted neck, played by plucking or strumming the strings.
banjo.example: I come from Alabama with my banjo on my knee...
banjo.exampleZh: 我來自阿拉巴馬州，膝上放著我的班卓琴…

hopper.definition: One who or that which hops.
hopper.example: To catch a big fish, use a hopper that jumps across the pond surface.
hopper.exampleZh: 要捕獲大魚，請使用跳過池塘表面的漏斗。

sinful.definition: Constituting a sin; being morally or religiously wrong; wicked; evil

foley.definition: The creation of sound effects, and their addition to film and TV images
foley.example: Many of the sounds heard in nature documentaries are created by foley artists.
foley.exampleZh: 自然紀錄片中聽到的許多聲音都是由擬音藝術家創作的。

cassette.definition: A small flat case containing magnetic tape on two reels, used to record and play back audio and video material.
cassette.exampleZh: 達米亞諾找到了一盤盒式磁帶。

sorting.definition: To separate items into different categories according to certain criteria that determine their sorts.
sorting.exampleZh: 阿爾及利亞需要分類設施。

sprung.definition: To weaken, as a joint, ligament, or muscle, by sudden and excessive exertion, as by wrenching; to overstrain, or stretch injuriously, but without luxation
sprung.example: His lawyer managed to get Tom sprung from prison.
sprung.exampleZh: 他的律師設法讓湯姆出獄。

prehistoric.definition: Having lasted from a remote period; having been of long duration; of great age, very old.
prehistoric.example: Jonas believed that the prehistoric pictograms depicted aliens.
prehistoric.exampleZh: 喬納斯相信史前象形文字描繪的是外星人。

renounce.example: to renounce a title to land or to a throne
renounce.exampleZh: 放棄土地或王位的所有權

howls.definition: The protracted, mournful cry of a dog or a wolf, or other like sound.

suspense.definition: The condition of being suspended; cessation for a time.
suspense.example: This story is full of suspense.

mugged.example: The children weren't interested in sitting still for a serious photo; they mugged for the camera.
mugged.exampleZh: 孩子們對安靜地坐著認真拍照不感興趣；他們喜歡安靜地坐著拍照。他們對著鏡頭搶劫。

stylish.definition: Having elegance or taste or refinement in manners or dress.

spleen.definition: In vertebrates, including humans, a ductless vascular gland, located in the left upper abdomen near the stomach, which destroys old red blood cells, removes debris from the bloodstream, acts as a reservoir of blood, and produces lymphocytes.
spleen.example: This fellow is up to something. I can feel it in my spleen.
spleen.exampleZh: 這傢伙有事。我能在我的脾臟中感覺到它。

protestant.definition: A member of any of several Christian denominations which separated from the Roman Catholic Church based on theological or political differences during the Reformation (or sometimes later).
protestant.example: 1915 November 3, decision in the case of the State of New Mexico v. Garrett, published in 1916 among the Decisions of the Department of the Interior in Cases Relating to Public Lands, volume 44 (edited by George J Hesselman), page 490: In the case of Hyacinthe Villeneuve a homestead entry had been allowed upon a tract of land that had been patented to the Santa Fe Railroad Company, whose grantees had expressed a willingness to reconvey in order that effect might be given to the equities of the homesteader, whereas in the present case the State stands in the position of a protestant.
protestant.exampleZh: 1915 年 11 月 3 日，新墨西哥州訴加勒特案的判決，發表於 1916 年內政部關於公共土地案件的判決中，第 44 卷（喬治·J·赫塞爾曼編輯），第 490 頁：願意恢復，以便對自耕農的權益產生影響，而在本案中，國家站在新教徒的立場上。

circumstantial.definition: (chiefly in the plural) Something incidental to the main subject, but of less importance.
circumstantial.exampleZh: 對於間接證據來說，這就夠了。

erection.definition: The act of building or putting up or together of something.
erection.example: The Empire State Building was once the world's tallest erection.
erection.exampleZh: 帝國大廈曾經是世界上最高的建築物。

pantry.definition: A small room, closet, or cabinet usually located in or near the kitchen, dedicated to shelf-stable food storage and/or storing kitchenware, like a larder, but smaller.
pantry.exampleZh: 我們的食品儲藏室完全空了。

flavour.definition: The quality produced by the sensation of taste or, especially, of taste and smell in combined effect.

gravel.definition: Small fragments of rock, used for laying on the beds of roads and railways, and as ballast.
gravel.example: The gravel crunched under our feet.
gravel.exampleZh: 礫石在我們腳下嘎吱作響。

raccoon.definition: A nocturnal omnivore native to North America, typically with a mixture of gray, brown, and black fur, a mask-like marking around the eyes and a striped tail; Procyon lotor.

binding.definition: To tie; to confine by any ligature.
binding.example: Just to make the cheese more binding
binding.exampleZh: 只是為了讓起司更有黏合力

invalid.definition: Not valid; not true, correct, acceptable or appropriate.
invalid.example: This invalid contract cannot be legally enforced.
invalid.exampleZh: 該無效契約不能合法執行。

touring.example: The Rolling Stones were still touring when they were in their seventies.
touring.exampleZh: 滾石樂團七十多歲時仍在巡迴演出。

spinach.definition: A particular edible plant, Spinacia oleracea, or its leaves.

ledger.definition: A book for keeping notes, especially one for keeping accounting records; a record book, a register.
ledger.example: The auditors will have a hard time deciphering this ledger.
ledger.exampleZh: 審計員將很難破解這個帳本。

quieter.definition: With little or no sound; free of disturbing noise.

majestic.definition: Having qualities of splendor or royalty.
majestic.example: My signature is meaningful, majestic and inimitable.
majestic.exampleZh: 我的簽名意味深長、雄偉且無與倫比。

historian.definition: A writer of history; a chronicler; an annalist.

insulin.definition: A polypeptide hormone that regulates carbohydrate metabolism.
insulin.example: Apart from insulin, the pancreas also produces enzymes.
insulin.exampleZh: 除了胰島素之外，胰臟還會產生酵素。

pagan.definition: A person not adhering to a main world religion; a follower of a pantheistic or nature-worshipping religion.

accommodate.definition: To render fit, suitable, or correspondent; to adapt.

colossal.definition: Extremely large or on a great scale.
colossal.example: A single puppy can make a colossal mess.
colossal.exampleZh: 一隻小狗就會造成巨大的混亂。

giraffe.definition: A ruminant, of the genus Giraffa, of the African savannah with long legs and highly elongated neck, which make it the tallest living animal; yellow fur patterned with dark spots, often in the form of a network; and two or more short, skin-covered horns, so-called; strictly speaking the horn-like projections are ossicones.

iceberg.definition: A huge mass of ocean-floating ice which has broken off a glacier or ice shelf
iceberg.example: The Titanic hit an iceberg and sank.
iceberg.exampleZh: 泰坦尼克號撞上冰山並沉沒。

renew.definition: To make (something) new again; to restore to freshness or original condition.
renew.example: I'd like to renew these three books.  Did you know that you can renew online?
renew.exampleZh: 我想續訂這三本書。  您知道可以在線上續訂嗎？

vigilante.definition: A person who considers it their own responsibility to uphold the law in their neighborhood and often does so summarily and without legal jurisdiction.
vigilante.example: A vigilante ghost is going around avenging his own brutal murder.
vigilante.exampleZh: 一名治安維持者的鬼魂四處遊蕩，為自己的殘忍謀殺報仇。

chewed.definition: To crush with the teeth by repeated closing and opening of the jaws; done to food to soften it and break it down by the action of saliva before it is swallowed.

cellular.example: I want a cellular phone, but I don't have enough money to pay for one.
cellular.exampleZh: 我想要一部手機，但我沒有足夠的錢來購買。

grammar.definition: A system of rules and principles for speaking and writing a language.

indecent.example: Jessica dressed like an indecent woman.
indecent.exampleZh: 潔西卡穿得像個不雅女人。

hutch.definition: A cage for keeping rabbits, guinea pigs, etc.
hutch.example: The boy went into the rabbit hutch.

pager.definition: A wireless telecommunications device that receives text or voice messages.

stumble.definition: A fall, trip or substantial misstep.

dispatcher.definition: A message sent quickly, as a shipment, a prompt settlement of a business, or an important official message sent by a diplomat, or military officer.
dispatcher.exampleZh: 瑪麗是一名計程車調度員。

stump.definition: The remains of something that has been cut off; especially the remains of a tree, the remains of a limb.

gator.example: The gator was coming straight at us.

apiece.definition: Each by itself; for or to each one
apiece.example: These melons cost a dollar apiece.

melts.definition: Molten material, the product of melting.

weakest.definition: Lacking in force (usually strength) or ability.
weakest.example: The weakest are the measuring stick of justice.

theatrical.definition: A stage performance, especially one by amateurs.
theatrical.example: He spoke with great enthusiasm of the theatrical performance.
theatrical.exampleZh: 他滿懷熱情地談到了戲劇表演。

distinguish.definition: To recognize someone or something as different from others based on its characteristics.

unnecessarily.definition: In an unnecessary way; not by necessity.
unnecessarily.example: He unnecessarily repeated much of what others had covered.
unnecessarily.exampleZh: 他不必要地重複了其他人已經報道過的大部分內容。

upstate.definition: The northern section of a state.
upstate.example: They went hunting in upstate New York.
upstate.exampleZh: 他們去紐約州北部打獵。

accuracy.definition: The state of being accurate; being free from mistakes, this exemption arising from carefulness; exactness; correctness
accuracy.example: The value of testimony depends on its accuracy.
accuracy.exampleZh: 證言的價值取決於其準確性。

cosmo.definition: A cosmopolitan, a cocktail containing vodka, triple sec, lime juice and cranberry juice.
cosmo.exampleZh: 我在《科斯莫》上讀到了這篇文章。

bitterness.definition: The quality of having a bitter taste.

fundraiser.definition: An event undertaken to get money by voluntary contributions for a particular activity, such as equipment for extracurricular sports programs, or to defray laboratory costs (salaries) for research of a cure for a particular disease.
fundraiser.example: This year's fundraiser will be a walkathon.
fundraiser.exampleZh: 今年的募款活動將是一場步行馬拉松。

circular.example: Your dictionary defines "brave" as "courageous", and "courageous" as "brave". That's a circular definition.
circular.exampleZh: 你的字典將“勇敢”定義為“勇敢”，將“勇敢”定義為“勇敢”。這是一個循環定義。

insolent.exampleZh: 你被解雇了，無禮的傻瓜。

pilgrim.definition: One who travels, especially on a journey to visit sites of religious significance.
pilgrim.example: Yanni is a pilgrim from Algeria.
pilgrim.exampleZh: 雅尼是一位來自阿爾及利亞的朝聖者。

handler.definition: One who handles something (especially manually) or someone.
handler.example: The handler of a weapon gets a symbolic sensation of deadly power.
handler.exampleZh: 武器的使用者會感受到致命力量的象徵性感覺。

purge.example: Sami wanted to purge the evil spirit our of Layla's body.
purge.exampleZh: 薩米想要清除蕾拉體內的邪靈。

slain.example: Our foes must all be slain.
slain.exampleZh: 我們的敵人必須全部被消滅。

firemen.definition: Someone (especially one who is male) who is skilled in the work of fighting fire.
firemen.example: The firemen quickly extinguished the blaze.
firemen.exampleZh: 消防員很快就將火撲滅。

herbal.definition: A manual of herbs and their medical uses
herbal.example: Herbal tea has a nice aroma and is good for a stuffy head.
herbal.exampleZh: 花草茶香氣宜人，對緩解頭悶有好處。

reasonably.example: They were able handle their disagreements reasonably.
reasonably.exampleZh: 他們能夠合理地處理分歧。

lawful.definition: A character having a lawful alignment.
lawful.example: Lawful money is always a land asset and can only be issued by an actual land jurisdiction government — not a corporation.
lawful.exampleZh: 合法貨幣始終是土地資產，只能由實際的土地管轄政府（而不是公司）發行。

mainstream.definition: The principal current in a flow, such as a river or flow of air
mainstream.example: His ideas were well outside the mainstream, but he presented them intelligently, and we were impressed if not convinced.
mainstream.exampleZh: 他的想法遠遠超出了主流，但他巧妙地表達了這些想法，即使不令人信服，也給我們留下了深刻的印象。

alimony.definition: A court-mandated allowance made to a former spouse by a divorced or legally separated person.
alimony.example: Ziri is behind on rent because of alimony.
alimony.exampleZh: 齊裡因為贍養費而拖欠房租。

nelly.example: Nils and Nelly walked across the mudflats to Langeoog.
nelly.exampleZh: 尼爾斯和耐莉穿過泥灘前往蘭格奧格。

uprising.definition: To rise; to get up; to appear from below the horizon.
uprising.example: The uprising failed to achieve its objectives.

cabaret.definition: Live entertainment held in a restaurant or nightclub; the genre of music associated with this form of entertainment, especially in early 20th century Europe.
cabaret.example: Tonight there is a performance of a known cabaret artist.
cabaret.exampleZh: 今晚有一位著名歌舞表演藝術家的表演。

disarm.definition: The act of depriving a person of a weapon they carry.

harmful.definition: Of a kind likely to be damaging; injurious
harmful.example: Wear a hat to protect your skin from harmful sunlight.
harmful.exampleZh: 戴帽子以保護皮膚免受有害陽光的傷害。

boulder.definition: A large mass of stone detached from the surrounding land.

manpower.definition: The total number of all available workers; the workforce.
manpower.example: Adding manpower to a late software project makes it later.
manpower.exampleZh: 在已晚的軟體專案中加入人力會使專案變得更晚。

horseback.definition: (usually with on, upon, etc.) The back of a horse.

thankfully.definition: In a thankful manner; giving thanks.
thankfully.example: I was almost late for work, but thankfully there wasn't much traffic on the roads.
thankfully.exampleZh: 我上班差點遲到，幸好路上交通不多。

recreate.definition: To give new life, energy or encouragement (to); to refresh, enliven.
recreate.example: I want to recreate my future.
recreate.exampleZh: 我想重新創造我的未來。

triad.example: Tom has the dark triad of personality traits.
triad.exampleZh: 湯姆具有黑暗人格特質。

portfolio.definition: A case for carrying papers, drawings, photographs, maps and other flat documents.
portfolio.example: I would like to introduce you to our portfolio of services.
portfolio.exampleZh: 我想向您介紹我們的服務組合。

dissolve.definition: A form of film punctuation in which there is a gradual transition from one scene to the next

robes.definition: A long loose outer garment, often signifying honorary stature.

racism.definition: Belief that there are distinct human races with inherent differences which determine their abilities, and generally that some are superior and others inferior.
racism.example: Martin Luther King spoke out against racism.
racism.exampleZh: 馬丁·路德·金公開反對種族主義。

pussycat.example: O grey pussycat, please don't purr. Grandpa might hear you.
pussycat.exampleZh: 哦，灰色的小貓咪，請不要發出咕嚕聲。爺爺可能會聽到你的聲音。

booby.example: The blue-footed booby is well named for its distinctive blue feet.
booby.exampleZh: 藍腳鰹鳥因其獨特的藍色腳而得名。

stingy.definition: Unwilling to spend, give, or share; ungenerous; mean

gymnastics.definition: A sport involving the performance of sequences of movements requiring physical strength, flexibility, and kinesthetic awareness.
gymnastics.example: Gymnastics was a significant part of the physical education curriculum.
gymnastics.exampleZh: 體操是體育課程的重要組成部分。

kaiser.definition: An emperor of a German-speaking country, particularly the Holy Roman Empire (962–1806), the Austrian Empire (1806–1918), or the German Empire (1871–1918) — often specifically Wilhelm II — or any emperor.
kaiser.example: The kaiser paid tribute to the sultan to avoid being attacked.
kaiser.exampleZh: 德皇向蘇丹致敬以避免受到攻擊。

attachment.definition: The act or process of (physically or figuratively) attaching.
attachment.example: I have such an attachment towards my fiancé!
attachment.exampleZh: 我對我的未婚夫有如此的執念！

registry.definition: A building in which things are registered or where registers are kept.
registry.example: In Germany, people can only get married at the registry office.
registry.exampleZh: 在德國，人們只能在登記處結婚。

valiant.definition: A person who acts with valor, showing hero-like characteristics in the midst of danger.
valiant.example: Her appearance presented a valiant attempt at smartness.
valiant.exampleZh: 她的外表表現出對聰明的勇敢嘗試。

crispy.definition: The well-baked fat on the surface of a piece of roasted meat.
crispy.exampleZh: 烤好的麵包捲應該是酥脆的。

trans.definition: To cross from one side to another of (gender, sex or something in that vein).

hawks.definition: A diurnal predatory bird of the family Accipitridae, smaller than an eagle.
hawks.example: It is illegal to hunt hawks or other raptors in many parts of the world.
hawks.exampleZh: 在世界許多地方捕獵鷹或其他猛禽是非法的。

unworthy.example: Boris deemed Melissa unworthy of such a responsibility.
unworthy.exampleZh: 鮑里斯認為梅莉莎不值得承擔這樣的責任。

myths.definition: A traditional story which embodies a belief regarding some fact or phenomenon of experience, and in which often the forces of nature and of the soul are personified; a sacred narrative regarding a god, a hero, the origin of the world or of a people, etc.
myths.example: Myth was the product of man's emotion and imagination, acted upon by his surroundings. (E. Clodd, Myths & Dreams (1885), 7, cited after OED)
myths.exampleZh: 神話是人類情感和想像力的產物，受到周遭環境的影響。 (E. Clodd, Myths & Dreams (1885), 7, 引自《牛津英語字典》)

stoop.definition: The staircase and landing or porch leading to the entrance of a residence.
stoop.exampleZh: 我永遠不會把自己降得那麼低。

radiant.definition: A point source from which radiation is emitted.

volleyball.definition: A game played on a rectangular court between two teams of two to six players which involves striking a ball back and forth over a net.

heinous.example: I hope they catch the person responsible for that heinous crime.
heinous.exampleZh: 我希望他們能抓住犯下這令人髮指罪行的人。

chord.definition: A harmonic set of three or more notes that is heard as if sounding simultaneously.
chord.exampleZh: 這個和弦進行聽起來很爵士。

roulette.definition: A game of chance, in which a small ball is made to move round rapidly on a circle divided off into numbered red and black spaces, the one on which it stops indicating the result of a variety of wagers permitted by the game.
roulette.example: to roulette a sheet of postage stamps

enjoyable.example: That was an enjoyable day; I had a lot of fun.
enjoyable.exampleZh: 那是愉快的一天；我玩得很開心。

torso.definition: The main part of the (human) body that extends from the neck to the groin, excluding the head and limbs.
torso.example: Ziri noticed that one of the manikins had red spots on its torso.
torso.exampleZh: 茲裡注意到其中一個人體模型的軀幹上有紅點。

softer.example: This towel feels a little bit softer.
softer.exampleZh: 這條毛巾感覺更柔軟一些。

delegation.exampleZh: 一個代表團接著一個代表團。

fangs.definition: A long, pointed canine tooth used for biting and tearing flesh
fangs.example: The vampire smiled at Edmundo and revealed her fangs.
fangs.exampleZh: 吸血鬼對著艾德蒙多微笑並露出了她的獠牙。

buggy.example: This software is so buggy that I don't know how anyone can use it!
buggy.exampleZh: 這個軟體bug太多了，不知道怎麼有人用！

uterus.definition: The womb, an organ of the female reproductive system in which the young are conceived and develop until birth.
uterus.example: How do you expect me to have children when I don't have a uterus?
uterus.exampleZh: 如果我沒有子宮，你怎麼指望我生孩子？

tally.example: What you're saying now doesn't tally with what you said earlier.
tally.exampleZh: 你現在說的和之前說的不符。

charcoal.definition: Impure carbon obtained by destructive distillation of wood or other organic matter, that is to say, heating it in the absence of oxygen.
charcoal.example: Charcoal, a substance similar to coal, is produced from wood.
charcoal.exampleZh: 木炭是一種類似煤的物質，由木材製成。

healer.definition: One who heals, especially through faith.
healer.example: Boris took Melissa to a healer.
healer.exampleZh: 鮑里斯帶梅麗莎去看治療師。

camouflage.example: Tom usually wears a camouflage jacket.
camouflage.exampleZh: 湯姆通常穿著迷彩夾克。

pronto.example: I need to finish this assignment pronto.
pronto.exampleZh: 我需要盡快完成這項作業。

mailman.definition: Someone (implied male) who delivers mail to, and/or collects mail from, residential or commercial addresses, or from public mailboxes

delusion.definition: A false belief that is resistant to confrontation with actual facts.
delusion.example: It is far better to grasp the universe as it really is than to persist in delusion, however satisfying and reassuring.
delusion.exampleZh: 掌握宇宙的本來面目比堅持妄想要好得多，無論這有多令人滿意和令人安心。

furry.definition: An animal character with human-like characteristics; most commonly refers to such characters created by members of the furry subculture.

crotch.definition: The area where something forks or branches, a ramification takes place.
crotch.example: There is a child sitting in a crotch of that tree.
crotch.exampleZh: 那棵樹的胯下坐著一個孩子。

sedan.definition: An enclosed windowed chair suitable for a single occupant, carried by at least two porters, in equal numbers in front and behind, using wooden rails that passed through metal brackets on the sides of the chair.
sedan.example: Tom's two-door sedan is surprisingly roomy.
sedan.exampleZh: 湯姆的兩門轎車出奇地寬敞。

vista.definition: A distant view or prospect, especially one seen through some opening, avenue or passage.
vista.example: a vista of pleasure to come

cubes.definition: A regular polyhedron having six identical square faces.

restrain.definition: To control or keep in check.

pinkie.definition: A little finger, the finger furthest on a hand from the thumb.
pinkie.example: I licked cream cheese off my pinkie.
pinkie.exampleZh: 我舔掉小指上的奶油乳酪。

uncommon.example: Bald eagles are an uncommon sighting in this state
uncommon.exampleZh: 在這個州，禿鷹並不常見

widely.definition: In a wide manner; across a wide area.

infiltrate.definition: Any undesirable substance or group of cells that has made its way into part of the body.
infiltrate.example: High-grade tumors often infiltrate surrounding structures.
infiltrate.exampleZh: 高級別腫瘤經常浸潤周圍結構。

sophomore.definition: A second-year undergraduate student in a college or university, or a second-year student in a four-year secondary school or high school.
sophomore.example: She was very mature for a sophomore and had several friends who were juniors or even seniors.
sophomore.exampleZh: 作為一名大二學生，她已經很成熟了，並且有幾個大三甚至大四的朋友。

wilder.exampleZh: 阿里·懷爾德，你這個小潑婦。

bailiff.definition: (law enforcement) An officer of the court, particularly:
bailiff.example: The defendant handed his paperwork to the bailiff rather than directly to the judge.
bailiff.exampleZh: 被告將其文件交給了法警，而不是直接交給了法官。

transmitted.definition: To send or convey something from one person, place or thing to another.

peril.definition: A situation of serious and immediate danger.
peril.example: Victory without peril is triumph without glory.
peril.exampleZh: 沒有危險的勝利就是沒有榮耀的勝利。

plaintiff.definition: A party bringing a suit in civil law against a defendant; accusers.
plaintiff.example: The plaintiff lost his motion for sanctions.
plaintiff.exampleZh: 原告的製裁動議失敗了。

bonnet.definition: A type of hat, once worn by women or children, held in place by ribbons tied under the chin.
bonnet.example: The bonnet was cool to the touch.

cheetah.definition: A distinctive member (Acinonyx jubatus) of the cat family, slightly smaller than the leopard, but with proportionately longer limbs and a smaller head. It is native to Africa and also credited with being the fastest terrestrial animal.
cheetah.example: The Saharan cheetah is seriously endangered.
cheetah.exampleZh: 撒哈拉獵豹已嚴重瀕臨絕種。

thicker.definition: Relatively great in extent from one surface to the opposite in its smallest solid dimension.
thicker.example: Condensed milk is thicker than normal milk.

accord.definition: Agreement or concurrence of opinion, will, or action.
accord.example: the accord of light and shade in painting

bullock.exampleZh: 他們討厭桑德拉布洛克。

clams.definition: Acronym of Clip-on Load Adjusting Mechanism. A device that can be fitted onto an oar to adjust set.
clams.example: Those sneakers cost me fifty clams!
clams.exampleZh: 那雙運動鞋花了我五十蛤！

binoculars.definition: A hand-held device consisting of a series of lenses and prisms, used to magnify objects so that they can be better seen from a distance, and looked at through both eyes.
binoculars.exampleZh: 齊裡抓起他的雙筒望遠鏡。

pinpoint.example: The investigators tried to pinpoint the source of the flames.
pinpoint.exampleZh: 調查人員試圖查明火焰的來源。

questionable.definition: Problematic; open to doubt or challenge.
questionable.example: It is questionable if the universe is open or closed.
questionable.exampleZh: 宇宙是開放的還是封閉的是值得懷疑的。

concierge.definition: One who attends to the wishes of hotel guests.
concierge.example: Our concierge team will locate the resources to fulfill your needs.
concierge.exampleZh: 我們的禮賓團隊將找到資源來滿足您的需求。

brook.definition: To use; enjoy; have the full employment of.
brook.example: I will not brook any disobedience.   I will brook no refusal.   I will brook no impertinence.
brook.exampleZh: 我不會容忍任何不服從的行為。   我不會容忍拒絕。   我不會容忍無禮的行為。

bodily.definition: Of, relating to, or concerning the body.
bodily.example: His bodily deficiencies were a heavy burden to him.
bodily.exampleZh: 身體上的缺陷對他來說是一個沉重的負擔。

hereafter.example: May Allah forgive his sins and elevate his rank in the hereafter.
hereafter.exampleZh: 願安拉寬恕他的罪孽並提高他在來世的地位。

dominion.definition: Power or the use of power; sovereignty over something; stewardship, supremacy.
dominion.example: the dominions of a king  the dominion of the passions
dominion.exampleZh: 國王的統治  激情的統治

hypnosis.definition: A trancelike state, artificially induced, in which a person has a heightened suggestibility, and in which suppressed memories may be experienced.
hypnosis.example: The hypnotist said, "All hypnosis is self hypnosis."
hypnosis.exampleZh: 催眠師說：“一切催眠都是自我催眠。”

civic.definition: Of, relating to, or belonging to a city, a citizen, or citizenship; municipal or civil.
civic.example: Thousands of people came to the Civic Center to show off their civic pride.
civic.exampleZh: 數千人來到市政中心展示他們的公民自豪感。

disability.definition: State of being disabled; deprivation or want of ability; absence of competent physical, intellectual, or moral power, means, fitness, and the like.

uncertainty.definition: Doubt; the condition of being uncertain or without conviction.
uncertainty.example: Every new chapter begins with uncertainty.
uncertainty.exampleZh: 每一個新的篇章都以不確定性開始。

housekeeping.definition: The chores of maintaining a house as a residence, especially cleaning.
housekeeping.example: The computer program does some general housekeeping involving initializing variables and opening files before beginning the main processing.
housekeeping.exampleZh: 電腦程式在開始主要處理之前執行一些常規內務處理，包括初始化變數和開啟檔案。

herring.definition: A type of small, oily fish of the genus Clupea, often used as food.
herring.example: He shoveled down the herring salad.

wonderland.definition: An imaginary or real place full of wonder or marvels.
wonderland.example: Alice will tell you that in Wonderland everything is topsy-turvy.
wonderland.exampleZh: 愛麗絲會告訴你，仙境裡的一切都是顛倒的。

andromeda.definition: Any shrub of the genus Pieris (family Ericaceae), having leathery leaves and small flowers.
andromeda.example: The Andromeda galaxy is the Milky Way’s biggest galactic neighbor.
andromeda.exampleZh: 仙女座星係是銀河系最大的銀河系鄰居。

pottery.definition: Fired ceramic wares that contain clay when formed.
pottery.example: The shelves were lined with pottery of all shapes and sizes.
pottery.exampleZh: 架子上擺滿了各種形狀和大小的陶器。

premium.example: There are many websites providing premium VR porn already.
premium.exampleZh: 已經有許多網站提供優質 VR 色情內容。

shrapnel.definition: An anti-personnel artillery shell used in WWI which carries a large number of individual bullets close to the target and then ejects them to allow them to continue along the shell's trajectory and strike the target individually.
shrapnel.example: The dog ate my sandwich, and there was shrapnel all over the place from him tearing open the bag.
shrapnel.exampleZh: 那隻狗吃了我的三明治，他撕開袋子，到處都是彈片。

planner.example: Johnny is a good planner. He starts his work in time to get it finished by the deadline.
planner.exampleZh: 約翰尼是一個很好的計劃者。他及時開始工作並在截止日期前完成。

broccoli.definition: A plant, Brassica oleracea var. italica, of the cabbage family, Brassicaceae; especially, the tree-shaped flower and stalk that are eaten as a vegetable.

jacked.example: He jacked the car up so that he could replace the brake pads.
jacked.exampleZh: 他把車頂起來，以便更換煞車片。

cactus.definition: Any member of the family Cactaceae, a family of flowering New World succulent plants suited to a hot, semi-desert climate.
cactus.example: Benedito's favorite plant is a cactus.
cactus.exampleZh: 貝內迪托最喜歡的植物是仙人掌。

unbelievably.definition: (manner) In a manner that one does not believe.
unbelievably.example: He gestured unbelievably. Everyone knew he was faking being sick.
unbelievably.exampleZh: 他做出難以置信的手勢。大家都知道他是裝病。

continuously.example: The river continuously flows to the sea, it will never pause to let us cross on dry land.
continuously.exampleZh: 河流不斷地流向大海，它永遠不會停下來讓我們在陸地上渡過。

fairness.definition: The property of being fair or equitable.
fairness.example: Some questioned the fairness of the new laws.
fairness.exampleZh: 有些人質疑新法的公平性。

motherland.example: Mother and motherland are greater than heaven.
motherland.exampleZh: 母親和祖國比天堂更偉大。

regretted.definition: To feel sorry about (a thing that has or has not happened), afterthink: to wish that a thing had not happened, that something else had happened instead.
regretted.exampleZh: 他對自己的話感到後悔。

narrative.definition: The systematic recitation of an event or series of events.
narrative.example: The plot is full of holes, but the narrative is extremely compelling.
narrative.exampleZh: 劇情漏洞百出，但敘事卻極為引人入勝。

monarch.definition: The ruler of an absolute monarchy or the head of state of a constitutional monarchy.

bonded.definition: To connect, secure or tie with a bond; to bind.
bonded.example: The gargantuan ape was bonded in iron chains and carted onto the stage.
bonded.exampleZh: 巨猿被綁在鐵鍊上，被推上舞台。

catcher.example: It happens that we have two copies of Catcher in the Rye.
catcher.exampleZh: 剛好我們有兩本《麥田裡的守望者》。

pretentious.example: Her dress was obviously more pretentious than comfortable.
pretentious.exampleZh: 她的穿著顯然比舒適更做作。

dictatorship.definition: A type of government where absolute sovereignty is allotted to an individual or a small clique.
dictatorship.exampleZh: 緬甸是一個軍事獨裁國家。

peaks.definition: A point; the sharp end or top of anything that terminates in a point; as, the peak, or front, of a cap.

shabby.example: They lived in a tiny apartment, with some old, shabby furniture.
shabby.exampleZh: 他們住在一間小公寓裡，裡面有一些破舊的家具。

jeopardize.definition: To put in jeopardy, to threaten.
jeopardize.example: We can't let anything jeopardize our mission tomorrow.
jeopardize.exampleZh: 我們不能讓任何事情危害我們明天的使命。

overrated.definition: To esteem too highly; to give greater praise than due.
overrated.example: My sister is an overrated singer.
overrated.exampleZh: 我姐姐是一位被高估的歌手。

horrid.example: The other girls in class are always horrid to Jane.
horrid.exampleZh: 班上的其他女孩總是讓簡感到害怕。

overdo.definition: To do too much; to exceed what is proper or true in doing; to carry too far.

rethink.definition: The act of thinking again about something.
rethink.example: This business plan of yours looks risky. It needs a rethink.
rethink.exampleZh: 你的這個商業計劃看起來有風險。它需要重新思考。

whine.definition: A long-drawn, high-pitched complaining cry or sound

unforgivable.exampleZh: 這種誹謗是不可原諒的。

rewind.example: I meant to pause the picture, but hit the rewind by mistake.
rewind.exampleZh: 本來想暫停畫面，卻誤按了倒帶鍵。

craziest.definition: Flawed or damaged; unsound, liable to break apart; ramshackle.
craziest.example: What's the craziest thing you've done this year?
craziest.exampleZh: 今年你做過最瘋狂的事是什麼？

interact.definition: A short act or piece between others, as in a play; a break between acts.

burgundy.definition: A variety of red wine from this region.
burgundy.example: Like most white Burgundy wine, it is made entirely from the Chardonnay grape.
burgundy.exampleZh: 與大多數勃根地白酒一樣，它完全由夏多內葡萄釀製而成。

trillion.definition: (short scale) A million million: 1 followed by twelve zeros, 1012.
trillion.example: The Andromeda Galaxy is about twenty trillion kilometers from Earth.
trillion.exampleZh: 仙女座星系距離地球約二十兆公里。

manhood.definition: The state or condition of being a human being
manhood.example: He feared the speedy decline of all manhood.
manhood.exampleZh: 他擔心所有男子氣概都會迅速衰退。

reservoir.definition: A place where anything is kept in store
reservoir.example: A reservoir was created to collect rainwater.
reservoir.exampleZh: 建造了一個水庫來收集雨水。

exhale.example: The earth exhales vapor; marshes exhale noxious effluvia.
exhale.exampleZh: 大地呼出水蒸氣；沼澤地呼出有毒的臭氣。

caucasian.definition: A native or inhabitant of the Caucasus.
caucasian.example: Sami taught in a predominantly Caucasian school.
caucasian.exampleZh: 薩米在一所以白人為主的學校任教。

matilda.definition: A bundle of possessions, often tied up in a sack; a swag.
matilda.example: John granted five charters to the City, and in this third charter he restored to the citizens two privileges, of which they had been deprived by Matilda and Henry II.
matilda.exampleZh: 約翰向這座城市授予了五項特許狀，在第三份特許狀中，他恢復了公民的兩項特權，這些特權被瑪蒂爾達和亨利二世剝奪了。

caramel.definition: A smooth, chewy, sticky confection made by heating sugar and other ingredients until the sugars polymerize and become sticky.
caramel.example: He drizzled caramel sauce over the cake.
caramel.exampleZh: 他把焦糖醬淋在蛋糕上。

slander.definition: A false or unsupported, malicious statement (spoken, not written), especially one which is injurious to a person's reputation; the making of such a statement.

rinse.example: I'll just give this knife a quick rinse.
rinse.exampleZh: 我會快速沖洗一下這把刀。

artwork.definition: A painting, drawing, sculpture or other piece of creative, visual art
artwork.example: The text has been reviewed, but the photographer hasn't delivered some of the artwork.
artwork.exampleZh: 文字已經審核，但攝影師尚未交付部分藝術作品。

stormy.example: a stormy season or a stormy day
stormy.exampleZh: 暴風雨的季節或暴風雨的日子

drunkard.definition: (somewhat derogatory) A person who is habitually drunk.

afghan.definition: A blanket or throw, usually crocheted or knitted.
afghan.example: Afghan democracy needs to be preserved even with nuclear bombs.
afghan.exampleZh: 即使使用核彈，阿富汗的民主也需要維護。

backside.definition: The back side of anything, the part opposite its front, particularly:
backside.example: The flaps and ailerons are connected to the backside of the wings.
backside.exampleZh: 襟翼和副翼連接到機翼的背面。

untouched.definition: Remaining in its original, pristine state, undamaged; not altered.
untouched.example: There are practically no untouched forests left in Europe.
untouched.exampleZh: 歐洲幾乎沒有未受破壞的森林。

wills.definition: One's independent faculty of choice; the ability to be able to exercise one's choice or intention.

tuxedo.definition: A typically black formal jacket worn by men.

bummed.definition: To sodomize; to engage in anal sex.

finer.example: Silt has a finer particle size than sand, but not as fine as clay.
finer.exampleZh: 淤泥的粒徑比沙子細，但不如黏土細。

manure.definition: Animal excrement, especially that of common domestic farm animals and when used as fertilizer. Generally speaking, from cows, horses, sheep, pigs and chickens.
manure.example: Fyodor shovels manure for a living.

monsignor.definition: An ecclesiastic title bestowed on some Roman Catholic clerics by the Pope
monsignor.exampleZh: 稱他為「大人」似乎太封建了。

seeker.example: Algeria accepted him as an asylum seeker.
seeker.exampleZh: 阿爾及利亞接受他作為尋求庇護者。

lending.definition: To allow to be used by someone temporarily, on condition that it or its equivalent will be returned.

postmortem.definition: An investigation of a corpse to determine the cause of death.
postmortem.example: The postmortem showed that she had been strangled.
postmortem.exampleZh: 驗屍顯示她是被勒死的。

dickens.example: She can go to the dickens for what she said.
dickens.exampleZh: 她可以去狄更斯那裡聽聽她說的話。

latch.definition: To close or lock as if with a latch.
latch.example: Ziri fixed the broken window latch.
latch.exampleZh: 齊裡修好了破損的窗閂。

oatmeal.definition: Meal made from rolled or round oats.
oatmeal.example: I bake oatmeal cookies every Monday.

midday.definition: Noon; twelve o'clock during the day

perks.example: Free coffee is one of the perks of the job.
perks.exampleZh: 免費咖啡是這份工作的福利之一。

outrun.definition: (sheepdog trials) The sheepdog's initial run towards the sheep, done in a curving motion so as not to startle them.
outrun.example: Can a tiger outrun a lion?

upload.example: You will have to convert your photograph to a jpg format if you wish to upload it.
upload.exampleZh: 如果您想上傳照片，則必須將其轉換為 jpg 格式。

bowie.example: David Bowie was born in south-east London.
bowie.exampleZh: 大衛·鮑伊出生於倫敦東南部。

copying.definition: To produce an object identical to a given object.

inland.definition: The interior part of a country.

bumping.definition: To knock against or run into with a jolt.

python.definition: A type of large constricting snake.

puberty.definition: A developmental phase brought about by the action of hormones as part of the maturing process. For humans, there are three in total.
puberty.exampleZh: 青春期會導致不可逆轉的身體變化。

exterminate.definition: To kill all of (a population of pests or undesirables), usually intentionally.
exterminate.example: We'll use poison to exterminate the rats.
exterminate.exampleZh: 我們要用毒藥消滅老鼠。

toots.definition: The noise of a horn or whistle.
toots.example: Toots Zynsky helped invent a special machine that produces the glass threads which are a signature of her pieces.
toots.exampleZh: Toots Zynsky 幫助發明了一種特殊的機器，可以生產玻璃絲，這是她作品的標誌。

cholesterol.definition: A sterol lipid synthesized by the liver and transported in the bloodstream to the membranes of all animal cells; it plays a central role in many biochemical processes and, as a lipoprotein that coats the walls of blood vessels, is associated with cardiovascular disease.
cholesterol.example: improve your cholesterol; lower your cholesterol
cholesterol.exampleZh: 改善你的膽固醇；降低膽固醇

methane.definition: The simplest aliphatic hydrocarbon, CH₄, being a constituent of natural gas, and one of the most abundant greenhouse gases.
methane.example: Cattle emit a large amount of methane.

sublime.example: The cities of planet Vulcan are more sublime than Earth's.
sublime.exampleZh: 瓦肯星球上的城市比地球上的城市更宏偉。

appendix.definition: Something attached to something else; an attachment or accompaniment.

aches.definition: Continued dull pain, as distinguished from sudden twinges, or spasmodic pain.

plank.definition: A long, broad and thick piece of timber, as opposed to a board which is less thick.
plank.example: Germanization was a central plank of German conservative thinking in the 19th and 20th centuries.
plank.exampleZh: 德國化是 19 世紀和 20 世紀德國保守主義思想的核心支柱。

loner.definition: One who is alone, lacking or avoiding the company of others.
loner.example: I actually enjoy being a loner.
loner.exampleZh: 我其實很享受獨處的感覺。

dashing.definition: To run quickly or for a short distance.

chevy.definition: A hunt or pursuit; a chase.
chevy.example: Rima and Skura drove a Chevy.
chevy.exampleZh: 裡瑪和斯庫拉開著一輛雪佛蘭。

miner.definition: A person who works in a mine.

mantle.definition: The shelf above a fireplace which may be also a structural support for the masonry of the chimney.
mantle.example: At the meeting, she finally assumed the mantle of leadership of the party.
mantle.exampleZh: 會上，她終於擔負起了黨的領導職務。

senile.example: My grandpa has become really senile these days.
senile.exampleZh: 這幾天我爺爺真的老了。

commando.definition: A small fighting force specially trained for making quick destructive raids against enemy-held areas.
commando.example: It's called commando when you don't wear underwear beneath your pants.
commando.exampleZh: 當你不在褲子下面穿內衣時，這被稱為突擊隊。

broth.definition: Water in which food (meat or vegetable etc) has been boiled.
broth.exampleZh: 當肉湯沸騰時，友誼就會綻放。

interaction.definition: The situation or occurrence in which two or more objects or events act upon one another to produce a new effect; the effect resulting from such a situation or occurrence.

sesame.definition: A tropical Asian plant (Sesamum indicum) bearing small flat seeds used as food and as a source of oil.
sesame.example: Sesame seeds should be ground and then eaten.

restraint.definition: Something that restrains, ties, fastens or secures
restraint.example: Institutionally, a major restraint is the copyright problem.
restraint.exampleZh: 從制度上來說，一個主要的限制是版權問題。

devour.definition: To eat quickly, greedily, hungrily, or ravenously.

respiratory.example: Algerian hospitals need more respiratory equipment.
respiratory.exampleZh: 阿爾及利亞醫院需要更多呼吸設備。

elope.definition: (of a married person) To run away from home with a paramour.

wreckage.definition: Something wrecked, especially the remains or debris of something which has been severely damaged or destroyed.
wreckage.example: Many survivors were rescued from the wreckage.
wreckage.exampleZh: 許多倖存者從廢墟中被救出。

lumber.definition: Old furniture or other items that take up room, or are stored away.
lumber.example: Nowadays, finding good lumber is hard.
lumber.exampleZh: 如今，尋找優質木材非常困難。

bologna.definition: A seasoned Italian sausage made from beef, pork or veal.
bologna.example: Nicolaus Copernicus studied canon law at the university of Bologna.
bologna.exampleZh: 尼古拉·哥白尼在博洛尼亞大學學習教會法。

mentality.definition: A mindset; a way of thinking; a set of beliefs.
mentality.example: Before he can succeed, he will have to shed the mentality that he can get by without hard work.
mentality.exampleZh: 在他能夠成功之前，他必須擺脫不勞而獲的心態。

celeste.definition: A musical instrument consisting principally of a set of graduated steel plates struck with hammers that are activated by a keyboard.
celeste.example: Celeste is making dumplings in the kitchen.
celeste.exampleZh: 塞萊斯特正在廚房包餃子。

meltdown.definition: Severe overheating of the core of a nuclear reactor resulting in the core melting and radiation escaping.
meltdown.example: Four years have passed since the meltdown at the Chernobyl nuclear plant, but the grim legacy of the Soviet catastrophe is still unfolding. https//web.archive.org/web/20041130094534/http://www.time.com/time/daily/chernobyl/chernobyl.index.html
meltdown.exampleZh: 切爾諾貝利核電廠熔毀已經過了四年，但蘇聯災難的可怕後果仍在顯現。 https://web.archive.org/web/20041130094534/http://www.time.com/time/daily/chernobyl/chernobyl.index.html

galactic.definition: Of or pertaining to milk, or the secretion of milk.

mastermind.definition: A person with an extraordinary intellect or skill that is markedly superior to his or her peers.
mastermind.exampleZh: 羅德里戈是該行動的策劃者。

strand.definition: The shore or beach of the sea or ocean; shore; beach.

burrito.definition: A Mexican dish consisting of a flour tortilla wrapped around a filling of meat and/or beans, cheese etc.
burrito.exampleZh: 他捲出了完美的墨西哥捲餅。

encrypted.definition: To conceal information by means of a code or cipher.

disciplinary.example: Debt can motivate or act as a disciplinary force for executives to achieve organizational efficiency.
disciplinary.exampleZh: 債務可以激勵或充當高階主管實現組織效率的紀律力量。

prosper.exampleZh: 阿爾及利亞經濟會繁榮嗎？

hardcore.definition: Broken bricks, stone and/or other aggregate used as foundations, especially in road and path laying.
hardcore.exampleZh: 他是一名鐵桿遊戲玩家。

vocabulary.definition: A usually alphabetized and explained collection of words e.g. of a particular field, or prepared for a specific purpose, often for learning.
vocabulary.example: My Russian vocabulary is very limited.
vocabulary.exampleZh: 我的俄語詞彙量非常有限。

vowed.definition: To make a vow; to promise.
vowed.exampleZh: 受委屈的女人發誓要報仇。

sling.definition: An instrument for throwing stones or other missiles, consisting of a short strap with two strings fastened to its ends, or with a string fastened to one end and a light stick to the other.

murderous.definition: Of, characterized by, or pertaining to murder or murderers.
murderous.example: She gave me a murderous look.
murderous.exampleZh: 她給了我一個充滿殺氣的眼神。

payday.definition: The day of the week or month, or the specific day, on which an employee's wages or salary is paid.

enable.definition: To make somebody able (to do, or to be, something); to give sufficient ability or power to do or to be; to give strength or ability to.
enable.exampleZh: 啟用基於密碼的身份驗證。

seaweed.definition: Any of numerous marine plants and algae, such as a kelp.
seaweed.example: Japanese sometimes put seaweed in their spaghetti dishes.
seaweed.exampleZh: 日本人有時會在義大利麵中加入海藻。

delirious.definition: Being in the state of delirium.

cyborg.definition: A person who is part machine, a robot who is part organic.
cyborg.example: Antonio demonstrated the superhuman strength of his cyborg.
cyborg.exampleZh: 安東尼奧展示了他的機器人的超人力量。

triumphant.exampleZh: 雅典娜，戰爭勝利的女僕…

hologram.definition: A three-dimensional image of an object created by holography.
hologram.example: Tom wasn't really performing at the concert. That was just his hologram.
hologram.exampleZh: 湯姆並沒有真正在音樂會上表演。那隻是他的全息圖。

hauling.definition: To transport by drawing or pulling, as with horses or oxen, or a motor vehicle.

projection.definition: Something which projects, protrudes, juts out, sticks out, or stands out.

creed.definition: That which is believed; accepted doctrine, especially religious doctrine; a particular set of beliefs; any summary of principles or opinions professed or adhered to.
creed.example: A creed is a manifesto of religious or spiritual beliefs
creed.exampleZh: 信條是宗教或精神信仰的宣言

acquitted.definition: To declare or find innocent or not guilty.
acquitted.example: The jury acquitted the prisoner of the charge.

meddle.definition: To interfere in or with; to concern oneself with unduly.

horseman.definition: A man who rides a horse.
horseman.example: Being a good horseman doesn't suffice in order to be a good leader.
horseman.exampleZh: 僅僅成為一名優秀的騎手並不足以成為一名優秀的領導者。

avalanche.definition: A large mass or body of snow and ice sliding swiftly down a mountain side, or falling down a precipice.

eyeball.example: We had an eyeball last year.

urgency.definition: The quality or condition of being urgent

bearer.definition: One who, or that which, bears, sustains, or carries.
bearer.example: I promise to pay the bearer on demand.
bearer.exampleZh: 我保證按要求向持票人付款。

fleas.definition: To cause to fly; put to flight; drive off (by frightening).

bonfire.definition: A fire in which bones are burned.
bonfire.example: Lorenzo was still sitting by the bonfire.
bonfire.exampleZh: 洛倫佐仍然坐在篝火旁。

capitalist.definition: A person who is a supporter of capitalism.

mercer.definition: A merchant dealing in fabrics and textiles, especially silks and other fine cloths.
mercer.example: Mr. Mercer, who in 1846 was the principal civil officer of Government at Badulla, sent me a jagged fragment of an elephant’s tusk, about five inches in diameter, and weighing between twenty and thirty pounds, which had been brought to him by some natives, who, being attracted by a noise in the jungle, witnessed a combat between a tusker and one without tusks, and saw the latter with his trunk seize one of the tusks of his antagonist and wrench from it the portion in question, which measured two feet in length.
mercer.exampleZh: 默瑟先生於 1846 年擔任巴杜勒政府首席文職官員，他寄給我一塊鋸齒狀的象牙碎片，直徑約 5 英寸，重量在 20 到 30 磅之間，這是一些當地人給他帶來的，他們被叢林中的噪音所吸引，目睹了有像牙和沒有像牙的人之間的戰鬥，並看到後者用鼻子上了一根牙齒的長度。

stomp.definition: A deliberate heavy footfall; a stamp.
stomp.example: She obliterated the cockroach with one stomp.
stomp.exampleZh: 她一腳就把蟑螂踩死了。

tempo.example: Beethoven's tempo was strange because his metronome was broken.
tempo.exampleZh: 貝多芬的節奏很奇怪，因為他的節拍器壞了。

chime.definition: A musical instrument producing a sound when struck, similar to a bell (e.g. a tubular metal bar) or actually a bell. Often used in the plural to refer to the set: the chimes.
chime.example: Hugo was a chime player in the school orchestra.
chime.exampleZh: 雨果是學校管弦樂團的編鐘手。

bumpy.definition: Rough; jumpy; causing or characterized by jolts and irregular movements
bumpy.example: Hang on tight. Bumpy road ahead.

festive.definition: Having the atmosphere, decoration, or attitude of a festival, holiday, or celebration.
festive.example: The room was decked out in festive streamers, with flowers everywhere.
festive.exampleZh: 房間裡掛滿了節慶的彩帶，到處都是花。

oppression.definition: The exercise of authority or power in a burdensome, cruel, or unjust manner.
oppression.example: The oppression of the poor by the aristocracy was one cause of the French Revolution.
oppression.exampleZh: 貴族對窮人的壓迫是法國大革命的原因之一。

mayhem.definition: A state or situation of great confusion, disorder, trouble or destruction; chaos.
mayhem.example: In all the mayhem, some children were separated from their partners.
mayhem.exampleZh: 在所有的混亂中，有些孩子與他們的伴侶失散了。

cheeseburger.definition: A hamburger containing cheese (usually one or more slices of processed cheese).
cheeseburger.exampleZh: 我的腳踏車是一個起司漢堡。

freelance.definition: Someone who sells their services to clients without a long-term employment contract.
freelance.example: He was a freelance writer for several magazines.
freelance.exampleZh: 他是多家雜誌的自由撰稿人。

zodiac.definition: The belt-like region of the celestial sphere approximately eight degrees north and south of the ecliptic which include the apparent path of the sun, moon, and visible planets.
zodiac.example: There's no classier zodiac sign than Capricorns themselves.
zodiac.exampleZh: 沒有比摩羯座更優雅的星座了。

whatnot.definition: A small unspecified object; bric-a-brac (in plural)
whatnot.example: All of the cakes and whatnot have been laid out, ready for the children's birthday party this afternoon.
whatnot.exampleZh: 所有的蛋糕什麼的都已經擺好了，準備今天下午孩子們的生日派對。

cylinder.definition: A surface created by projecting a closed two-dimensional curve along an axis intersecting the plane of the curve.
cylinder.example: When the two-dimensional curve is a circle, the cylinder is called a circular cylinder. When the axis is perpendicular to the plane of the curve, the cylinder is called a right cylinder. In non-mathematical usage, both right and circular are usually implied.
cylinder.exampleZh: 當二維曲線為圓時，圓柱體稱為圓柱體。當軸線垂直於曲線平面時，圓柱稱為直圓柱。在非數學用法中，通常隱含右式和循環式。

scots.definition: A local tax, paid originally to the lord or ruler and later to a sheriff.
scots.example: Do the Scots have their own language?
scots.exampleZh: 蘇格蘭人有自己的語言嗎？

holed.definition: To make holes in (an object or surface).
holed.exampleZh: 彈片在船體上留下了一個洞。

pouch.definition: A small bag usually closed with a drawstring.
pouch.example: The joey snuggled down into the warm kangaroo pouch of his mother.
pouch.exampleZh: 小袋鼠依偎在媽媽溫暖的袋鼠育兒袋裡。

takeoff.definition: The rising or ascent of an aircraft or rocket into flight.
takeoff.example: The flight was smooth, but the takeoff was a little rough.
takeoff.exampleZh: 飛行很順利，但起飛時有點困難。

encore.definition: A brief extra performance, done after the main performance is complete.
encore.example: Can I get an encore? We want more!
encore.exampleZh: 我可以再演一次嗎？我們想要更多！

blindfold.definition: A covering, usually a bandage, for the eyes, blocking light to the eyes.
blindfold.example: I put a blindfold over my boyfriend's eyes and told him I had a surprise for him.
blindfold.exampleZh: 我用眼罩蒙住男友的眼睛，告訴他我要給他一個驚喜。

phenomena.definition: A thing or being, event or process, perceptible through senses; or a fact or occurrence thereof.
phenomena.example: Extreme weather phenomena are becoming ever more frequent.
phenomena.exampleZh: 極端天氣現象變得越來越頻繁。

precedent.definition: An act in the past which may be used as an example to help decide the outcome of similar instances in the future.

schmuck.definition: A jerk; a person who is unlikable, detestable, or contemptible because he or she is stupid, foolish, clumsy, oafish, inept, malicious, or unpleasant.
schmuck.example: A schmuck is someone who is easily taken advantage of.
schmuck.exampleZh: schmuck 是指很容易被利用的人。

hatchet.definition: A small light axe with a short handle; a tomahawk.
hatchet.example: Sami and Layla buried their hatchet.

pollen.definition: A fine granular substance produced in flowers. Technically a collective term for pollen grains (microspores) produced in the anthers of flowering plants. (This specific usage dating from mid 18th century.)
pollen.example: "...and ther was good wyne of Gascoyne,... as well of pollen, as of other vitailes..." Froissart, Jean, 1338?-1410?; Berners, John Bourchier, Lord https//archive.org/details/chroniclefroiss00froigoog
pollen.exampleZh: 「……還有加斯科因的好酒……以及花粉和其他維泰爾……」Froissart，Jean，1338？ -1410？ ；伯納斯，約翰·布爾奇耶勳爵 https://archive.org/details/chroniclefroiss00froigoog

statute.definition: Written law, as laid down by the legislature.
statute.example: The statute of limitations has already passed for this crime.
statute.exampleZh: 該罪名已經過了訴訟時效。

dialed.definition: To control or select something with a dial, or (figuratively) as if with a dial.

penitentiary.definition: A state or federal prison for convicted felons; (broadly) a prison.
penitentiary.example: Daniel was imprisoned for thirty-five years in that penitentiary.
penitentiary.exampleZh: 丹尼爾在監獄裡被監禁了三十五年。

combo.example: The jazz combo played nightly at the little restaurant.
combo.exampleZh: 爵士樂組合每晚都會在小餐廳演奏。

coping.definition: To deal effectively with something, especially if difficult.

hypothetically.definition: In a hypothetical way; as a hypothesis.
hypothetically.exampleZh: 他提出了假設性的建議。

mayonnaise.definition: A dressing made from vegetable oil, raw egg yolks and seasoning, used on salads, with french fries, in sandwiches etc.
mayonnaise.example: We served a lobster mayonnaise as a starter.
mayonnaise.exampleZh: 我們提供了龍蝦蛋黃醬作為開胃菜。

bowel.definition: A part or division of the intestines, usually the large intestine.
bowel.example: Tom was diagnosed with irritable bowel syndrome.
bowel.exampleZh: 湯姆被診斷出患有腸躁症。

evolutionary.definition: Of or relating to the biological theory of evolution.
evolutionary.example: The evolutionary history of marine mammals includes land-dwelling ancestors.
evolutionary.exampleZh: 海洋哺乳動物的演化史包括陸地祖先。

vineyard.definition: A grape plantation, especially one used in the production of wine.
vineyard.example: The vineyard of Château Margaux stands as the producer of one of the world's greatest and most sought-after red wines.
